title: "Human-Centric SAR Image Colorization for Intuitive Geospatial Decision-Making"
authors: Vijay A Sangolgi, Mithun B Patil, Rajrajeshwar S Margam, Manav Mule, Viresh Monappa Sutar, Gaurav N Biradar
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) images are, in principle, full of details of their structures, but these images are usually presented in grayscale, which makes their reading and understanding less straightforward to human observers. The research called "SAR Image Colorization for Comprehensive Insight using Deep Learning Model (h)", is about making the interpretation of SAR data easier by assigning colors to the grayscale images of SAR data through these advanced deep learning techniques. The fresh and innovative model (h) embraces the use of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) and generative models to explore the relationship between SAR intensity features and natural color distributions. This simple colorization step goes beyond the user-friendliness of the image by making it easier for object recognition, terrain classification, and disaster monitoring also. A matching dataset of SAR and optical images is used for training the model, so that the color translation is not only correct but it also respects the context. The grayscale SAR images are converted into three-dimensional realistic images that allow intuitive decision-making in remote sensing applications. The study is an example of deep learning helping human perception to reach out to raw SAR data and making complex geospatial information, thus, more straightforward and more easily manageable.

title: Framework for Gym Posture Estimation Using MediaPipe and OpenCV
authors: Koushal Patidar, Mukesh A Zaveri
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: This paper presents real time gym posture monitoring system that leverages computer vision technique for precise gym exercise evaluation. The proposed framework utilizes MediaPipe for accurate pose estimation by detecting key body landmarks and OpenCV for image processing and visualization. The system is capable of tracking user movements, calculating joint angles using trigonometric formulations, and providing real-time feedback during common strength training exercises such as bicep curls, squats, deadlifts, lateral raises, and planks. Each exercise is evaluated based on specific biomechanical angle thresholds, and a weighted accuracy mechanism is introduced to prioritize critical joints for each movement. The system classifies each frame into three posture categories: correct, needs improvement, or incorrect, allowing fine-grained form assessment beyond simple repetition counting. Experimental evaluations across multiple videos demonstrate the system's capability to operate in diverse lighting and camera conditions, although some limitations remain in exercises involving occluded joints or improper camera alignment.

title: Resilient Zero-Emission Energy Hubs: a Multi-Layer Control Approach
authors: Arian Zahedmanesh, Kashem M. Muttaqi, Hafiz Bilal Qadir, Shahab Pasha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper proposes a zero-emissions energy hub (ZEEH) as a utility-scale, resilient energy center operating exclusively on variable renewable energy (VRE) and green storage/conversion technologies. Hydrogen electrolysis, storage, cogeneration, and vehicle-to-grid (V2G) integration are employed to enhance demand flexibility and resilience. A multi-layer receding horizon (MRH) control framework is developed for quasi-real-time energy management and market participation, incorporating transfer learning to mitigate data scarcity, improve forecasting accuracy, and reduce computational complexity. The ZEEH is modeled as a DC microgrid, with advanced droop-based control strategies to manage active/reactive power and mitigate voltage variations under uncertain grid conditions. Case studies show that the MRH effectively co-optimizes electricity, heat, and hydrogen to meet demand with zero emissions, demonstrating the ZEEH's potential as a flexible, utility-scale green energy system.

title: Intelligent SDN Traffic Engineering with MADRL
authors: Aarush Dev, Suchismita Rout, Harish Kumar Patnaik, Satyendr Singh, Sudhansu Shekhar Patra
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: A growth in the rate of network traffic and the growing complexity of applications has demonstrated inherent weaknesses in traditional software-defined networking (SDN) approaches to traffic engineering. This research suggests a new Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning (MADRL) system to be complemented with the advanced ensemble machinelearning methods to form a smart SDN controller with the ability to find an optimal set of traffic-engineering policies using its progressive interaction with the environment. It penetrates into issues of network traffic engineering by the application of a quite elaborate multi-model ensemble consisting of Random Forest, Extra Trees, Gradient Boosting, XGBoost, LightGBM, Ridge Regression, and Elastic Net algorithms in order to reduce latency, increase throughput, improve predictivity of congestion, and the effectiveness of load balancing in various network topologies. The successful implementation enjoys excellent <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$</tex> scores, in fact, close to 1, throughout all the testing stages thereby achieving almost perfect prediction accuracy of network optimization parameters while at the same time, they deliver great improvements in the key metrics of SDN such as an average throughput increase of 97.2 %, congestion decrease by 85.6 %, and latency overhead kept to a minimum for all the tested network scenarios.

title: Intelligent Fundus Image Analysis for Early Detection and Classification of Diabetic Retinopathy
authors: Mekala Bhaskar, Gona Suruchi, Macharla Ashwini, Kota Dhruthi, Kothapalli Ramya, Polu Srinivas Reddy
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Retina Scope is an advanced artificial intelligence (AI) tool designed to assist in the early detection of retinal diseases through deep learning techniques. It identifies initial indicators of conditions such as diabetic retinopathy, which, if left untreated, may result in vision loss or blindness. The system analyzes retinal images captured by fundus cameras and highlights potential anomalies for further examination by medical specialists. By prioritizing high-risk cases, Retina Scope helps reduce the diagnostic burden on ophthalmologists, enabling faster and more accurate decision-making. This approach supports early intervention, which can significantly improve patient outcomes. The overarching goal of this development is to create a scalable and reliable solution that contributes to the advancement of ophthalmology and enhances the reach of telemedicine services.

title: Adaptive Fuzzy-Tuned Series Resistor for Fault Ride-Through in DFIG-Based Wind Turbines
authors: Subhendu Sekhar Sahoo, Kalyan Cha tterjee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Doubly Fed Induction Generator (DFIG)-based wind turbines are highly sensitive to grid disturbances, particularly voltage dips during faults, which may trigger disconnection and compromise grid stability. This paper presents an Adaptive Fuzzy-Tuned Series Resistor (AFSR) strategy for effective Fault Ride-Through (FRT) enhancement in DFIG systems. By dynamically adjusting the external resistance in the rotor circuit using a fuzzy logic controller, the proposed method suppresses rotor overcurrents and maintains operational integrity during grid faults. Detailed modeling of the DFIG, comprehensive FRT methodology, and extensive simulation results validate the effectiveness of the AFSR approach. Comparative analysis demonstrates improved transient stability, reduced torque oscillations, and rapid post-fault recovery, making it a robust and intelligent solution for modern wind power integration.

title: Scalable Tea Leaf Disease Detection Using a Fine-Tuned EfficientNetV2B3 Model
authors: G Sunil, Shanvi Chauhan, Saurabh Fulara, Chandra Kishore
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Tea is a significant beverage to the world and farming industry of tea has continued playing a key role in supporting many agricultural economies in the world. Tea planting industry has its share of challenges as the tea plants are attacked by reasons such as foliar diseases, which pose a threat to the quality of the yields as well as the earnings of their farming community. These diseases can be rapidly identified, which helps sustainable agriculture methods and supports the level of food security. Recently developed research based on EfficientNetV2B3 develops an automated tea leaf disease classification system. The namespace analysis employed 885 publicly available pictures which included eight classes of plant diseases including Red leaf spot, Algal leaf spot, Birds eyespot, Gray blight, White spot, Anthracnose, Brown blight and healthy leaves. The researchers employed TensorFlow dataset pipeline which allowed them to perform preprocessing on their dataset by way of resizing and allowed caching, prefetching and shuffling of their data, after which they divided the data into 708 training images and 177 validation images. The suggested model obtained accurate identification in 87% of the cases when used in the validation set and this proves the effectiveness of the combined model in working with agriculture. The system development will be beneficial in the disease monitoring intelligence besides supporting the causes of good health as well as responsible usage and sustainable crop production that makes it a promising tool in cementing the tea agriculture systems.

title: PokeClinicX: Enhancing Recruitment Rate in Clinical Trials through Predictive Modeling
authors: Aayush Sharma, Yashita, Yash Tripathi, Chetna Sharma, Ankita Sharma, Mukesh Kumar Verma
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The recruitment of participants in clinical trials remains one of the key bottlenecks in medical research. Conventional recruitment estimation methods guarantee unreliable estimates for recruitment rates (RRs), leading to inefficiency in planning and executing clinical trials. Using a predictive modeling approach that estimates RRs from multi-source cross-sectional data with socio-demographic and psychological elements, PokeClinicX is addressing this gap. Through machine learning enhanced algorithms, the system captures and estimates hidden relationships between characteristics of the sample population that influence voluntary subject participation. This application will ultimately produce more accurate and generalizable recruitment estimates than traditional statistical methods.The modelling approach is two-phased. The first phase is an external model, where each tuple of data is classified to some kind of Pokémon type and takes into account their psychological and behavioral features. By classifying tuples of data to Pokémon types, it patterns within the –source cross sectional data allowing for the envision development variability in recruitment patterns. The second phase must conduct internal modelling in which to build separate predictive models for each Pokémon type in order to predict an RR they may display specific to that population segment. Furthermore, to avoid duplication, starting the analysis with all Pokémon types and conditionally eliminating in case one display unreasonable recruitment patterns should be consider in the modelling database.

title: Predicting Heart Attack Risk From Retinal Fundus Images by Using the EfficientNet-B0 Model
authors: Kailasam T, Jeya Selvan M, Shalini A, Madhan M
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases remain a leading cause of mortality worldwide, with early detection being critical for effective intervention. The non-invasive nature of retinal fundus imaging allows doctors to identify microvascular alterations, which serve as indicators for assessing the risk of developing coronary heart disease. In this study, a novel framework was proposed for predicting the risk of heart attack using retinal fundus images combined with EfficientNet-B0, a deep convolutional neural network architecture optimized for both accuracy and computational efficiency. Fundus images were collected and preprocessed through resizing, normalization, and data augmentation techniques to enhance model generalization. Key vascular and structural features were extracted and transformed into feature embeddings, which were then fed into the EfficientNet-B0 model to generate hierarchical feature maps. Fully connected layers with ReLU activation and a final SoftMax classifier were employed to predict high- and low-risk cardiac conditions. The model was trained and validated using annotated retinal datasets from clinical studies spanning multiple years, with cross-fold validation applied to ensure robustness. The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed framework achieved high predictive performance, capturing subtle vascular abnormalities correlated with cardiovascular risk. The system achieves an accuracy rate of 98.92%, indicating that it can serve as a non-invasive, automated screening system for detecting cardiovascular problems in their early stages. The results demonstrate that combining retinal imaging with deep learning technology produces effective methods for early cardiac health monitoring.

title: Performance Analysis of Low RCS Structures Based on Dielectric Patterning for Radar Stealth Applications
authors: Harikrishna Paik, G. Neeraja Rani, Nettem Adithya Valli, Sivangi Ravikanth, R. Manikandan, Jyothi Prasad M
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: This paper proposes a low radar cross-section (RCS) metallic conductor plate using the dielectric loading technique for modern radar systems. The loading of the dielectric slabs with different patterns of appropriate thickness and other electrical properties leads to phase cancellation of the reflected EM wave, and hence, optimal RCS suppression of the target is achieved. The RCS reduction performance is analyzed for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90 \text{mm} \times 90 \text{mm}$</tex> conductor plate operating at X and Ku bands with hybrid, stepped hybrid, and checkerboard loading configurations. Rigorous parametric studies are carried out to examine the RCS suppression behaviour of a metallic target. The results show wide band RCS reduction in the range 7.68-18.92 GHz and 7.5-16.5 GHz for the hybrid and stepped hybrid loading structures, respectively. Peak RCS reduction of more than −23 dB has been achieved under normal incidence. For the oblique incidence, the angular stability up to 60° is observed for both TE and TM mode polarizations. The presented design's simple and planar configuration, reduced height, and ease of fabrication are the attractive features that may make it suitable for stealth applications in advanced radar systems.

title: IoT-Enabled Accident Prevention System: Real-Time Detection of Sleep Deprivation, Alcohol Influence, and Heart Attack Symptoms
authors: Boopathi Raja G, Karthika R, Karthikeyan K G, Pavithra S P, Sandeep T, Divyaa N
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: One major global safety concern is traffic accidents brought on by drunk drivers, sleepy drivers, and unexpected medical conditions like heart attacks. Long-distance driving and nighttime driving are common times for fatigue-related crashes, and drinking alcohol reduces cognitive function and reaction time, which raises the risk of accidents. Unexpected cardiac episodes while driving might also have serious repercussions if they are not treated right away. Automotive safety has advanced, but identifying and reducing these human elements is still a major problem, particularly in real-time situations. Current driver safety systems usually concentrate on identifying a particular risk factor, like drunk driving or sleepiness. While alcohol detection systems use breath analyzers built into the ignition system, drowsiness detection frequently uses eye-tracking cameras or steering behaviour analysis. Nevertheless, there are drawbacks to these techniques, like the need for the driver to actively blow into the sensor or their sensitivity to sunlight. Furthermore, most traditional systems lack real-time health monitoring to identify crises like heart attacks. They may therefore be unable to appropriately address intricate, multifaceted risk scenarios that arise while driving and lack complete coverage. The technology instantly turns on a parking light and safely stops the vehicle if the driver falls asleep, gets drunk, or exhibits symptoms of a heart attack. Through the simultaneous management of several risk factors, this technology dramatically enhances driver safety and lowers the likelihood of accidents.

title: Evaluating CNN and Transfer Learning Models for Handwritten Odia Character Recognition
authors: Rabinarayan Panda, Sachikanta Dash, Sasmita Padhy, ShazidWahid Khandakhani, Ramarao Nayapamu
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The most recent breakthroughs in Generative AI made it possible to create models that can do more complicated things like pattern recognition and classifying images. The application of the deep learning and transfer learning methods to the Odia Handwritten Character Dataset (OHCD) is discussed in this paper. Transfer learning is important in that it can help resolve cross-domain problems, such as knowledge acquired with models trained on large-scale datasets can be transferred to the Odia script domain, thus enhancing performance with small annotated datasets. Two benchmark datasets were created and released to help this study: OHCD_GIETV1 and OHCD_GIETV2. The standard, pre-trained models VGG16 and ResNet were utilized, and the VGG16 was first utilized as a feature extractor. Custom dense layers were introduced on top and the model was finally optimized with Adam optimizer in 20 epochs. The ImageDataGenerator was used to extend the data augmentation with shearing, zooming, and horizontal flipping. InceptionV3 could show competitive results, but it is not accurate enough to implement it. To address this shortcoming, a custom CNN was developed adding several convolutional layers, max-pooling, flattening, and dense layers, and longer training periods to learn more deeply. Besides, VGG16 as the base layer in transfer learning was further optimized to increase the features extraction and the accuracy of classification. The evaluation included the accuracy and loss measures and the results obtained indicated that the Voiceless character dataset combined with VGG16 obtained accuracy measures, which were near to 96%, and this proves that transfer learning works in Odia handwritten character recognition.

title: CityGuard: A Forensic Investigation Framework for Smart Cities
authors: Md Ismail Hossain, Ragib Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: Smart cities rely heavily on interconnected cyber physical systems to manage infrastructure, transportation, public safety, and environmental monitoring. These systems generate vast amounts of data and are prone to failures, attacks, and disputes arising from unexpected incidents. However, current smart city infrastructures lack a unified forensic investigation framework to collect, store, and verify critical event data securely. In this paper, we propose CityGuard, a forensic investigation framework designed for smart cities. CityGuard securely collects logs from diverse urban modules such as traffic signals, surveillance systems, public transport IoT, and environmental sensors. The framework ensures the integrity and confidentiality of these logs using cryptographic techniques, including hash chains and Bloom filters, while supporting tamper evident and privacy aware storage. Additionally, CityGuard allows authorized investigators to verify logs and reconstruct incident timelines accurately. We implement a prototype using real-world smart city data and evaluate its performance regarding log integrity verification, storage overhead, and proof creation time. Our results demonstrate that CityGuard can operate efficiently within a smart city ecosystem and provide trustworthy forensic capabilities for urban safety, compliance, and post-incident analysis.

title: Optimized ADMET-Driven Molecular Design Using GRU-GCN Architecture with Multi-Objective Optimization
authors: Sapna Kushwah, M. Chidhvilas Tanay, Avadh Kishor, Pramod Kumar Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: High failure rates due to inadequate ADMET (Absorption, Distribution, Metabolism, Excretion, and Toxicity) profiles are often a challenging factor in drug discovery, affecting the clinical success of potential drugs. In this paper, we introduce an improved ADMET-driven reconstruction methodology employing a GRU/GCN-based encoder and GRU-based decoder architecture, optimized through multi-objective optimization (MOO) methods. Our proposed model maps SMILES (Simplified Molecular Input Line Entry System) representations of compounds into latent vectors and reconstructs valid chemical compounds from there. The innovation is the combination of multi-objective optimization, while balancing four key components, namely, reconstruction precision, structural confirmation, chemical validity, and improved ADMET properties. Experimental analysis shows the model's better performance in predicting drug-like molecules with better ADMET properties compared with traditional approaches. This paper presents a novel methodology for the drug discovery process, with the combination of deep learning (DL) and MOO ensuring chemical feasibility and pharmacological interest.

title: Task-Specific Resource Orchestration for Effective Concurrent Heterogeneous Task Completion in ISCC Systems
authors: Yu Ding, Yangting Chen, Weidang Lu, Nan Zhao, Xianbin Wang
abstract: Effective provision of integrated sensing, communication, and computation (ISCC) services in future networks will inevitably increase their operational complexity. The distinct requirements of diverse tasks for tailored ISCC devices further exacerbate the challenge of adaptively allocating constrained resources among concurrent tasks. To address these difficulties, a task-specific joint resource orchestration scheme is proposed in this paper to enhance the effectiveness of ISCC operation and heterogeneous tasks completion. Specifically, the completion of concurrent heterogeneous tasks by different devices relies on the task-specific sharing of limited resource among sensing, real-time data computing and delay-tolerant data processing. Consequently, a value of multi-task completion (VoC) indicator is designed to connect and balance among the diverse demands from concurrent tasks, including computing rate, time delay, and sensing performance. The VoC is then maximized by collaborative optimization of multi-dimensional resources, including transmit beamformer, local and offloading CPU-cycle frequency, data factor assignment and computation capacity. To solve this challenging optimization problem with the lack of close-form solution and coupling of multi-variables, we first transform it into an equivalent form that is tractable to handle. Next, the problem is decomposed into several subproblems, which can be approximately solved by iterative updates. Simulation results demonstrate the performance enhancement of the proposed scheme is superior to the benchmarks.

title: Low Energy Building Management Using Arduino Microcontroller
authors: Chamakura Krishna Reddy, Rampelli Manojkumar, Prasanta Kumar Jena, M. Prashanth, Balusu Srinivasa Rao, C Venkata Subba Reddy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)

title: Two-Stage Coordinated Fault Recovery in Active Distribution Networks with Energy Storage and Controllable Loads
authors: Huo Jian, Zhou Chunsheng, Liang Haowen, Chen Kuo, Li Zhuqing, Zhang Ruizhe, Qiao Xueyang, Xia Shiwei
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: In active distribution networks (ADNs), diverse resources including distributed generation (DG), energy storage systems (ESS), and controllable loads (CL) offer significant flexibility for post-fault self-healing via islanding. Traditional recovery strategies, however, fail to coordinately utilize these resources and rely on deficient viability criteria, resulting in unreliable restoration outcomes. To address these issues, this paper proposes a two-stage fault recovery method for ADNs that integrates ESS and CL. In the first stage, stable islands are constructed: “island cores“ are initially formed by matching CL nodes with the nearest reliability nodes (DG/ESS). Subsequently, a priority-driven expansion is performed, where each incremental step is validated by multi-dimensional constraints to progressively form stable islands of maximum size. In the second stage, network reconfiguration is performed: for the remaining de-energized areas, a multi-objective optimization model is formulated to maximize restoration benefits while minimizing response costs. The model optimizes tie-switch statuses and the dispatch of ESS and CL to achieve an economically optimal restoration. Case study results demonstrate that the proposed method fully leverages the synergistic support potential of flexible resources, yielding restoration plans superior to conventional approaches in both the dynamic stability of islands and overall economic efficiency.

title: Integrating Machine Learning and Statistical Methods: A Hybrid SVM-KNN Model for Rainfall Prediction
authors: S Sudhakar Reddy, D. Victorseelan, Babitha N, S. Sathiyavathi, Medha V. Deshpande, Manu Y. M
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Agriculture is the backbone of India's economy, and rainfall prediction is a key component of it. Forecasting rainfall accurately helps farmers plan for weather conditions by giving them timely information on projected precipitation, which is a crucial element impacting crop yields. The availability of accurate rainfall data allows for more informed crop planning, which in turn boosts agricultural output and the economy. A new feature selection strategy based on the CFS wrapper architecture is introduced in this research, along with data preprocessing techniques including cleaning and normalisation. This methodology is resilient. The KNN classifier uses two separate training datasets generated using Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifiers, which are trained using either a binary or one-class technique. After that, the KNN model is built and adjusted so that it performs the classification task as well as possible. They test the hybrid SVKNN classifier on various datasets and measure its performance. It combines SVM and KNN techniques. Achieving a high classification accuracy of 96.83% in the experiments proves that the suggested model is successful. This study offers a substantial addition to agricultural planning and decision-making in India by demonstrating the practical applicability of the SVKNN model for rainfall prediction.

title: Evolution of English Literature in the Age of Artificial Intelligence: challenges and Opportunities with socio-cultural conflicts
authors: Kartikey, Gopal Krishna, Anika Kumari, Sakshi Koli, Shravan Kumar, Rashmi Chaudhary, Kapil Joshi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Artificial learning has had a significant impact on English literature and has changed literary creation and analysis. In the digital age, literature writing, interpretation, and preservation have been reconstructed using AI mechanisms. This research investigates the difficulties and prospects associated with AI in English literature, emphasising the implications of originality and authorship, as well as the position of human creativity in AI-augmented literary manuscripts. This paper reviews the current literature and discusses the potential impact of AI-generated literature, the use of machine learning for literary analysis, and ethical considerations in AI-generated stories, and investigates existing research studies and AI-based litmus models. By unlocking text secrets, AI will analyse text to find themes, patterns, and sentiments, but it will also help authors generate stories automatically. However, there are still concerns over authenticity, partiality, and the potential for traditional literary skills to deteriorate. Engage with AI, teach it to fulfill your requirements, surpass your expectations, and take chances you wouldn't otherwise take. For this purpose, a balanced use of AI in literature is beneficial, as it does not compromise literary exploration but upholds human originality. Future research should focus on reducing the biases of AI generation, enhancing AI literary model interpretability, and guaranteeing moral authorship in AI-guided literary works.

title: Cambricon-GS: An Accelerator for 3D Gaussian Splatting Training With Gaussian-Pixel Hybrid Parallelism
authors: Rui Wen, Zhifei Yue, Tianbo Liu, Xinkai Song, Jin Li, Di Huang, Jiaming Guo, Xing Hu, Zidong Du, Qi Guo, Tianshi Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) is a breakthrough in 3D reconstruction using 3D Gaussians. However, even on highend GPUs like the NVIDIA A100, reconstructing complex scenes remains time-consuming, taking over 15 minutes. The main bottleneck is α-computation, which accounts for 71.25 % of training workload, yet 93.03 % of it is invalid due to the localized influence of Gaussians. To address this issue, we propose Cambricon-GS, an accelerator for 3DGS training with Gaussian-Pixel hybrid parallelism. At the software level, we introduce a hybrid parallel workflow that breaks the limitation of conventional pixel-only parallelism through two key techniques: Center-Pixel Gaussian Culling (CPGC), which eliminates invalid Gaussians early, and SeedDriven Gaussian Region Exploration (SDGRE), which reduces invalid computation for partially valid Gaussians by selectively exploring valid regions. Overall, the workflow significantly reduces α computations, lowering the workload to 17.99 %. At the hardware level, Cambricon-GS decouples α-computation and α blending into GUnits and PUnits, organized in a 2D mesh-based NoC that supports asynchronous execution and efficient data routing. We further boost performance via Gaussian/Pixel load balancing and tiled SSIM based pipelining. The evaluation results show that Cambricon-GS achieves 19.63×, 14.86×, 15.42×, 2.98× and 2.63× speedup, and 78.62×, 63.00×, 61.72×, 3.89× and 3.22× energy saving, compared to A100, GSCore, GBU, GSArch, and GauSPU, respectively, with negligible image quality loss.

title: Thyroid Disease Identification via EfficientNetB2: Leveraging Deep Neural Networks for Early Detection
authors: Komuravelly Sudheer Kumar, Goldy Verma, Shraddha Kaparwan, Royal Madan
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Hyperthyroidism and hypothyroidism are thyroid diseases that can harm human health by influencing the brain, heart and ability to have children. Correctly identifying these disorders in the early stages allows patients to recover faster and gets treatment underway more efficiently. Typically, doctors order imaging and blood tests for diagnosis and each can be time-consuming and costly. It is suggested that using a modified version of EfficientNetB2 for deep learning will improve and simplify how thyroid disorders are diagnosed. The model was set up with two divisions—0 for healthy and 1 for thyroid illness—using photos of more than 7,200 different thyroid conditions. Making some changes to the data, transforming the images, keeping them the same size and enriching it resulted in a robust model. On the assessment of thyroid illness and healthy groups, the EfficientNet B2 model performed extremely well and was accurate 90% of the time. Using a confusion matrix, loss curves and classification criteria, the performance of the model was evaluated, leading to a well-balanced strategy that helps avoid many unneeded positive or negative results. This work supports the healthcare sector by providing easy access to early detection, encouraging health care and encouraging the development of sustainable medical methods. The findings prove the instrument can detect thyroid illnesses early, as EfficientNetB2 is an automation method that runs fast and is capable of scaling up.

title: Performance of Fast Electric Vehicle Charging Stations by Energy Storage Devices based Grid Connected Hybrid Renewable Energy Sources
authors: N. Kiran, Shannmukha Naga Raju Vonteddu, Ajay Babu Bathula, Srinivasa Rao Burri, Vipashi Kansal, S. Senthil Kumar, B. Rajagopal Reddy, Ajay Sudhir Bale, Siva Ganesh Malla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Electric Vehicles (EVs) are becoming a trending concept in present research fields. Many kinds of EVs are developed worldwide and are running successfully on roads. Hence, charging stations must be established at many places and also highly required to improve their performance as well as maintenance. An efficient as well as fast charging station is proposed in this paper where powered by grid connected hybrid renewable energy sources. A detailed mathematical analysis is compressed to make this work more effective. Loads at different locations across the south India is considered while developing the proposed method. Considering the availability of energy resources in those regions, their dynamism, and techno-economic feasibility, a hybrid grid-connected renewable energy system, complete with a battery storage unit and power conversion module, has been designed for the proposed charging stations. These sites/places are also connected to ecofriendly EV charging stations, which come with various incentives. The optimization results indicate that after incorporating on-grid hybrid renewable energy resources, there is a significant reduction in key performance metrics. Specifically, the Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) has decreased by 87.50%, and the Net Present Cost (NPC) has dropped by 95.26%. This translates to a reduction in LCOE from ${\$}$ 0. 0 3 3 8 4 per kWh to ${\$}$ 0. 0 0 4 0 7 per kWh, and a decrease in NPC from ${\$}$0.09172 to ${\$}$0.00435 million, respectively.

title: Two-Stage Critical Contingency Assessment for Power System Security Enhancement
authors: Yunqi Wang, Ghulam Mohy-ud-din, Rui Jiang, Hakan Ergun
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Reliable operation of modern power systems requires prompt identification of critical contingencies that could induce voltage collapse. Conventional contingency screening methods based on static power flow and voltage stability indices (VSIs) efficiently rank outage scenarios, but they cannot capture transient voltage dynamics. On the other hand, time-domain simulations offer accurate insight into post-disturbance voltage recovery at the cost of heavy computation. This paper presents a two-stage critical contingency assessment framework focused on voltage stability. In the first stage, a fast VSI-based screening of all single line and generator outages is performed using continuity AC power flow analysis, ranking contingencies with poor voltage stability margins. In the second stage, only the screened critical cases are subjected to detailed dynamic simulations to evaluate transient voltage stability under fault-clearance conditions. The proposed two-stage static-dynamic approach drastically reduces simulation burden while maintaining accuracy in identifying voltage collapse-prone events. Case studies on the WSCC 9-bus system demonstrate that the method filters out non-threatening contigency and reliably spots the truly critical contingencies, guiding timely preventative control actions.

title: Hybrid Particle Swarm and Gravitational Search Optimization for Intelligent Battery Health Estimation
authors: M. S. Hossain Lipu, Md Ibrahim, Shaheer Ansari, Kashem Muttaqi, Danny Sutanto
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper presents a novel hybrid optimization framework for data-driven estimation of the state of health (SoH) of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs). Existing data-driven SoH estimation methods struggle to select suitable model hyperparameters that consider capacity regeneration phenomena and to identify meaningful data samples from LIB parameters. The specific contribution to this research lies in the integrated optimization strategy, which bridges the limitations mentioned. The dataset has 31 features, comprising MIT-Stanford lithium-ion battery profiles. It employs an intelligent hybrid approach that combines the gravitational search algorithm (GSA) with particle swarm optimization (PSO) to fine-tune recurrent neural network (RNN) parameters, such as the hidden layer neurons and learning rate. The combined GSA and PSO algorithms integrated with RNN improve the SoH estimation accuracy through better search efficiency and faster convergence. The 31 LIB parameter samples are closely linked to capacity degradation and are well-suited to form the data framework. The proposed model demonstrates high accuracy in SoH estimation, particularly when applied to cell c33 from MIT-Stanford lithium-ion battery profiles. Results show that the RNN optimized with the GSA-PSO algorithm for the c33 dataset achieved a mean squared error (MSE) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.30 \times 10^{-8}$</tex>, a root mean square error (RMSE) of approximately 0.0142, and a mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 0.0096.

title: Smart Farming Assistant: A Multilingual AI-Powered Crop Recommendation System Using Real-Time Weather and Soil Data
authors: Vigneshkumar M, N.R. Sathis Kumar, V. Sahana, R. Harini, S. Muthulakshmi, K. Roshini
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Many emerging economies still rely heavily on agriculture, but small-scale farmers sometimes encounter obstacles like unpredictability in the weather, a lack of knowledge about soil health, and restricted access to market and crop-related information. In order to empower farmers by offering real-time, location-specific crop recommendations, this research describes the design and implementation of a Smart Farming Assistant, a multilingual, AI-powered web application created with Streamlit. In order to provide precise, timely recommendations that assist farmers in choosing the best crop to grow, the system combines a number of data sources, such as soil condition metrics (Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium, pH, and rainfall) and real-time meteorological data from the WeatherAPI.The machine learning module at the heart of the application uses Random Forest and Decision Tree classifiers that have been trained on a carefully selected dataset that includes weather and soil conditions matched to the best crop varieties. Real-time recommendations are made possible by the model's ability to dynamically adjust to current climatic variables. Farmers can enter their location and soil conditions to obtain pertinent agricultural insights thanks to the application's user-friendly and intuitive layout. The solution's compatibility with the Sustainable Development Goal (SDG) 2: Zero Hunger of the UN, which emphasizes raising revenue and productivity through smart agricultural methods, is a crucial component. Future extensibility of the backend includes possible integration with real-time market updates, satellite imagery for precision farming, and APIs for real soil data (Soil Grids).

title: Resilient Power Distribution Systems Through Risk-Informed Metrics and Strategic Sectionalizing Switching
authors: Ali Reza Kheirkhah, Jamshid Aghaei, Kianoush Emami, Hassan Baji, Sanath Alahakoon, Piet Janse Van Rensburg, Jonatas Boas Leite
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The work presents a structured framework to assess investment decisions aimed at improving the resilience of power distribution networks. The central strategy quantifies the economic impact of energy not supplied through Monte Carlo trials across various outage scenarios. The method also models probabilistic vulnerability, particularly for rare but high-impact events. To mitigate these risks, a genetic algorithm is employed for optimal switch relocation, and a smart grid application is integrated to support restoration strategies. The framework is validated on a real-world 136-bus test system, demonstrating its effectiveness in guiding resilient and cost-efficient operational decisions.

title: V-Rex: Real-Time Streaming Video LLM Acceleration via Dynamic KV Cache Retrieval
authors: Donghyuk Kim, Sejeong Yang, Wonjin Shin, Joo-Young Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Streaming video large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for real-time multimodal tasks such as video captioning, question answering, conversational agents, and augmented reality. However, these models face fundamental memory and computational challenges because their key-value (KV) caches grow substantially with continuous streaming video input. This process requires an iterative prefill stage, which is a unique feature of streaming video LLMs. Prior works reduce excessive cache overhead by utilizing the KV cache retrieval algorithm, which offloads the full KV cache to CPU memory or storage, then selectively fetches the most relevant entries. Nevertheless, due to its iterative prefill stage, they suffer from significant limitations, including extensive computation, substantial data transfer, and degradation in accuracy. Crucially, this issue is exacerbated for edge deployment, which is the primary target for these models. The memory footprint exceeds the memory capacity within minutes of video streams, making low-latency, energy-efficient inference infeasible. In this work, we propose V-Rex, the first software-hardware co-designed accelerator that comprehensively addresses both algorithmic and hardware bottlenecks in streaming video LLM inference. At its core, V-Rex introduces ReSV, a training-free dynamic KV cache retrieval algorithm. ReSV exploits temporal and spatial similarity-based token clustering to reduce excessive KV cache memory across video frames, and dynamically adjusts token selection per transformer layer and attention head to minimize the number of selected tokens. To fully realize these algorithmic benefits, V-Rex offers a compact, low-latency hardware accelerator with a dynamic KV cache retrieval engine (DRE), featuring bit-level and early-exit based computing units, as well as hierarchical KV cache memory management. Evaluated on COIN benchmarks, V-Rex achieves unprecedented real-time of 3.9-8.3 FPS and energy-efficient streaming video LLM inference on edge deployment with negligible accuracy loss. While DRE only accounts for 2.2% power and 2.0% area, the system delivers 1.9-19.7× speedup and 3.1-18.5× energy efficiency improvements over AGX Orin GPU. This work is the first to comprehensively tackle KV cache retrieval across algorithm and hardware, enabling real-time streaming video LLM inference on resource-constrained edge devices, with clear potential for scalable deployment in large-scale server environments.

title: Energy Management System Using AWS IoT
authors: Sohail Akhtar, Anjali Gupta, Rahul Choudhary, Gaurav Raj
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Effective energy consumption management is the key to ensuring environmental sustainability and minimizing operational expenditure in residential, commercial, and industrial sectors. The current study describes the design and development of an IoT-based Energy Management System (EMS) using Amazon Web Services (AWS) for real-time monitoring, analysis, and automated alerts. The system retrieves energy consumption data via an MQTT-based publisher-subscriber model using AWS IoT Core. AWS Lambda functions treat the data, which are kept in Amazon DynamoDB for long-term, scalable, and queryable analysis. Real-time logging and visualization are offered by Amazon CloudWatch through a specific dashboard, allowing dynamic monitoring of consumption behaviors. Additionally, a special CloudWatch alarm identifies unusual usage and activates Amazon Simple Notification Service (SNS) to notify users with instant email updates. The combination of monitoring, storage, visualization, and proactive alerts facilitates timely decision-making and anomaly detection. The system provides a robust foundation for future integration of artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) models to support predictive analytics and multi-location optimization.

title: Enhancing Short-Term Photovoltaic Cluster Power Forecasting via Spatiotemporal Generalized Weather Patterns Recognition and Dynamic Graph Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Zhijun Zhao, Haonan Dai, Yuqing Wang, Fei Wang, Gang Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Due to the wide geographical coverage of the PV cluster, the power stations in it are easy to be in different weather types, resulting in varying output characteristics. However, because the cluster-scale weather patterns do not exist physically, it is difficult to comprehensively consider the different weather types of the power stations in the cluster and classify the patterns to improve the forecasting ability of the model. There is still a blank in this field at present. To fill this gap, this paper proposes a short-term PV clusters power forecasting method based on spatiotemporal generalized weather patterns recognition and dynamic graph convolutional neural networks. Firstly, the irradiance at the cluster scale is defined, and the similarity between forecasted and historical measured irradiance is calculated to identify similar daily power. Secondly, the Gaussian Mixture Model clustering algorithm is applied to classify the historical power data of the PV cluster, and the future weather patterns of the cluster are forecasted based on the similar daily power and the classification model. Finally, the short-term power forecasting model of dynamic graph convolutional neural network is constructed to capture the dynamic spatio-temporal correlations in the cluster. The validity of the proposed method is verified by the data of a PV cluster in northern China.

title: AI-Driven Safety Monitoring for Industrial Boilers: Early Fault Detection with Contrastive Learning
authors: Binaman Amuri, Fatima Tu Zahra, Mujdat Soyturk
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: In most Industry 4.0 systems, fault detection typically begins only after components start to malfunction, by then, performance and safety may already be compromised. This is especially critical in industrial boilers, which are essential to manufacturing and process heating. Faults such as excess air, fouling, and scaling can lead to serious safety risks like combustion instability and overheating, while also reducing energy efficiency by hindering heat transfer and increasing fuel consumption. This research proposes a contrastive learning-based anomaly detection approach designed to detect early signs of deviation, before faults become severe enough to impact system functionality. Unlike traditional threshold-based methods, which often raise alarms too late, our method learns patterns of normal behavior and identifies subtle shifts that indicate a developing problem. The model is trained on synthetic data generated using a Matlab/Simulinkbased Simscape model of the Viessmann Vitorond 200 Gas-Fired Boiler VD2 Series 380, simulating both healthy and faulty operational scenarios. By distinguishing between safe and unsafe operating patterns at an early stage, the system enables timely safety interventions and identifies inefficient operating modes that lead to wasted energy. Experimental results show that our approach detects emerging faults more quickly than conventional methods, enabling predictive maintenance and realtime optimization. This contributes to safer, more reliable, and energy-efficient boiler operations.

title: Split Federated Learning-Enabled Deep Q-Networks for Generalized Path Planning in Distributed IoT Edge Platform
authors: Hyeongjin Kim, Jalel Ben-Othman, Byungju Lee, Hyunbum Kim
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) devices and edge-platform robots require privacy-aware path planning under strict compute and bandwidth constraints, where continual on-device learning from real-world data is desirable but costly. We propose and evaluate Split Federated Learning (SFL)–enabled Deep Q-Networks (DQN), termed SFL-DQN, which partitions the Q-network into a lightweight on-device encoder and a shared server-side action–value head with online/target stabilization. Devices keep raw observations local and exchange only intermediate feature representations and their gradients; periodic Federated Averaging (FedAvg) realigns client encoders to mitigate representation drift under heterogeneous, non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) experience. In grid-world navigation with one moving obstacle, SFL-DQN matches a non-federated, non-split DQN baseline in episodic return and task success and attains Breadth-First Search (BFS)–equivalent shortest-path optimality on held-out maps. With K clients, each device uses only 1/K of the single-device interaction budget (e.g., 1,000 vs. 4,000 episodes when K=4), reducing the client-side training burden by offloading the value-learning head to the server. Split–federated training introduces feature-level communication overhead and does not necessarily reduce system-wide energy under all network conditions; instead, SFL-DQN enables a practical edge–cloud learning loop without raw-data aggregation.

title: mCardiacDx: Radar-Driven Contactless Monitoring and Diagnosis of Atrial Fibrillation
authors: Arjun Kumar, Noppanat Wadlom, Jaeheon Kwak, Si-Hyuck Kang, Insik Shin
publish: IEEE Journal of Translational Engineering in Health and Medicine
abstract: Arrhythmia is a common cardiac condition that can precipitate severe complications without timely intervention. Among them, atrial fibrillation (AF) is the most common form. While continuous monitoring is essential for timely diagnosis, conventional approaches such as electrocardiogram (ECG) and wearable devices are constrained by their reliance on specialized medical expertise and patient discomfort from their contact nature. Existing contactless monitoring, primarily designed for healthy subjects, face significant challenges when analyzing reflections from AF patients due to disrupted spatial stability and temporal consistency caused by underlying irregular heart contractions. In this paper, we introduce mCardiacDx, a radar-driven contactless system that accurately analyzes these complex reflections and reconstructs heart pulse waveforms (HPWs) for AF monitoring and diagnosis. The key technical contributions of our work include a novel precise target localization (PTL) technique that accurately locates heart reflections despite spatial disruptions, an encoder-decoder model (HPR-Net) that effectively transforms these reflections into HPWs, addressing temporal inconsistencies, and a final analysis module for AF monitoring and diagnosis. Our evaluation on a dataset of 48 subjects (24 healthy, 24 with AF) in a seated, normal breathing, real-world setting shows that both mCardiacDx and the PTL technique significantly outperform the state-of-the-art approach in monitoring and diagnosing AF. Objective: To develop a contactless radar-driven system, mCardiacDx, that overcomes reflection disruption challenges in AF patients to accurately reconstruct interpretable heart pulse waveforms (HPWs) for monitoring and diagnosis. Methods and procedures: We introduce a PTL technique to locate heart reflections despite spatial disruptions, and an encoder-decoder model (HPR-Net) to robustly process reflections and reconstruct interpretable HPWs, addressing temporal inconsistencies. The HPWs are then processed by a final analysis module for AF monitoring and diagnosis. mCardiacDx is validated against a state-of-the-art approach (baseline) on a dataset of 48 subjects (24 healthy, 24 with AF) in a seated, normal breathing, real-world setting. This validation confirms the system’s robustness and generalizability to real-world seated scenarios variations in posture and environment. Results: mCardiacDx significantly outperforms the baseline in both monitoring and diagnosis. HPW fidelity ( Dynamic time warping (DTW) score) for AF patients improves from 5.92 to 2.92. HR/RR interval median absolute percentage error (MedAPE) reduced (e.g., HR from 9.10 % to 2.94 %; RR interval from 8.42 % to 2.95 %). Our system achieves superior diagnostic performance with 0.93 accuracy, and 0.91 recall (sensitivity), significantly surpassing the baseline’s accuracy of 0.85 and recall of 0.75, while both maintain a specificity of 0.96. Conclusion: mCardiacDx is a robust, non-contact system for continuous cardiac care, addressing a critical gap in real-world AF monitoring and diagnosis. Clinical impact: mCardiacDx offers a passive and continuous solution for AF monitoring and diagnosis that reduces patient discomfort from wearable devices and enabling consistent detection of cardiac events in home or clinical settings. Clinical and Translational Impact Statement: mCardiacDx provides a non-contact solution for AF monitoring and diagnosis, addressing the critical limitation of existing wearable and contactless devices, and demonstrating high potential for passive, continuous integration into regular home cardiac care.
