title: FACE: Fully Overlapped PD Scheduling and Multi-Level Architecture Co-Exploration on Wafer
authors: Zheng Xu, Dehao Kong, Jiaxin Liu, Dingcheng Jiang, Xu Dai, Jinyi Deng, Yang Hu, Shouyi Yin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: The rapid expansion of large language models (LLMs) parameter scales imposes unprecedented demands on compute, memory, and communication resources for inference deployment. Wafer-scale chips, leveraging advanced packaging technologies, deliver high-density integration of compute and memory with high die-to-die (D2D) communication bandwidth, providing a compelling architectural approach to satisfy these resource requirements. However, its unprecedented chip area introduces significant architectural design complexities. Waferscale chips feature a multi-level architecture spanning the wafer, die, and core levels, involving numerous critical design parameters and trade-offs, which still lack systematic understanding and exploration. Moreover, this poses major challenges for LLM serving scheduling. Existing methods, largely adapted from GPUbased systems, fail to fully leverage the advantages of waferscale chips and mitigate their limitations, making it difficult to efficiently translate massive hardware resources into actual performance gains. To address these challenges, we introduce FACE, a coexploration framework for jointly optimizing multi-level architecture and serving scheduling. We first establish a flexible and extensible wafer-scale hardware template to systematically explore the optimal architecture and micro-architecture parameters. Leveraging the fine-grained control and high interconnect bandwidth of wafer-scale chips, FACE implements an LLM scheduling strategy that achieves fully overlapped prefill-decode execution and efficient KV cache management, maximizing hardware resource utilization to improve LLM service quality. Our evaluation demonstrates that FACE can achieve an average overall performance improvement of 3.68 × across various LLM models and datasets compared to the state-of-the-art (SOTA) LLM serving system on wafer-scale chips.

title: SAGe: A Lightweight Algorithm-Architecture Co-Design for Mitigating the Data Preparation Bottleneck in Large-Scale Genome Sequence Analysis
authors: Nika Mansouri Ghiasi, Talu Güloglu, Harun Mustafa, Can Firtina, Konstantina Koliogeorgi, Konstantinos Kanellopoulos, Haiyu Mao, Rakesh Nadig, Mohammad Sadrosadati, Jisung Park, Onur Mutlu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Genome sequence analysis, which examines the DNA sequences of organisms, drives advances in many critical medical and biotechnological fields. Given its importance and the exponentially growing volumes of genomic sequence data, there are extensive efforts to accelerate genome sequence analysis. In this work, we demonstrate a major bottleneck that greatly limits and diminishes the benefits of state-of-the-art genome sequence analysis accelerators: the data preparation bottleneck, where genomic sequence data is stored in compressed form and needs to be first decompressed and formatted before an accelerator can operate on it. To mitigate this bottleneck, we propose SAGe, an algorithm-architecture co-design for highly-compressed storage and high-performance a ccess of large-scale genomic sequence data. The key challenge is to improve data preparation performance while maintaining high compression ratios (comparable to genomic-specific compression algorithms) at low hardware cost. We address this challenge by leveraging key properties of genomic datasets to co-design (i) a lossless (de)compression algorithm, (ii) hardware that decompresses data with lightweight operations and efficient streaming accesses, (iii) storage data layout, and (iv) interface commands to access data. SAGe is highly versatile, as it supports datasets from different sequencing technologies and species. Due to its lightweight design, SAGe can be seamlessly integrated with a broad range of hardware accelerators for genome sequence analysis to mitigate their data preparation bottlenecks. Our results demonstrate that SAGe improves the average end-to-end performance and energy efficiency of two state-of-the-art genome sequence analysis accelerators by 3.0×-32.1× and 13.0×-34.0×, respectively, compared to when the accelerators rely on state-of-the-art software and hardware decompression tools.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning Approaches for Healthcare Fraud Detection: A Review, Case Study, and Framework
authors: Parasdeep, Gitanjali Arora, Raghav Mehra, Suresh Kaswan, Dharmesha Upadhaya, Kangan Soni
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Healthcare fraud imposes a significant financial burden on payers worldwide, yet most detection systems still rely on static rule sets that struggle to capture evolving fraud patterns. In recent years, Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) have shown clear advantages by learning complex behaviors from claims, provider, and utilization data. This paper presents a structured review of 42 studies published between 2015 and 2025, examining supervised, unsupervised, and hybrid approaches for healthcare fraud detection. The review highlights consistent performance gains from ensemble methods but also identifies recurring gaps, including limited external validation, sparse use of privacy-preserving collaboration, and weak linkage between model outputs and operational return on investment (ROI). Unlike prior surveys, this work contributes in three distinct ways. First, it synthesizes evidence with attention to real-world constraints such as interpretability, drift, and investigative capacity. Second, it includes a small-scale case study using the Kaggle Healthcare Fraud dataset, where Random Forest, XGBoost, and Isolation Forest models were compared; ensemble methods achieved the best balance of accuracy, recall, and F1-score. Finally, it proposes a deployable reference framework that integrates preprocessing, hybrid models, explainability, and federated options for secure collaboration. Together, these contributions provide both a comprehensive overview and actionable guidance for building scalable, investigator-friendly fraud detection systems.

title: Default Loan Prediction Using Machine Learning
authors: Ashok Kumar Bandla, V N S R Murthy, B. Patrisamma
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Predicting loan default poses an important problem for financial institutions, since it has direct consequences for assessing credit risk, protect profitability and the performance of the loan portfolio itself. Traditionally, models used only credit scores and the financial history of a borrower, which never fully captured the complexity and non-linearity of borrower behavior. For this reason, poor predictions and financial risks were likely to occur. This study sets forth a loan default predictive system based on advanced machine learning models, including ensemble models such as Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Voting Classifier, and Stacking Classifier to maximize predictive accuracy. The dataset was located from Kaggle and contained approximately 4000 instances and consisted of the demographic characteristics of the borrower, financial history, attributes of the loan, and other characteristics which are categorical. To improve the model, data preprocessing measures took place, including label encoding, polynomial feature creation, normalizing data, and handling missing data. In consideration of the imbalance of the classes, SMOTETomek was used to create a sampling method that would balance the representation of defaulters and non-defaulters. Out of all the models experimented with, Stacking Classifier was the most accurate with a score of 93.46%, and outperformed all the models of Logistic Regression (69.32%), Random Forest (92.61%), Gradient Boosting (81.82%), XGboost (69.89%) and Decision Tree (86.65%). Stacking Classifier was shown to also have positive scores in Precision, Recall, and F1 across both classes as well. Therefore, it can be concluded that ensemble learning is a useful method to improve . Keywords: Loan Default, Machine Learning, Financial Risk, Credit Score, Random Forest, Logistic Regression, Decision Trees, Gradient Boosting, Stacking Classifier, SMOTETomek, Flask Web Application, Loan Eligibility Prediction.

title: Stage-Wise Power Loss Dissection of Totem-Pole Bridgeless PFC: From Simulation to Real-Time HIL Validation
authors: Prachi Wadhwaan, Prateek Nigam, Kishor Thakre
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Diode bridge rectifier (DBR) based boost converters are the conventional choice in AC-DC systems for power factor correction, but nearly 48% of conduction losses arise from the combined DBR and boost diode. This paper presents a detailed design and comparative power loss analysis of the totem-pole bridgeless boost converter against the conventional DBR-boost circuit. The study highlights its capability to reduce conduction losses, mitigate voltage stress, and enhance efficiency, with further evaluation of interleaving benefits. The design and performance of the converter are validated through MATLAB/Simulink simulations and real-time hardware-in-the-loop testing using OPAL-RT. Comparative stage-wise power loss analysis demonstrates the superior performance of the totem-pole configuration in terms of unity power factor achievement and ripple-free DC-link voltage generation. The results also provide a conclusive assessment for comparing efficiency of the two converters by evaluating power losses in each individual component of both the converters.

title: A Hybrid Sensor–Image Framework for Smart Farming Using Deep and Ensemble Learning
authors: J Umamageswaran, J Collin Ryan, Shashini Vasudevan
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Smart farming is vital in combating the agricultural challenges that the world faces through the use of sustainable resource management. Unfortunately, most of the current machine learning systems are only capable of handling unimodal data and single-model approaches, which limits their adaptability and scalability. This research introduces a multi-task ensemble-based framework that merges farm image data with IoT sensor streams to improve crop–weed classification and environmental anomaly detection.Visual features are derived from the use of the pre-trained CNNs (ResNet50, VGG16), whereas the sensor data are the parameters like temperature, humidity, CO, LPG, and smoke. The merged feature space is of 4610 dimensions, including statistical descriptors and temporal patterns. Simple models—Logistic Regression, Ridge Classifier, KNN, Random Forest, and Extra Trees—are combined with voting and stacking ensembles, trained with 5-fold stratified cross-validation and normalization-based preprocessing.The innovative method obtains the F1-scores of 0.9667 for multi-class weed–crop classification, 0.9956 for binary crop–weed discrimination, and an accuracy of 0.9889 for anomaly detection, which proves that the ensemble integration of CNN feature vectors and sensor data is a powerful, efficient, and effective approach for precision agriculture.

title: A Dynamic Average Consensus-Based Distributed Control for DC Microgrids Using Virtual Power Tracking
authors: Zuliang Huang, Zhao Yang Dong, Yuechuan Tao, Shuying Lai, Tianjing Wang, Zuqing Zheng, Tong Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The distributed control plays an increasingly important role in energy storage systems (ESSs) of DC microgrids due to its inherent plug-and-play nature. In this paper, a novel distributed control strategy for virtual power tracking is introduced, leveraging a discrete-time dynamic average consensus algorithm. This approach enables the accomplishment of precise current sharing, bus voltage restoration, and state-of-charge (SOC) balancing without reliance on droop control mechanisms. Moreover, the proposed second-order average consensus algorithm can obtain accurate global average information in networks with variable communication delay. Through this algorithm, each local controller transmits the discrete signal to its neighbors and processes the received information discretely. Hence, low communication is guaranteed. The effectiveness of the presented strategy is validated through simulations conducted in MATLAB/SIMULINK.

title: ML-Driven Prediction of Invasive Mechanical Ventilation in Sepsis: Insights from a tertiary care hospital in India
authors: Shivi Mendiratta, Vinay Gandhi Mukkelli, Esha Baidya Kayal, Puneet Khanna, Amit Mehndiratta
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: In critical illness, particularly in low- and middle-income country Intensive Care Units (ICUs), artificial intelligence (AI) can enable early prediction of invasive ventilation before clinical deterioration. This study developed machine learning based model to predict invasive mechanical ventilation requirements in patients with sepsis. Dataset of 1013 patients and 104 features obtained from AIIMS, Delhi ICUs was used for modelling. Features were selected using SHAP and seven machine-learning models were developed on 70% dataset with five-fold cross-validation, followed by testing on the remaining 30%. The best performing model was observed to be GentleBoost-Ensemble achieving test results as accuracy 88.5%, AUROC 0.94, sensitivity 90.8%, specificity 86.2%, PPV 86.8% and NPV 90.3%. Top SHAP selected features were Glasgow Coma Scale (11.44%), PCO2 (8.55%), Vasopressor dose (6.06%), SOFA (5.09%) and pH (4.84%). These findings highlight the potential of interpretable AI-models to support early, data-driven intubation decisions in septic patients that may improve clinical outcomes.

title: Detection and Classification of Microbial Quality for Milk Products based on Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Premananda Sahu, Srikanta Kumar Mohapatra, Jayashree Mohanty, Diksha Thakur, Ashwani K Sharma, Alok Kumar Agrawal
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The commonly used microbiological detection methods are labor demanding, time consuming and inappropriate when dealing with large scale quality monitoring in dairy industries due to their inability to monitor the quality in real-time. To mediate such issues, this study suggests a combined deep learning model entailing both Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) and Recurrent Neural Networks (RNN) to detect and classify microbial quality of milk products. The dataset includes 12,245 samples, which are classified into the fermented and the spoilage classes, and was obtained both on the publicly available repositories and in laboratory tests. The CNN part and the RNN part have the role of extracting spatial features as well as capturing temporal dependencies and this results in better performance in classification. Experimental data show that the proposed scheme has 98.64 % accuracy, F1-scores greater than 97%, and it outperforms the standard machine learning models, SVM, Random Forest, and XGBoost. The suggested method is non-destructive, non-costly, and extremely scalable to the purpose of real-time milk quality procuring. Also, the framework could be easily incorporated into the automation of dairy production lines, where the framework has a high potential to be used as a tool in preserving food safety and further automation of food processing plants.

title: Modelling the Impact of ESG-Bond Issuance on Corporate Credit Ratings and Cost of Capital Using Econometric and Machine-Learning Approaches
authors: D. Anjaneyalu, M. V. Subramanyam, A. K. Neeraja Rani, Shaik Inthiyaz, G. Siva Prasad, P. Sanjay Kumar
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Bond issuance associated with Environmental, Social and Governmental (ESG) has become a very important strategic tool, which involves companies pursuing greater financial security, indicating sustainability, and obtaining lower-cost capital. The paper represents a modeling of the effect of ESG-bond issues on corporate credit ratings and cost of capital, combining econometric panel-data modeling with state-of-the-art machine-learning architectures. The study separates the causal impacts of sustainability-related debt upon creditworthiness with the help of fixed effects estimators, ordered logit specifications, and counterfactual prediction designs. The data is captured by complementary machine-learn methods (such as gradient boosting and explainability methods) that capture non-linear interactions and effects of ESG attributes amongst firm financials and bond-market behavior. Findings suggest that the ESG-themed bond issuers of firms can also attain significant but statistically significant credit rating improvements, mainly due to decreased perceived transition risk and increased disclosure quality. At the same time, the weighted average cost of capital of ESG-active companies falls, caused by lower yield spreads and a positive investor screening. The incorporation of explainable AI solutions also reveal the important predictors of the sustainability risk scores, leverage dynamics, and bond maturity profiles. The results provide a contribution of policy value to the financial components of sustainable debt markets and the changing function of AI-enhanced modeling in credit evaluation frameworks.

title: Fish Farm Water Quality Monitoring System Using IoT
authors: Ashutosh Satapathy, Timmala Hrutik, Gorli Uday Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Aquaculture is concerned with developing an IoT-based smart monitoring system that will enhance fish health and performance. Water quality is an important contributing factor to the health and performance of farmed fish, as their entire life is based on the water quality in which they live. To tackle the issues related to maintaining good quality water, a unique IoT-based fish farm water quality monitoring system is proposed. Conventional water quality monitoring methods, like instruments, titration, and kit methods are typically labor intensive, time consuming and lack accuracy when it comes to achieving instant results. The proposed system incorporates sensors that can monitor water quality parameters, including temperature, pH and turbidity. The typical temperature of a fish farm is from 25 °C to 31 °C, pH in the range of 4 to 11, and turbidity in a clear condition of less than 10 NTU. Real-time direct measurement allows for rapid monitoring of fish health and quick responses to abnormalities. The system is designed with a user-friendly interface for ease of use. The system delivers true accurate real-time assays, and pumps are turned on automatically whenever the water parameters exceed a preset threshold (temperature of 25 ° to 31 °C, turbidity of 0 to 10 NTU, pH of 6 to 7) for immediate corrective measures.

title: A Data-Driven Hybrid Model for Reliable Detection of Diabetic Retinopathy
authors: Deepika Deepika, Inderpal Singh, Anshu Vashisth, Balraj Singh, Gagandeep Kaur
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy is an eye condition that can cause blindness by damaging the retina's so-called optic nerves. Among the examinations that are effective in verifying diabetic retinopathy are pupil dilation, optical coherence, and visual acuity tests. Tomography is slow, and it affects patients. By means of a model rule, the fuzzy inference is used to treat patients and make decisions. Through a simple GUI, Intraocular pressure (IOP), vision field (VF), visual acuity (VA), blood glucose level, high-density lipoprotein (HDL), low-density lipoprotein (LDL), hemoglobin glycated sugar, blood pressure, and triglycerides are the 10 input variables used by the suggested fuzzy expert model. Hemorrhage, microaneurysm, exudate, and blood vessels were presented as the pathological details that were detected by adaptive histogram equalization. Although the expert system model is established on the basis of only ten characteristic variables and fundus imaging data, it can distinguish severe, moderate, mild diabetic retinopathy eyes from normal eyes in a more effective way and at a lower cost of computing. The complete test sections were done on a fuzzy system with an ophthalmologist's supervision. The proposed system is found to be 98% accurate, 97% sensitive, and 100% specific. The results are verified through the typical process of an ophthalmologist.

title: An Explainable AI Approach to Alzheimer’s Prediction using Ensemble Methods on Imbalanced Data
authors: Kande Archana, Mahesh Karre, K. Bharath Kumar, K. Balamurugan, Madhavi Karumudi, Bazani Shaik
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative condition that necessitates early detection to enable effective interventions and slow its progression. This research presents an explainable machine learning approach using ensemble classifiers to predict Alzheimer’s risk based on demographic, clinical, cognitive, and lifestyle factors. Mutual Information (MI) was employed for feature selection, and models using Extra Trees, XGBoost, and AdaBoost classifiers were developed. The findings of this study reveal that ensemble methods, particularly XGBoost, significantly outperform individual models in predicting Alzheimer’s disease risk. The dataset, sourced from Kaggle, includes 2,149 patient records with 33 input features. After comprehensive preprocessing, which involved managing outliers, scaling, and oversampling for minority classes, the data was split into training and testing sets in an 80:20 ratio. Performance was evaluated using metrics such as accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score, and ROC-AUC. Among the models, XGBoost achieved the highest performance, with a test accuracy of 92.1%, a recall of 90.0%, and an ROC-AUC score of 96.7%, with AdaBoost and Extra Trees closely following. The confusion matrix indicates that XGBoost had the most true positives (271) and the fewest false negatives (30), highlighting its superior reliability in identifying positive AD cases. To enhance model transparency, SHAP (SHapley Additive Explanations) was used to analyze feature importance. SHAP summary and bar plots identified the top predictors. Overall, this study demonstrates the effectiveness of ensemble classifiers, especially XGBoost, in predicting Alzheimer’s and emphasizes the importance of Explainable AI for clinical trust. The use of SHAP improves interpretability, making the proposed system a practical and transparent tool for healthcare professionals aiming for early detection of Alzheimer’s risk.

title: A Novel Multilevel Transformerless Grid Integrated PV Inverter for Reduced Common-mode Current
authors: Amrita Singh, Atul Raj Singh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Grid connected Photovoltaic (PV) systems are establishing worldwide for numerous applications. Transformerless PV inverters play a crucial role in the solar power industry, primarily due to their cost-effectiveness, lightweight design, and high efficiency. However, these systems face significant challenges, particularly in managing ground leakage currents, which can endanger human safety due to the additional ground currents that flow through the resonant circuit parameters. This work presents a novel configuration that produces a multilevel voltage at output terminals by utilizing a modified sinusoidal multi-carrier PWM technique for controlling the inverter. Furthermore, super junction MOSFETs are employed as the primary switches. The key benefits of this proposed configuration include the elimination of reverse recovery issues, operation at high efficiency, and the reduction of ground current and current distortion. This work incorporates various modes of operation to enhance the understanding of the proposed methodology. A closed loop control system is developed for single phase grid connected transformerless PV system. Ultimately, the proposed topology and its performance evaluation are validated through Simulink supported OPAL-RT based results. The Hardware - in the Loop (HIL) configuration on OPAL-RT platform is utilized to present various responses in the results section.

title: MEDICARE-AI Integrated Learning Approach for Tumour Detection and Prediction
authors: Shanmugam, Jitheeswaran. V, Logeshwaran. T
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: MEDICARE-AI is a complete AI-powered system that diagnoses and predicts tumors. It can look at the different types of cancer using different types of imaging. like MRI, CT, Xray, and dermoscopy. By using advanced CNN and Transformer-based architectures, the system can locate, segment, and measure cancers in organs such as the brain, lung, breast, liver, and skin with high accuracy. This is enabled by effective preprocessing, multi- task learning, and attention techniques that can provide consistent performance for a wide range of tumor appearances. MEDICARE-AI also predicts the characteristics and aggressiveness of tumors that help in early diagnosis and treatment planning. This unified framework exhibits high accuracy, flexibility, and clinical potential for comprehensive multi-tumor screening and decision support.

title: Reliability of Renewable Integrated Systems Considering Demand Response
authors: Khanh Giap, Nga Nguyen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Renewable energy sources (RESs) with abundant supply have positive impacts on the reliability of power systems by diversifying the energy supply. Nonetheless, with the largescale deployment of RESs, total inertia and regulation capability of the system deteriorate, and the power system becomes more susceptible to frequency deviations. Therefore, penetration of RESs in the grids is limited to ensure system stability. This limit, however, decreases the supply of the system compared to scenarios where all available RESs are integrated. To advance RES penetration, this paper proposes an improved method that uses a demand response control loop within the system frequency response model to improve the stability of RESintegrated systems by minimizing the frequency nadir. The demand response control loop is further enhanced using Particle Swarm Optimization to fine-tune the PI controller. The impacts of the governor dead band and time delay on the frequency response are taken into account to create a more realistic system. This proposed framework will then be used within the Monte Carlo Simulation to assess system reliability. The result is achieved on the IEEE-RTS 79 system to show the efficacy of the proposed method.

title: Trustworthy and Explainable Brain Tumor Segmentation: A Multi-Dimensional Survey of Classical and Deep Learning Approaches
authors: Snehayan Das, Mansi Sharma, Narayan Chaturvedi
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Accurate segmentation of brain tumors in MRI is vital for early diagnosis, treatment planning, and monitoring patient outcomes. Deep neural networks, especially U-Net and its variants, have driven major advances, but challenges remain. This survey comprehensively reviews both classical and recent deep learning methods, proposing a unified taxonomy that categorizes approaches by network architecture, learning paradigm, and deployment setting. We highlight often-overlooked evaluation criteria by introducing novel metrics (e.g. Model Robustness Score, Temporal Consistency Score) to assess clinical robustness beyond Dice/IoU. We also comparatively examine model explainability (e.g. clarity of attention maps) and fairness across demographic/anatomical groups, and critique methods for resource-efficient, on-device deployment. Our analysis synthesizes state-of-the-art trends across U-Nets, adversarial and transformer models, self-supervised learning, and federated training, and outlines open challenges for future research.

title: Dual-Band Miniaturized DGS Patch Antenna for High-Efficiency Sub-6 GHz 5G Systems
authors: S. Janarthanan, M. Anto Bennet, Krishna Kanth Varma Penmatsa, S. Varalakshmi, M. Subalatha, T Sampath
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Sub- 6 GHz 5G user equipment demands compact, low-cost antennas that maintain high radiation efficiency and stable coverage. Conventional microstrip designs often struggle to simultaneously achieve miniaturization, dual-band operation, and robust performance on lossy substrates such as FR-4. Existing dual-band patch antennas based on slot loading, EBG structures, or metasurfaces typically incur increased complexity, larger electrical footprints, or limited bandwidth. To address these limitations, this work proposes a dual-band miniaturized defected-ground-structure (DGS) integrated patch antenna with an inset-fed rectangular radiator optimized for sub- <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 ~ G H z} \mathbf{~ 5G}$</tex> bands. The antenna geometry is tuned to generate two wellseparated resonances around 3.50 GHz and 4.90 GHz while preserving a small electrical size suitable for integration in user terminals. A combined dataset of full-wave EM simulations and experimental measurements is used, including reflection coefficient responses, realized gain, radiation efficiency, and E/Hplane patterns across both bands, with S-parameter and far-field data obtained using a vector network analyzer and anechoic chamber. The measured results confirm dual-band operation with fractional bandwidths above ten percent, realized gains of approximately 4.5 dBi and 5.3 dBi in the lower and upper bands, and radiation efficiencies close to ninety percent. Radiation patterns remain quasi-broadside with half-power beamwidths in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$70-80^{\circ}$</tex> range and cross-polarization suppression above <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 1 ~ d B}$</tex>, indicating good polarization purity. Our results indicate that this proposed zeroth-normalized SCDGS-based miniature antenna represents a very good trade-off in size, bandwidth selfefficiency, and gain making it a suitable contender for future sixth-generation compact sub- 6 GHz 5G user equipment.

title: Practical Implementation of GPU-Based Computing at the Grid Edge for Resilience Scenarios
authors: Reuben De Souza, Eduardo Cotilla-Sanchez
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper presents a practical implementation of GPU-accelerated computing at the grid edge to enhance power system resilience through next-generation smart meters. Advanced Metering Infrastructure (AMI) systems rely predominantly on centralized processing architectures, which limit real-time response capabilities during grid disturbances. This work proposes the integration of GPU-enabled computational platforms directly within smart meter to enable local execution support for power system analytics, fault detection algorithms, and optimization routines. The proposed framework uses the Julia programming language to leverage highperformance parallel computing capabilities while maintaining code portability and development efficiency. We use two experimental scenarios to benchmark the computational feasibility of this approach: sparse linear system solutions representative of power flow analyses, and multi-stage production cost simulations incorporating unit commitment and economic dispatch operations. Results demonstrate that computationally intensive power system algorithms, such as those supporting resilience scenario calculations, can be effectively executed at the distribution edge using commercially available embedded GPU hardware.

title: Comparative Evaluation of Handcrafted Image Descriptors with IBK Classifier for Automated Skin Disease Classification
authors: Ayyappan G, Senthil K
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The right and timely diagnosis of dermatological problems continues to be critical in order to come up with requisite medical therapies alongside the treatment regimes. They evaluate IBK (Instance-Based k -Nearest Neighbor) as a classification system on analysis of six feature extraction method that include Auto Color Correlogram Filter (ACCF), Binary Patterns Pyramid Filter (BPPF), Fuzzy Color and Texture Histogram Filter (FCTHF), Fuzzy Opponent Histogram Filter (FOHF), Pyramid Histogram of Oriented Gradients (PHOG) and Simple Color Histogram Filter (SCHF) in recognizing skin disease patterns. Users used the Skin Diseases Image Dataset of Kaggle with 38,000 high-resolution images having different skin diseases classifications. Testing performance metrics were composed of Accuracy, Precision, Recall, ROC along with PRC and Execution Time, which were considered as standard. BPPF+IBK and SCHF+IBK produced the best classification results of 92.98% and FCTHF+IBK had the best recall of 0.96 and execution time of 0.01 seconds. The items of information acquired by FOHF+IBK detection revealed the outstanding PRC performance of 0.96. The study has shown some significant results regarding the relationship between accuracy indices and computational cost that can be used to develop novel lightweight systems of skin diseases detection to support clinics.

title: An Integrated Approach to Green Cloud Solutions for Energy-Efficient Sustainable IT and Carbon Footprint Reduction
authors: Mamta B Savadatti, Kushalatha M R, Vathsala. M. K, Shantarani S Birajdar, Hamsalekha R, Santhrupth B. C., Ajay Sudhir Bale, Mantripragada Ramaa Gayatri
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Cloud computing has become a very important part in everyday life, but this has also made a lot of carbon footprint because of the energy consumption in the data centers. The pandemic had affected these emissions, and they quickly came back, which has shown the requirement for sustainable solutions which will help in fighting the increase in carbon footprint. For these problems, the green computing technology will give probable solutions by promoting the technology that would be responsible enough to decrease these effects of harming environment. It will have techniques like smarter system designs, operations that are energy efficient, and smart techniques for optimization. This study explores how the above set principles can reduce the overall digital carbon footprint and help to create economically viable businesses. This approach provides a forward path for technology progress and profitability aligning with the environment sustainability which is a necessary component for business longevity.

title: Tab-Transformer Based Deep Learning Framework for Electricity Theft Detection in Smart Grids
authors: Manohar Mishra, Rajat Kumar Sahu, Bighnaraj Naik, Geetanjali Bhoi, Jai Govind Singh, Rupendra Kumar Pachauri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Electricity theft (ET) remains one of the most critical challenges for power utilities, leading to substantial financial losses. Presently, the smart grid (SG) architectures are offering new opportunities to address this issue, as these systems are able to generate vast amounts of consumer consumption data that can be used for detecting Electricity theft via cuttingedge machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) methods. Nevertheless, prevailing approaches often face limitations such as overfitting and poor performance on imbalanced datasets, resulting in reduced forecast accuracy. In order to address these challenges, this paper presents a novel DL framework for electricity theft detection (ETD) in Internet of Things (IoT)enabled smart grids, employing a TabTransformer based model. Initially, electricity consumption data from the Theft Detection Dataset 2022 (TDD2022) is pre-processed through missing value imputation, outlier removal, and normalization techniques to ensure data quality and consistency for model training. Afterward, the ETD is performed using the TabTransformer, which effectively models tabular data with both categorical and numerical features while addressing overfitting through its attention mechanism. The stated approach is evaluated across multiple performance metrics, including accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. The output performance considering the experimental results demonstrate that the TabTransformerbased approach significantly outperforms existing approaches, providing a robust and accurate solution for ETD in SGs.

title: Data-Driven Outlier Processing of Net Demand Time Series for Power System Reliability Evaluation
authors: Nusrat Subah Binte Shakhawat, Tan Nhat Pham, Pranta Dash Gupta, Rakibuzzaman Shah, Nima Amjady, Syed Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Net demand time series often contain outliers due to various factors such as data acquisition errors, sudden load fluctuations, renewable generation variability, and network topology changes. Traditional statistical methods for outlier detection often assume specific statistical distributions of datasets, limiting their adaptability to real-world power system data. This paper presents a data-driven outlier processing framework that leverages non-parametric probability distribution to identify and categorize outliers in net demand time series. A non-parametric probability density function is formulated at each time slot, and outliers are identified using adaptive thresholds determined from the percentile energy of the functions. The identified outliers are categorized using an artificial intelligence (AI)-driven classification algorithm, linking anomalies to physical phenomena, such as load changes, renewable generation fluctuations, and network topology variations. The processed data is then utilized in an optimal power flow (OPF) model to evaluate system reliability by calculating the Expected Energy Not Supplied (EENS). The proposed method is validated using IEEE 33-bus network and real-world datasets from a distribution network in Australia and enables the assessment of power system reliability with minimal data.

title: Spatiotemporal Anomaly Detection in Smart Grids Using Latent Representation Learning and Bidirectional LSTM
authors: Abid Ali, Yuanqing Xia, Muhammad Fahad Zia, Zunaira Huma, Mohamed Benbouzid
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: In a smart grid, anomaly detection is important for assessing the power consumption, identifying abnormalities, and maintaining accurate forecasts. However, traditional approaches often inadequately address complex dependencies, and time-series nonlinearity adversely affects stability, reliability, and accuracy. Therefore, a hybrid deep learning-assisted model that utilizes an autoencoder (AE) and a bidirectional long short-term memory (BiLSTM) model is proposed for anomaly detection. AE efficiently identifies the underlying deviation by learning latent-space-based compressed representations through encoding and decoding. BiLSTM captures the temporal dependencies of data by processing them in forward and backward directions, allowing the utilization of both spatial and temporal features for efficient anomaly detection. The proposed model is evaluated using a power consumption dataset containing anomalies. The experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed model in terms of accuracy (99.64 %), row-wise accuracy (99.26 %), and F1-score (93.89 %), highlighting its suitability and scalability for anomaly detection in power consumption data in a smart grid environment.

title: An IoT-Driven Cardiovascular Disease Detection Model Optimized Using Genetic Algorithm and SVM
authors: Kaliappan A, Venkatesan P, Biswajit Barman, R.Prabha, Sandeep C S, Ashwini Kumar Saini
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The ability of CRP to predict the future of cardiovascular disease diagnosis has gotten a lot of interest around the world. High levels of C-reactive protein have been associated to sudden cardiac death, peripheral artery disease, heart attacks, and strokes. Higher levels of C-reactive protein in people with percutaneous angioplasty and acute coronary syndrome are linked to more ischaemia and mortality. These links help us understand atherothrombosis, which is when inflammation and fat build up in the body over time. This work uses advanced data preparation techniques to make the input properties of machine learning models better so that they can find cardiovascular disease. SFFS is used to adjust the settings when features are being picked. The main goal is to improve an SVM classifier by using a GA to do so. A direction-based crossover operator makes it easier for this GA to change its parameters and classify things. The program was able to correctly forecast heart illness 97.46% of the accuracy. The results show that hybrid machine learning methods can make diagnostics and assessments of cardiovascular risk more accurate.

title: Innovation Strategies and Commercial Viability of Electric Vehicle Battery Management Systems
authors: Ch. Hari Govinda Rao, Sharmeela Chenniappan, Attada Jyothsna, Srinivasarao Boddepalli, Guruvulu Naidu Ponnada, Batta Madhu Sudhana Rao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: This study utilizes simulation to study the dynamics of electric vehicle (EV) batteries and battery management systems (BMS). The project is to simulate and improve battery management system functions, such as temperature control, cell balancing, and state-of-charge regulation, within the framework of diverse electric vehicle scenarios employing dynamic modeling. The study evaluates how battery management system options affect battery performance, efficiency, and longevity using modeling tools. In addition to providing insights that can direct the development of complex battery management system algorithms to enhance electric vehicle propulsion, the simulation-based process enables an improved awareness of the complex interactions between battery systems and battery management systems. By boosting the usability and integration of battery management systems with electric vehicle batteries, this work advances modeling methods. This system assesses and shows the battery temperature, charging/discharging current, and State of Charge (SOC) for the specific battery model. Microcontrollers are used in conjunction with digital and analog sensors for monitoring purposes. For this project, lithium-ion batteries were selected. Because lithium-ion batteries are lightweight and have a high energy density, they are ideal for electric scooters that require greater range and mobility. The project is to provide valuable information for the effective integration of lithium-ion batteries in electric scooters, with an emphasis on sustainability and energy efficiency gains.

title: Insulation Resistance Estimation Method Using Multi-Switching in Low-Voltage DC IT Systems Based on an Open-Type Midpoint Grounding Configuration
authors: Chan-hyeok Oh, Ki-Yeon Lee, Seung-Taek Lim
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: In low-voltage direct current (LVDC) IT systems, continuous monitoring of the insulation condition and rapid disconnection in the event of a fault are essential for ensuring safety. The conventional open-type midpoint grounding method for insulation resistance estimation keeps the switch open during normal operation to block leakage current and periodically turns it on and off to measure the voltage and current across the midpoint resistor. From these measurements, the insulation resistances of the positive (+) and negative (-) poles are estimated. While this approach reduces continuous losses and enables both insulation assessment and fault detection, its accuracy decreases significantly when the insulation resistances of the two poles are similar or when measurement errors are present. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a multi-switching-based insulation resistance estimation method. The proposed scheme applies a sequence of switch operations to create multiple circuit conditions, capturing voltage and current responses for each switching state. Transient periods immediately after switching are excluded, and only steady-state measurement data are used to compute valid voltage-current pairs. These multiple measurement sets are formulated into simultaneous equations to estimate both insulation resistances of the positive (+) and negative (-) poles concurrently. Furthermore, a weighting process based on measurement quality is applied to improve estimation reliability.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Model Fusion for Enhanced Cervical Cancer Identification and Classification
authors: Aarti Pimpalkar, Mayuresh Gulame
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Unusual cell progression in the cervix, a vital uterine anatomical assembly, is the cause of cervical malignancy, the subsequent most common malignancy to infect women. One cannot stress the prominence of prompt discovery, which is why Pap smears, colposcopies, and Human Papillomavirus (HPV) testing are among the screening techniques used to identify possible concerns and start rapid action. These screening methods require the specific expertise of skilled medical professionals and pathologists because cancer diagnosis is essentially subjective. In directive to address the requisite for operative and intellectual screening, this paper presents a innovative style that uses pre-trained deep neural network (DNN) prototypes for feature extraction, such as Alexnet, Resnet-101, Resnet-152, and InceptionV3. Alongside the refinement of these prototypes, several machine learning techniques are integrated; ResNet152 exhibits remarkable performance, attaining an amazing accurateness rate of 98.08% sensitivity 97.56% and specificity 97.42. Notably, the SIPaKMeD dataset, which is used in this study and is publicly available, helps to make our findings more transparent and repeatable.

title: Review of the Modern Passive Cell Balancing Techniques Implemented to the Batteries of EVs
authors: Shukla Karmakar, Aashish Kumar Bohre
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Recently, the focus before purchasing a vehicle has hugely shifted towards Electric vehicles (EVs) compared to conventional fuel-based vehicles. This fact has occured considering the environmental, economic, and societal friendliness of EVs. Despite the various attractive returns of EVs, the major limitation to their reliable driving is their battery. So, the prime challenge is protecting and prolonging the life of the battery, keeping cost-effectiveness in mind. Nowadays, the reliable, simple, and cost-effective technique that has proven to satisfy the demands of running EVs for long distances safely is the Passive Cell Balancing (PCB) technique. The PCB technique is executed for balancing the cells in the batteries of the EVs in a smart way. The cells are balanced for their parameters for instance State of Charge (SoC) levels, voltages, currents, and power levels. The operation is executed by managing the charge and current level of the cells. The cell having the maximum SoC is discharged to the cell having the minimum SoC within the battery pack via resistors. The PCB technique is greatly grouped into the Fixed shunt resistor and Switched shunt resistor type techniques. This technique suitably finds its application in low to medium-power batteries of EVs. The pros, cons, types, and the latest applications of the PCB technique in EVs are highlighted in this work.

title: Explainable AI for Early Diagnosis of Alzheimer’s Disease: A Transparent Deep Learning Approach
authors: Debyanshu Tiwari, Dharampreet Singh, Gaganjot Kaur, Niyaz Ahmad Wani, Jatin Bedi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease is a degenerative cognitive disease that primarily affects the memory and behavior of an individual. Early and accurate prediction of Alzheimer’s disease is essential for timely prevention, which can potentially slow down the progression of the disease. Earlier diagnostic strategies relied on clinical evaluations, but these methods are very time-consuming. In recent years, deep learning has emerged as a powerful method for evaluating medical images. It is capable of detecting early indicators of Alzheimer’s disease. The ongoing advances in deep learning have shown hope in improving the early prediction of Alzheimer’s disease. This research used a neural network for deep learning for the early prediction of Alzheimer’s disease with an accuracy of 84.97±0.84%. Additionally, techniques like Explainable AI such as SHAP (SHapley Additive Explanations), including summary charts and local and global explanations, provide valuable insights, which enable the researchers to identify the critical factors of the disease. The principal objective of our research on forecasting Alzheimer’s disease with Explainable AI is to develop an interpretable model for the early diagnosis of the condition via SHAP, hence enhancing trust in AI-driven prognostics.

title: Analysis of a Hybrid Solar-Biogas Energy System with Energy Storage for Stable Power Grid Using Machine Learning-Based Forecasting
authors: Titus Kabirdas, Mohamad Nassereddine, Haile Rajamani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This study analyzes the feasibility of a hybrid solar biogas energy system coupled with battery storage for dairy farming applications. A synthetic dataset was developed to model methane production based on manure input and digester <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{p H}$</tex>, while solar power output was modeled using irradiance data. Machine learning models, including linear regression, Random Forest, and XGBoost, were trained to predict biogas power generation. At the same time, Random Forest, XGBoost, SVR, MLP Regressor, and KNN were employed to predict solar power output. Three energy conditions of a realistic dairy farm - low, average, and high production were analyzed with and without battery support. The findings demonstrated that in a location with consistent sunny conditions throughout the year, prioritizing solar panel installation along with biogas offers greater energy reliability and cost-effectiveness than investing in large-scale battery storage. The hybrid system thus enhances energy security and promotes sustainable practices for dairy farms in similar environments.

title: Optimal Traffic-Informed Design and Operation of EV Charging Stations in Power Distribution Grids
authors: Mousa Alizadeh, Ali Moradi Amani, Lasantha Meegahapola, Mahdi Jalili, Oliver Hill
abstract: This paper introduces a novel electric vehicle charging station (EVCS) siting framework that simultaneously optimizes power distribution and transportation network performance. Addressing the limitations of traditional approaches, which often neglect the dynamic demand of EVCS, our framework employs a multi-objective optimization strategy that jointly determines EVCS location and capacity, alongside battery energy storage system (BESS) deployment. By integrating data-driven techniques, we optimize for grid stability, traffic congestion, and infrastructure costs. Real-world simulations, utilizing actual energy consumption data, demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework. Notably, the optimized BESS effectively mitigates voltage fluctuations and peak load challenges, while the EVCS placement minimizes travel costs and queuing delays, ensuring compliance with both power and traffic constraints.

title: A Deterministic Real-Time System for Monocular Depth Estimation on a Low-Cost Edge AI Platform: From Kernel to Inference
authors: Vivek Choudhry, Shwetank Shekhar, R.A. Ratish, Akash Kumar Pradhan, Md Sadullah
abstract: The rapid growth of autonomous systems in automotive and industrial applications demands perception pipelines that are both accurate and temporally deterministic. Although deep learning has significantly advanced monocular depth estimation, deploying such models on resource-constrained edge devices while meeting real-time safety requirements remains challenging. Conventional general-purpose operating systems often introduce unpredictable latency, making them unsuitable for safety-critical control loops.This paper presents an end-to-end, low-cost real-time edge AI framework implemented on the Raspberry Pi 5. A custom Linux kernel with the PREEMPT-RT patch is employed to achieve hard real-time scheduling with microsecond-level determinism. On this platform, a lightweight convolutional neural network, ADALITE, is introduced for monocular depth estimation. The model combines an efficient encoder–decoder architecture with a simplified multi-head self-attention mechanism and is optimized through knowledge distillation from a state-of-the-art teacher network. Experimental results demonstrate worst-case scheduling latencies below 200 μs under heavy system load and competitive depth estimation accuracy on the KITTI dataset. The proposed system achieves real-time inference suitable for collision mitigation, demonstrating that reliable and deterministic edge AI is feasible on affordable consumer hardware.

title: A Novel Deep Learning Technique to Model and Predict the Spread of Cybersecurity Threats
authors: Amani Ali Ahmed Ali, Ammar Thabit Zahary, Talal Ahmed Ali
abstract: Malware, ransomware, and botnet attacks are examples of cyberthreats that are constantly evolving and spreading throughout digital infrastructures, resulting in serious operational and financial harm. The absence of proactive countermeasures frequently leaves organizations unprepared. There is a pressing need to create strong artificial intelligence methods to anticipate cyberthreats because cyberattacks are becoming more frequent and sophisticated. In this investigation, authors present a machine learning-depended (NRM) Non-parametric Regression Model and a deep learning strategy called (DSPM) Deep Sequential Prediction Model to forecast cyber threats across various datasets. Authors use publicly accessible cybersecurity datasets to train and evaluate the suggested models. The suggested models are contrasted with current techniques for predicting cyber threats and assessed using (MAE) Mean Absolute Error. The better prediction ability of the suggested models is illustrated by our experimental findings. While the baseline SVM recorded 25,350 (19%), the PAFE model at (0.75%), the Deep networks model with regularization techniques (Dropout, DropConnect) at (0.43%), Stacked Ensemble, autoencoder, GRU, and MLP (SE-AGM) model at (0.57%), and the model based on gray photos with deep learning (GDMC) at (0.74% and 2.64%), the suggested DSPM and NRM models accomplish MAEs of 1,622 (rate of error 0.62%) and 602 (0.1%), respectively.

title: A Lightweight Real-Time Disaster Assessment Semantic Segmentation Model for Autonomous Aerial Vehicles Remote Sensing
authors: Liang Zhao, Xuebin Zhou, Ammar Hawbani, Na Lin, Lianbo Ma, Qiang He, Majjed Al-Qatf
abstract: Semantic segmentation of high-resolution remote sensing imagery plays a critical role in applications such as disaster assessment. However, deploying large models on Autonomous Aerial Vehicles (AAVs) remains challenging due to inherent conflicts among accuracy, model size, and computational efficiency. To address these challenges, we propose FMC-ULite, a novel lightweight architecture designed to achieve a better balance between accuracy and efficiency for real-time processing. Our model incorporates four key innovations, including a Fast Fourier Transform (FFT)-based fusion module for enhanced edge feature extraction and noise suppression in the frequency domain, a simplified MobileNetV3-Large encoder that substantially reduces parameter count, a cross-layer feature fusion (CLFF) module to effectively integrate multi-scale semantic and detail information, and an attention-gated decoder with multi-scale dilated convolutions to prioritize critical disaster regions. Furthermore, an adaptive combined loss function is introduced to alleviate class imbalance. Experiments conducted on the RescueNet dataset show that our model achieves competitive accuracy compared to advanced lightweight methods under a comparable parameter budget, demonstrating its strong suitability for real-time disaster assessment using AAVs.

title: HCPMR: A Hierarchically Coordinated Proximal Multi-Hop Routing Scheme for FANETs in Mission-Critical Environments
authors: Juhi Agrawal, Anil Kumar, Muhammad Morshed Alam, Muhammad Yeasir Arafat
abstract: In mission-critical environments, designing efficient routing protocols for unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) is challenging due to limited energy resources, high-speed mobility, and highly dynamic link conditions. This paper proposes a hierarchical routing framework, termed hierarchically coordinated proximal multi-hop routing (HCPMR) scheme, which integrates multi-agent proximal policy optimization (MAPPO) with graph neural networks (GNNs) to enable scalable, stable, and energy-efficient UAV communication. HCPMR organizes UAVs into energy- and stability-aware clusters, where GNN- assisted intra-cluster coordination is performed by cluster leaders, who also manage inter-cluster routing through periodically synchronized exchanges under a centralized training and decentralized execution (CTDE) paradigm. GNN-based embeddings allow UAVs to capture spatial dependencies within their local neighbourhoods, enabling adaptive routing with reduced control overhead, enhanced reliability, and improved energy efficiency. The proposed scheme incorporates a multi-objective reward function to jointly optimize energy consumption, fairness, delivery reliability, and terrain adaptability. Simulation results using NS-3/ns3-gym across urban, desert, and mountainous scenarios demonstrate that HCPMR increases packet delivery ratio by 6.5% compared to coordinated Q-learning-based multi-hop routing (CQMR) and by 16% compared to improved Q-learning–based multi-hop routing (IQMR). Moreover, HCPMR achieves approximately 13% higher residual energy than CQMR and 58% higher than IQMR, while reducing control overhead by 20% and 38.5%, respectively. These results establish HCPMR as an adaptive and efficient routing framework for dynamic FANETs in mission-critical operations, effectively enhancing routing decisions through GNN-driven embeddings and MAPPO-based coordination.

title: Byzantine-Resilient Federated Learning with Trust-Aware Task Scheduling for Heterogeneous UAV Swarms
authors: Weian Guo, Yao Xiao, Dongyang Li, Shi Cheng, Yinbei Li, Jinfei Liu, Wuzhao Li, Lei Wang, Qidi Wu
abstract: The deployment of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) swarms for collaborative intelligence in urban environments presents unprecedented opportunities for smart city applications, yet faces critical security challenges from Byzantine attacks in distributed learning scenarios. This paper presents a comprehensive framework that jointly addresses Byzantine-resilient federated learning and trust-aware task scheduling for heterogeneous UAV fleets operating under adversarial conditions. We propose a novel aggregation mechanism that employs multi-metric anomaly detection, combining gradient norm analysis, statistical residual examination, and directional alignment assessment to identify and mitigate malicious model updates. The framework incorporates a dynamic trust management system that maintains behavioral profiles of participating agents, enabling adaptive isolation of compromised nodes while preserving system performance. Furthermore, we develop a security-conscious task scheduler that considers real-time trust scores, resource constraints, and environmental threat indicators to optimize mission allocation across the swarm. Our approach uniquely couples learning objectives with operational requirements, allowing UAVs to adapt their training parameters based on perceived threat levels. Theoretical analysis establishes convergence guarantees under Byzantine presence and demonstrates trust score separation between honest and malicious agents. The proposed framework provides a practical solution for deploying secure collaborative learning in mission-critical UAV applications.

title: A Dual-mode FSK Wireless Power and Data Transfer System with 1.1 Mbps Data Rate and Rectifier Output Power of 148 mW for Implantable Medical Applications
authors: Congyi Qian, Sai Li, Heng Liang, Hongyuan Zhang, Songping Mai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Biomedical Circuits and Systems
abstract: This paper proposes a wireless power and data transfer (WPDT) system for implantable medical applications, featuring a simple structure, high data rate (DR), and efficient power transmission. To streamline the frequency-shift keying (FSK) data transmission link, the FSK modulator integrates merely one oscillator and one frequency divider, while the FSK demodulator requires only one D flip-flop and one delay unit. This minimalist design generates two FSK carrier signals with a large frequency difference, simultaneously enhancing the data transmission rate and reducing the bit error rate (BER). To meet the WPDT system’s requirements for high power transmission under transient conditions and low coupling coefficients, a coupled network capacitive compensation technique is employed. This method significantly enhances the power transmission capability of one carrier frequency, enabling greater power delivery to the load (PDL) of that carrier frequency during non-data transmission periods. Relevant circuits were fabricated using the 180 nm BCD process, and a prototype WPDT system based on these circuits has been successfully developed. Test results show that under a low coupling scenario (17.5 mm coil spacing), the system achieves a PDL of 148 mW while maintaining a DR of 1.1 Mbps with a BER below 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">–8</sup>. This work fully verifies the system’s feasibility and provides an efficient, reliable technical solution for wireless power supply and data transmission in implantable medical devices.

title: Deep Learning-Based Joint Activity Detection and Channel Estimation for Cell-Free Massive MIMO
authors: Yuhan Qin, Yuchao Meng, Xiaoxu Zhang, Pingzhi Fan, Zhengquan Zhang, Ming Xiao, Zheng Ma
abstract: Massive machine-type communications (mMTC) is a key scenario for 6G networks featured by ultra-high connection density and sporadic data transmission, which may lead to high signaling overhead and transmission latency with traditional multiple access protocols and network structures. Grant-free non-orthogonal multiple access (GF-NOMA) and cell-free massive MIMO (CF-MIMO) offer effective solutions. This paper proposes a model-driven deep learning algorithm, namely Iterative Shrinkage-Thresholding Algorithm Network (ISTANet), for joint active user detection and channel estimation (JADCE) in high-activity CF-MIMO systems, which employs a convolutional neural network (CNN) for nonlinear transformations and incorporates skip connections to enhance training stability and reconstruction performance. The simulation results demonstrate its adaptability to varying pilot lengths and its outstanding detection performance, especially in high-activity scenarios.

title: A Dual Hybrid Data-Driven Strategy for Soft Measurement of Dioxins Emission
authors: Tianzheng Wang, Jian Tang, Hao Tian, Zi Wang, Jiakun Chen, Wen Yu, Junfei Qiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Accurately detecting dioxin (DXN) emission concentration from municipal solid waste incineration (MSWI) process is essential, yet it remains a significant challenge. This article presents a novel dual hybrid data-driven strategy for developing a soft measurement model. The strategy achieves both the virtual-real data hybrid and improved main-compensation structure hybrid, significantly enhancing the interpretability and applicability of soft measurement. A virtual simulation mechanism dataset is generated through an orthogonal experimental design and implementation, based on a coupled numerical simulation model for DXN formation and the operating conditions of an actual MSWI power plant. An enhanced main-compensation structure soft measurement model is developed by fusing interpretable quasi-Newton decision trees (QNDT), interval type-3 fuzzy broad learning systems (IT3FBLS), and variable step size least mean square (VSS-LMS) ridge regression, using both real historical process data and virtual simulation mechanism datasets. The offline model training and online model verification stages are described in detail. Experimental results demonstrate that the strategy significantly outperforms conventional single hybrid data-driven approaches and state-of-art methods in terms of generalization and interpretability.

title: XNet: Enhancing Physical Activity Intensity Assessment With Attentional Multidomain Fusion and Visual Analytics_supp1-3666726.pdf
authors: A. Mendoza Villarroel
abstract: Sedentary behavior (SB) is a major global health concern, necessitating accurate physical activity (PA) intensity monitoring. Conventional machine-learning (ML) methods using accelerometers struggle to generalize due to variability across populations, sensors, and activities, leading to inconsistent real-world performance. This study presents XNet, a dual-domain deep learning (DL) model for classifying PA intensity and estimating energy expenditure. XNet features a hierarchical multihead architecture that independently extracts temporal and frequency features from multiple sensors, then integrates them via a novel attentional feature fusion (AFF) module applied in two stages: first aggregating sensor features, then fusing domain embeddings. This hierarchical approach outperforms single-stage fusion and provides interpretable attention weights revealing sensor and domain contributions. Frequency-domain features are essential for generalization: in cross-dataset evaluations, XNet achieved the highest F1-score of 70.5 while maintaining robust sedentary detection (88% TPR), and in open-set scenarios, it achieved an F1-score of 77.0, surpassing all DL and hand-crafted baselines. We validated XNet on multiple public datasets and a new dataset of 105 participants. Furthermore, our analysis shows that lightweight 1D-convolutional spectral encoders yield better out-of-distribution generalization than transformer and graph attention (GAT) network alternatives, while benchmarking confirms that AFF outperforms nine fusion strategies in balancing accuracy, efficiency, and robustness to sensor failure. The model adapts to physiological signals (heart rate and ECG) and exhibits low inference latency (~25 ms), making it suitable for on-device deployment. A complementary visual analytics framework uses attention weights to facilitate expert auditing, thereby promoting transparent and equitable health monitoring.

title: Accelerated Iterative Learning Control Using Fractional High-Order Update Rule for LTI Systems
authors: Zihan Li, Dong Shen, Xinghuo Yu
abstract: This study proposes an accelerated iterative learning control scheme using a fractional high-order update rule (FHUR) to improve the convergence rate for linear time-invariant systems. High- and low-order power update terms are used to handle large- and small-tracking errors, respectively, thereby accelerating convergence. Two learning mechanisms are proposed and shown to be optimal among various learning gain selections. The inherent nonlinearity in the FHUR poses significant challenges for the convergence analysis. To address this, a disturbed composite nonlinear mapping method is introduced. Using this method, the tracking errors are proven to converge either to an invariant set or to a set of limit cycles, depending on the underlying learning mechanism. Any desired tracking precision can be achieved by adjusting the parameters in the FHUR. Numerical simulations confirm that the FHUR presents a promising alternative to the commonly used proportional-type update rule for achieving accelerated convergence.

title: Secure Data Sharing Framework with Fine-grained Access Control and Privacy Protection for IoT Data Marketplace
authors: Woojin Jeon, Donghyun Yu, Ruei-Hau Hsu, Jemin Lee
publish: IEEE Transactions on Network and Service Management
abstract: The proliferation of IoT devices has led to an exponential increase in data generation, creating new opportunities for data marketplaces. However, due to the security and privacy issues arising from the sensitive nature of IoT data, as well as the need for efficient management of vast amounts of IoT data, a robust solution is necessary. Therefore, this paper proposes a secure data sharing framework with fine-grained access control and privacy protection for the internet of things (IoT) data marketplace. For fine-grained access control of the data in the proposed protocol, we develop the hidden attributes and encryption outsourced key-policy attribute-based encryption (HAEO-KP-ABE) that outsources high-complex operations to peripheral devices with high capability to reduce the computation burden of IoT device. It achieves data privacy by hiding attributes in the ciphertext and by preventing entities that do not hold the data consumer’s secret key material (including SA/CS) from running the match test on stored ciphertexts before decryption. It also has an efficient match test algorithm which can verify that the hidden attributes of the ciphertext match the access policy of the data consumer’s private key without revealing those attributes. We demonstrate the proposed protocol satisfies the security features required for the data sharing process in an IoT data marketplace environment. Furthermore, we evaluate the execution time of the proposed protocol according to the number of attributes and show the practicality and efficiency of the proposed protocol compared to the related works.

title: Real-Time Frequency Characterization of Chirped Lasers via Finite-Difference Phase Measurements
authors: Xuebing Zhang, Javier Pérez Santacruz, Jac Romme, Amir Abbas Kashi, Qianhao Lin, Gijs van Elzakker, Marcus Dahlem, Dongjae Shin, Ruud Oldenbeuving
abstract: Laser instantaneous frequency (LIF) describes the time-varying frequency of a laser signal, defined as the temporal derivative of the oscillation phase divided by 2π. This concept is crucial in understanding frequency noise, phase noise, and chirped optical signals, where the frequency changes over time. Most current optical frequency measurements rely on frequency domain analysis, which face limitations when applied to rapidly chirped lasers. Unlike frequency-domain methods, time-domain methods provide a more accurate representation of the time varying frequency of chirped lasers, which is crucial for real-time applications. The widely used Hilbert transform-based method describes the LIF measurement but has slow real-time responses, a relatively low measurement speed, a not on-chip-compatible structure, and the ambiguity of the chirp sign, which hampers its use, particularly in real-time applications. This paper introduces a novel real-time LIF estimator for chirped lasers, offering higher accuracy, reduced algorithm complexity, and an on-chip integration solution. Experimental demonstrations include applications for FMCW LiDAR adaptive nonlinearity correction and real-time photoelectric control loops.

title: Distributed Task Planning Method for Synchronous Execution of Heterogeneous Tasks in Uncertain Environments_supp1-3670128.mp4
authors: Liang Lu
abstract: Aiming at the challenges in planning for the synchronized execution of multiple geometrically heterogeneous tasks by swarms in uncertain environments, this work proposes a distributed task planning method. This method is intended to overcome the inefficiencies in traditional methods caused by fragmented task modeling, high sensitivity to environmental uncertainty, and the risk of collaborative deadlocks. First, a Conditional Value-at-Risk (CVaR) model is introduced to dynamically estimate task execution time under uncertainty. Second, multi-geometry tasks (area, line, point) are uniformly transformed into equivalent point-task sets via grid-based decomposition and key-point discretization. Finally, a cooperative task dependency graph is embedded into a distributed auction consensus mechanism with cycle detection to prevent deadlocks, and an adaptive cooperative obstacle avoidance strategy based on task progress is integrated to ensure synchronized and collision-free motion. Simulation results demonstrate our method’s superiority over benchmarks in completion time, success rate, and efficiency. Its practicality is validated through real-world UAV tests, while scalability experiments confirm its robustness in large-scale scenarios. This work provides a solid theoretical foundation and technical support for efficient coordination of unmanned swarms in complex and dynamic environments.

title: DSHD-CAM: High-Throughput RRAM CAM Leveraging Dynamic Shifted Hamming Distance for Genome Analysis
authors: Chenxin Jiang, Shen Zhang, Tiankuo Zheng, Rui Li, Yuhao Shu, Yajun Ha
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Genome analysis has been critical in various applications, such as infectious disease control. High throughput is an essential requirement for genome analysis in data-intensive scenarios, which requires acceleration by content-addressable memory (CAM) with parallel comparison capability. However, existing genome analysis CAMs still face inadequate throughput issues due to large cell area, excessive array storage redundancy, and inefficient comparison algorithms. To address these issues, we propose a high-throughput dynamic shifted Hamming distance (SHD) resistive random access memory (RRAM)-based CAM (DSHD-CAM) that leverages the characteristics of genome analysis. First, we propose a compact RRAM-based CAM cell utilizing one-hot encoding and time-domain computation to minimize cell area. Second, we propose a dense CAM array utilizing an efficient storage scheme to reduce array storage redundancy. Third, we propose a dynamic SHD search algorithm filtering out low-match-potential cases to reduce search latency. Compared to the state-of-the-art (SOTA), DSHD-CAM achieves an average of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$7.58\times $</tex-math> </inline-formula> higher throughput under the same area constraints while maintaining competitive sensitivity and precision.

title: Dual Function Metasurface: mm-Wave Radiator and Gain Enhancement Surface for Antenna-on-Chip
authors: Zere Iman, Yiyang Yu, Heng Wang, Muhammad Ikram, Atif Shamim
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Antennas and Propagation
abstract: With the advent of 6G, Antenna-on-Chip (AoC) is becoming more relevant than ever. However, due to the lossy nature of silicon (Si) in standard chip fabrication processes, these antennas suffer from poor gains and radiation efficiencies. An elegant solution is to incorporate an Artificial Magnetic Conductor (AMC) structure beneath the AoC to isolate the Si substrate. This solution works well for mm-Wave frequencies, however, the AMC is typically a large structure, as compared to the AoC, and is limited to a single function of isolating the Si substrate. Enhancing the functionality of the AMC can optimize the performance of such compact designs, where efficient use of space as well as integration of multiple functionalities is of paramount importance. Therefore, this work explores the potential of employing the AMC as a metasurface (MTS) antenna within the framework of AoC systems. The key focus of this work is to design a dual-functional MTS, which works as an AMC to enhance the gain and radiation efficiency of an AoC at one frequency, and as an independent radiator at another frequency. As a proof of concept, we have shown that an on-chip monopole antenna’s gain and radiation efficiency is enhanced by 5.5 dBi and 20% respectively at 79 GHz through an integrated AMC structure. It is shown that this AMC can be optimized to radiate as an independent MTS antenna through Characteristic Mode Analysis (CMA) with a variety of radiation patterns for various modes. The fabricated prototype demonstrates one of these modes, where the MTS antenna shows a measured peak gain of 4.0 dBi at 94 GHz with a broad boresight radiation pattern.

title: Simultaneous High-Sensitivity and Rapid-Response Acetylene Detection Using a 3D-Printed Single-Fiber Photoacoustic Probe
authors: Ming Zhang, Jiayang Sun, Yuchen Guan, Jiawei Xing, Tianli Gao, Guojie Wu, Zhenfeng Gong, Wei Peng
abstract: Achieving high detection sensitivity while maintaining rapid response is a persistent challenge in dissolved gas analysis, particularly for fiber-optic photoacoustic sensors constrained by diffusion mechanisms. In this work, we report a simultaneous high-sensitivity and rapid-response acetylene (C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>) sensor enabled by a novel 3D-printed single-fiber photoacoustic probe. Unlike conventional designs that sacrifice gas exchange speed for acoustic confinement, our probe features a permeable multi-aperture cavity specifically engineered to co-optimize fluid dynamics and acoustic pressure accumulation. Numerical simulations and experimental results demonstrate that this architecture effectively eliminates the gas transport bottleneck, reducing the thermodynamic equilibrium time to just 9.5 s, which is an improvement of over 90% compared to traditional cantilever-based diffusion models.
