Despite its open structure, the probe maintains exceptional acoustic performance, achieving a detection limit of 21.7 ppb for C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>H<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> within a minimal effective volume of 0.3 mL. Furthermore, the single-fiber configuration is shown to yield a signal amplitude 5 times higher than dual-fiber counterparts by minimizing acoustic energy leakage. These results validate the proposed probe as a robust solution for real-time, high-precision transformer oil monitoring, successfully overcoming the trade-offs inherent in miniature photoacoustic devices.

title: Transmission Delay Minimization for NOMA-Based F-RANs
authors: Yuan Ai, Xidong Mu, Pengbo Si, Yuanwei Liu
abstract: 抓取摘要失败: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='ieeexplore.ieee.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /document/11421616/ (Caused by ProtocolError('Connection aborted.', ConnectionResetError(104, 'Connection reset by peer')))

title: Harmonic Quality Enhancement of Single-Phase 7Level CHB-Multilevel Inverter Using TriplenHarmonic Elimination and Unequal DC-Link Based SHM-PAM Schemes: A Comparative Study
authors: Samudra Panda, Palisetty Maneesh, Sourabh Kundu, Subrata Banerjee
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The application of single phase multilevel inverters (MLI) in any grid connected system require its proper modulation to deliver high quality output power. In this study, two different switching techniques has been introduced to develop selective harmonic minimization pulse amplitude modulation (SHM PAM) scheme for a single-phase 7-level cascaded H-bridge (CHB) MLI. In the $1^{\text {st }}$ scheme, an SHMPAM strategy based on self-elimination of triplen-order harmonics is developed with additional minimization of other lower harmonic components. In the second proposed scheme, a novel SHM-PAM approach using unequal DC-link voltages is presented. This scheme offers increased degrees of freedom, allowing for the minimization of all lower-order odd harmonic components and a reduction in the total harmonic distortion (THD) of the single-phase MLI output. Both switching schemes are capable of minimizing harmonic components up to $25{ }^{\text {th }}$ order, thereby producing high-quality output voltage with the satisfaction of two key grid code standards; namely, CIGRE WG 36-05 and EN 50160. MATLAB simulations and experimental studies conducted on a CHB MLI setup confirm the theoretical validity of both switching techniques. A comparative analysis of the two schemes reveals that while both are suitable for renewable energy generation systems, the second switching scheme provides superior output voltage quality, with a better harmonic profile and lower THD.

title: Advanced MPPT Controllers for Hydrogen Vehicle Systems: A Critical Simulative Analysis
authors: Saini Vamshi Krishna, Ch Hussaian Basha, Buddhadeva Sahoo, Nagaraju Dharavat, N.Karthick
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Renewable energy has more future growth for the automotive industry because of its tremendous features, such as clean energy delivery to the consumer, providing better efficiency for hydrogen-related vehicles, and effectively maintaining the uniform energy supply to any electric vehicle. In this work, a Polymer Membrane Fuel Cell (PMFC) technology is considered for running the four-wheeler hydrogen vehicle system. This polymer material has the merits of being capable of running under negative temperature conditions, very few seconds are enough to function the module, and provides a good dynamic response. However, this module creates disturbances in the supply of energy. So, the swarm intelligence-based Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) approach is applied to the polymer module to find the exact working MPP position of the supply module. This algorithm takes a few operational iterations, better convergence rate value, is easy to develop, and low cost for the integration of the system. Finally, a DC-DC circuit is utilized for the hydrogen vehicle to move the voltage levels from a lower value to a higher value. The proposed system is studied using the MATLAB software.

title: Dynamic State Estimation-Based Attack Detection Scheme to Supervise Line Current Differential Relays
authors: Shayan Soltani, Navid Vafamand, Siavash Yari, Dariush Salehi, Masood Mottaghizadeh, Akhtar Hussain, Innocent Kamwa
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Line current differential relays (LCDRs) are commonly used to safeguard high-voltage transmission lines; however, they are intrinsically susceptible to cyber-attacks due to their dependence on communication networks. This study presents how such security gaps may be exploited using false data injection attacks (FDIAs), potentially causing incorrect trip signals. To mitigate this problem, a novel detection method is introduced to distinguish FDIAs from actual internal faults. The approach functions by comparing the locally acquired and the estimated positive-sequence voltages at the relay location. Voltage estimation is performed through two Kalman Filters (KFs), each designed using state-space representations of the transmission line under both normal and faulty conditions, incorporating both local and remote data. Under standard operating conditions, the voltage predicted by one of the KFs corresponds well with the actual measured value, as the model accurately reflects system behavior. However, in the event of an FDIA, both filters deviate from the system's actual dynamics, resulting in noticeable differences between the estimated and measured voltages. Validation of the proposed method on the IEEE 39-bus system through simulations confirms its robustness and accuracy.

title: Early Detection of Cucumber Leaf Diseases with VGG16 for Smart Agricultural Monitoring
authors: Kanegonda Ravi Chythanya, Vishnu Kant, Pallavi Semwal, Anzar Ahmed
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Diseases affecting cucumber leaves severely reduce crop yield and endanger food security, making crop management unsustainable. A deep learning method for automated cucumber leaf disease diagnosis using a fine-tuned VGG16 model is discussed in this study. Three classes were defined for this study: Disease 1, Disease 2 and Healthy Leaves. The categories considered were Downy mildew, Powdery mildew and Healthy leaves. The images downtown were divided into training, validation, and test sets in the ratio of 80:10:10. The model achieved an excellent test set accuracy of 96.0%. All three classes achieved high precision and recall values, and F1-scores of 0.95, 0.97, and 0.96 for Downy mildew, Healthy leaves, and Powdery mildew respectively. Classification performance was highly accurate, with few exceptions as seen in the error matrix. In addition, the convergence of training and validation accuracy can be observed throughout the training process. The optimal validation accuracy is achieved after the 19th epoch. The implementation of this model in precision agriculture helps to reduce labor-intensive tasks and limit the impact of diseases on crops. Artificial intelligence plays a vital role in helping achieve targets like ensuring food security, improving the health of people and promoting sustainable development in agriculture. Our model was found to be both dependable and adaptable, helping to enhance detection of plant diseases in contemporary farming practices.

title: SAMAGRA AI-Driven Unified Citizen Welfare Scheme Portal
authors: Jebakumar Immanuel D, Sabrish D, Krishnakanth N, Nishanth N
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Samagra thus stands out as an AI-driven, integrated platform that simplifies the process of finding out about, checking eligibility for, and applying for government welfare schemes. It integrates rule-based eligibility assessment with Aadhaar-based validation of beneficiaries through a mock data set and provides the right, personalized scheme recommendations to citizens. It also offers secured online applications, real-time tracking of application status, and reasoning in cases of rejections. Administrators will also gain insights on scheme demand, active users, and demographic trends on Power BI-integrated dashboards. In essence, the platform depicts how intelligent automation can reduce errors and prevent duplications in claims to achieve cost reductions, help the government improve the quality-ofservice delivery, and urban citizens.

title: Deep Learning for Coffee Leaf Disease Detection: Convolutional Neural Networks in Agricultural Applications
authors: Kanegonda Ravi Chythanya, Vishnu Kant, Pallavi Semwal, Anzar Ahmed
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The classification of coffee leaf diseases through automated methods helps to enhance agricultural output levels and maintain environmentally friendly agricultural systems. The research implements a CNN model to handle four major coffee leaf diseases, which reduce yield and degrade quality namely rust and red spider mite infestation and miner and phoma. Kaggle provided the dataset of 8,022 images that are grouped into five disease categories namely healthy, miner, phoma, red spider mite, and rust. The implemented model used CNN layers with batch normalization and dropout features alongside fully connected layers delivered 77% accuracy in validation tests. The model exhibited strong abilities to detect both healthy leaves and rust disease yet it mistakenly recognized rust as healthy or miner as rust disease occasionally. Deep learning potential has been demonstrated to improve food security while lowering pesticide usage together with enabling environmentally sustainable agricultural practices. The model's performance can be enhanced through additional diverse data sets and advanced techniques, which will generate improved precision and operational speed for detecting diseases in agricultural fields thus supporting sustainable development goals in agriculture.

title: Differentiated Task Design and Feedback of SOLO Classification Method Based on Large Language Model-A Case Study of Parabola Teaching in High School
authors: Jiali Tao
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Human-Computer Interaction, Neural Networks and Deep Learning
abstract: None

title: PS-Informer: An Informer-Based Wave Forecasting Model for Wave Energy Converter Applications
authors: Mohammad Amin Jarrahi, Kamyar Mehran
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Accurate multi-step wave forecasting is critical for optimizing power take-off (PTO) and ensuring the survivability of Wave Energy Converters (WECs). Conventional models are often site-specific and fail to generalize to locations without historical data. This paper presents the Pyramid-Sparse-Informer (PS-Informer), a Transformer-based architecture combining multi-scale temporal encoding with dynamic sparse attention to capture both global and local wave patterns efficiently. Trained on data from nine geographically diverse buoys, a single PS-Informer model simultaneously forecasts significant wave height, peak period, and peak direction for an unseen site. On a 7-day horizon, it achieves normalized RMSEs below 0.12 and reduces average error by 22% compared to an LSTM baseline. These results demonstrate a robust, data-efficient framework that enables reliable, zero-shot wave forecasting, supporting WEC integration into power system scheduling, market bidding, and real-time control in remote or data-scarce locations.

title: Swift: High-Performance Sparse-Dense Matrix Multiplication on GPUs
authors: Jinyu Hu, Huizhang Luo, Hong Jiang, Marc Casas, Kenli Li, Chubo Liu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Sparse-Dense Matrix Multiplication (SpMM) on GPUs has gained significant attention because of its importance in modern applications and the increasing computing power of GPUs in the last decade. Previous SpMM studies have focused on the importance of storage format and load balance for the overall performance of SpMM on GPUs. However, very little attention has been paid to the efficacy of coalesced memory access in improving the efficiency of data loading, which incurs a notable overhead that amounts to an average of more than 32% of the overall performance, according to our experimental observation. Existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) solutions fail to adequately support coalesced memory access of both sparse and dense matrices between the global memory and threads on GPUs. In this paper, we propose an efficient algorithm called Swift.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>Swift is available at https://github.com/MinttHu/Swift.git that speeds up the loading of both sparse and dense matrices of SpMM on modern GPUs. Leveraging coalesced memory access, Swift achieves high loading efficiency by sorting both the columns of the sparse matrix and elements of the dense matrix based on the number of non-zero elements and balancing the load by handling the regular and irregular parts differently and judiciously. Swift takes the Compressed Sparse Column format as an implementation case study to prove the concept and gain insights. We conduct a comprehensive comparison of Swift with four SOTA solutions: ASpT, cuSPARSE, RoDe, and Sputnik, using the full SuiteSparse Matrix Collection as the workload. The experimental results on RTX 4080s, RTX 3090Ti, A100, and V100 demonstrate that our method outperforms the baselines significantly.

title: Leveraging ASIC AI Chips for Homomorphic Encryption
authors: Jianming Tong, Tianhao Huang, Jingtian Dang, Leo de Castro, Anirudh Itagi, Anupam Golder, Asra Ali, Jeremy Kun, Jevin Jiang, Arvind, G. Edward Suh, Tushar Krishna
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Homomorphic Encryption (HE) provides strong data privacy for cloud services but at the cost of prohibitive computational overhead. While GPUs have emerged as a practical platform for accelerating HE, there remains an order-ofmagnitude energy-efficiency gap compared to specialized (but expensive) HE ASICs. This paper explores an alternate direction: leveraging existing AI accelerators, like Google's TPUs with coarse-grained compute and memory architectures, to offer a path toward ASIC-level energy efficiency for HE. However, this architectural paradigm creates a fundamental mismatch with SoTA HE algorithms designed for GPUs. These algorithms rely heavily on: (1) high-precision (32-bit) integer arithmetic to now run on a TPU's low-throughput vector unit, leaving its high-throughput low-precision (8-bit) matrix engine (MXU) idle, and (2) fine-grained data permutations that are inefficient on the TPU's coarse-grained memory subsystem. Consequently, porting GPU-optimized HE libraries to TPUs results in severe resource under-utilization and performance degradation. To tackle above challenges, we introduce CROSS, a compiler framework that systematically transforms HE workloads to align with the TPU's architecture. CROSS makes two key contributions: (1) Basis-Aligned Transformation (BAT), a novel technique that converts high-precision modular arithmetic into dense, low-precision (INT8) matrix multiplications, unlocking and improving the utilization of TPU's MXU for HE, and (2) Memory-Aligned Transformation (MAT), which eliminates costly runtime data reordering by embedding reordering into compute kernels through offline parameter transformation. Our evaluation on a real single-host Google TPU v6e refreshes the SoTA Number Theoretic Transform (NTT) throughput record with up-to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.43 \times$</tex> throughput improvement over WarpDrive on a NVIDIA A100. Furthermore, CROSS achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$451 \times, 7.81 \times, 1.83 \times, 1.31 \times, 1.86 \times$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.15 \times$</tex> higher throughput per watt than OpenFHE, WarpDrive, FIDESlib, FAB, HEAP, and Cheddar, respectively, establishing AI ASIC as the SotA efficient platform for HE operators. Code: https://github.com/EfficientPPML/CROSS.

title: SARRA: A Secure Adaptive and Resilient Routing Framework of FANETs with Hybrid Blockchain and Deep Q-Network Intelligence
authors: S. Gowthami, T. Kalavathi Devi, P. Sakthivel, Vivek Pandiya Raj
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), referred to as Flying Ad Hoc Networks (FANETs), play a crucial role in disaster response due to their ability to enable rapid communication without the need for existing infrastructure. However, their high mobility, frequent link disruptions, energy problems, and security risks all have a detrimental effect on the networks' performance and stability. To address these challenges, this paper introduces SARRA (Secure Adaptive Resilient Routing and Authentication), a hybrid intelligent model. SARRA integrates Deep Q-Network-based routing, lightweight blockchain authentication, Particle Swarm Optimization for efficient cluster head selection, Ant Colony Optimization for robust path recovery, and a Federated Learning-based Intrusion Detection System to detect threats while preserving privacy. The DQN agent provides active choices of the best paths to forward by considering multidimensional state variables such as residual energy, the quality of links, hop distance, degree of congestion, and the trust score, and blockchain provides decentralized and tamper-proof node authentication. Decades of simulations on the NS-3 environment demonstrate that the SARRA is superior to standard AODV, LEACH, and base DQN protocols in terms of Packet Delivery Ratio improvement of 19.6 percent, end-to-end delay reduction by 17.3 percent, average energy consumption decrease by 22.8 percent, and throughput increase by 25.1 percent. In addition, In addition, 18.4 more routing overhead is minimized, the network lifetime is prolonged by 21.7 and the FL-IDS detects intrusions with an accuracy of 94.6 with a false positive probability of 3.2. The experiment outcome validates that SARRA is an effective solution to provide highly resilient and fault-tolerant disasterresponse networking by virtue of the fact that it greatly increases the security, adaptability, and fault tolerance of FANET communication systems.

title: Deep Spectrogram-Based EEG Decoding of Visual Stimuli Using a Novel CNN Architecture
authors: Ajinkya Tamhankar, Abhijeet Kokare, Lokesh Khedekar, Amol Bajrang Chincholkar, Pranali Dhawas, Saundarya Raut
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Electroencephalography EEG - based decoding of visual stimuli has gained traction in computational neuroscience and brain-computer interface applications. This study explores the feasibility of using EEG spectrograms to classify visual stimuli using deep learning. A novel convolutional neural network, SpectrCNN, was developed, consisting of 6 convolutional layers, 2 dropout layers, 1 batch normalization layer, 1 max pooling layer, 1 flatten layer, and 1 fully connected (linear) layer. The model achieved a test accuracy of over 90 % across 20 classes, demonstrating its capability to extract meaningful spatial patterns from EEG spectrograms. While dataset imbalance posed a challenge, SpectrCNN outperformed existing EEG-based classification methods, reinforcing the potential of deep learning in neural signal interpretation. These findings contribute to advancements in cognitive neuroscience, offering prospects for future developments in EEG-based image reconstruction and brain-inspired artificial intelligence.

title: Transformer Lifespan Estimation Using Advanced Machine Learning and Condition Monitoring Data
authors: Kanaka Raju Kalla, Bokam Divakar, Ravi Kumar Bodda, Venu Malagavelli, Jaganmohan Rao Tarra, Badana Kiran Kumari, Kappala Sweta, Kondeti Jithendra, Bhargav Kumar Jaddu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The reliable estimation of transformer lifespan is essential for effective asset management and grid reliability in power systems. This paper proposes a data driven approach using the CatBoost machine learning algorithm to predict the remaining life expectancy of power transformers based on condition monitoring parameters. A dataset containing 470 real world records with dissolved gas analysis, insulation characteristics, and oil quality indicators is utilized. The model achieves a test $R^{2}$ score of 0.8369, with low error margins across MAE, MSE, and RMSE metrics. Feature importance analysis reveals that health index, water content, power factor, and interfacial voltage significantly influence aging behavior. The results highlight CatBoost’s superior predictive accuracy and interpretability, making it a promising tool for condition-based maintenance and lifecycle planning in electrical utilities.

title: FlexTrail: Human-in-the-Loop Simulation for Real-Time Exploration of HVAC Demand Flexibility
authors: Xinlei Zhou, Han Du, Emily Yap, Wanbin Dou, Mingyang Huang, Zhenjun Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper introduces FlexTrail, a human-in-the-loop simulation platform for exploring demand flexibility in building HVAC systems. The rising penetration of variable renewable energy sources makes such flexibility increasingly important, yet testing demand flexibility strategies in real buildings is costly and disruptive, making simulations a critical alternative. Conventional simulations are typically offline and non-interactive, requiring predefined control logic and restarts for even minor changes, which limits their ability to reflect operational realities. FlexTrail overcomes these constraints by coupling a high-fidelity simulator with a runtime dashboard, enabling users to adjust setpoints, schedules, and demand response modes during runtime and to quantify flexibility in real time against a stored baseline trajectory. A case study on a net-zero office building in Australia demonstrates user-driven demand response scenarios, measurable flexibility outcomes, and a shortened path from hypothesis to evidence. This interactive approach provides an intuitive and practice-oriented means of engaging with grid-responsive building operations.

title: Duty Ratio Modulated MTPA Control for Sensorless Induction Motor Drives in Off-Grid Solar Water Pumping System
authors: Anand Vardhan Pandey, Anjanee Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: This study describes the design and analysis of a two-stage improved sensorless direct torque flux control(I_DTC) water pumping system, using a solar photovoltaic (PV) induction motor (IM) drive, which is duty ratio regulated. Duty ratio improved sensorless direct torque control (I_DTC) can help to mitigate torque and flux ripple. The typical DTC scheme’s primary concerns are torque and stator flux ripple. The MTPA approach and (I_DTC) scheme are used in this work to increase the duty ratio efficiency and hence boost performance. Integrating the proposed techniques with existing methods in the motor drive system clearly results in lower current and torque ripples. The achieved benefits include reduced steady-state torque ripple, enhanced dynamic performance, and decreased stator current, making it highly beneficial for solar photovoltaic-based water pumping applications. To assess its performance, the suggested system is emulated in a MATLAB/Simulink environment across a wide range of insolation variations.

title: Multi-Lingual Contextual Audio Transcription and Translation to Selected Native Languages
authors: Olubusola Olufunke Nuga, Kamoli Akinwale Amusa, Ayorinde Joseph Olanipekun, Sikiru O. Ismail, Samuel Blessing Owusu, Joshua Oluwatimileyin Salako, Sikirulahi Opeyemi Abdulkareem, Ahmed Oluwafemi Lawal, Peter Olamide Onabanjo
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: This paper presents a contextual audio transcription and multilingual translation system designed for native languages. Utilizing advanced technologies such as AssemblyAI for accurate audio-to-text conversion, BERTopic for contextual topic modeling, and OpenAI’s API for indigenous language translation, the system demonstrates exceptional performance. AssemblyAI achieves low word error rates (WER), outperforming other transcription models. BERTopic effectively extracts meaningful topics, surpassing traditional models like Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) in coherence and interpretability. The translation component, powered by GPT-4, produces accurate and contextually appropriate translations with low perplexity scores. This integrated approach bridges communication gaps in multilingual and multicultural contexts, offering a valuable tool for educators, professionals, and content creators to promote inclusivity and digital accessibility. While challenges with noisy data and language diversity remain, this system highlights the effectiveness of combining transcription, topic modeling, and translation tasks.

title: NextGen IoT Security: A Decentralized Model for Data Integrity and Access Control
authors: Kishan Makadiya, Madhu Shukla
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: With the rapid growth of digital data and inter-connected devices, the integration of blockchain and Internet of Things technology offers a promising chance to revolutionize data management approaches. With today's global connectivity, the management and securing of ever-increasing amounts the amount of data produced by Internet of Things (IoT) devices is becoming an increasing concern. This study investigates a new solution to address such a concern by integrating blockchain technology into IoT systems. The aim is to develop a strong framework that takes advantage of the decentralized and secure character of blockchain and the wall-to-wall connectivity offered by the IoT. This integration is intended to address important needs such as data integrity, The integration aims to tackle crucial issues like data integrity, privacy, and unauthorized access. With the connectivity and sensing capabilities of the IoT, along with the blockchain technology of decentralized consensus and immutable ledgers, businesses are able to develop secure systems to retrieve, transfer, and store such data. In the experiments conducted on the Ethereum blockchain platform, it has been found that the blockchain and IoT technology solution not only secures data but also facilitates information management. The experiment results demonstrate that the proposed solution is able to protect against tampering, increase the transparency of transactions, and to minimize the chances of unauthorized access. The framework is scalable and highly adaptable, and it is therefore a promising solution for various applications. This framework employs an innovative approach to ensure secure and efficient data management.

title: Modeling and Simulation of a Dual-Battery System for Thermal Runaway Prevention in Electric Vehicles
authors: Sasi Preetha D, Ruba M, Bruntha R, Balaji V, Hariharan M, Divya Keshavi M
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Burning of carbon-based fuels causes environmental pollution. To avoid this environmental threat, the best replacement available is electric vehicles. The electric vehicles make use of lithium-ion batteries, which can store more energy in less volume. The major problem with lithiumion batteries is that at high-temperature conditions, thermal runaway comes into action and causes the risk of explosion of the electric vehicles. To prevent the explosion of the batteries, they should be operated in the safer temperature range. This paper introduces a methodology to sort out this issue by a dual battery system that activates only one battery at a time, which has a lower temperature than the threshold, and keeping another battery in idle condition, which has a temperature higher than the threshold. By using this methodology, chances of thermal runaway can be reduced by limiting the maximum operating temperature to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4 5}^{{\circ}} \mathbf{C}$</tex>.

title: A Symmetric Bipolar Isolated Combined Cuk-SEPIC Converter Using Single Switch
authors: Gustavo Orue, Hafiz Furqan Ahmed, Mohsin Jamil
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This work presents a newly developed isolated symmetric bipolar DC-DC topology with an adjustable high voltage gain that can be regulated through independent parameters. The proposed topology delivers equal-magnitude positive and negative output voltages referenced to a common ground, based on the combination of the typical characteristics found in the Single-Ended Primary-Inductor Converter (SEPIC) and the inverting output seen on the Ćuk converter that operates with a single active switch. The design features two key control parameters: the transformer turns ratio and the switching duty cycle, providing flexible voltage gain control. A turns ratio of one enables a wide buck-boost voltage range, while increasing the ratio facilitates integration with low-voltage renewable sources connected to bipolar DC grids. The proposed converter maintains a continuous input current with minimized ripple, making it highly appropriate for integration with renewable energy sources and energy storage systems. Its performance is substantiated through both theoretical modeling and simulation evaluation, using a model designed to operate in continuous conduction mode (CCM).

title: Renewable Energy and Loads Uncertainty Modeling for Power System Planning
authors: Yuli Astriani, Pinto Anugrah, Mithulananthan Nadarajah
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Large-scale renewable energy (RE) integration creates significant grid reliability challenges, such as unmet load and excess generation. Considering the distinct daily patterns from RE sources and load profiles, this study proposes comprehensive uncertainty modeling for RE resources and load profile diurnal variations to mitigate this reliability issue. The clear sky index (CSI) is used to enhance the standard Beta probability distribution to model solar photovoltaic (PV) uncertainty. Meanwhile, maximum likelihood estimation is used to optimize Weibull parameters for modeling wind turbine (WT) output power uncertainty. Moreover, this study introduces variability-based load uncertainty modelling. The load with lower variability is modeled using a Gaussian mixture model, while the load with higher variability is modeled using K-means clustering with kernel density estimation. With the proposed framework, this study generated 20 uncertainty scenarios of PV power, wind power, and load variability. The result shows a realistic uncertainty spread, while preserving the original trend. Furthermore, a modified IEEE 9-bus system is utilized to simulate the generated scenarios on a power grid. The simulation results demonstrated the potential of unmet loads and excess generation, which can be used in determining risk-based operational planning.

title: Resilience Enhancement of the ITER Pulsed Power Electrical Network Using Grid-Forming STATCOM
authors: Dengge Jia, Lei Li, Jun Tao, Yiyun Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: To ensure stable operation of the ITER machine, its Pulsed Power Electrical Network (PPEN) necessitates an advanced power compensation system characterized by high dynamic response capability, substantial capacity, and strong resilience to provide required inertia and damping under extreme plasma control conditions. This paper proposes a Grid-Forming (GFM)-based Static Synchronous Compensator (STATCOM) control framework integrated with Linear Active Disturbance Rejection Control (LADRC) for the PPEN future upgrade. The integrated Virtual Synchronous Generator (VSG) control maintains voltage and frequency resilience by imitating the response characteristics of Synchronous Generators (SGs) to provide synthetic inertia and damping during large grid disturbances. Simulations validate the controller's effectiveness against grid contingencies (e.g., phase jumps, voltage sags, and abrupt frequency changes), demonstrating its strong potential for practical deployment in the nuclear fusion scenarios. Significantly, under extremely harsh external frequency disturbances, the proposed LADRC-GFM control combination achieves superior transient performance, specifically with reduced overshoot and oscillation in critical control signals, compared to conventional GFM compensators. This work provides a resilience enhancement framework addressing operational challenges in the ITER PPEN.

title: Conduit: Programmer-Transparent Near-Data Processing Using Multiple Compute-Capable Resources in Solid State Drives
authors: Rakesh Nadig, Vamanan Arulchelvan, Mayank Kabra, Harshita Gupta, Rahul Bera, Nika Mansouri Ghiasi, Nanditha Rao, Qingcai Jiang, Andreas Kosmas Kakolyris, Yu Liang, Mohammad Sadrosadati, Onur Mutlu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: 抓取摘要失败: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='ieeexplore.ieee.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /document/11408437/ (Caused by ProtocolError('Connection aborted.', ConnectionResetError(104, 'Connection reset by peer')))

title: Design of Machine Learning Methodology for Cardiovascular Disease Classification and Early Prediction based on Big Data
authors: Amar Paul Singh, Yogesh Mohan
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Predictive analytics in cardiovascular disease (CVD) diagnosis is one of the most crucial areas where the application of machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) may significantly contribute to the improvement of early detection and prevention-related practices of the disease. The given research takes advantage of the Framingham Heart Study dataset to compare and assess various models, such as Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Support Vector Machine, Gradient Boosting, Deep Neural Network, and Hybrid Model proposed. The models were experimented in the same experimental environment and stringent preprocessing, hyperparameter optimization, and balancing of the data used to guarantee fairness and reproducibility.The findings indicate that deep learning models have notable performance improvement in predictive performance and generalization ability compared to the conventional ML models, even though there are many options which would give a good baseline performance. The Hybrid Model proposed had the best evaluation measures-Accuracy of 0.953, Precision of 0.957, Recall of 0.949, F1-Score of 0.953 and AUC of 0.982 that beat all the other methods. These results justify the possibility of integrating deep neural architectures and feature selection through gradient-boosting-based feature-selection to enhance the credibility of the CVD risk prediction systems.

title: SSSM-Based Data-Driven Real-Time Wind Power Forecasting for Inertia Support Scenario
authors: Liu Hang, Zhao Jinbin, Zhang Lijun, Jia Feng, Li Zhenkun, Zhang Zhiquan, Ge Xiaolin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Recurrence-based models inherently possess structural advantages in capturing temporal dependencies within wind power sequences, making them widely adopted in wind power forecasting applications. However, their strictly sequential computing mechanism leads to significantly constrained computational efficiency, thereby limiting their applicability in inertia support scenarios that demand both high precision and stringent timeliness. To overcome this limitation, we employ the Selective State Space Model (SSSM). This study is the first to apply this recurrent neural network that supports parallel recursion to the inertia support scenario, leveraging its structure for the rapid and precise modeling of temporal dependencies. Experiments on a real-world wind farm dataset show the proposed method achieves a significant <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 8. 7 8 \%}$</tex> improvement in timeliness while maintaining accuracy comparable to the state-of-the-art LSTM-based approach.

title: A Double Deep Q-Learning Based Real-Time Energy Management Optimization for Microgrids
authors: Xiangru Shi, Flavie Didier, Abderrezak Badji, Sheikh Izzal Azid, Maurizio Cirrincione, Salah Laghrouche
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The uncertainties introduced by distributed energy resources bring significant challenges to the real-time operation of microgrids, particularly in managing the fluctuating generation and load profiles required for optimal energy management system (EMS) performance. This study proposes a deep reinforcement learning-based framework to achieve real-time scheduling of battery storage within a grid-connected microgrid. The control problem is formulated as a Markov Decision Process (MDP), a Double Deep Q-Network (DDQN) agent is trained to generate optimal charging and discharging actions based on observed system states. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed EMS controller can effectively derive optimal control policies, ensuring system stability, cost efficiency, and energy selfsufficiency.

title: Ultra-Short-Term Reginal Photovoltaic Power Forecasting Based on Satellite Cloud Images and Graph Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Chenglin Ren, Zhen Shao, Qinggui Chen, Qian Chen, Guangqiang Peng, Maolan Peng, Haonan Dai, Fei Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Accurate ultra-short-term forecasting of regional photovoltaic (PV) output is of great significance for enhancing the security and stability of power systems. To address the inadequate consideration of spatiotemporal correlations among cloud clusters in different sub-regions in existing regional PV forecasting research this paper proposes an ultra-short-term regional PV power forecasting method based on satellite cloud images and Graph Convolutional Neural Networks (GCNs). First, the target area is divided into four sub-cluster regions based on solar irradiance resources and geographical information, and representative PV plants are selected using statistical principles. Then, satellite cloud images of each sub-cluster are obtained, and a convolutional autoencoder is employed to extract cloud image features. Finally, the power outputs of representative sites in each sub-cluster are used as node features in the graph, while the spatiotemporal correlations of cloud features among sub-cluster are used to construct a dynamic directed adjacency matrix. A GCN-based forecasting model is then developed to perform ultra-short-term PV power forecasting. Simulation results based on real-world data from the southeastern region of Tibet, China, verify the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Dynamic Mode Identification and Frequency Stability Enhancement for Grid-Forming Converters
authors: Shan Jiang, Rui Kong, Ye Zhu, Frede Blaabjerg, Georgios Konstantinou
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Grid-forming (GFM) converters are expected to mitigate frequency variations and oscillations during grid contingencies. However, generic damping design of GFM converters cannot ensure frequency stability of power systems with spatial-temporal uncertainties. In this paper, the dynamic mode decomposition (DMD) method is introduced to extract frequency-domain dynamic modes from the time-domain frequency response of GFM converters during grid events. Even without access to detailed system configuration, dynamic modes enable GFM converters to assess frequency stability in a data-driven manner. This paper also proposes a damping enhancement method tailored for those critical modes with insufficient stability margin. The narrow band damping power introduced by a bandpass filter compensates for the damping of critical modes and mitigates both power and frequency oscillations.

title: Cooperative Game-Theoretic Inverse Reinforcement Learning for Smart Grid Scheduling
authors: Ganesh Babu R, Arthi C S, Nallathambi A, Vankayala Chethan Prakash, Santhi K, Arunkumar R
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The growing penetration of renewable energy resources and distributed energy units introduces significant challenges in ensuring sustainable, fair, and efficient scheduling in smart grids. Traditional non-cooperative optimization approaches often produce inequitable cost distribution and unstable scheduling, limiting both renewable integration and stakeholder participation. To address these challenges, this paper presents a Cooperative Game-Theoretic and Inverse Reinforcement Learning (CGT-IRL) framework for renewable-integrated smart grid scheduling. In the proposed model, generators, storage units, prosumers, and electric vehicles are modeled as rational players that infer hidden reward functions from expert demonstrations and historical operational data under uncertainty. Coalition values derived from IRL-informed scheduling outcomes are allocated using cooperative game-theoretic solution concepts, including the Shapley Value, Nucleolus, and Core, thereby ensuring fairness and coalition stability. The proposed framework is validated on IEEE benchmark test systems under multiple renewable penetration and demand scenarios, and compared against IRLonly, CGT-only, and non-cooperative baselines. Experimental results indicate that CGT-IRL reduces total operating costs by 8–12%, increases renewable energy utilization by up to 15%, and provides more equitable benefit allocation than the baselines. By linking interpretability-driven learning with fairness-oriented coalition decision-making, the study demonstrates a scalable and socially responsible scheduling strategy for next-generation sustainable smart grids.

title: A Comprehensive Review of Modern Therapeutic and Wearable Technologies in Rehabilitation
authors: Gurjeet Kaur, Harshraj N. Gadbail, Sujal Zade
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The modern rehabilitation is changing due to the advanced technological integration that allows accurate biofeedback, enhancing patient engagement, and providing favourable functional recovery outcomes. Recent research has shown that intelligent therapeutic devices can aid custom and remotely controlled exercise programs, which enhance therapy effectiveness and compliance rates. The system of immersive virtual and augmented reality (VR/AR) has demonstrated considerable opportunities in enhancing motivation and faster motor learning with the help of interactive and task-based training. Robotic exoskeletons and wearable sensors in neurorehabilitation are objective and quantitative data used as the basis of adaptive and data-driven therapeutic interventions. Occupational and physical therapy interventions that are combined to form hybrid rehabilitation technology further foster patient autonomy, as well as continuity of care. Moreover, consumer-grade wearable devices of consumer grade provide available solutions to the long-term monitoring of neurological disorders and sleep-related disorders in the clinical and home-based contexts. In general, the introduction of evidence-based rehabilitation technologies has great potential for patientcentred care. The next steps to be undertaken in research should be a large-scale clinical validation, systems interoperability, and economic solutions to make them accessible to more people in a broader manner.

title: Classifying Telugu Sentiments Using Transformed-Based Methods
authors: Elijah Francis, P Praveen
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: In resource-rich languages like English, sentiment analysis is a crucial part. However, because of a number of issues, like an insufficiency of training corpora, poor-resource languages like Telugu have seen little effort. NLP models and the lack of benchmark datasets for assessment. The recent explosion of transformed-based models in NLP makes it possible to achieve remarkable results in these tasks. Though they are giving good results, especially for languages where very less data is available. researchers are becoming more and more involved in investigating the possibilities of transformed based models that have already been pre-trained in multiple languages. The study evaluates the Telugu sentence-level sentiment classification performance of four multilingual pre-trained transformer models:IndicBERT, RoBERTa, DeBERTa, and XLM-RoBERTa. assessed the effectiveness of each of the four models with our datasets, which includes multiple Telugu language domain datasets. We evaluated these models' performance on three distinct datasets and found encouraging results. XLM-RoBERTa classifies binary sentiment with a decent accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 0. 0 9} {\%}$</tex>. For Telugu language sentiment analysis.

title: Survey and Design of Optimized Network-On-Chip Router
authors: Sowmya B J, Manjunath Managuli
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: A system on a chip is an intricate network of interconnected functional components. Since its architecture is bus-based, there is problem in proper communication necessitating the development of a system exhibiting parallelism and high scalability. Network on a chip (NoC) offered many of these features and addressed the communication bottleneck issue. It essentially operates on the principle of core interconnection via on-chip networks. Bus-based communication System on chip (SoC) problems are resolved by network on chip (NoC) technology. Network on a chip offers high speed and reliable data while maintain power requirements. The routers that make up a NoC network route data packets. Hence, this is the essential component that must be appropriately constructed to execute an effective NoC architecture. The primary limitations of classic NoC router architecture include complexity of circuits, significant crucial delay in path, utilization of resources, high speed execution and energy efficiency. It has been difficult to develop a NoC with low area overhead, high performance, and low latency. In order to understand and work toward a NoC router design that demonstrates better performance, exhibit power savings and minimized latency and can be utilized with families of FPGA, this work analyzes basics of design of NoC router, its component parts and study of previous work and their advantages and disadvantages for NoC router topologies. The work towards the design of enhanced Network on chip router with maximum benefits is attempted.

title: Financial Institution Customer Segmentation Using Unsupervised Learning – A Data-Driven Approach to Customer Profiling
authors: Shivam Tiwari, Garima Vijh, Nisha Sharma, Surbhi Vijh, Sumit Kumar, Krati Rastogi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Banks gather a lot of information on their customers' transactions, but it’s not simple to understand the pattern of information easily. Identifying spending patterns, detecting fraud, and offering personalized financial services requires effective customer segmentation. Traditional methods rely on fixed rules, which do not adapt well to changing customer behaviour and fail to properly group different types of customers. Some machine learning methods, like supervised classification, need labelled data, which is often unavailable. Basic clustering methods also struggle when dealing with large and complex banking datasets. In this research, the eight different clustering techniques K-Medoids, DBSCAN, HDBSCAN, BIRCH, Gaussian Mixture Model (GMM), K-Means, Agglomerative Clustering, and Affinity Propagation are tested— to put bank clients into groups depending on how much money they have in their accounts and how often they make transactions. We measured the effectiveness of these methods using three key evaluation scores: Davies-Bouldin Index, Silhouette Score, and Calinski-Harabasz Index. According to findings, K-Means had the highest Silhouette Score of 0.8606, while BIRCH and Agglomerative Clustering has outcome of 0.8528. The Calinski-Harabasz Index gave Affinity Propagation a score of 6904.06, but DBSCAN and HDBSCAN produced meaningless clusters and so had missing values in the assessment. Based on the results of this study, banks can improve their targeted marketing, risk assessment, and fraud detection by using centroid-based and hierarchical clustering approaches to segment their consumers. Banks can use these results to improve their data analysis methods and create more useful consumer segmentation models.

title: Real time Traffic Prediction and Management Using Reinforcement Learning Algorithm
authors: Ashok Kumar Bandla, Y. Lavanya, S. Anatha Kranthi, G. Kaladhar, K. Ramesh Babu, Amaralingeswara Rao Nune
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Urban mobility is increasingly challenged by rising population density and traffic congestion. This study explores the application of Reinforcement Learning (RL) techniques in smart traffic management, specifically for optimizing traffic signal control. We propose an innovative RL framework that adjusts signal timings based on real-time traffic conditions, utilizing both historical data and live sensor inputs. Extensive simulations conducted in a realistic urban environment demonstrate that our RL approach reduces average vehicle delay by up to 30% compared to traditional methods. Additionally, the framework achieves a 25% increase in intersection throughput, indicating significant improvements in traffic efficiency. Our analysis also confirms the model's scalability across various urban layouts and traffic patterns, showcasing its adaptability. This research contributes valuable insights into intelligent transportation systems, emphasizing the potential of RL for enhancing urban mobility. The findings suggest that integrating advanced technologies into traffic management can effectively address urban challenges. Future work will focus on real-world implementation and further optimization of the proposed RL model.

title: A Novel High Gain Boost Common-Ground Inverter Without Shoot-Through Issue
authors: I Chen Hung, Hafiz Furqan Ahmed, Mohsin Jamil
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper presents an innovative common-ground inverter that offers both voltage boosting capability and inherent prevention of shoot-through issues. The first notable feature of the proposed inverter is its high voltage gain, which enables the generation of a high output voltage from a low input voltage, thereby broadening its applicability. he second key feature is its ability to operate without the need for dead time. In the proposed design, a dedicated switching cell is integrated to inherently prevent shoot-through, eliminating the need for dead time, simplifying control signal design, and reducing the risk of increased total harmonic distortion (THD). The third feature is the inverter's common-ground configuration, which effectively suppresses leakage current. As a result, the proposed structure comprehensively addresses the issues of limited voltage gain, shoot-through risk, and leakage current. A detailed circuit analysis is provided, along with simulation and experimental verifications.

title: A Federated Privatized Generative System for Smarter Image Integrity and Context-Tuned Tamper
authors: Kalaiselvi S, Rajesh M, Lalithambikai S, Rubigha M, S. B. Manoojkumaar, B. Mohanraj
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The risks of preserving the integrity of the visual media has been exacerbated by a fast pace of development of various forms of digital manipulation, especially in the fields with sensitivity, like journalism, law enforcement, and digital archiving. Centralized approaches of securing integrity verification in traditionally used images often encounter some pitfalls of it regarding data confidentiality, single-point vulnerability, and non-generalizability to heterogeneous user settings. The proposed work comes up with the GenFedNet, a framework that incorporates both federated and generative learning and performs tampered image content mitigation and recovery securely and in a decentralized manner. GenFedNet has the potential to support privacy-preserving training compared to contextual accuracy by integrating the following: (1) Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) to detect the anomaly in localization, (2) Vision Transformers (ViT) to extract the global features and (3) Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) to restart the reconstruction of the semantics. There is local production of structural maps then the data is shared to form global model, and there is no exchange of raw image data hence the system is confidential. The system has an adaptive thresholds-based anomaly detection and a similarity-based scoring to predict authenticity. A vast body of experiments over simulated tampering environments supports the solidity of the framework with a tamper detection rate of 94.73% and a structural integrity of more than 91.5 rates after recovery. The findings show that GenFedNet is effective to increase reliability of image verification and provide consistent and high-fidelity restoration without limitations to user privacy. This is scalable technology that establishes a new benchmark of such a distributed image forensics and integrity assurance systems

title: Transferring Packetized Energy Across Cells with Asynchronous Forwarding
authors: Christoph Hildenbrand, Klemens Schneider, Dominik Schulz, Marc Hiller, Veit Hagenmeyer, Martina Zitterbart
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Energy routers are novel power electronic devices, which can link previously unconnected parts of the power grid. They can steer the electric currents in a meshed power grid, helping to unburden overloaded power lines. Additionally, the energy routers partition the power grid into electrical cells which can operate independently of the wide area synchronous grid. In this paper, we advance the 'Energy Packet Grid', a novel control architecture to organize the envisioned future power grid with energy routers. In this architecture, energy is exchanged with 'Energy Packet Transfers', which can be viewed as short-term contracts between energy routers and other power electronic devices. The core contribution of this paper is 'Asynchronous Forwarding', a protocol that organizes Energy Packet Transfers across multiple cells and energy routers. It provides a flexible and loss-efficient way to organize power flows across cells. We evaluated Asynchronous Forwarding in our custom simulator and in hardware experiments, using real-life power electronic devices, demonstrating the protocol's feasibility.

title: A Web-Based Smart Farming System with Voice Control and Multilingual Assistance
authors: Dr. V.V.A.S Lakshmi, Navadurga Meda, Sudhakar Babu Prathipati, Adikesava Reddy Daggula, Laxmi Inti, Nayan Rai
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: In India, many farmers are not highly literate and as such adoption of agricultural applications that are text-intensive is not easy despite being frequent users of mobile devices. Majorities of the existing platforms offer solutions to crop diseases yet they cannot ensure that farmers can understand and put into action the advised measures. Investigating the usability of the application through a survey of 20 farmers, we have revealed that language barriers and complex interface of applications has a major impact on usability. In order to overcome this dilemma, we have put forward a solution where farmers will have ready access to a farmer-friendly system of agriculture combined with a voice helper, which can represent several local languages. This voice controllable interface means that users who are of low literacy can operate without any problems. Our system is based on the machine learning (ML) and deep learning (DL) algorithms to precisely find out crop diseases based on image recognition and environmental data. It then gives personal recommendation to farmers and plant doctors to manage the disease efficiently. The system also recommends the most suitable crops on the basis of the real-time environmental conditions and also gets voice survey feedback of the users to track the adoption and also customize based on the responses.

title: A Fixed Frequency LLC Converter with Phase Shift Controlled Semi Active Rectifier for PV Applications
authors: K S Phani Kiranmai, Amanuel Kiros, Mebruk Kedir, Hussain Shareef, Rachid Errouissi, SVK Naresh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper proposes phase angle control of the rectifier (PAC-R) of LLC resonant converter offering a high gain to accommodate wide input voltage variations while maintaining a stable output. To enhance control flexibility in the converter, the lower-side diodes of output diode bridge rectifier are substituted with MOSFET switches. The converter operates at resonant frequency, which simplifies the magnetic component design and lower circulating currents. Additionally, the use of MOSFETs in place of diodes in the rectifier reduces conduction losses, thus enhancing overall efficiency under normal operating operation. The inherent soft switching capability of all switching devices utilized in the proposed control method significantly contributes to the increased system efficiency. The paper details the operational stages, converter gain characteristics, and design guidelines of the converter parameters. Simulation results are included to validate the converter's advantages and confirm its functionality.

title: Fine-Tuning BERT Models for Conversational Question Answering on the CoQA Dataset
authors: Shreya S, Varsha Venkataraghavan, Chandrashekhar Pomu Chavan
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: This paper explores the fine-tuning of pre-trained language models for the task of Question Answering (QA) within a specific domain. The approach focuses on leveraging the power of six pre-trained language models, which are BERT, ALBERT, RoBERTa, MobileBERT, TinyBERT, and ELECTRA to address domain-specific QA challenges. Using the CoQA dataset, we investigate the effectiveness of these models when fine-tuned on a question-answering task, demonstrating improvements in accuracy and performance. Our experiments reveal key insights into the behavior of these models and their ability to adapt to a specialized domain, using a combination of evaluation metrics such as Average Exact Match (EM) and Average F1 scores. Additionally, we present visualizations to better understand their efficiency. The results show that fine-tuning these models yields significant improvements over base models, confirming their applicability in real-world domain-specific QA tasks.

title: Context Aware Real Time Data Processing Algorithms for Enhanced Performance in Edge Computing Networks
authors: Nidal Al Said
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Edge computing has become a disruptive technology to address the increased need of low latency and context-based data processing in the resource-constrained IoT and IIoT settings. Yet, the traditional methods are not flexible and resistant to changes in making decisions in a dynamic environment. This paper presents CANE-Net (Context-Aware Neural Edge Network), a neural network-based intelligent framework that is lightweight, but capable of processing real-time data and changing in accordance with the differences in the context. CANE-Net incorporates a purposeful encoder of context, alongside an adaptive architecture of GRU, to make temporal representation and situational responsiveness better. The model is trained and tested on the Edge-IIoTset cybersecurity dataset, having a well-defined Python-based implementation. The experimental test shows up with 98.5 accuracy and results indicate that CANE-Net is always better in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score than the current baseline models, which means that it is highly reliable and effective. In general, this paper demonstrates that context-aware neural processing plays a crucial role in enhancing realtime edge analytics and has an intelligent and scalable basis with which to be implemented in smart cities, industrial systems, and edge-oriented security applications.

title: EmpaAI: Intelligent Query-Based Mental Health Assistance
authors: Suresh Babu Dasari, Hrutik Timmala, Sai Vadaparthi, Laxminarayana Thungula
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: EmpaAI is an AI-powered mental health chatbot designed to provide intelligent, context-aware support by lever-aging Generative AI and retrieval-based questionanswering techniques. The system allows users to upload text-based documents, including PDFs and Word files, which are processed for semantic understanding and contextual retrieval. It comprises multiple modules, including document ingestion, user query input, language model processing, text chunking, embeddings generation, vector storage, and a retrieval-based QA chain. By utilizing a pre-trained Large Language Model (LLM)[9] and vector-based search in Chroma, EmpaAI ensures that user queries are matched with the most relevant document sections to generate meaningful, empathetic, and evidence-based responses. Unlike conventional chatbots, EmpaAI enhances mental health support by delivering fast, accurate, and contextually relevant answers, bridging the gap between users and mental health knowledge. This system aims to make self-led mental health assistance more accessible, efficient, and reliable, empowering individuals with AI -driven insights while ensuring data privacy and security.
