title: CNN-Based Thoracic Imaging Analysis for Pneumonia Detection using Chest Radiographs
authors: Serena Eugene Francis, Chaitra M, Mohamed Faisal Hussain
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Pneumonia is still one of the top causes of morbidity and mortality worldwide, posing a serious and prevalent health issue across all age groups. The suggested approach leverages deep learning, particularly Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), to automatically identify pneumonia by analysis of chest radiographs. This methodology involves preprocessing, normalization, and data augmentation processes to elevate the model’s generalization ability. The resultant framework is trained on labeled datasets to proficiently differentiate between healthy lungs and those affected by pneumonia. Standard metrics such as F1-score, precision, recall, and accuracy are used to measure its performance. This proposed solution aims to assist radiologists by enabling them to make quick and precise diagnoses, seeking to lessen their workload and errors.

title: Comparative Review of Advanced Bidirectional DC-DC Converters for EV Charging Applications
authors: Phularenu Das, Souvik Mondal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) and Grid-to-Vehicle (G2V) EV charging with great efficiency and a broad voltage range are made possible by sophisticated bidirectional DC-DC converters. This review critically compares four recent converter topologies: an extendable quadratic converter, a wide-range high-gain converter, a modified-SEPIC based lowvoltage charger, and general DC-DC/DC-AC bidirectional architectures. We examine each design’s architecture, voltage conversion ratios, semiconductor stress or utilization factor (SUF), control methods (e.g., deadbeat control), component count, and practical feasibility. Key differences from conventional boost-buck converters (which suffer low voltage transfer ratios) are highlighted. An illustrative summary table compares these topologies side-by-side. We discuss implementation issues - such as maintaining a common ground to avoid EMI issues, trade-offs between component count and gain, and control complexity - and identify future directions like wide-bandgap devices and single-stage integration. This comprehensive analysis informs the selection and development of high-performance bi-directional converters for nextgeneration EV chargers.

title: Threat Modeling and Mitigation of Social Engineering Attacks in Information Security Domains
authors: Sanjam Bhardwaj, Achyut Shankar, Shuchi Mala, Nidhi Mishra, Aakanshi Gupta
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Social Engineering is an essential, powerful and easy-to-execute attack in the world of penetration testing. It relies on the ability of the attacker to manipulate, execute and set-up physical as well as digital attack vectors under social engineering to reach the intended goal of the attacker. Organizations and individuals of all degrees and walks of life are vulnerable to this type of attack since it utilizes the weakest link in security to breach it, which often are the people of the organization or the individual themselves. Social engineering attacks are almost, if not all, offensive attacks utilizing various tools and tactics to breach security. In the world of IT and computing, social engineering attacks have become increasingly and alarmingly more common and continue to grow at an exponential rate around the world. Social engineering encompasses a broad range of attacks, including fraudulent phone calls and conventional phishing. This paper highlights the various tools, tactics and measures used by attackers to breach security in a practical manner and also helps learn about a robust and efficient defensive strategy against the said attacks.

title: Characteristic Investigation of Active Distribution Networks Considering Demand-Side Response
authors: Ran Tao, Ranran An, Ruijin He, Lei Chen, Hongkun Chen, Yao Guo
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The integration of diverse distributed energy resources (DERs) with various control strategies into distribution networks has created notable challenges in analyzing operational characteristics. This paper presents a comprehensive study of active distribution networks (ADNs) that incorporate demand-side response. It begins by developing models for both shiftable loads and reducible loads, focusing on the time-based load shifting of shiftable loads and the capacity to adjust power levels of reducible loads. Then, a digital model of the ADNs is created, and simulation case studies evaluate the effectiveness of demand-side response, demonstrating a 6.5% decrease in load fluctuation amplitude. Coordinated control of energy storage systems (ESSs) ensures the state of charge (SOC) remains within a proper range. Additionally, optimizing the integration of demand response with distributed generation significantly improves voltage profiles at network nodes and greatly reduces branch network losses.

title: Harnessing the Sun: A Comprehensive Review of Solar Panel Applications in Industrial and Domestic Environments
authors: Betha Rajeswari, Satish KumarGudey, Ellapu Neelima, Doddi Prem Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Based on power demand, renewable energy sources play a critical role worldwide. In this paper, various types of solar panels applicable in the commercial and domestic sectors are discussed. Each panel has its unique specifications of cost, efficiency, durability, and sustainability, and compares to the others. Mono-crystalline, Polycrystalline, Thin-Film, and emerging technologies like Bifacial and Perovskite are used in the multiple sectors of both the Industrial and domestic sectors. Monocrystalline solar panels have the highest efficiency and long durability and are commonly used in industries and efficient while compared with polycrystalline, which budget friendly and commonly used in domestic sector. Thin-film solar panels, which are lightweight and more flexible commonly used in the large-scale industrial and agricultural sectors. This work intends to summarize the various solar panels used to meet energy demand in both the industrial and domestic sectors.

title: An Innovative Cloud-Edge Computing Disaster Management Infrastructure with Blockchain Inclusion: Promoting Safe and Open Emergency Response Across Dispersed Networks
authors: Sivasankaran S, Ahmad Abdelhafiz Ali Samhan, A. Balamurugan, Ala’a Al-Shaikh, E. N. Vijaya Kumari, G. Karthikeyan
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: In a time of rising climate events, natural disasters, and emergencies, the need for comprehensive, secure and decentralized disaster management systems is more pressing than ever. Current centralized infrastructures generally experience latency, restricted coverage, and susceptibility to SPOFs even in the geographically remote or infrastructure-challenged areas. In this paper, we present an integrated disaster management framework based on the cloud-edge computing architecture and blockchain integration to provide a real-time coordination mechanism with a strong guarantee of the data integrity, transparency, and verifiable agreement among the emergency response networks. The proposed solution takes advantage of cloud computing based centralized data analytics and resource orchestration as the major mechanism, and at the same time, utilizes edge computing for speedy on-the-spot data processing near to the disaster area so as to minimize the time of information delays and improving the efficiency of critical decision support. With the introduction of blockchains, the system supports tamper-resistant logging of events as well as confidential communication and traceable coordination among those stakeholders, in particular from departments of public safety, rescue teams, emergency response teams, hospitals, emergency medical services, and government agencies. The decentralization of blockchain makes it more resilient to infrastructure outages and no single entity has control over mission-critical data. They propose a three-layer architecture, including the perception layer (sensors, drones, IoT devices), the edge-cloud fusion layer (edge nodes and the cloud servers for real-time analytics) and the blockchain coordination layer (immutable ledger and smart contract execution). Smart contracts are utilized to automate emergency processes of access control, resource dispatch, and inter-agency coordination to increase efficiency and accountability. Simulation results show that the architecture can decrease the response time by 40% and improve fault tolerance by using distributed consensus protocols. The developed framework is applied on scenarios of disasters such as earthquake response management, flood monitoring and pandemic mitigation, and is trained on both simulated and real emergency management data. The proposed system scales better, provides greater security, and is more trustworthy than the conventional centralized schemes. It also features inter-device and device-routed messaging which facilitates uninterrupted communication in highly challenged or resource-starved environments.

title: Air Quality Forecasting Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Divyanshi Singh, Ananya Srivastava, Rajesh Kumar Shrivastava
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Recent years have seen the rise of air pollution as a serious environmental problem, particularly in cities where industrial waste and vehicle emissions significantly deteriorate the quality of the air. Making decisions on environmental policy and public health depends heavily on forecasting the Air Quality Index, or AQI. In this work, we use dataset info to investigate several deep learning and machine learning models for time series-based AQI prediction. Random Forest, Linear Regression, Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), and Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) were the models that were used. The R2 score, Mean Squared Error (MSE), Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and classification metrics following the binarization of the AQI values have all been used to assess the effectiveness of these models. While the linear regression provided a R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> of 0.92, deep learning models represented great capability in getting the temporal dependencies. However all these challengers like class imbalance have significantly affected the performance of the model, especially in binary classification tasks. Our comparative study has revealed that the insights, like the weaknesses and the strengths of every approach has given us the way for real time, efficient air quality monitoring solutions.

title: Dynamic Reference Anchor Selection for Enhanced TDoA-Based UWB Localization in Complex Scenarios
authors: Yiping Yang, Lei Zhang, Zhen Xi, Yu Sun, Qiwei Chen, Mingming Zhang
abstract: Previous studies on time difference of arrival (TDoA)-based ultra-wideband (UWB) localization typically employed an arbitrary and fixed reference anchor during the localization process. However, this scheme fails to maintain stable and high-quality performance in complex scenarios, since the localization of mobile targets suffers from multipath interference and the change in sensor-target geometry, while the reference anchor amplifies this effect. To address this issue, we present a dynamic reference anchor selection algorithm, which determines the reference anchor by evaluating the factor that fuses geographical factor and link quality. In addition, we integrate dynamic reference anchor selection into an optimized UWB localization approach to achieve high accuracy. The position is calculated by a constrained Taylor algorithm after UWB calibration and finally fine-tuned by a Kalman filter. The proposed approach was validated using data collected in both laboratory and office scenarios. The experimental results show that the 3D dynamic localization error of the proposed approach reaches 4.9 cm on average. The dynamic reference anchor selection algorithm we proposed improves the overall localization accuracy by 18.4 %, with a dominance of 87.8 % and 100 % in the laboratory and office scenario, respectively. Furthermore, the localization stability and reliability are also validated with a reduction in maximum localization error and avoidance of extreme outliers.

title: Citymind: A Multimodal Federated Transformer Framework for Real-Time Urban Anomaly Detection and Adaptive City Response
authors: Gude Sujatha, Srinivasa Rao Tottempudi, Rahimullah Shaik, Gowthamkumar Reddy Mittoor, Banka Madhavi, Boddu L V Siva RamaKrishna
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: A tremendous amount of data is constantly being generated by sources such as traffic and weather cameras, environmental sensors, digital infrastructure in city areas. In this situation, it has become a big challenge for the smart city managers to detect unusual events in real-time. In this paper, we have proposed a machine learning system based on the METR-LA city traffic dataset that detects abnormal conditions related to traffic and mobility. The proposed framework involves understanding the relationships between road segments by integrating temporal modeling with graph-based spatial learning and works accordingly to the dynamic changes that take place in the traffic flow. Using LSTM and transformer-based sequence modeling techniques, the system predicts normal traffic behavior and identifies deviations from that normal state - such as those associated with traffic congestion, accidents, or infrastructure faults. Experimental results show that, compared to traditional threshold-based and isolation-based methods, the proposed approach achieves greater accuracy, faster convergence, and lower false alarm rate. These results demonstrate that this approach can be effective in providing intelligent decision support to smart city traffic control systems.

title: Video Game Addiction, Mental Health, and Social Well-Being Through Structural Equation Modeling
authors: Tarika Singh Sikarwar, Nidhi Jain, Monika Gupta, Seema Mehta, Rachit Jain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Video games have become fun and popular amongst all age groups. The research begins by highlighting the widespread popularity of video games across various age groups and points out that while gaming can be enjoyable, overindulgence may negatively affect physical and mental health [1]. The research investigates how addiction to video games affects mental health, personal relationships. The study uses a sample of young individuals who completed questionnaires designed to assess their level of video game addiction, their mental health, and the quality of their relationships. The data collected was analyzed using linear regression and structural equation modeling. The results suggest that video game addiction has a significant negative impact on mental health and personal relationships. Specifically, excessive gaming may cause distractions that may lead to mental health risks (like anxiety or depression) and strain personal relationships (such as family or peer connections). The study emphasizes the importance of addressing video game addiction, as it can lead to serious repercussions such as mental health issues and social isolation. It suggests that excessive gaming interferes with essential activities like academics and relationship-building.

title: Grammar Error Detection and Correction in English Writing Using Sequence Labeling Networks
authors: Gurprem Kaur, Poonam Dhiman, Vaneet Kaur, Deeksha Verma, Shalini Kumari, Nabanita Roy
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The application of sequence labeling networks on grammar error detection and correction (GEDC) in English writing primarily on LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) networks integrated with Conditional Random Fields (CRF). To determine the performance of this method, we consider two publicly distributed databases: the FCE (First Certificate in English) corpus, and the NUCLE (National University of Singapore Corpus of Learner English). The findings indicate greater enhancements of conventional rule-based systems and the previous models of machine learning in identifying and rectifying prevalent maladies in writing. The model proposed presented an F1-Score of 0.89 with the FCE corpus in respect to the subject-verb agreement errors and 0.84 in respect to the NUCLE corpus in the case of tense consistency errors. The model has also demonstrated good results when dealing with prepositional error (F1-Score = 0.75) and article usage error (F1-Score = 0.80). The model attained an overall accuracy of 89%, which is better compared to the rule-based system, which has a tendency of achieving an average accuracy of 70-75 percent.Notwithstanding, this model suffered discourse level errors and word choice errors, having 0.68 F1-Score on word choice mistakes, which means that more advanced problems of grammar were still problematic. The paper indicates the prospect of sequence labeling networks in automating the grammar correction using the results to show deep learning models can be effective in identifying and correcting large sets of grammatical errors. The next step of work will be aimed at the necessity to improve the model to facilitate the treatment of more complex syntactic and discourse-related errors, which will further promote GEDC systems.

title: Message from the Technical Program Committee Co-Chairs
authors: Moussa Ayyash, Kamesh Namuduri
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: It is our great pleasure to welcome you to the 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST), held from September 23-25, 2025, in Orlando, Florida, USA.

title: RIHA: Report-Image Hierarchical Alignment for Radiology Report Generation
authors: Yucheng Chen, Yang Yu, Yufei Shi, Conghao Xiong, Xulei Yang, Si Yong Yeo
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Radiology report generation (RRG) has emerged as a promising approach to alleviate radiologists' workload and reduce human errors by automatically generating diagnostic reports from medical images. A key challenge in RRG is achieving fine-grained alignment between complex visual features and the hierarchical structure of long-form radiology reports. Although recent methods have improved image-text representation learning, they often treat reports as flat sequences, overlooking their structured sections and semantic hierarchies. This simplification hinders precise cross-modal alignment and weakens RRG accuracy. To address this challenge, we propose RIHA (Report-Image Hierarchical Alignment Transformer), a novel end-to-end framework that performs multi-level alignment between radiological images and their corresponding reports across paragraph, sentence, and word levels. This hierarchical alignment enables more precise cross-modal mapping, essential for capturing the nuanced semantics embedded in clinical narratives. Specifically, RIHA introduces a Visual Feature Pyramid (VFP) to extract multi-scale visual features and a Text Feature Pyramid (TFP) to represent multi-granularity textual structures. These components are integrated through a Cross-modal Hierarchical Alignment (CHA) module, leveraging optimal transport to effectively align visual and textual features across various levels. Furthermore, we incorporate Relative Positional Encoding (RPE) into the decoder to model spatial and semantic relationships among tokens, enhancing the token-level alignment between visual features and generated text. Extensive experiments on two benchmark chest X-ray datasets, IU-Xray and MIMIC-CXR, demonstrate that RIHA outperforms existing state-of-the-art models in both natural language generation and clinical efficacy metrics.

title: Dual-Input AWG with Adaptive Regression Algorithm for High-Precision FBG Interrogation
authors: Qichao Wang, Jiawen Lu, Yifei Chen, Dongyu Wang, Wanghua Zhu, Enze Zhou, Ji Zhang, Binfeng Yun, Guohua Hu, Yiping Cui
abstract: High-precision interrogation of fiber Bragg grating (FBG) sensors is critical for advanced sensing applications. We propose a time division multiplexing (TDM) interrogation scheme that combines a standard dual-input arrayed waveguide grating (AWG) with an adaptive LASSO-assisted polynomial regression algorithm, eliminating the traditional trade-off between hardware complexity and measurement accuracy. The core architecture leverages a high-speed optical switch to alternately excite two specifically positioned input ports of a standard AWG with a 1.6 nm channel spacing. Thus, it synthesizes an interleaved “virtual” channel grid with an effective 0.8-nm spacing without widening the optical passband. This relaxes the 3-dB bandwidth requirement for continuous interrogation by a factor of two and avoids the insertion loss penalty associated with passband broadening. Algorithmically, LASSO regularization enables self-adaptive selection of polynomial order while compensating for system nonlinearity and suppressing overfitting. Experiments demonstrate continuous interrogation over 34 nm with a root mean-square error (RMSE) of 0.31 pm and a wavelength resolution of 0.13 pm. Compared with single-input AWG schemes, the proposed TDM architecture simultaneously achieves higher accuracy, a wider dynamic range, and finer resolution without modifying the AWG layout, offering a practical route toward low cost and scalable high-precision fiber sensing networks.

title: Near-Field Focusing and Beam Shaping Through Cooperative RIS for Communications and Sensing
authors: Álvaro F. Vaquero, Álvaro Pendás-Recondo, Sergio M. Feito, Manuel Arrebola
abstract: This letter derives an electromagnetic model to account for the effect of multiple reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RISs) within the same scenario. The model supports any number of RISs with arbitrary positions, orientations, and sizes, accounts for near-field (NF) interferences, and allows evaluation of field coverage at any point in space. After its formulation, the model is applied to evaluate focusing and beam shaping scenarios, the latter targeting wider coverage than a single spot, which is of interest in many communications and sensing applications. Remarkably, in the focusing example, multiple RISs working cooperatively can achieve performance comparable to that of a larger single RIS, whereas in the beam shaping case, the loss of coherent superposition in space has a more pronounced impact on performance in the collaborative scenario.

title: Transfer Learning for Human Motion Prediction: Improving Accuracy under Data Scarcity
authors: Marco Casarin, Michael Vanuzzo, Mattia Guidolin, Monica Reggiani, Stefano Michieletto
abstract: Human Motion Prediction (HMP) consists of anticipating future human poses based on motion history. While recent approaches achieved strong performance on benchmark datasets, their applicability to real-world scenarios remains limited due to the scarcity of context-specific motion data. In this work, we propose a unified, model-agnostic Transfer Learning (TL) framework for HMP to improve prediction accuracy under datascarce target domains. The framework leverages pre-training on a large dataset of generic motions to learn a transferable motion prior, followed by systematic adaptation to context-specific actions using different TL techniques. We present a comprehensive evaluation protocol to characterize prediction accuracy across multiple representative HMP architectures, target datasets, and TL strategies and to evaluate training efficiency introducing dedicated TL metrics. Beyond performance gains, we analyze the impact of source-target dataset distance and model complexity on TL effectiveness. The results show that TL consistently improves accuracy across all models and target datasets, reducing error up to 32.95% compared to training from scratch. In addition, TL enhances convergence speed, significantly reducing training epochs. These findings demonstrate TL as an effective and versatile approach for improving HMP under data scarcity, and provide methodological insights for its deployment in real-world context-specific applications.

title: Stochastic Sampled-Data Fuzzy Security Control for Nonlinear Markov Jump Distributed Parameter Systems With Time-Varying Delay
authors: Feng-Liang Zhao, Zi-Peng Wang, Junfei Qiao, Xiao-Wei Zhang, Huai-Ning Wu, Tingwen Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This article focuses on the attack-tolerant and fault-tolerant cooperative fuzzy security control problem for nonlinear delayed Markov jump distributed parameter systems (DPSs) under the stochastic sampling that randomly switches between two sampling periods. First, a Takagi-Sugeno (T-S) fuzzy partial differential equation (PDE) model is presented to accurately describe the nonlinear delayed Markov jump DPSs. Subsequently, in consideration of possible random deception attacks and actuator faults, an attack-tolerant and fault-tolerant cooperative fuzzy security control approach with stochastic sampling is proposed under spatially point measurements (SPMs). Then, linear matrix inequality (LMI)-based sufficient conditions that guarantee the stochastically mean-square exponential stability of closed-loop nonlinear delayed Markov jump DPSs are obtained by employing a mode-dependent Lyapunov functional (LF). Lastly, two examples are given to illustrate the effectiveness of the presented control scheme.

title: A Comprehensive Dataset and Simulation Framework for Fault Analysis in Power Transmission Systems: Dataset Description and Statistical Insights
authors: Maher Kouraichi, Majdi Mansouri, Anis Sakly, Mohamed Trabelsi, Faisal Mnif
abstract: High-quality power transmission datasets are essential for developing fault detection, classification, and location algorithms. Existing resources often lack completeness, providing data from limited measurement points and failing to represent the full network behavior. This paper introduces a structured simulation framework and a multimodal dataset based on the IEEE 9-Bus benchmark to address these gaps. The framework integrates MATLAB/Simulink-based control logic, programmable fault injection, dynamic parameterization, and graph-theoretic modeling to encode precise electrical distances between faults and 18 measurement points. A hybrid simulation strategy generates 429 scenarios covering 11 fault types across six transmission lines, producing synchronized raw and RMS voltage–current waveforms and associated metadata. To ensure realism and usability, the dataset is consolidated into a continuous timeline, eliminating deterministic timing cues and supporting robust machine-learning applications. Comprehensive statistical characterization, including distributional analysis, correlation metrics, and waveform inspection, reveals clear topology-driven signatures, distance-dependent attenuation, and balanced fault-type representation. These properties make the dataset ideally suited for benchmarking advanced protection schemes and developing graph-based, distance-aware, and machine-learning models for fault diagnosis in transmission networks. The resource is fully reproducible and designed to accelerate research in intelligent fault analysis.

title: Millimeter-Wave FMCW-MIMO Radar System Using Point-Cloud Feature-Based Decision Tree for Vehicle In-Cabin Infant Occupant Detection
authors: Kotone Sato, Steven Wandale, Koichi Ichige, Kazuya Kimura, Ryo Sugiura
abstract: This paper presents a method for detecting infants in passenger vehicles using a millimeter-wave FMCW-MIMO radar system with a concise yet interpretable Approximate Decision Tree (ADT) model. Unlike conventional approaches that rely on approximately 70 features and opaque machine learning classifiers, the proposed method reduces the feature set to about 20 by applying Constant False Alarm Rate (CFAR) and clustering algorithms to radar point-cloud data, and then consolidating the most effective splitting rules extracted from an XGBoost ensemble into a single ADT. This feature reduction not only improves interpretability but also preserves high accuracy, achieving a missed-detection rate of 0.6% compared with 1.8% in baseline experiments. The proposed pipeline allows transparent model visualization, enabling differentiation between infants and other objects even in challenging scenarios with moving items in empty seats. These results demonstrate that the method achieves both strong predictive performance and model interpretability, making it well-suited for safety-critical automotive applications.

title: Digital Multimeters Voltage Sampling Performance Comparison
authors: Rado Lapuh, Jan Kučera, Jakub Kováč, Luis Palafox, Boštjan Voljč
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This paper evaluates the sampling performance of the Fluke 8588A digital multimeter, introduced in 2019, and compares it to that of the Keysight 3458A, well established in metrology laboratories. We measured the frequency and phase response as well as noise contributions from timing jitter, 1/f noise and frequency independent noise for all DC voltage ranges. The results show that the capabilities and performance of the two multimeters vary significantly depending on the operating modes and parameters selected for the measurement. This analysis reveals that the Fluke 8588A introduces a new set of performance trade-offs rather than being a direct replacement for the Keysight 3458A..

title: A Precision-Enhanced 1x De-Embedding Method Incorporating a Hybrid Transformer-CNN Network
authors: Si-Yao Tang, Xing-Chang Wei, Richard Xian-Ke Gao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: The conventional 1x automatic fixture removal (AFR) de-embedding method gains wide application due to its simplicity and efficiency, which requires only a single de-embedding standard. However, impedance discontinuities on the fixture can significantly compromise its accuracy. To address this limitation, this work proposes a precision-enhanced de-embedding method that integrates a hybrid Transformer-CNN network into the conventional 1x AFR framework, particularly for fixtures with impedance discontinuities at their terminals. By learning and compensating for the non-idealities introduced by fixture impedance discontinuities, the proposed method substantially enhances the accuracy of the traditional 1x AFR method without the need for additional de-embedding standards. This hybrid neural network-assisted de-embedding method offers a practical and scalable solution for high-precision characterization in scenarios where fixture discontinuities are non-negligible.

title: A Site-Specific Framework for Battery Sizing in Grid-Constrained EV Charging Infrastructure
authors: Muhammad Ashar Ayaz, Abhishek Mandaliya, M A Farhan, Anshi Rai, Vidya Sagar, Balu Jegganathan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The rapid depletion of fossil fuel reserves, rising greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions, and global efforts to reduce environmental impact have accelerated the adoption of Electric Vehicles (EVs), which now present new highpower load challenges for the electrical grid-particularly through the widespread deployment of DC Fast Chargers (DCFCs). To address range anxiety and performance expectations comparable to internal combustion engine vehicles (ICEVs), EV manufacturers have increased battery capacities and charging speeds, intensifying demand during peak grid hours. In response to the resulting grid stress, this paper proposes a battery energy storage system (BESS) integrated with DCFC infrastructure to reduce peak load congestion and enhance operational efficiency. The design is based on a detailed analysis of one year of historical and operational charging data from five distinct DCFC sites, providing insight into hourly, daily, and seasonal usage trends. These patterns inform the data-driven design and sizing of localized storage solutions, contributing to a more resilient, sustainable, and efficient EV charging ecosystem.

title: 41-Level Hybrid Asymmetric Cascaded Multilevel Converter with 4:1 Voltage Ratio
authors: Satyabrata Sahoo, Indrajit Sarkar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Multilevel converters (MLCs) are widely employed in High Power AC applications due to their ability to generate high-quality sinusoidal voltage and current waveforms. This superior performance is achieved by employing a higher number of output voltage levels, which reduces total harmonic distortion (THD) and improves waveform quality. However, increasing voltage levels increases the size and power loss due to higher umber of components like switches, driver circuits, passive components etc. In this work a 41 level switched capacitor asymmetric MLC topology is proposed and its modulation scheme, a capacitor voltage regulation scheme ensuring balanced voltages is presented in detail. The presented MLC is a cascade of two power cells in each phase having asymmetric dc link voltages of voltage ratio 4:1 which can results in a total harmonic distortion (THD) of both leg and line voltage waveforms to be 2.86% and 1.70% respectively under modulation index ($m_{i}$) of 1.0. Further, the losses and the converter efficiency is calculated in detail and efficiency is calculated as 98.72%. For the converter operational and performance details, simulation studies are performed in MATLAB/Simulink and the resulting outcomes are provided.

title: Design and Analysis of Switched Controlled Capacitor Compensation Technique for achieving Constant Power Under Large Misalignment Condition
authors: Bibek Kumar, Avik Bhattacharya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Inductive Power Transfer (IPT) is the alternative method to charge electric vehicles. This is gaining popularity by solving problems like the primary limitations of electric vehicles, i.e. range extension, safety, and convenience. Despite many benefits, IPT has a limitation of tolerance to misalignment. This paper proposes an LCC-S compensation IPT converter with a switch-controlled capacitor (SCC) on the primary side to solve the problem of large misalignment and load variation. SCC is able to make a smooth variation of the capacitor to adjust the capacitance of the primary side shunt capacitance ($C_{f 1}$) to keep power constant under load variation and misaligned conditions. This paper also focuses on the novel CP-CV mode of charging instead of the conventional CC-CV mode of charging. CP-CV mode charging has the ability to charge the vehicle faster than the conventional CC-CV mode of charging, reduces thermal stress along the battery, and increases battery life. For verification of the proposed work, A MATLAB simulation of a 1 kW LCC-S IPT converter is performed with SCC compensation technique. Index Terms-Wireless charging, Large misalignment, fast charging, constant power (CP) - constant voltage(CV), Zero Voltage Switching (ZVS)

title: Enhancing Workforce Management through Self-Supervised Learning and Reinforcement Learning for Productivity Prediction
authors: Lalit Singla, Sahil Nayyar, Suprina Sharma, Shailja Gera, Shiva Mehta
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The article introduces a hybrid system, which is a combination of self-supervised learning (SSL) and reinforcement learning (RL), facilitating to predict productivity among employees in connection with the analysis of the activity at workstations. The traditional method of predicting productivity is frequently restricted in use by subjective ratings and long and tedious data gathering. The proposed model uses SSL to obtain substantial features using a large amount of unlabeled workstation activity logs, such as keystrokes, mouse clicks, application usage, and screen time. SSL is able to recognize trends that relate to increased productivity with the application of contrastive learning. The reinforcement learning is used to further train the models, maximise their predictions and real time update them basing on the feedback and hence attain a progressively accurate forecasts as a behaviour of the employees adjust. The set of employee workstation activities that was applied to the model on a large scale is composed of several weeks activities. The hybrid approach of using the SSL-RL had a high accuracy of 85 percent as compared to the baseline models, which was 15 percent better. The given F1 score was 0.79, which equates to even performance, whereas the precision and the recall were found to be 80% and 78%, respectively. The model's ability to discriminate between productive and non-productive behaviors was also demonstrated by a ROC-AUC score of 0.88. The accuracy of training and validation ended up at 90 and 85 percent respectively by the last epoch, and the training and validation loss plummeted to 0.15 and 0.25 as against 0.45 and 0.5 previously. Optimal convergence was acquired with the learning rate of 0.005 and 15 epochs. The study makes the case of the effectiveness of integrating SSL and RL in worker productivity forecasting, proposing a future-proof and dynamic solution to make workforce management more data-driven.

title: Analysis and Prediction of TEC Variations Using Different ML Algorithms
authors: Madithati Reddy Sagar, Kuruva Sai Sudheer, K. Vinod Kumar, S. Towseef Ahmed, Putta Venkata Krishna, S. Fowzia Sulthana
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: To estimate the local ionosphere's total electron concentration (TEC) with high precision in order to facilitate the use of satellite location, measurement controlling and navigation, we try to explore an empirical machine learning models for predicting VTEC, or vertical total electron content, on a single station receiver located in Bangalore (IISC) 13.02° N &: 77.57°, which is significant for monitoring space weather and ionospheric irregularities. In this analysis GNSS derived Vertical TEC (VTEC) predicted over IISC using 04 different machine learning models, including Bagged Trees, Random Forest Regression, XG Boost, and K-Nearest Neighbors. These models were evaluated using RMSE and R2 metrics. This study effectively predicted VTEC values and the results obtain is XG Boost and Bagged Trees are performed well with an Root mean square error (RMSE) value of 3.034 and 3.035 respectively and R2 value is of 0.985 and 0.984 respectively. Comparing to other models like KNN and Random forest were not much effective as XG Boost and bagged trees. This models when compare to other deep learning approaches like Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) which are complexity in nature, requires more data and also consumes more time along with high GPU is required for training. Accurate TEC prediction is therefore essential for reliable GNSS applications and seism ionospheric studies.

title: An Intelligent Deep Learning Framework for Automated Pest Detection in Coconut Trees
authors: Selin Chandra C S, K. Sharmila
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Rapid increase of pest associated yield losses in coconut plantations has led to the acute need for the development of scalable, accurate and field-ready diagnosis tools. Traditional manual methods of inspection are labor-intensive, subjective and are often inadequate for early detection of destructive pests such as the rhinoceros beetle larva, red palm weevils and eriophyid mites. To overcome this problem, an intelligent deep learning framework for automatic pest detection in coconut trees is proposed in this study, which takes advantage of a hybrid CNNTransformer architecture, optimized for aerial imaging, taken using drones and aerial-based imaging devices. The model is equipped with advanced image augmentation, multi-scale feature extraction and localization using attention mechanism to make it more robust in complex plantation environment with occlusion, variable lightening conditions, and canopy density. Extensive experiments performed on a newly curated dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 4, 2 5 0}$</tex> annotated coconut tree pest images show that it can give superior performance (97.8 % detection accuracy, 96.9% precision, 97.3% recall, and 0.98 mAP) as compared to ten state-of-the-art baselines. The results validate the potential of the framework to be used in real-time in precision agriculture systems, allowing early intervention and less pesticide use as well as better crop management. Concluding results show that intelligent pest detection through deep learning can be very beneficial for sustainable coconut farming and decision support capability for large-scale plantation monitoring.

title: A Near-Threshold Machine Learning Accelerator for Energy-Efficient Edge Inference
authors: Keerthi Kassan V, S. Joshua Kumaresan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: NTC-EdgeMAC is a near-threshold machine learning accelerator designed for high energy efficiency in edge inference. The architecture integrates several coordinated techniques, including a heterogeneous mixed-precision MAC array optimized for near-threshold operation, adaptive bodybias control with dynamic voltage and frequency scaling to address process and temperature variations, and in-situ timingerror detection with lightweight correction for improved resilience. Workload-aware optimizations such as early-exit processing and per-layer precision adjustment further reduce energy consumption. A cycle-accurate RTL model synthesized in a 28-nm CMOS process demonstrates significant reductions in energy and energy‒delay product for models such as MobileNetV2, with accuracy loss maintained below 0.5%. Detailed descriptions of the hardware architecture, control mechanisms, and comparative performance with recent lowpower accelerators are presented.

title: Towards a Big Data Analytics Maturity Model: A New Approach to the Assessment of Information System Alignment
authors: Ezziyati Jalila, Elmaallam Mina, Elhaloui M'barek
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Networking, Intelligent Systems &amp; Security (NISS)
abstract: Most models for assessing alignment between the business and the Information System are static models that approach the achievement of alignment. However, in a context characterised by significant changes in the environment, it is becoming essential to assess both the maturity of the alignment and the organisation's ability to maintain and evolve it. Previous research has already highlighted the role of big data analytics in boosting alignment. Based on a literature review of models for assessing information system alignment, this research proposes a new model for assessing the dynamics of alignment by evaluating the maturity of big data analytics.

title: Advancing Public Safety Through Emerging Technologies: a Holistic Approach to Innovation
authors: Rama Devi Drakshpalli
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: This paper presents an interdisciplinary exploration of emerging technologies with a focus on their application in enhancing public safety. The research addresses several key technological domains and their integration into public safety systems, with emphasis on both operational effectiveness and personnel well-being. Key areas discussed include Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) for crime prediction, decision-making, and pattern detection. Smart Algorithms that automate operational responses and optimize resource allocation. Digital Twins used for emergency simulation and training environments. Intelligent Systems that coordinate various data inputs for improved situational awareness. Edge and Cloud Computing enabling real-time analytics and distributed data processing. Internet of Things (IoT) for real-time sensor and device connectivity across public safety networks. Blockchain for immutable data integrity, forensic logging, and secure communication. Forensics and Cybersecurity tools to safeguard and audit public safety data infrastructure. and Health and Wellness Technologies to monitor and support public safety personnel. Through a comprehensive literature review and a structured methodology, the paper identifies existing gaps in traditional public safety systems. A highlevel solution the Intelligent Public Safety Ecosystem (IPSE) is proposed, which leverages integrated, intelligent infrastructure across domains. The paper analyses case examples demonstrating how these technologies improve operational efficiency, trust, and responsiveness. It also discusses challenges and future directions to create scalable, human-centered, and ethically responsible public safety systems.

title: Hierarchical BiLSTM with Attention for Text Summarization
authors: Ankit Singh Chandel, Kapil Gupta, Amit Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: This paper proposes a method to summarize articles by retaining the most important information while removing less relevant content. Our approach uses a hierarchical model with attention that works in two stages. First, it processes the words in each sentence using a bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) with an attention mechanism, which helps the model focus on the most meaningful words. Then, it analyses the relationships between sentences across the whole document using another BiLSTM with attention. This two-level structure helps the model understand both the details within sentences and the overall structure of the article. The model is trained on the CNN/Daily Mail news dataset. We have labelled the most important sentences in the articles based on how closely they matched these summaries. The model then selects the top-scoring sentences to form the final summary. The system is evaluated using ROUGE scores, which compare the model's summaries to human ones. The results, with ROUGE-I: 0.34, ROUGE-2: 0.12, and ROUGE-L: 0.21 show that the model performs well in capturing key information. This work provides a useful tool for text summarization and shows how neural networks can better understand and represent the structure of long documents.

title: Innovative AI Models for Real-Time Personalized Educational Experiences: Reducing Cognitive Load in Augmented Reality (AR) Learning
authors: P.R. Therasa, Nabeel Al-Milli, J. Krishnaraj, M. Dinesh, T. Vijayanandh, Sangeetha Priya. B
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Augmented Reality (AR) has become hugely popular as a revolutionary aid for educational purposes, which allows learners to manipulate virtual content and to view it in a realistic, very interactive manner. Although it has the potential to positively impact engagement and motivation, AR environments can generate too much cognitive load as a result of quantity and complexity of the stimuli. This paper addresses this challenge by examining the use of state-of-the-art AI (Artificial Intelligence) models within AR learning systems for providing real-time, personalized teaching that successfully models and minimizes cognitive load. The ultimate aim is to design a framework that can adjust content, complexity and pacing to the learners’ cognitive state and learning styles. The proposed monitoring system uses DL and RL techniques to capture learner interaction behavior along with physiological signals (eye movement and reaction time) and performance characteristics. These datapoints are run through AI algos to estimate cognitive load and adjust AR content accordingly. For example, the system may reduce visual information, change the timing of feedback, or offer scaffolding support that is determined in real time. This adaptive learning process prevents the learners from getting too far ahead of themselves or being force-fed too much complexity, which in turn allows the system to be more easily related to and remembered. Personalization is performed by learner modeling, in which AI develops personalized cognitive models and learner-specific cognitive profiles by LSTM networks. These profiles drive the system’s decision making, guaranteeing that the content delivery is adapted to the user’s pace of learning, his/her preferences, and prior knowledge. By consistently modeling from user data, the AI system grows alongside the learner, providing more precise and beneficial customizations as it learns. Experimental investigations of the AI AR enhanced learning environment, with learners in various educational domains, i.e., natural and life sciences, mathematics and language learning, show considerable reduction in extraneous cognitive load, and enhanced learner satisfaction, task completion, and test performance. AI integration does not just improve usability it also means scalable learning solutions that cater to all - even in the case of limitations among users. This study emphasizes the need for intelligent systems that can lead to a new approach in digital education. By combining AR with live AI-driven personalization, the doors to new types of learner-centered pedagogical design can be laid open. The results add to the body of research in the emerging area of cognitive-aware learning technologies and have practical implications for developers and educators who seek to leverage AR to achieve the most effective, engaging, and cognitively balanced educational experiences.

title: SmartWall: An Infrastructure for Sensory and Computation-Capable Buildings Using Sustainable and Low-Cost Smart Materials
authors: Md Ismail Hossain, Sharif Noor Zisad, Nasim Uddin, Ragib Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: As urban populations grow and climate-related risks intensify, the need for resilient, intelligent, and sustainable infrastructure has never been more critical. Traditional building systems often lack the real-time awareness and adaptability required to ensure safety, efficiency, and long-term sustainability. In this paper, we present the design and implementation of SmartWall - a sensory and compute-capable building construction component, developed using low-cost materials and equipped with multimodal IoT sensors to capture both environmental and structural data in real-time. These sensors monitor temperature, humidity, fire hazards, and movement through accelerometer and gyroscope readings. Sensor data is processed locally by a Raspberry Pi 5 unit and then wirelessly transmitted to a real-time dashboard for monitoring and intervention. This capability supports emergency decision-making and enhances situational awareness for public safety officials. To enhance power efficiency and integration, we introduce wireless charging via induction coils, eliminating the need for external wiring. Furthermore, we incorporate FRP (Fiber Reinforced Polymer) enclosures based on advanced 3D sandwich composite technology to encapsulate the sensing components. These enclosures offer superior protection against harsh environmental conditions such as fire, impact, and moisture, while allowing secure wire routing and sensor mounting for functionality and durability. We also address practical challenges such as power management, thermal buildup, and system resilience, proposing solutions that enhance the system's reliability. With SmartWall, we can build smart and affordable infrastructure systems to enhance sustainability and public safety in future smart cities.

title: RT-AIBI: A Real-Time AI-Augmented Business Intelligence Framework Leveraging Cloud Data Lakes for Agile Decision-Making
authors: B.Akshai Aanand, Frederick Ruby Helen, B. Lavaraju, Kaviyarasu.V, Prasanna.B, Lingesh Kumara Pandi
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: In the evolving landscape of digital enterprises, agility in decision-making is contingent on real-time insights derived from complex, large-scale datasets. This study introduces RT-AIBI, a Real-Time AI-Augmented Business Intelligence system that unifies cloud data lakes with smart analytics to support quick, precise, and situationally aware business decision making. The main modules are StreamFusion-Net, a latency- and heterogeneity-aware real-time data ingestion engine; InsightTransformer-BI, Transformer-based contextual prediction model; and AQO, an Adaptive Query Optimizer based on Reinforcement Learning that personalizes query execution plan to shorten delays. The information is consumed by a variety of sources such as transactional systems, IoT sensors, APIs, and social media, stored in a cloud-native Delta Lake that has ACID compliance and schema evolution. Experimental tests indicate substantial performance improvement: the ingestion latency of RT-AIBI can be reduced by up to 35% versus Kafka/Kinesis pipelines, query execution up to more than 50% versus legacy and cost-based optimizers. The model is achieved on InsightTransformer-BI with an average performance of 93.4 % accuracy on the test set better than 4.5-5.2 % in LSTM and Random Forest baselines on various tasks including salesoline forecasting, churn prediction or demand estimation. The dashboard performance is improved by 60%, facilitating interactive, agile data exploration. The architecture of RT-AIBI provides the following characteristics: scalability, the ability to have low latency analytics, and semantic alignment to business contexts. This qualifies it to work well with dynamic fields such as retail, fintech, and supply chain management where real-time intelligence is paramount. The research highlights the possibility of the combination of AI, cloud-native infrastructure and intelligent query planning to alter current knowledge about business intelligence systems.

title: Energy Efficient 16 Transistor Full Adder Circuit
authors: Selvaganesh. M, Sujay Snehan.S, Sujith. AP, Mangalam H, Sudarshan. S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: A speedy increase is been found with-inside the vicinity of Integrated Circuits (IC) technology. All the ICs are to be designed in an optimized manner so they meet all of the necessities of being faster, occupying much less vicinity and decreased energy consumption. Adder is the maximum essential constructing block in VLSI, microprocessors and digital signal processors. So, Energy efficient 16 transistor full adder circuit is the proposed work, with one of a kind strategy to lessen the delay time and energy consumption. The important goal of this paper for the full adder is feeding not only low power and high speed but additionally with driving prospect. The different logics have been compared with the results to show which is the appropriate one for the low delay time and has low power consumption. The proposed energy efficient circuit is designed using optimized logic with Cadence virtuoso 90 nm technology node and shows 35% power reduction and compared with the traditional method.

title: State Estimation by Optimal Micro-PMU Placement in Radial Distribution Network
authors: Asjad Ali, Noor Izzri Abdul Wahab, Muhammad Lutfi Othman, Rizwan A. Farade
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Distribution system state estimation provides essential data for system monitoring and control. Micro-Phasor Measurement Units <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mu$</tex>-PMUs) provide high-resolution measurements, which are critical for accurate state estimation in distribution networks. However, equipping the entire network with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu$</tex>-PMUs is financially burdensome and operationally challenging. This article presents a hybrid grey wolf optimizer (GWO) and whale optimization algorithm (WOA) for optimal placement of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\mu}$</tex>-PMUs. To obtain the state estimation values of voltages and angles, MATPOWER power flow is employed for true state estimation, and the WLS algorithm is employed for measuring the estimated values from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\mu}$</tex>-PMUs with different levels of standard deviations in the weight matrix of WLS. The results indicate that the estimated values deviate minimally from the true values, maintaining accuracy levels of up to 95% even with higher standard deviations. These findings demonstrate that the proposed approach delivers highly accurate state estimation while significantly reducing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\mu}$</tex>-PMUs deployment requirements, confirming its effectiveness for cost-efficient and reliable power system monitoring.

title: Integrating Blockchain into Smart Tourism: Towards an Enhanced User Experience
authors: Meryem Lasaad, Yassin Elgountery, Abdeslam Jakimi, Mohamed Oualla, Rachid Saadane, Abdellah Chehri
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Networking, Intelligent Systems &amp; Security (NISS)
abstract: The fast-paced changes of technology have evolved the tourism field and now more than ever it is a well-connected space that orbits around the user— giving birth to what is known as smart tourism. This work investigates the possibilities of fusing blockchain technology with smart tourism frameworks: an approach aimed at boosting the overall user experience. With blockchain offering transparency, security, and decentralization as its core features, stakeholders can enhance their services in addition to winning consumer trust toward effective operations. A clear picture of challenges surrounding adoption is painted: technological barriers and regulatory issues are outlined among them. Through a detailed analysis on how different actors within the tourism sector can interact while using these models, this study proposes a guide that could be used as a roadmap toward implementing blockchain initiatives for smart tourism. The synthesis underscores that marrying blockchain with smart tourism does not only pledge improved operational efficiencies but also promises to take out unessential costs while injecting some level of personalization and most importantly security during user travel experiences.

title: An Experimental and Techno-Economic Investigations of PCM, Fins and Water-Based Solar PV Panel Cooling Strategy
authors: Rajvikram Madurai Elavarasan, Mithulananthan Nadarajah, Sivasankar Gangatharan, Raja Dhanarasu, Md. Rabiul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Performance of the solar photovoltaic (PV) panel is highly influenced by the temperature profile of the region. The tropical and desert regions are highly suitable for solar PV energy harnessing due to the high reception of solar energy. But rise in temperature beyond the panel's optimal temperature results in efficiency drop. To address this issue, the combination of Phase Change Materials (PCMs), fins and water play a vital role in thermal management to keep the panel in specified range and regulates the temperature stress. However, adding PCM, fins and water raises the installation costs, which makes the user feel uneconomical. Hence, this work presents an experiment-based techno-economic analysis to evaluate the technical performance and economic feasibility of adopting the integrated cooling technique using PCM, fins and water. A comparative analysis has been carried out which includes the comparison based on power production, temperature profile, net present value (NPV), levelized cost of energy (LCOE) and payback. With the aid of the proposed cooling scheme, 17.5% temperature reduction is accomplished which reduces PV degradation and results in expanded life span by 63.2%. The NPV and LCOE value is highly appreciable due to increased power output. The upfront cost of the panel rises by 16% which makes the payback prolong by an additional 2 years where the normal PV pays back in 8.2 years. However, the incremental cash inflow due to the incorporation of cooling strategy and the extended life span of PV offsets the limitations. The comprehensive techno-economic analysis of the proposed solar PV cooling strategy significantly enhances its applicability to industrial sectors, offering a robust justification for its impact on energy yield, operational efficiency, and financial viability.

title: Towards Energy-Aware 5G Deployment: An Adaptive Power Estimation Framework with Stochastic Analysis
authors: Raihan Bin Mofidul, M. J. Hossain, Md. Morshed Alam, Muhammad Ahsan Zamee
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The energy demands of Fifth Generation (5G) base stations (BSs) have increased significantly due to higher user density, data-intensive applications, and the expansion of infrastructure in urban areas. Traditional estimation models often rely on static assumptions or synthetic heatmaps, limiting their accuracy in capturing real-time, zone-specific variations in user activity and power consumption. This study aims to develop an adaptive, area-wise power estimation framework for 5G BSs using stochastic user behavior modeling, ensuring more accurate assessments of energy consumption across residential, office, and shopping areas based on practical usage trends. A Poisson-based Monte Carlo simulation is employed to determine hourly data usage patterns per zone, using empirically derived user connectivity and traffic data. Downlink data demand is refined by incorporating the active-to-maximum user ratio and an hourly usage intensity factor reflecting regular or heavy data usage. These adaptive values quantify the incremental power load, which drives total power estimation encompassing active antenna units, baseband units, and auxiliary systems under dynamic network demands. The model is validated using deployment data from Ultimo, NSW. The outcome reveals distinct temporal demand patterns across urban zones, with the estimated overall power consumption showing strong alignment with actual measurements, achieving a root mean square error (RMSE) of 6.636 and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> score of 0.98535. The proposed framework offers an adaptive data-driven solution for estimating the required total power of 5G BSs in a specified area. This enables network operators to optimize energy use, improve capacity planning, and enhance sustainability across diverse urban deployments.

title: EchoNews: An AI-Powered RSS Aggregation and Summarization System for Personalized News Delivery
authors: Suresh Bharathi B, Charan Siva Kumar, Lakshmi Narasimha Swamy, Sankara Mahalingam M
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The extreme increase in digital journalism has led to information oversupply, thus it is challenging to f ilter out the relevant and reliable information, which is indeed trustworthy [1], [2]. The recent development of AI-driven summarization [3], [4] and recommender systems [5] [6] has shown promise, although limited to offline evaluations, or thin slices of the overall solution [8], [9]. In the research work, we introduce EchoNews, an artificial intelligence-based news aggregator that combines the features of RSS feed technology with natural language processing (NLP) to parse articles in real-time, extract abstractive summaries, and distinguish the sentiment-specific classifications [10] [12]. A machine learning driven recommendation engine is able to change content delivery to fit some user behavior and historical user interactions dynamically [13], [14]. The system also uses keyword extraction to effectively classify and the result uses simplistic interface to facilitate easy access by the user [15]. Integrating the use of structured RSS data flows with state of the art AI technologies enables EchoNews to offer a scalable technology roadmap with practical deployment that benefits increased access to news, information overload mitigation, and well-informed decision-making.

title: PinDrop: Breaking the Silence on SDCs in a Large-Scale Fleet
authors: Peter W. Deutsch, Harish D. Dixit, Gautham Vunnam, Carl Moran, Eleanor Ozer, Sriram Sankar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Silent Data Corruptions (SDCs) pose a significant and often hidden threat to the reliability of large-scale computing infrastructure, as they can silently compromise data integrity without immediate detection. Detecting such behaviors at hyperscale is often challenging due to their intermittent nature and the vast diversity of hardware and workloads in a large-scale fleet. This work addresses these challenges by introducing PinDrop, a characterization methodology that leverages continuous, high-frequency testing infrastructure across millions of servers to gather information about SDCs at scale. By leveraging extensive test-suites tailored to mimic real-world applications and exercise a wide range of CPU features, we provide the most comprehensive characterization of SDC failures to date, analyzing over 500 million test executions across millions of devices. Our findings reveal that 0.035% of tested machines suffer from at least one SDC failure during their lifetime. Examining years of data (rather than just a testing snapshot in time), we observe SDCs emerging long after initial deployment and persisting over time. Detailed analysis shows that an average of 0.0024% of tested machines begin failing in each quarter they are tested beyond an initial burn-in period, confirming a fundamental need for continuous testing. Our findings also provide further insights into SDC behaviors at-scale, including failure breakdowns across architectures, test families, specific core IDs, and output-level behaviors.

title: Data-driven Control of Discrete-time System with Dynamic Event-triggered Strategy
authors: Aastha Yadav, Dipayan Guha, Richa Negi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Motivated by recent advances in data-dependent control, this paper inspects the dynamic stability of a discrete-time control system with a data-driven dynamic event-triggered strategy, aiming to enhance the adaptability and efficiency of closed-loop control system performances. The formulated methods rely solely on the input matrix and state data collected from unstable experiments, without requiring explicit identification of system parameters. The control design is carried out in two stages: first, a state feedback controller is designed to ensure exponential stabilization of the system; later, a dynamic event-triggered strategy is augmented to guarantee optimal utilization of the communication medium and reduce data traffic. Both stages involve solving semidefinite programs constrained by linear matrix inequalities (LMIs) that depend on the collected random data through empirical simulations. The event-triggering mechanism uses a reference thresholding scheme, and the admissible value of the threshold parameter is found using the Sprocedure. This variable allows the triggering condition to adapt over time, enabling the system to minimize unnecessary communication and computation while preserving stability and performance. Simulation results demonstrated the mastery and practicability of the developed data-driven control framework for a discrete-time dynamical system.

title: Electric Vehicle-Based Frequency Support in Low-Inertia Power Grids
authors: Ram Prasad Pandey, Nga Nguyen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Due to the reduced system inertia resulting from the increased integration of renewable energy sources (RES), the likelihood of frequency instability following disturbances has increased. Decarbonization efforts are hindered by grid operators' frequent limitations on RES penetration in order to maintain frequency security. Electric vehicles (EVs) operating in vehicle-to-grid (V2G) mode offer a fast, distributed solution to support grid frequency during transients. While this potential has been widely studied through an analytical approach and model-based simulation, this study presents a combination of optimal scheduling analytically and a performance evaluation using OPAL-RT. In addition to the frequency constraints, converter voltage security and low-voltage ride-through requirements are also taken into account during scheduling. The obtained scenarios are then modeled in the real-time simulator to obtain frequency responses with and without EVs. The proposed framework bridges the gap between simulation-based theory and the practical implementation of EV-based frequency support.

title: Modbus TCP/IP Attack Detection Using Lightweight Active Probing in Smart Grid Environment
authors: Manish Bhardwaj, Rishabh Gupta, Santosh Biswas
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The MODBUS TCP/IP protocol inherently lacks essential security mechanisms, rendering it particularly vulnerable to cyberattacks, notably replay attacks on the smart grid. This paper proposes and evaluates a novel, non-intrusive security framework to identify and mitigate MODBUS TCP/IP replay attacks. The system used a discrete event system (DES) model to meticulously track command lifecycles through a command state table (CST) and MODBUS TID sequencing anomaly detection. A key innovation is integrating a multi-stage adaptive probing A mechanism triggered by heuristic analysis of suspicious transactions. The proposed detector uses the NATiG (Network Attack Testbed in Grid) co-simulation framework. It achieves a high Detection Rate (99.7%) for targeted replay attacks and an exceptionally low False Positive Rate ($\mathbf{0. 1 2 \%}$) under realistic network conditions. The adaptive probing mechanism is highly effective in disambiguating suspicious events. Enhances smart grid SCADA systems.

title: Effective Damping Factor Estimation in Inertia-Aware Power Systems Using Ambient Data
authors: Narges S. Ghiasi, Morteza Nouri, Ramtin Hadidi, Seyyed Mohammad Sadegh Ghiasi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The growing share of inverter-based resources (IBRs) in modern power systems necessitates accurate characterization of system dynamics to ensure stability and resilience. While inertia estimation has been extensively studied, the associated damping factor remains a critical but often overlooked parameter. This paper presents a novel methodology for effective damping factor estimation in inertia-aware power systems, leveraging frequency and power measurement data during normal operation. The approach is validated on IEEE test systems and evaluated under scenarios where synchronous generators are replaced with grid-following and grid-forming IBRs, with and without fast frequency response (FFR). The approach is validated on IEEE test systems and evaluated under scenarios where synchronous generators are replaced with IBRs with different controlling systems. These studies demonstrate the method's ability to estimate damping across a range of resource types, making it suitable for real-time applications and future grid planning in low-inertia environments.

title: A Traffic Flow Prediction Model with Dynamic Graph Convolutional Neural Network Based on Generative Adversarial Learning
authors: Jiajia Zheng, Jiawei Shen, Zhenyu Gu
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Human-Computer Interaction, Neural Networks and Deep Learning
