title: Cross-modal Knowledge Distillation for Oriented Object Detection in Modality Missing Visible-Infrared Images
authors: Yifan Xi, Ting Lu, Jiacheng Lu, Xudong Kang, Shutao Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Visible and infrared light images reflect object characteristics in different aspects, which has attracted much attention for object detection in recent years. Nevertheless, the existing multimodal detection networks may fail in the absence of modality. In order to address this problem, a new cross-modal knowledge distillation network (CMKD-net) is proposed for oriented object detection in visible and infrared images. In brief, a teacher-student (T-S) learning network is constructed, where the T-network aims to learn a discriminative feature representation from multimodal images and then guides the S-network training with incomplete modality. Here, multi-dimensional feature distillation (MDFD) and inter-instance relation distillation (IIRD) are designed for cross-modal knowledge propagation. Specifically, the MDFD considers pushing the T-S networks to learn a similar data distribution and feature representation through channel-spatial dimensional feature consistency constraints. The IIRD contributes to retaining the relation structure between individual targets in multimodal images via inter-instance relation modeling and similarity distance measurement. Moreover, to avoid the bias of feature extraction caused by discrete quantization in traditional pooling operations, a rotation-adaptive RoI Pooling (RA-RoI Pooling) is introduced by calculating the continuous double integral within each bin of oriented objects. Ablation experiments and comparison experiments on the VEDAI and DroneVehicle datasets can demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed CMKD-net.

title: Energy-Efficient Federated Learning in Mobile Edge Computing via Fine-grained Energy Planning Client Selection_supp1-3668371.pdf
authors: Laizhong Cui
abstract: Federated learning (FL) on mobile edge devices suffers from heterogeneous computation and communication capabilities, time-varying wireless channels, and strict battery constraints. Existing client selection methods typically optimize per-round performance while ignoring heterogeneous energy consumption and per-device energy budgets, which may cause premature client dropout and degraded model accuracy. This paper proposes FEPCS, a Fine-grained Energy Planning Client Selection scheme for energy-efficient FL in mobile edge environments. At the client level, FEPCS dynamically adjusts local training workloads based on residual energy and estimates transmission energy using historical channel information. At the system level, it introduces a progressive participation rate and an energy budget deviation metric, and combines these with data contribution into a unified selection criterion. Extensive simulations under heterogeneous settings show that FEPCS achieves higher test accuracy, significantly extends client survival time, and effectively reduces client dropouts compared with existing schemes, while operating under the same energy constraints.

title: Path Planning using a One-shot-sampling Skeleton Map
authors: Gabriel O. Flores-Aquino, Octavio Gutierrez-Frias, Juan Irving Vasquez
abstract: Path planning algorithms fundamentally aim to compute collision-free paths, with many works focusing on finding the optimal distance path. However, for several applications, a more suitable approach is to balance response time, path safety, and path length. In this context, a skeleton map is a useful tool in graph-based schemes, as it provides an intrinsic representation of the free workspace. However, standard skeletonization algorithms are computationally expensive, as they are primarly oriented towards image processing tasks. We propose an efficient path-planning methodology that finds safe paths within an acceptable processing time. This methodology leverages a Deep Denoising Autoencoder (DDAE) based on the U-Net architecture to compute a skeletonized version of the navigation map, which we refer to as SkelUnet. The SkelUnet network facilitates exploration of the entire workspace through one-shot sampling (OSS), as opposed to the iterative or probabilistic sampling used by previous algorithms. SkelUnet is trained and tested on a dataset consisting of 12,500 two-dimensional dungeon maps. The motion planning methodology is evaluated in a simulation environment with an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) in 250 previously unseen maps and assessed using several navigation metrics to quantify the navigability of the computed paths. The results demonstrate that using SkelUnet to construct the roadmap offers significant advantages, such as connecting all regions of free workspace, providing safer paths, and reducing processing time. These characteristics make this method particularly suitable for mobile robots in structured environments.

title: Modeling and Performance Analysis of Single Phase PWM Rectifier
authors: Akshit Anupam Ekka, Monalisa Pattnaik
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: PWM rectifiers are used to convert AC voltage to DC while achieving improved power factor and minimized total harmonic distortion (THD) on the AC side. This work presents a detailed modeling approach of a single-phase PWM rectifier based on a dual-loop proportional-integral (PI) controller. The converter operation is analyzed, followed by mathematical modeling of its control structure comprising of an inner current loop and an outer voltage loop to ensure DC-link voltage control and AC-side current reference tracking. Frequency domain analysis based design is used to tune the PI controller parameters. The performance of the designed controller has been verified by simulation in MATLAB Simulink environment. This work will be extended to implement a single-phase AC onboard charger, employing the PWM rectifier as the grid-side converter cascaded with a DC-DC converter for charging EV batteries.

title: Under Water Image Driven Pollution Monitoring using Unmanned Water Surface Vehicle
authors: Surajit Chattopadhyay, Suvajit Ghosh, Sachin Mandal, Surojit Bhowmik, Barsha Dhara, Saumili Mondal
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Underwater imaging and surveillance are critical in numerous fields such as environmental monitoring, defence, underwater archaeology, disaster management, and marine biology. Traditional underwater missions often rely on bulky and expensive systems that lack scalability, sustainability, and autonomous operation. With growing emphasis on renewable energy integration and autonomous systems, the present work proposes an innovative solution: a Solar PV-based Pico-Grid driven Unmanned Water Surface Vehicle (UWSV) named Hansa 1.1. This work aims to design a compact, energy-efficient, and self-sustaining unmanned vehicle capable of performing underwater imaging and surveillance. By integrating a lightweight solar pico-grid on the vehicle and leveraging modern techniques in image scanning and learning, the system enables robust underwater object identification, even in a harsh aquatic bed. The solar pico-grid not only empowers the vehicle with continuous operational capability but also contributes to sustainable engineering goals. The research embodies a multidisciplinary approach interlinking solar power engineering, embedded systems, robotics, image processing, and artificial intelligence to develop a scalable, affordable, and environmentally friendly solution for real-time underwater exploration.

title: Vulnerability Assessment and Active-Passive Defense Strategies for Cyber Physical Power Systems Considering Load Shedding and Node Survival Rate
authors: Chen Huixin, Jia Yilin, Liu Chang, Han Yi, Xia Shiwei
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Targeting the threats of frequency instability and cascading failures in Cyber-Physical Power Systems (CPPS) under high penetration of renewable energy, this paper proposes a vulnerability assessment method considering source-load uncertainties. Firstly, based on stochastic generation scenarios of wind, solar, and energy storage, a frequency vulnerability index is constructed, oriented towards the Automatic Generation Control (AGC) process. Combined with a topological vulnerability index that characterizes network integrity, a combination weighting method integrating subjective and objective approaches is employed to determine the weights, forming a composite vulnerability index system. Secondly, to validate the effectiveness of this assessment system, the results are applied to an active and passive synergistic defense framework, where its capability to enhance system security is examined by guiding the optimal deployment of defense resources. Finally, through simulations on a modified IEEE 118-bus system coupled with a scale-free cyber network, the effectiveness of the proposed vulnerability index in accurately identifying the system's weak links is verified, and it is demonstrated that the defense strategy formulated based on this index can significantly improve system security.

title: Improving the Post-Fault Recovery and Frequency Control Ancillary Services Capability of BESS: Analyzing the Meter Blocking Logic and Filter Constant
authors: Muhammad Saad
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This study analyses the impact of new design logic upgrades to BESS on increasing its capability for providing Frequency Control Ancillary Services (FCAS) in the Australian Market. The project is a 100 MW/200 MWh BESS, that went through a control logic upgrade by introducing a new clearing time delay on the PT failure function to improve the maximize the capability in FCAS markets. Main upgrades also incorporate adjustments in the Tesla Site Controller (TSC), including meter blocking logic and adjustments to filter time constants. These modifications address challenges such as postfault active power recovery and response time in low Short Circuit Ratio (SCR) conditions. The study compares the new model's performance with standards and market requirements. The addition of timer delays the return of the TSC to normal operation, post-fault; avoiding the active power dips and improves FCAS responsiveness, which enhances the revenue generation, while also highlighting challenges related to fault ride-through performance. The paper highlights the optimized control system of the BESS to enhance the grid stability and participation in ancillary services. Studies carried out to assess any impact to ongoing compliance due to the updated model logic, P(f) droop parameter changes and PT failure logic (meter blocking) implementation.

title: An 8-Bit ALU Based in AXI4-Lite for Applications Along with SOCs
authors: Pasuluri Bindu Swetha, Shaik Mohammad Shoaib, ChandraMouli R, K Siva Rama Prasad, Charan Sai Raja Vennakandla, S. Fowzia Sultana
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The phenomenal upsurge in embedded, portable, and System-on-Chip (SoC) platforms has been a great encouragement in the demand for computational units whose output power is less while the functionality is more phenomenal. The Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU), as an integral constituent of any digital processor, greatly affects the entire consumption of energy and its performance. This paper presents the IEEE journal-formatted design and simulation of a low-power 8-bit ALU integrated with an AMBA AXI4-Lite slave interface using Verilog HDL. The power-saving architecture supports arithmetic and logical operations that include addition, subtraction, AND, OR, XOR, and shift operations together with power optimization techniques such as clock gating and operand isolation. The AXI4-Lite interface provides standardized, low-overhead communication with external masters, realizing SoC integration. Functional verification and synthesis have been performed on FPGA design tools, and power analysis confirms measurable dynamic power consumption reduction without compromising timing constraints. Scalable and modular, the proposed ALU architecture can be used for energy-efficient embedded and FPGA-based applications.

title: AI-Driven Hybrid Deep Learning Model for Snake Classification and Venom Identification Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Mohammed Ali Shaik, Abdul Rahim, Mohammad Nasreen, Shweta Sharma, Seema Ahmed, Masrath Parveen
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Snake bites pose a serious threat to human life mainly in the rural and wooded areas where access to quality health care could be limited. It is crucial in deciding on whether the snake involved is venomous or nonvenomous to increase the chances of treatment options that are appropriate for the snake bite. However, conventional identification methods involve the use of some expertise, which may not always be feasible. This paper aims at developing AI-based image classification framework that uses deep learning algorithms to accurately classify snakes by their images that are taken as input. The model utilized in the current research is a combination of a range of CNN such as ResNet, MobileNet and Inception, VGG-16 in an effort to enhance the classification accuracy. The model is developed on a realistic dataset of snake images and the proposed features as well as techniques of feature fusion are used to increase model’s credibility and interactivity.

title: Evaluating DF-GNC Approach in Predicting Sustained Oscillations for Converter-Based Systems
authors: Chongbin Zhao, Wenyan Jia, Chunpeng Zhang, Qirong Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Predicting the amplitude and frequency of (natural) sustained oscillations remains a significant challenge in wideband oscillation analysis. Traditional control theory provides an intuitive graphical solution by combining the describing function (DF) with the generalized Nyquist criterion (GNC). However, its applicability to complex, high-order power converter systems remains unclear. This paper addresses this gap by first demonstrating the principles and implementation of the approach with a low-order case. Subsequently, we analyze a scenario involving power converters where the method fails (the solved information is incomplete or inaccurate) and examine its limitations in cases where it does apply. Finally, we provide a comprehensive discussion to evaluate the approach, highlighting its reliability issues and identifying potential alternatives. The study concludes that the DF-GNC approach cannot guarantee consistent accuracy in predicting sustained oscillations.

title: Deep Learning-Based Network Intrusion Detection Systems: A Comprehensive Survey of Methods, Evolution, and Research Perspectives
authors: Parvathy S Lal, Asha Ashok.
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Network Intrusion Detection Systems (NIDS), indispensable as they are, are facing extremely sophisticated, evasive, and large-scale cyberattacks in modern networks. The traditional signature-based approaches still work for known threats, but fail to detect zero-day attacks and also seem to struggle with detecting dynamically evolving traffic patterns. That is the reason deep learning (DL) has emerged as a game-changer for NIDS, particularly because of its capability to automatically extract complex representations from high-volume network data. In the past 10 years, there have been a multitude of different deep learning architectures proposed, including autoencoders, convolutional neural networks (CNN), recurrent neural networks (RNN), long shortterm memory (LSTM) networks, hybrid CNN-LSTM models, and graph neural networks (GNN) along with recently proposed transformer-based attention mechanisms to enhance accuracy and robustness against intrusion detection systems. This survey aims to provide a detailed review of DL-based NIDS with a focus on the chronological evolution of techniques, methodological innovations, and the limitations therein. Then highly influential research works are discussed in detail, focusing on how later work addressed the problems of those earlier approaches. Following this will be a taxonomy of DL-based NIDS, followed by comparative insights, open challenges, and perspectives for future research including interpretable DL, adversarial resilience, federated learning, and behavior-driven hybrid models.

title: A Novel Leakage Energy-Assisted Quasi-Z-Source Converter for High Voltage Gain and Soft Switching in Renewable Energy Applications
authors: Joni Sk, Avik Bhattacharya, Sharmili Das
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: This paper presents a high-gain, non-isolated DC-DC converter for renewable energy sources like PV and fuel cells. It features soft-switching and a quasi-Z-source-inspired network with a coupled inductor and switched-capacitor to boost efficiency and voltage gain. This arrangement aims to improve power conversion efficiency, manage energy transfer effectively, provide high voltage gain, and reduce component stress. A common active clamp circuit supports both switches concurrently, effectively suppresses voltage overshoots, and recycles leakage energy. To minimize switching losses and improve dynamic performance, soft switching for both switches is accomplished when the coupled inductor magnetizing current is kept within a specific operating range. The converter is suitable for integrating modern power architectures due to its low input current ripple, reduced electromagnetic interference, and common ground reference. Comprehensive time-domain simulations conducted in PLECS or MATLAB/Simulink validated the proposed architecture with a 250 W system operating at a 32 V input and a 400 V output. Simulation results validate the proposed design, confirming its reliable and efficient high-voltage DC conversion for renewable energy applications.

title: AI-Powered Smart Vision Mouse Interface for Touchless Human Computer Interaction
authors: Tanvi Khonde, Chetan Puri, Janhavi Hajbe, Shreya Talvekar
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The AI-Powered Smart Vision Mouse Interface integrates deep learning, computer vision, and speech recognition technologies to bring touchless human-computer interaction into the modern age. This solution is primarily directed at ensuring accessibility, hygiene, and user comfort by enabling cursor manipulation and carrying out mouse operations through gestures of the hand and voice commands recorded in real-time by a webcam and microphone, respectively. While speech recognition enables natural language instructions to be executed through this interface, Media Pipe and Convolutional Neural Network-based models detect and classify gestures. It offers a cheap, hardware-neutral solution that can be exploited in many different contexts. Experimental results have been able to show exceptional efficiency and responsiveness during real-world scenarios, despite its average gesture detection accuracy being 95.3% and voice command accuracy being 89%.

title: LFC Profile Improvement of an AC Microgrid employing walrus optimized T2-fuzzy Controller under cyber attacks
authors: Prakash Chandra Sahu, Sunil Kumar Bhatta, Sarat Chandra Swain, Buddhadeva Sahoo, Subhransu Ranjan Samantaray
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The micro-grids are heavily incorporated into the power system of today’s electric networks. The resilience of the microgrid is being directly impacted by cyber-physical attacks and parameter uncertainties, which are becoming a compelling concern on load frequency regulation. A resilience-based frequency regulation strategy for a microgrid under various operational situations, including step and random load changes and varying wind speed patterns, is presented in this work. In order to strengthen the system against outside threats, a cyber-attack model is also taken into account when formulating the problem. A robust type-2 fuzzy control scheme is used to guard against cyberattacks and system parameter uncertainties. Focusing to optimum controller design, an intelligent walrus soft techniques is recommended for this study. Additionally, a real-time T-HIL test bench is used to validate the suggested control schemes. As said, the most notable enhancement in dynamic performance for frequency regulation is offered by the suggested type- 2 fuzzy controller. Experimentally, it has been conferred from the areal frequency response that, recommended type-2 fuzzy control scheme significantly reduces the settling time through 36.84% and 168.42% in relative to the Fuzzy PID and PID controllers.

title: Towards Smart Power Quality Accountability by Using Fuzzy Rule-Based Approach
authors: Eduardo V. Liberado, Matheus B. Arcadepani, Paulo F. Silva, Alex F. Silva, Bruno O. Zarpellon, Luis de Oro Arenas, Helmo K. Morales Paredes
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The analysis of power quality issues and accountability is a challenging task, especially in grids on which multiple power quality issues, such as low power factor, load imbalance and harmonic distortion, affect both voltages and currents simultaneously. Hence, this paper presents an expert system that performs power quality accountability analysis. The core of the decision-making process in this expert system is performed by fuzzy rule-based systems. These systems have power quality indices with limits defined by international standards as inputs. They also provide information on accountability for each power quality issue identified at a certain point of measurement in the grid. The power quality analysis by the expert system can be performed either in real-time, or offline, on demand time basis, being the latter adopted in the presented application case, in which 5-days measurement data from a smart meter is analyzed by the proposed expert system.

title: Robust Energy-Saving Control Strategies for Agricultural UAV Electromechanical Systems Based on Modular Hybrid Optimization Algorithms
authors: Sun Jing
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: This study extensively explores the energy consumption increase problem of agricultural unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) under low-altitude, intense disturbance, and high load fluctuation situations. Based on a modular hybrid optimization method, the study develops an energy-efficient control technique for electromechanical propulsion systems.First, a dynamics and energy consumption model of the propulsion system is established, consisting of a brushless DC motor, an electromagnetic-aerodynamic coupled load, and an inverter control module. Simultaneously, a full-link energy performance index incorporating copper losses, switching losses, and additional aerodynamic losses is constructed. Then, a fractionalorder sliding mode controller (FOSMC) is designed, introducing the Caputo fractional-order operator to characterize the historical energy accumulation effect. A disturbance sensing observer (DSO) is also constructed. The DSO utilizes a nonlinear gain and adaptive filtering structure to achieve real-time estimation of external disturbances. To achieve synergistic optimization of multi-source energy consumption suppression and control performance, a hierarchical multi-objective hybrid optimizer (HM-MOA) is proposed. HM-MOA consists of a threelayer parameter search structure composed of a genetic algorithm, differential evolution, and a particle swarm optimization algorithm. This model can optimize FOSMC parameters, DSO sensitivity, and PWM modulation strategy. The experiment applied a BLDC 1000 W propulsion system as the benchmark platform. Results show that compared to the unoptimized traditional sliding mode controller, the proposed method reduces overall energy consumption by 21.4%, copper loss by 17.8%, motor switching loss by 23.6%, and aerodynamic additional loss by 15.2%. In terms of tracking performance, the response time is shortened by 31.7%, overshoot is reduced by 28.4%, and steady-state error is reduced by 35.1%. Under strong disturbance scenarios, DSO keeps the external disturbance estimation error within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 3.5 \%$</tex>, a 46% improvement compared to the model without an integrated disturbance observer.

title: Hazardous Gas Sensing Technologies: A Review of Chemiresistive, Electrochemical, and Optical Fiber Sensors with a Vision for UAV-Based Emergency Response
authors: Maurizio Manzo, Md Nazmus Sakib
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: Hazardous gases such as ammonia <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathbf{N H}_{3}\right)$</tex>, chlorine <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{Cl}_{2}\right)$</tex>, hydrogen sulfide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{S}\right)$</tex>, and sulfur dioxide <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left(\text{SO}_{2}\right)$</tex> pose serious health risks during industrial accidents, chemical spills, and emergency events. First responders are often the most vulnerable during such situations due to limited early detection capabilities. This review presents recent advancements in hazardous gas sensor technologies. The paper covers three major sensor types: chemiresistive, electrochemical, and optical fiber-based sensors. Key developments in sensing materials, detection limits, response times, and environmental durability are discussed. The authors also highlight an ongoing effort to develop a UAV-based gas sensing platform that utilizes a four-channel Long Period Fiber Grating (LPFG) array coated with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{SnO}_{2}, \text{ZnO}, \text{Pf}$</tex>, and Pt for selective gas detection.

title: Comprehensive Web Vulnerability Assessment Using Open-Source Intelligence Techniques
authors: Sangavi V, Abishake S, Dhayal S R
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The OSINT Website Explorer is a comprehensive cybersecurity solution developed to identify and assess vulnerabilities in web applications through the use of open-source intelligence (OSINT) techniques By automating the collection and analysis of publicly available data such as domain details, SSL configurations, subdomains, and exposed services the tool streamlines the vulnerability assessment process and minimizes the need for manual intervention It integrates advanced scanning tools including OWASP ZAP, Nmap, and Nikto to detect application-layer, server, and network-level weaknesses A machine learning module enhances the platform by identifying potential phishing domains based on URL analysis The system presents results through a user-friendly interactive Streamlit dashboard that offers real-time alerts visual summaries risk scores based on CVSS and exportable reports in various formats Developed using a modular Python-based architecture with workflow optimization and security compliance in mind the OSINT Website Explorer offers organizations a powerful and scalable approach to continuously monitor and secure their digital assets It enables timely detection and mitigation of vulnerabilities enhances organizational preparedness and fosters a proactive security culture in today’s evolving cyber threat landscape.

title: A Graph-Based Method to Reveal Communities and Influential Products from Reviews Through Stylometric Analysis
authors: A M Abirami, S Sumitra
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: In this digital era, customer reviews serve as a powerful source for understanding product perception and reviewer behavior. This study presents a graph-based approach to uncover hidden communities and identify influential products using stylo-metric features extracted from a large-scale food review dataset. Each product is characterized by writing-style attributes such as sentence complexity, lexical richness, and readability. A similarity graph is constructed where nodes represent products, and edges reflect stylometric similarity above a given threshold. By applying degree centrality measure and Louvain community detection technique, we detect dense clusters of products reviewed in a stylistically similar manner. The impact of different similarity thresholds (1.0, 0.95, 0.90 and 0.85) on community structure and degree centrality is analyzed using metrics such as maximum degree, number of edges and Adjusted Rand Index (ARI). The findings demonstrate that stylometry can meaningfully differentiate review clusters, detect influential products, and expose stable community patterns even across varying thresholds. This method provides a foundation for social media review analysis in recommender systems, marketing insights, and fraud detection and can be extended with semantic or sentiment-based features for richer interpretation.

title: Design and Implementation of Precise Slope Algorithms for Servomotor Position Control Using Raspberry Pi Pico
authors: Md. Abu Hena Safiq Kamal, Md. Shamim Anower, Nasiruzzaman Nasir
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper presents the design and implementation of Digital, cost-effective, and high-precision slope algorithms on the Raspberry Pi Pico for controlling the position of a DC servomotor (MG996R). The algorithms are developed based on a straight-line approximation derived from the servomotor and ADC specifications. They are executed on the RP2040 dual-core Arm Cortex-M0+ processor to determine the desired servo position using angle commands and ADC readings. Controllers are employed to regulate the PWM signal, ensuring smooth and accurate positioning of the servomotor. Additionally, Thonny Python IDE and Proteus-ISIS are used to simulate the servomotor position control systems. The proposed methods demonstrate 100 % accuracy in simulation and 99.9999 % accuracy in hardware implementation. Besides, its response time is so high (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5 \mu ~\mathrm{s}$</tex>) due to high clock frequency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{2 0 0 M H z})$</tex>. As a result, these techniques are well-suited for meeting the operational requirements of electric vehicles like electric buses, electric trains, electric aircraft, and electric ships, offering enhanced control in DC servomotor applications.

title: ReThermal: Co-Design of Thermal-Aware Static and Dynamic Scheduling for LLM Training on Liquid-Cooled Wafer-Scale Chips
authors: Chengran Li, Huizheng Wang, Jiaxin Liu, Jingyao Liu, Zhiheng Yue, Xia Li, Shenfei Jiang, Jinyi Deng, Yang Hu, Shouyi Yin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: With the increasing demand for high computational power in Large Language Models, wafer-scale chips have emerged as a solution, providing the necessary integration and computing capability to meet these needs. However, their ultralarge area and extreme heat dissipation introduce critical thermal management challenges under liquid-cooling environments. In addressing this issue, we identify two key opportunities and three major challenges: the behavior-thermal black box, the waferscale simulation bottleneck, and the runtime heat-schedule drift. To tackle these challenges, we propose ReThermal, a holistic scheduling framework that integrates three innovations. First, we introduce behavior-driven thermal modeling to capture workload-induced compute, communication, and heat coupling patterns at the system level. Second, we develop a DNNaccelerated wafer-scale thermal simulator that enables fast and accurate temperature prediction, significantly reducing simulation time. Third, we implement an adaptive thermal-aware scheduling strategy that coordinates compile-time and runtime decisions to dynamically optimize task placement. Evaluations show that ReThermal reduces peak temperature by up to 8.0° C and improves throughput by up to 39.23 %, providing a scalable and effective thermal control solution for future liquid-cooled wafer-scale systems.

title: Research on Text Difficulty Grading of International Chinese Textbooks Based on CNN-LSTM
authors: Han Gao, Xin Sun
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Human-Computer Interaction, Neural Networks and Deep Learning
abstract: None

title: HealNet-Auto: A Generative AI–Driven Self-Healing Framework Using Adaptive AutoML Pipelines
authors: R.Arun Kumar, Krish Raghul, Arjun.G, Sugadev.V.M, Ragul Tamilarasan, Gowtham Prabhakaran A
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: In an era of increasingly complex software systems, the ability to autonomously detect, diagnose, and recover from runtime failures is pivotal for ensuring reliability, availability, and resilience. In this paper we introduce HealNet-Auto, a new type of self-healing software framework that harmonizes Generative AI with AutoML pipelines to provide intelligent, adaptive, and explainable recovery schemes. HealNet-Auto is an auxiliary system that constantly reads system logs, telemetry, and performance data with the help of AutoGluon-optimized data containers to identify anomalies at an accuracy of 96.8%. When an anomaly is detected, the system uses a fine-tuned CodeT5 model to produce root cause summaries and categorize faults, such as logic error, configuration and dependency failure. Reinforcement learning improves decision-making engine, which dynamically chooses the healing strategy, which could be restart, rollback, or patching over the code. In case of patching selection, generative model generates possible fixes which are eventually semantically checked and dynamically tested. The patch framework has a patch successful rate of 90.7% with an average of 13.1 seconds in the average recovery and only 3.9% in rollback triggers in various fault situations. A wide variety of comparisons with common systems proves the efficacy of HealNet-Auto in relation to the precision, adjustability, and healing fastness. We are contributing an in-depth, end-to-end healing pipeline that is fully automated, continuously learning, and explains itself; that is, our contributions can be deployed in cloud-native, microservice, and edge settings. This available framework has a huge potential of transforming the architecture of resilient, self-managing computer software.

title: Robust Facial Expression Recognition with VGG16: Large-Scale Training and Cross-Dataset Validation on AffectNet and FER2013
authors: Aditya Wagh, Mahesh Dongare, Vedant Nadhe, Omkaresh Kulkarni, Rutuja Rajendra Patil, Gagandeep Kaur, Koustubh Prabhakar Nilje
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Facial Expression Recognition (FER) is important for computer vision applications like human-computer interaction, mental health monitoring, and intelligent systems. This paper looks into using the VGG16 architecture, a commonly used deep convolutional neural network (CNN), for effective FER by training on two key datasets: AffectNet (around 450,000 images) and FER2013 (about 35,000 images). To improve performance in real-world situations, we include preprocessing and data augmentation methods that address changes in lighting, pose, and class imbalance.Unlike many earlier studies, we focus on thorough cross-dataset validation, training on one dataset and testing on the other. This helps us evaluate how well our approach generalizes to real-world scenarios. The experimental results feature accuracy metrics, learning curves, confusion matrices, and performance analysis for each class. VGG16 reaches a top accuracy of 85.4% on AffectNet and 71.8% on FER2013. It also identifies common mistakes, like confusing "fear" with "surprise." This study shows that VGG16 is effective in FER when paired with scalable training, specific preprocessing, and strict validation, emphasizing its potential for practical use.

title: Blockchain enabled AI-agent based peer-to-peer energy trading and fault management system
authors: Gulla Rakesh, Devesh Shukla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The distribution system automation that could facilitate large scale renewable integration is being developed and deployed across the globe. The major challenge in this endeavor lies in the mechanism to facilitate intra and inter energy trading for microgrids and active distribution systems. The operational complexity and uncertainties of the grid further increases with the incorporation of EV charging stations. Blockchain based mechanisms are being explored to facilitate the peer-to-peer energy trading among various participants in the grid. In this paper, a decentralized energy trading and fault management system for distribution networks has been proposed. The trading platform is implemented on Ethereum using smart contracts deployed through Hardhat and operates under a double auction mechanism to determine the market clearing price. A decentralized application (DApp), developed with JavaScript and Ether.js, provides users with a secure interface for peer-to-peer energy transactions. The proposed framework was validated on a modified IEEE 13-bus system, where the double auction mechanism successfully cleared a $\mathbf{4 k W h}$ market at a price of 0.0226 ETH. The AI-driven control demonstrated robust performance, correcting a voltage swell from 1.08 per unit back to the nominal range and triggering fault alerts via a cloud-based workflow. We have also developed cloud-based messaging app that could convey the transaction status and billing information to the consumers if required. This integration of blockchain-enabled trading with cloud agent-based fault management system supports transparency, and could enhance the overall resiliency of the grid.

title: A Novel Grey Wolf Optimization Algorithm with Enhanced Exploration, and Exploitation Ability
authors: Padarbinda Samal, Ashish Pradhan, Tandin Dorji, Alivarani Mohapatra, Ranjeeta Patel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The new Enhanced Grey Wolf Optimization (EGWO) algorithm presented in this research is intended to enhance the exploration and exploitation capabilities for resolving six benchmark test functions. In the EGWO algorithm the exploration, and exploitation ability is improved by cubical update of the a parameter of the original grey wolf optimization algorithm. Further, the positions of the wolves are adjusted dynamically to enhance the convergence characteristics of the GWO algorithm. By comparing the performance of the proposed EGWO to the traditional GWO, metrics including the test function’s average, worst, best, and standard deviation values are used. The outcomes show that the original GWO algorithm is routinely outperformed by the EGWO.

title: Ensemble-Based Diabetic Retinopathy Detection Using CNN, Random Forest, and AdaBoost
authors: Jitendra Parmar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Early detection of diabetic retinopathy (DR) plays a key role in preventing vision loss. In this paper, we built a hybrid approach that combines the use of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) with ensemble learning techniques to deal with the problems of DR classification. Three different CNN architectures were tested for their ability to extract retinal features, and individually, they achieved accuracies ranging from 64.91 to 70.54%, with Model 3 performing the best. Further sophistication was achieved by the predictive accuracy of the Ensemble learning, and the use of the Optimal Weighted Ensemble approached 72.91%, reinforcing its potential to capitalize on disparate model strengths. It illustrates the prevalence of class imbalance and the effect on DR severity levels that are underrepresented and effectively shows the usefulness of preprocessing techniques (data augmentation) to address these problems. Future directions enhance the applicability of the model in the clinic by integrating advanced architectures, improving class balance strategies, and expanding to other ophthalmic conditions.

title: Secure Key Association, Forger Identification and Dealer Forgery Detection for a ($t, n$) Threshold Setup
authors: Ayush Mitra, Anindya Kumar Biswas, Shrutayou Biswas, Anirbaan Sarkar, Saakshi Sharma, Shristi Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Considering an open network where secure communication and collaboration are essential, sharing a common secret key among geographically distant participants give rise to significant challenges. For ensuring data confidentiality and integrity, participants/devices need to be protected specially against threats, fraudulent dealers and malicious entities. This research highlights a framework regarding secure communication that assimilate detection and obedience mechanisms during both share distribution and reconstruction phases for secret sharing. Based on our protocol, three cases based on security measures are shown which allows the Dealer to create and distribute share-based assurance from a generated polynomial, which ensures each share is verifiable by each participant. Genuine participants can also detect and prevent any forgery from a group of cooperating participants. In a group of size t, we assume there is at least one honest participant, rest (t-1) are forgers, in worst case. A matrix is used to reconstruct the original polynomial and also to fetch the original secret even in the present of multiple dishonest parties. Validation of every share distributed among multiple insiders through proper assurance, ensures a strong and secured protocol and maintains integrity.

title: Robust CLIP-Based Multimodal Fake News Detection in Noisy Social Media Environments
authors: Anugrah Shrivatava, Aditya Raj, Aryan Roy, Rohit Kumar Kaliyar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Multimodal fake news detection requires strong models with the ability to perform well under real-world data imperfections and class imbalance. This paper introduces a CLIP-based approach validated on a stratified fraction of the Fakeddit dataset (50,000 samples, 60.7% real vs. 39.3% fake news) with state-of-the-art performance using a fault-tolerant pipeline with BART text summarization, URL-based image loading with fallback strategies, and class-imbalance reduction. Our model achieves 91.24% test accuracy and 0.9715 ROC AUC, with excellent per-class performance: 93% F1-score for fake news (94% precision, 91% recall) and 89% F1-score for real news (87% precision, 91% recall), showing balanced effectiveness in spite of the 1.54:1 class imbalance. The confusion matrix shows strong minority-class detection, correctly identifying 2,851/3,136 fake samples (90.9% recall) while keeping 4,430/4,844 real news detections (91.5% precision). Major innovations are dynamic truncation of text (23% of posts abridged through BART-large-CNN), adaptive attention gates for modality reliability weighting, and focal loss optimization, reducing training epochs by 43% compared to baseline CLIP fine-tuning. Batch-wise evaluation results in uniform performance (87.9-94.9% per-batch accuracy) with a final test loss of 0.2105, achieving better performance than current multimodal architectures by 6.8% F1-score. The system's computational efficiency-40% less GPU memory usage than typical CLIP implementations-combined with the robustness of the system to 6.2% inherent image URL failures and synthetic corruption tests (only 2.1% performance loss at 20% failure rates), makes it a feasible solution for real-time social media applications. This piece ensures that strategic architectural treat-ments can surpass data scarcity and imbalance issues, gaining high reliability without full-scale dataset requirements.

title: American Sign Language Recognition Using Deep Learning for Static Gesture Classification
authors: Himanshu Suyal, Shiv Naresh Shivhare, Suryansh Chaudhary, Arnav Kathpal, Raja Madhvendra Singh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: This paper presents a deep learning system for recognizing static American Sign Language (ASL) alphabet gestures from images. The proposed model fine-tunes a MobileNetV2 convolutional neural network (pre-trained on ImageNet) to classify 29 sign classes (the 26 letters plus additional signs for space, delete, and nothing) using a dataset of over 87,000 images. Key training strategies, including extensive data augmentation (random rotations and flips) and regularization (dropout), were employed to improve generalization. The optimized model achieved a high validation accuracy of 92.6%, with real-time inference capability enabled by MobileNetV2's lightweight architecture (3.4 million parameters). We analyze the results with a confusion matrix, identifying commonly misclassified signs (such as the letters ‘M’ and ‘N’, which have very similar hand shapes) and nearly perfect true-positive rates for more distinct signs. Comparison with other compact CNN architectures demonstrates that MobileNetV2 offers an excellent balance of accuracy and efficiency, underscoring its suitability for deployment in resource-constrained devices to assist the hearing-impaired community.

title: Optimal Scheduling of Active Distribution System Considering Emission Cost and Battery Degradation
authors: Adarsh K Siju, Swathi Krishna, Deepak M
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The incorporation of battery storage and renewable energy to distribution system makes the power solutions sustainable and reliable. However, the existing scheduling frameworks focus only on short-term operating costs without considering the battery wear or pollutant gas emissions from the generating source. To fill this gap, this paper develops an active distribution system scheduling (ADS) framework which co-optimizes the operating cost with emission penalties and battery degradation. A mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) model is employed for unit commitment and economic dispatch, which is solved in Pyomo using the Gurobi solver. For battery degradation forecasting, a neural network-based model is implemented in PyTorch. The degradation and emission-aware ADS model makes optimization possible via an iterative decoupled heuristic that balances economic, environmental, and technical goals. Case studies show that the operation of a sustainable distribution system is possible with significant reduction in daily battery degradation (63.4%) and total cost of the system even while lowering the emissions.

title: Near Real-Time Speech Translation from English to Kannada Using Whisper Model
authors: Amrutha A, Radhika M. Pai
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Recent developments in end-to-end speech-to-text systems, which skip intermediate transcription steps, have brought major progress in Spoken Language Translation (SLT). However, a key challenge remains the lack of large parallel datasets that link spoken speech input with translated text, especially for underrepresented languages like Kannada. In this work, we present a near real-time English-to-Kannada transcription and translation system. It uses Whisper, a state-of-the-art Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) model, alongside Google Translator, a Neural Machine Translation (NMT) for translating transcribed text into Kannada. By making use of GPU acceleration, the system delivers translations quickly and with high accuracy. Among the available Whisper models trained, tiny model performed well with Word Error Rate (WER) of 12.35% for near real-time transcription compared to the base model which showed WER of 13.24%.

title: A Cryptographically Enhanced LLM-Based Chatbot Architecture with Session-Specific Polynomial-Matrix Encryption
authors: Sauvik Bal, Shillpi Mishrra, Mohit Das
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The purpose of this research paper is to develop a secure chatbot system that uses session-based polynomial-matrix encryption and Large Language Models (LLMs). The main purpose is to secure user data and maintain privacy while communication has done. In this proposed method each chat session has an own secret key. Due to this, hackers cannot locate or use previous session data. The method complicates it for a user to guess or steal information by employing encrypted text, or ciphertext, instead of normal text. Because the security is based on solving complex mathematical puzzles, it is resistant to repeated attacks. Tests show that the proposed method works better than more conventional encryption methods such as homomorphic encryption in terms of giving fast, correct responses with less delay. The solution is suitable for real-world use due to its flexibility and ease of integration with existing LLM APIs. The method builds trust between human beings and AI since it ensures that all conversations are secure and private, a feature that is useful in sectors such as healthcare, finance, and the military where the privacy of data is important. The end aim of this project is to develop chatbots that are safe and advanced enough for use on a daily basis.

title: Enhanced Ripple Reduction for Single-Phase AC to DC Converters
authors: Joshua Wong, Md Rezanul Haque, Leonardo Callegaro, Foad Taghizadeh
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper introduces an improved method for reducing DC output ripple in single-phase AC-DC converters through the use of an Active Ripple Filter (ARF). The proposed approach leverages an established estimation-based control strategy that estimates the double-frequency output current of a single-phase H-bridge converter. By doing so, it eliminates the need for physical current sensors, which are typically required in conventional ARF systems. Traditional ripple mitigation techniques often rely on large passive components, which can increase system size and cost. While ARFs have been developed to address these issues, they still generally depend on current sensors for effective switching control while they are unable to satisfactorily reduce the dc-bus ripple. The method presented in this paper overcomes these limitations by estimating the ripple current, enabling efficient ripple suppression without sensor feedback for the AC - DC converter. The system is modelled and simulated in MATLAB, and its performance is compared with that of conventional sensor-based ARF systems. Simulation results show that the proposed technique achieves a 16.88% improvement in DC voltage ripples reduction compared to conventional designs.

title: Dynamic Risk Stratification in ICU Sepsis: A Deep Reinforcement Learning Approach Using Synthetic Data
authors: Shivam Solanki, Venkata Phanikrishna, Manthan Hirani, Pratik Shah
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Early identification of sepsis deterioration in the intensive care unit (ICU) is vital, yet most bedside scores provide only single-time-point risk estimates and ignore how organ failure patterns evolve. We frame dynamic risk stratification for ICU sepsis patients as a reinforcement learning problem and design a dueling double deep Q-network (D3QN) that predicts, at four-hour intervals, whether a patient is improving and, ultimately, whether the full eighty-hour stay ends in overall improvement. Training uses a privacy-safe synthetic data set of 2,164 patient stays, each with twenty sequential observations and forty-two clinical features, with a calculated Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (SOFA) score. The agent receives step-wise rewards for accurate four-hour predictions and a larger terminal reward based on the cumulative Sequential Organ Failure Assessment (SOFA) trend. After 10,000 training episodes, the model attains 75% accuracy on 433 previously unseen trajectories. Learning curves show stable convergence, and analysis confirms a clinically desirable bias toward correctly flagging non-improving cases. These results suggest that deep reinforcement learning agents can capture temporal improvement patterns and could provide a foundation for real-time bedside decision support, pending validation on real electronic health record data.

title: Real-Time Cyberbullying Detection Using NLP and Ensemble Learning Techniques
authors: Jebakumar Immanuel D, Nivetha S, Sakthi Sharmitha M, Sharma T K, Gowthamani M
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Cyberbullying, particularly on social media platforms, has become one of the serious menaces wherein the user, especially children and young adults, faces harassment and even emotional trauma through abusive or aggressive messages. Its detection is difficult because online language evolves, comprising sarcasm, slang, mixed languages like Hinglish, abbreviations, emoji, and between-the-lines meaning. The keyword-based traditional systems are unable to understand these complexities. The aim of this research work is to introduce an AI-based system for the detection of cyberbullying using Natural Language Processing (NLP) and supervised machine learning models that are deployed on real-time monitoring. This system preprocesses the social media text by removing noise and normalizing slang, thereby converting unstructured language into useful data. The resultant features are then classified as bullying or non-bullying based on the learned patterns. It presents the results from several classification models and shows that the Random Forest classifier performed best because of its strong generalization and reduction in prediction errors. The results prove the role that automated AI moderation can play in detecting such harmful content early, sending timely alerts, and thereby supporting a safer digital communication environment for all users.

title: Cross-Regional Adaptation of Deep Learning Architectures for PV Power Forecasting Under Climatic Uncertainty
authors: Prince Aduama, Ameena Saad Al Sumaiti, Khalifa Hassan Al Hossani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper presents a Leave-one-Region-out (LoRo) transfer learning approach for PV power forecasting across eight geographically diverse regions and multiple horizons. Three deep learning architectures, LSTM, Basic Transformer, and Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) are employed in this work for a comprehensive evaluation of the developed approach. The simulation results demonstrate that transformer-based architectures achieve superior overall performance, with TFT transformer and Basic Transformer obtaining lower RMSE and MAE errors compared to LSTM, while maintaining skill scores exceeding 0.7 against persistence baselines. However, regional analysis reveals significant geographic variability, with LSTM emerging as the optimal choice in six of eight regions due to superior computational efficiency and regional adaptation characteristics. The study provides systematic deployment guidelines: LSTM for resource-constrained environments and short-term predictions; Basic Transformer for extended horizons in variable climates; and TFT for maximum accuracy applications. These findings establish a framework for climate-aware LoRo PV power forecasting, enabling optimized architecture selection based on geographic context and operational constraints in practical deployments.

title: Enhancing Energy Demand Predictions with Seasonal Patterns and Dual Temporal Inputs in a Hybrid GPI-DSSM Model
authors: Rishi Malyala, Kuthsav Thattai, Anam Malik, Jayashri Ravishankar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel approach to energy demand forecasting by integrating dual-temporal analysis with weather-based features and annual lag sequences. Building upon the foundational Graph Patch Informer-Deep Sequential State Memory (GPI-DSSM), this framework evolves into hybrid Transformer-Gated Recurrent Unit (GRU) model that combines recent historical data with sequences from the same period in the prior year, to effectively capture both short-term trends and recurring seasonal patterns. Weather data, including temperature and humidity, is dynamically incorporated to adjust weights, enhancing forecast accuracy during high-impact periods. The model demonstrates high precision in predicting biweekly demand, balancing immediate fluctuations with longterm cyclic behavior. Extensive experiments on high-resolution datasets evaluate the impact of varying input sequence lengths, highlighting significant accuracy improvements as the sequence length increases. Leveraging multi-GPU optimization, the model achieves scalability and efficiency in processing largescale data. Results indicate substantial reduction in forecasting errors compared to conventional methods, underscoring the efficacy of integrating dual-temporal and weather-driven features.

title: A Computer Vision-based System to Detect Indian Road Signs using Deep Learning in Real-Time
authors: Gautam Kothari, Amit Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: There is a rapid growth of transport infrastructure in India, with highways and smart city projects. In smart city and urban road development, maintaining road signs is crucial for traffic safety and autonomous driving, or ADAS. For this task, an accurate and efficient road sign detection system is required that works in real time using low-budget cameras and surveillance systems. For Indian road scenarios, there are no major datasets that capture and categorize the road signs into the classes prescribed by the Indian Road Congress. In our work, we have collected and annotated road sign images for detection and classification tasks. We have also trained and tested CNN-based YOLO family models and the transformer-based DETR model for real-time detection suitable for edge devices, emphasizing minimal inference time. We found YOLOv11m to be the best model with an mAP@0.5 of 0.958, and a recall of 0.908 confirming the utility of this model for automatic detection of road signs in Indian scenarios.

title: AI-based Periapical Lesion Segmentation in 2D Periapical Radiographs using Residual U-Net
authors: N.Deepa, Alpa Gupta, Aayush Kumar Singh, Gul Mittal
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The accurate diagnosis of periapical lesions from dental radiographs is a critical yet challenging task in clinical dentistry, often subject to inter-observer variability. This paper presents a pilot study on the application of a deep learning model for the automated binary semantic segmentation of these lesions using 2D periapical radiographs. We propose a Residual U-Net (Res-UNet) architecture, designed to leverage the featurerich representation capabilities of residual connections within the proven U-Net framework. The model was trained and evaluated on a custom dataset of 179 periapical radiographs containing lesions, collected from a dental college department. Through a rigorous process of preprocessing, expert-guided annotation, and data augmentation, a final dataset of 716 images was generated. Trained over 200 epochs with a Dice loss function, our Res-UNet model achieved a promising Dice coefficient of 73.5 percent on a held-out test set. Qualitative analysis reveals the model’s ability to accurately delineate lesion boundaries and, in some cases, provide more concise segmentations than manual annotations. The results demonstrate the potential of Res-UNet as a robust tool for automated periapical lesion analysis, though further validation on larger, more diverse datasets is necessary to enhance its generalization and address limitations such as false positive predictions in low-contrast images.

title: Single-Stage Sensorless Induction Motor Drive with Lyapunov-Based Control for Solar PV-Powered Irrigation Pumps
authors: Anand Vardhan Pandey, Anjanee Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: This study uses Lyapunov function-based techniques to propose a new, improved non-linear space vector regulated direct torque control (SVM-DTC) for solar PV-fed induction machine (IM) drive. A new enhanced adaptive incremental and conductance (EAIC) MPPT technique to extract the maximum power possible from the SPV array is employed.IM control systems have made extensive use of non-linear sliding-mode management techniques and feedback linearization. Nevertheless, torque oscillations for load and parameter choices, as well as the chattering (torque-ripple) phenomenon, are drawbacks of non-linear sliding mode control. Additionally, the feedback linearity control depends on exact plant parameters and occasionally eliminates certain significant nonlinearities, which could cause the system to become unstable. This paper proposes an improved nonlinear SVM-DTC for IM, which has the benefits of lowering chattering and guaranteeing system stability. An IM is subjected to simulations, and its ongoing performance is examined in relation to a wide range of dynamic changes and parameter adjustments.

title: Generative Adversarial Networks for Image Augmentation: Architecture, Training, and Real World Applications
authors: Shlok Mehta, Tisha Negandhi, Prasenjit Bhavathankar
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
