title: Identification of Biomarkers in Alzheimer Disease using Artificial Intelligence
authors: Ameen Heshmat Ali Rasheedee, Amit Kumar Singh, Jappreet Kaur, Vivek Kumar Garg
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The identification of reliable biomarkers is a key aspect of the early detection and treatment of Alzheimer's disease (AD). The advances in artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning (ML) have changed this field, allowing the combination of complex multimodal data to identify new biomarkers. The following review highlights the existing applications, advantages, and limitations of the utilization of AI in biomarker discovery for AD. Though CSF biomarkers and neuroimaging are still the focus, AI has brought new scope with pattern analysis in genetic, electrophysiological, and behavioral data. Despite progressing, there are challenges still remaining, including diversity of data, methodological deficiencies, and noninvasive and low-cost measurements. Moreover, gender differences and risk factors such as APOE ε4 status and age make it even more challenging for the disease profile. Resolution of such issues through inclusive data collection and advanced AI models can lead to clinically actionable biomarkers to improve early diagnosis, risk prediction, and personalized treatment plans in Alzheimer's disease.

title: Blockchain and Machine Learning-Based Certificate Organization Framework: An Advanced Style to Organizational Credential Authentication
authors: Premananda Sahu, Saurabh Tembhurne, Bhipinder Singh, Sathish Krishna Anumula, Umang, Aldrin Manon
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: This paper proposes a robust agenda for certificate validation, enhancing security through the integration of ML and blockchain technologies. The system uses a machine learning agenda with two layers. The first model looks at the text parts of the certificate, such as ID, category of certificate, designation of the issuer and recipient, and the dates it was issued and will expire. The 2nd model forms the certificate's visual elements at the same time as the first one. Again, there is a cross-check that uses an OCR extract of the recipient's name and the metadata stored in the blockchain to make sure everything is correct. There are two main parts to the interface: "Issue Certificate" and "Verification." The "Issue Certificate" module lets users create novel digital certificates with metadata factors they choose. It also adds visual security features like signatures, logos, watermarks, and QR codes that are made in real time. The "Verification" module, on the other hand, uses IPFS and Pinata to store old or new certificates on the Sepolia test network in a way that makes them unchangeable and verifiable. This included the model provides a refined and adaptable solution to the challenges of issuing and validating certificates, addressing text security and forgery values for image & text files, while also safeguarding the certificates with blockchain technology to ensure their integrity. It works very well to make by inspection of digital certificates more trustworthy and open. The investigational results display a valid percentage rate of 96.88 %.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Early Detection of Skin Cancer from Dermoscopic Images
authors: Shivakumar. E, M. Jayaprakash
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The melanoma is most severe and significant skin cancers across the globe and early detection is essential to lower mortality rates as well as treatment costs. The dermoscopic imagery analysis is a non-invasive diagnosis option, however automated classification is often impeded by various issues such as hair artifacts that cross over the lesion area in the image, low contrast, and varying illuminations. Therefore, this paper proposes a novel learning framework that includes a comprehensive preprocessing operation to improve feature representation and a hybrid learning approach for cancer detection with high accuracy. The proposed pre-processing mechanism performs hair removal using a Deep Contrast Restoration Network (DCRN) to enhance lesion details and texture. The hybrid learning-scheme based on convolutional operation as a separate branch that extracts local texture and boundary features, and another branch includes a Vision Transformer (ViT) to model the global lesion structure. The classification module integrates the features of both modules and performs cancer detection and classification. The performance assessment on the HAM10000 dataset shows higher performance achieved by the proposed system with an accuracy and F1-score of 98% and 97.5%, respectively. It also outperformes the CNN and pure transformer model.

title: IoT-Integrated Geospatial Analytics for Automated Farmland Area Estimation Using Machine Learning
authors: Remanshu Thakur, Hemanshu Trivedi, Yash Borikar, Kamlesh Kalbande, Pooja Kalbande
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Accurate farmland area estimation is crucial for precision agriculture, resource allocation, and crop yield prediction. Traditional methods rely on manual surveys and remote sensing, which can be time-consuming, expensive, and inaccurate. The article suggests a framework for IoT-integrated geospatial analytics that uses ML techniques to automate the calculation of farming area. The system uses IoT-enabled sensors, GPS, and GIS tools to gather geospatial data in real-time. The data is then analyzed using sophisticated machine learning models for boundary delineation and land classification. The suggested model is contrasted with conventional remote sensing methods and verified using ground-truth datasets. The reliability, effectiveness, and cost-effectiveness of this approach have significantly improved, according to outcomes, making it suitable for large-scale agricultural monitoring. The paper emphasizes how automated agriculture operations and decision-making could be significantly revolutionized by IoT and ML-driven geospatial analytics.

title: Application of Vision Transformers for Automated Textile Defect Detection on Edge Devices
authors: Srinivas Talasila, Vijaya Kumar Gurrala, Srinija Pallerla, Kandela Abhilash, K Nagasreevardhan
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Fabric quality control plays an important role in textile production by reducing wastage and ensuring customer satisfaction. Manual inspection is time-consuming, susceptible to human error, and not suitable for high-speed production. Addressing these limitations, this work proposes a fabric defect inspection system that uses the strength of lightweight CNN and Vision Transformers (ViT) integrated on Raspberry Pi to enable autonomous and real-time quality inspection. The experimental results demonstrated that the proposed MobileViTv2 model achieved the highest accuracy of 99.43% and exhibited strong robustness across different kinds of fabrics and categories of defects. The model is successfully deployed on the Raspberry Pi module. The device is designed to be portable, operated continuously, energy efficient, and easy to install on existing fabric production lines. This work highlighted the potential of deploying advanced AI inspection systems on low-cost embedded hardware to modernize old-style industries with smart and automated quality control mechanisms.

title: Integrating Convolutional Neural Network and Vision Transformer Models:A hybrid Approach For Gender Classification using Hand Biometrics
authors: Abhijit Patil, Kodge B G
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Human hand is unique biometric modality that contains both the information like palmar and dorsal biometric information. The Gender classification using hand images is one of the critical tasks in forensics, biometric systems and human-computer interaction. Traditional CNNs are capable of extracting local features such as edges, texture and patterns. However, they lack the capacity to capture long-term spatial dependency across the image. Vision Transformers (ViTs), on the other hand, leverage a self-attention mechanism to acquire long-term relationship, but they require a substantial amount of data and computational power. In this work, we propose a hybrid framework that combines CNN-ViT’s models with a lightweight Simulated Transformer block, which integrate strengths of both architectures. CNN-ResNet-18 layers are first employed which perform local feature extraction from hand images, followed by transformer-based global modeling for global reasoning. The Proposed work, effectively handle variations in pose, illumination and occlusion, outperforms compared with using baseline CNN models or transformer model alone in terms of classification accuracy, precision, recall and F1-score.

title: Development of Smart Prosthetics with Neural Control Using AI
authors: Iype Cherian, Deepak Suresh Asudani, P. Bainalwar, Nitin Rakesh, Vishal Salve, Akhil Gupta
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The creation of smart devices has completely changed the field of therapy, giving people who have lost limbs better movement and usefulness. Even though traditional implants work properly, they are often hard to control and can't be changed to fit the user's needs. Putting brain control together with artificial intelligence (AI) seems like a good way to get around these problems. Neural control systems let the user's nervous system talk directly to the artificial device, which allows them to move more naturally and intuitively. AI systems improve this connection by letting changes be made in real time and learning from how the user acts, which makes the experience more personalised. This essay looks at the progress made in neurally controlled smart prosthetics, focusing on how AI can improve the user experience, boost performance, and make the devices more adaptable. It looks at different neural interfaces that are used in device development right now, such as brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) and systems that use electromyography (EMG). It also talks about AI methods like machine learning, deep learning, and reinforcement learning, which are very important for making the artificial devices adaptable and self-controllable. This paper talks about the problems and restrictions of using AI and brain control together in devices by looking at new case studies and examples. These include problems with understanding signals, using too much energy, and keeping the system stable. Finally, suggestions for the future are made that will focus on making brain control systems work better, finding more uses for them, and talking about the moral and social effects of these technologies. Putting AI and brain control together in smart prosthetics will likely make people's lives a lot better by giving them more useful, personalised, and smooth artificial solutions.

title: Machine Learning and Deep Learning for Osteoporosis: A Review of Imaging-Based and Clinical Data Applications
authors: Anni Tang, Haiwen Xu

title: Hybrid Transformer-LSTM Solar & Renewable Energy Forecasting for Resilient Microgrid Management
authors: Rajeswari Swain, Mainak Biswas, Arnab Ghosh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The increasing need for renewable energy sources makes solar power a crucial component of smart microgrids. In order to predict solar energy production and manage energy distribution, this study suggests a smart design that makes use of AI and the IoT. Data collected in real-time from energy meters and weather sensors is processed by Internet of Things devices using artificial intelligence models such as Artificial Neural Networks and Extreme Gradient Boosting. Fog computing is used by the system to improve local analysis, which in turn improves response times and decreases delays. This approach facilitates renewable energy transitions, optimizes resource usage, and promotes grid stability. This method relies heavily on solar generation forecasting, predictive energy control, and intelligent grid systems. With the help of AI and the Internet of Things, microgrids may improve their predictions.

title: A Hybrid Islanding Detection Approach for Inverter-Interfaced Distributed Generation
authors: Saheb Dhibar, Ankit Mishra, S.R. Mohanty, Avirup Maulik
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Islanding detection is crucial for personnel and equipment safety. Islanding detection under zero power mismatch conditions remains challenging. In this context, we present a “hybrid islanding detection” technique for inverter-interfaced distributed generation. The approach has three stages. In the first stage, disturbances are classified as non-islanding phenomena if the symmetrical components of the point of common coupling voltage exceed preset thresholds. The second stage of the algorithm deals with the passive islanding technique and is activated if the threshold in stage 1 is not violated. In the passive (second) stage, the frequency excursion at the point of common coupling is monitored, and islanding is declared if the frequency excursion is more than the threshold. In case the frequency threshold in stage 2 (passive) is not activated, stage 3 (active) of the algorithm is activated by injecting low-frequency current disturbances in the q -axis component of the grid side converter. The frequency deviation at the point of common coupling is monitored after disturbance injection. Islanding is identified in case the frequency deviation in stage 3 exceeds the threshold. The proposed approach has zero “non-detection zone” and can successfully discriminate between islanding and non-islanding events, with islanding detection within 200 ms. Simulation studies using MATLAB Simulink 2024a validate the efficacy of the method proposed in this paper.

title: Adversarial Machine Learning for Robustness in Federated Learning Environments
authors: R. Rajashri, Shuvo Dutta, Ramandeep Kaur, Charul Nigam, Amit Verma, Siddharth Shankar Mishra
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) supports collaborative model training on edge devices without revealing user data. Nevertheless, such a distributed nature also makes it vulnerable to a range of adversarial threats, including model poisoning, backdoor attacks, and Byzantine faults. In this paper, we propose a strong adversarial training framework for FL settings that combines secure client screening, adaptive weight clipping, cosine similarity-based update filtering and federated adversarial sample injection. We put forth a systematic classification of attack vectors, and present a scalable adversarial defense that is robust to dynamic and stealthy adversaries. Experiments on three typical FL datasets, i.e., MNIST, CIFAR-10 and FEMNIST, indicate the effectiveness of our approach. In invert a backdoor attack on CIFAR-10, the ASR was dropped from 92.1% (FedAvg baseline) to 14.3% with our method, and the accuracy was 81.1% (vs. 59.2% baseline). On MNIST as well our framework achieved an accuracy of 96.8% under poisoning (ASR 11.6%), as opposed to 91.2% accuracy with 84.7% ASR in the absence of a defense. The system introduces no more than 10% computing overload per training round and keeps the communication efficiency for less than 12% bandwidth increase. These findings demonstrate that our approach greatly improves robustness with little loss of utility, and that it is suitable to real federated deployments.

title: High-Performance Feature Extraction for Dimensionality Reduction and Enhanced Accuracy in Diabetic Retinopathy Detection
authors: Garidepalli Revathi, Shanker Chandre
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is one of the main vision loss problems among diabetic individuals and therefore, there is need to detect the problem when in progress so that clinical intervention can be instituted. Existing methods may have difficulty visualizing trends in high-dimensional retina image data, which is provided as a solution to the search of commonly used DR detection methods. This article presents a new DR hospital framework that scales well by incorporating features from multiple backbones that represent different modalities and sophisticated attention mechanisms along with dimensionality reduction. MobileNetV3-Small, EfficientNet-B0, and DenseNet-169 extract features, and the complementary strengths of the three offer a rich and wide variety of pathological patterns. They are also improved with the help of Global Attention Block (GAB) and Channel Attention Block (CAB), which highlight salient retinal areas including microaneurysms and exudates. PCA is used to eliminate redundancy and preserve assumptions of significance critical diagnostic information improving classification performance and computational efficiency at the same time. Respective datasets, APTOS and EyePACS, being used to evaluate the proposed model demonstrate significant accuracy of classification (up to 96.47 percent), with higher accuracy in detecting more severe stages of DR. The framework is far superior to the traditional single-backbones based models and even shows the potential to be used clinically in real-time. In future, explainable AI integration and nonlinear feature selection will be considered to increase interpretability and performance on different settings.

title: Innovative Thermal Design: How Nature is Redefining Heat Management in Supercomputing
authors: Siddharth Jain, Damanpreet Kaur, Rajan Kakkar, Nikhil Sharma, Tiyas Sarkar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: The urgent need to properly manage thermal conditions of supercomputing systems has become increasingly important because of their rising complexity and performance levels. The three standard cooling techniques that use air cooling fans alongside liquid circuits along with elaborate refrigerant systems prevail yet fail to strike equilibrium between environmental sustainability and operational performance. The deployment of such conventional cooling methods consumes vast amounts of power and results in substantial operational expenses alongside environmental deterioration. A meaningful change has occurred through implementing nature-based cooling solutions during the past few years. Thermal load reduction and energy reduction become achievable through innovative utilization of environmental resources including cold air from outdoor temperatures, ocean water, underground temperatures and glacial environments by organizations today. Several real-life examples prove natural cooling methods effective for large-scale high-performance computing operations: Microsoft uses underwater facilities while Facebook operates in Arctic conditions and Google accesses ocean water for cooling. The study evaluates heat management evolution by studying standard cooling practices against nature-driven heat removal solutions. The research explores ways to use natural elements for improving thermal efficiency while simultaneously reducing operational costs toward creating sustainable computing systems. The paper demonstrates through quantitative assessment and concrete environmental applications that natural elements are effective solutions for supercomputing heat management.

title: Ultra-Low-Power Wearable ECG Model with Cost-Effective Wireless Sensing System Through Convolutional Neural Network
authors: V. Lavanya, Hayel Khafajeh, Gonigunta Siva Prasad, J Shubangi, Rakesh Chandrashekar, M. Dinesh
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Research is being conducted on an Ultra-Low-Power Wearable ECG Model with Cost-Effective Wireless Sensing System Through Convolutional Neural Network (UWECWC) for applications in health monitoring, structural assessment, and environmental sensing. A deep learning framework has been designed to detect epileptic seizures by processing Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) and Electroencephalogram (EEG) data through convolutional neural networks (CNNs), using sliding window techniques to enhance classification while optimizing computational resources and reducing artifacts. A low-power wearable Electrocardiogram (ECG) model enables real-time detection of cardiac arrhythmias during physical activity, featuring energy harvesting via solar panels and a compact electronic design that promotes communication and power management. A wireless sensing system designed for ultra-high-performance concrete uses a two-probe resistance measurement technique along with low-power management features to enable continuous and energy-efficient monitoring. The following parameters are calculated for UWECWC is sensitivity and specificity calculation, power consumption, accuracy calculation, amplitude calculation, and sensitivity & AUC calculation.

title: FocusCam: A Real-Time Deep Learning System for Detecting User Distraction Using Webcam Video
authors: Satya Sri Dheeraj Motupalli, Kurakula Sai Dinesh, Anjali T
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Maintaining our focus during online learning or any computer based works has become a big problem for us due to distractions like using our phones, yawning from fatigue and short attention spans. This results in a significant drop in productivity and learning efficiency in such environments where direct supervision is not possible. To overcome this problem we propose FocusCam a lightweight real-time distraction monitoring system that checks the webcam feed to determine if a person is focused or distracted. Our model makes use of CNN models for Yawn detection, gaze detection and finally phone usage detection module YOLO during online learning sessions. the high quality datasets that were available such as YawDD and MPIIGaze have been preprocessed to emulate webcam like footage and achieved a accuracy of 97 % in predicting yawning and 80 % accuracy for gaze detection. These results demonstrate that combining multiple models results us in an effective tool to measure the attention. Future improvements can be made by integrating temporal modeling by the help of LSTM architectures.
