title: Intelligent Energy Management System of Electric Vehicles using Botox Optimization based Fuzzy Logic Algorithm
authors: Anand Singh, Sumit Verma, Prashanth Gs, G. Veeranna, Pramod Mehra, S. Senthil Kumar, N. Karuppiah, Ajay Sudhir Bale, Siva Ganesh Malla
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are attracting and growing up in many countries worldwide. Only battery storage system is the main power source in the EVs. An ultracapacitor is also used along with battery storage system. Hence, the energy management system is a very crucial and highly priority task in any EV for their best performance and life time. Many converters are included in EV for various applications. Hence, Fuzzy logic controllers based control methods are developed in this paper. However, conventional fuzzy logic controllers are having their limits, hence Botox optimization algorithm (BOA) is developed to train the fuzzy logic controllers. These strategies predominantly depend on the specialized knowledge and experience of professionals in the field. By leveraging fuzzy logic, these control methods can effectively handle the uncertainties and complexities associated with energy distribution and storage in EVs, leading to improved performance and reliability. This advanced control mechanism is designed to enhance various performance metrics including energy consumption, lithium battery output current, and peak power. Various results validate the flexibility of the BOA-based fuzzy energy management system in effectively distributing power across different driving conditions. Comparative analyses are performed regarding the power-sharing capabilities among proposed BOA-fuzzy (BOA-F), IWO-F, and WIO-F strategies. The primary goal of this research is to extend the lifespan of the battery by reducing both the output power and overall energy consumption. The BOA-F method enhances the battery’s SoC to $\mathbf{1 5. 6 \%}$ in comparison to the other methods. This improvement reduces the battery’s charging and discharging rates, thereby prolonging the lifespan of the battery while complying with the SoC limitations of the ultracapacitor.

title: Impact of Virtual-Inertia-Based Grid-Forming Inverters on Power System Oscillations
authors: Maysam Abbasi, Yuchen Zhang, Firuz Zare, Arindam Ghosh, Yateendra Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Increasing levels of renewable energy sources (RES) in the form of inverter-based resources (IBRs) affect the overall inertia of the power system. Grid-forming (GFM) inverters have the potential to contribute to synthetic inertia and enhance the oscillatory stability of the system. This paper presents a comparative analysis of the performance of GFM inverters and conventional synchronous generators (SGs). The GFM inverters are implemented using a virtual synchronous machine (VSM) control model, whereas conventional SGs use the GENROU model. PowerWorld Simulator and DSATools are employed for steady-state and dynamic (time-domain) simulations. The Single-Machine Infinite-Bus (SMIB) system and the benchmark two-area Kundur system are used to investigate the damping performance of GFM inverters under varying grid strengths. The performance of system damping is evaluated using conventional eigenvalue analysis and the matrix pencil method. The simulation results show that GFM inverters with VSM control provide acceptable damping performance compared to conventional SGs and have the potential to play a key role in future low-inertia power grids.

title: Fuzzy–Logistic Regression Approach for Analysing Consumer Behaviour and Shopping Preferences in E-Commerce
authors: G Vasanti, M. Rani, Prashant Gupta, RVS Praveen, Ankit Kumar, Dhivya Ramasamy
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Understanding and predicting consumer behaviour is essential for success in the constantly competitive e-commerce environment. Conventional classification models frequently encounter difficulties in addressing the basic confusion, subjectivity, and complexity of online shopping behaviours. The proposed study introduces an innovative hybrid framework that combines fuzzy logic with logistic regression to improve interpretability and predictive accuracy in modelling e-commerce consumer behaviour. By fuzzifying essential variables such as session duration, sentiment ratings, and cart abandonment likelihood, the model integrates complex behavioural patterns and combines them into a logistic regression model for probabilistic result prediction. The suggested system was evaluated using a real-world e-commerce dataset obtained from Kaggle, demonstrating superior performance compared to baseline models such as SVM, Random Forest, and conventional logistic regression. The system attained an accuracy of 97.4%, precision of 96.9%, recall of 97.8%, F1-score of 97.3%, and an AUC-ROC of 0.986. These findings highlight the model's efficiency in identifying and categorising consumer intent. This technology integrates the interpretability of fuzzy rule-based logic with the predictive precision of statistical modelling, providing it exceptionally appropriate for commercial use. The suggested research provides a scalable and elucidative method for improving consumer profiling, conversion forecasting, and targeted engagement inside digital commerce environments.

title: A Row-level Energy Assisted Current Transfer Approach for Power Recovery from PV Arrays During Partial Shading
authors: Priya Ranjan Satpathy, Vigna Kumaran Ramachandaramurthy, Ali H. Mahmoud
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Partial shading significantly affects the PV arrays by decreasing the power output due to current mismatch across rows and the activation of bypass diodes. Existing solutions often encounter operational limitations under complex and dynamic shading scenarios, including cost, complexity, implementation, and scalability. Static reconfigurations, usually referred to as the cost-effective approach to shading mitigation, remain limited by their inability to adapt dynamically or recover energy from shaded rows. This paper proposes a row-level energy-assisted current transfer (REACT) approach to recover the shadinginduced mismatch loss and the power during operation. The REACT approach implements the minimum components to temporarily absorb surplus current from unshaded rows and inject it into the output bus, without bypassing shaded modules. The REACT approach is evaluated in the MATLAB/Simulink environment and tested under five distinct shading scenarios, benchmarked against the existing conventional system with seriesparallel and total-cross-tied connections, along with static reconfiguration for a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$6 \times 6$</tex> array. The simulation analysis shows that the REACT approach can recover the power of PV arrays with 98.5% efficiency compared to conventional system, making it a compelling solution for improving energy in PV systems.

title: A Comparative Study on a Multi-Source Hybrid Machine Learning Framework Integrating Deep Learning and Swarm Intelligence for Intelligent IoT Forensics and Threat Classification
authors: Mashıya Afroze F, V. Poornima
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The exponential rise of the Internet of Things (IoT) has led to the interchange of vast amounts of data between network-connected devices. There are many security lapses and breaches as a result of the wide-ranging connectivity between IoT devices. Numerous benefits come with the quick expansion of IoT devices, but new security and forensics difficulties also arise. Digital investigators and practitioners face significant obstacles when interacting with IoT devices to probe cybercrimes in a timely and forensically sound manner, as a result of the vast amount of evidences generated by the billions of devices in the IoT system. The study's primary goal is to create a framework that performs forensic investigation on resource-constrained IoT using a combination of forensic technologies and machine learning to detect various kinds of attacks. The feasibility of Deep Neural networks (DNNs) in IoT Forensics (IoTF) is examined in this study to detect the attacks using the operating system logs. This work suggests utilizing an optimum set of parameters to train a Salps Swarm Optimization Algorithm (SSOA) for DNN. The suggested SSOA-DNN method is compared with the ML classifiers including KNN, RF, SVM, DT, LDA and NB Classifiers. The following metrics are used to evaluate how effective ML models are: (1) Accuracy, (2) Precision, (3) Recall, and (4) F-Measure. The results show that the SSOA-DNN outperforms with an accuracy of 96.37% than the other ML classification algorithms in IoTF Analysis.

title: Leukocytes Classification Using ViT From Blood Smear Image
authors: CH Manasa, K Raj Kiran, T Haribabu, V Mahendra, P Gowrisankar, P Kiranbabu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Blood count is an essential technique for illness diagnosis through the analysis of blood cell imagery with sophisticated technology. This procedure is conventionally characterized by intrusive methods, requiring significant time and financial resources. Contemporary methodologies utilize deep learning (DL) to optimize this process, enhancing its speed and cost-efficiency. Due to the resemblance of blood cell pictures, visually differentiating multiple blood types is difficult. We propose patch-based feature extraction and classification utilizing a Vision Transformer (ViT) to reliably categorize various kinds of blood cells while mitigating overfitting and degradation. The Vision Transformer (ViT) employs a patch-based feature extraction method to identify leukocytes (LKCs). This design was verified with the Raabin BC and BCCD datasets. The model is refined using preprocessing methods like normalization and data augmentation. Simulations were performed with a batch size of 128, employing the Adamw optimizer for 50 epochs. Our model attained an impressive accuracy of 97.97% on the Raabin dataset and 99.64% on the BCCD dataset, surpassing current state-of-the-art methods. These findings indicate that ViT may be advanced into therapeutically valuable tools for identifying LKCs in blood cell pictures, hence enhancing diagnostic efficiency substantially.

title: Hybrid Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN)BiLSTM Framework for Enhanced Short-Term EV Load Forecasting
authors: Abdul Hafeez, Nasser Al-Emadi, Atif Iqbal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Accurate short-term electric vehicle (EV) load forecasting is essential for the effective integration of EVs into smart grids, facilitating balanced energy consumption and reducing load variability. This paper introduces a novel hybrid framework that integrates Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) with Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory networks (BiLSTMs) to improve the efficacy of short-term EV load forecasting. The GAN model enhances the existing training dataset, while the BiLSTM model identifies temporal dependencies in the EV load patterns. The proposed approach is evaluated using EV charging station data from Palo Alto, California, and results demonstrate significant improvements in forecasting accuracy compared to other Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) based models. The hybrid GAN-BiLSTM framework achieved the best accuracy with RMSE of 0.0283, MAE of 0.0220, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.9235, confirming its superiority in minimizing prediction errors as compare other RNN-based models. This result offers an effective solution for improvement in the prediction of short-term EV demand at the charging station, which contributes to better grid stability and energy management.

title: Advanced Dispatchable Virtual Oscillator Grid-Forming Control
authors: Muntathir Al Talaq, Muhammad Bakr Abdelghany, Ahmed Al-Durra, Hatem Zeineldin, Tarek H.M. EL-Fouly
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Dispatching virtual oscillator control (dVOC) is a promising technology for grid-forming (GFM) that mimics the dynamic behavior of physical oscillators. Large-signal stability represents a critical consideration in power system design and operation, emphasizing the capability of energy sources to maintain or restore synchronization following substantial disturbances. However, GFM's transient stability analysis often overlooks the impact of inner control loops, which can result in inaccurate assessments. This study examines the effects of the inner control loops and current reference angle on the transient stability of dVOC, demonstrating that incorporating the inner loops enables a more comprehensive understanding of the GFM's dynamic behavior. Furthermore, a novel control framework, termed enhanced dVOC (E-dVOC), is proposed. The E-dVOC enhances system flexibility by introducing additional feedback mechanisms in the outer control loop and a feed-forward component within the inner loops, thereby improving fault ridethrough capability. In numerical simulations, the proposed E-dVOC is compared with other advanced GFMs. The results demonstrate that E-dVOC exhibits superior adaptability and stability under large-signal disturbances, significantly expanding the region of attraction (RoA) and achieving a faster dynamic response compared to other types of GFM.

title: Crop Recommendation and its Optimal Pricing using CATBOOST
authors: K. Sreeja, K. Vinay Reddy, B. Pranav, M. Tejaswini, A.Sai Madhav Raj, M.A. Jabbar
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The farming industry continues to play an essential role in India by using suitable crop types. The cost of using inputs and generating outputs is important for farming sustainability. This paper shows how an agricultural system that makes crop decisions and pricing improvements based on machine learning. learning by making use of web technologies. The model uses CatBoost when processing the data. important factors such as Nitrogen(N), Phosphorus(P), Potassium(K), along with rainfall, recommending the best crop for a place depends on checking the soil pH, air humidity, and temperature. region. To build a ShopBot Interface, developers rely on ReactJS, which is connected. assist farmers in locating seeds that save cost other essentials. Web Scraping is used by the ShopBot to obtain the latest prices. access to data on products’ availability from several online platforms. This paper offers the lowest prices without browsing through many different websites and recommends the crop through a machine learning model. In conclusion, this feature enhances agricultural productivity by enabling farmers to achieve higher yields with minimal inputs. Through the integration of technology, farming practices become more efficient, sustainable, and resource-friendly.

title: Cascaded Active Disturbance Rejection Control of Islanded Microgrid with Plug and Play Capability
authors: Siddhant Singh Maurya, Trapti Jain, Amod C. Umarikar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Active Disturbance Rejection Control (ADRC) has gained significant popularity in power electronics control due to its strong disturbance rejection capability. For microgrid applications, ADRC can achieve highly decoupled voltage control in <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d-q$</tex> reference frame. Available literature shows that <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$d-q$</tex> voltage control combined with droop equations is commonly used to operate multi-converter systems in islanded microgrids. This paper presents a cascaded voltage and current control strategy using ADRC for power electronic conversion in Distributed Generation (DG). The proposed control framework enhances system functionality by providing improved dynamic performance, robustness, and plug-and-play capability. A modified control is introduced in virtual synchronous generator (VSG) to eliminate repetitive voltage comparisons offering flexibility in control. The proposed method is modeled and simulated in Real Time Digital Simulator (RTDS) and MATLAB. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed control approach in ensuring stable and resilient microgrid operation. Additionally, a full-order small signal stability analysis is conducted to evaluate the microgrid's stability with constant impedance loads.

title: SUFS: IoT Based Urban Crop Health and Irrigation System
authors: A. Sakthi Preetha, Jayarengan R V, Yajnesh Sivaraman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: In an era of rapid urbanization, food security and sustainability are major challenges. This paper presents the design and implementation of a Smart Urban Farming System (SUFS) using Internet of Things (IoT) and for real-time crop health monitoring and automating irrigation. The system consists of an ESP32 microcontroller, integrated with multiple sensors to monitor real- time environmental conditions such as temperature, humidity, soil moisture, light intensity and air quality. The system monitors the plants' health and triggers irrigations when the sensor reading fails to meet the required threshold parameters. The system is designed to support small to large scale urban business farmers such as terrace gardens, vertical garden setups, etc. Experimental analysis shows improved water efficiency and better plant health. This project/system aims to empower business setups to contribute to sustainable farming practices in urban surroundings.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence for Ransomware Detection
authors: Noura Ouerdi, Nour el houda Rahmani, Ayoub Seghrouchni
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Networking, Intelligent Systems &amp; Security (NISS)
abstract: Complex ransomware has become a serious cyber threat, employing sophisticated methods to evade conventional detection systems. Ransomware detection has been enhanced by enhancing the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML). The opacity of these models has given rise to concerns about whether they are trustworthy and whether it is possible to interpret how decisions are made by these models. XAI seeks to bridge the knowledge gap by offering comprehensive information on model decision making, thereby promoting transparency and compliance with regulatory requirements. This paper provides a global overview of XAI techniques for ransomware detection, examining their performance, shortcomings, and potential for future research.

title: Active Wake Optimization Control for Offshore Wind Farms Based on Model Predictive Control and Kalman Filter
authors: Yang Liu, Jiahao Lin, Ling-Ling Huang, Shurong Wei, Ruanming Huang, Zhenkun Li, Lujie Liu, Zixu Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper presents an improved pitch control strategy for wind turbines (WTs) by integrating Model Predictive Control (MPC) with a Kalman filter to address control signal fluctuations under variable wind conditions. While MPC effectively handles system constraints and dynamic optimization, it often produces abrupt control outputs that can increase actuator wear and reduce system stability. To overcome this, a Kalman filter is applied at the output stage to smooth the control signal in real time. Simulation results on a three-turbine wind farm demonstrate that the proposed method not only improves pitch control smoothness but also enhances power generation and reduces mechanical loads. In a 300 -second test scenario, the strategy increases total power output by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8214.60 ~\mathrm{W} \cdot ~\mathrm{h}$</tex> and significantly reduces tower base bending moment and spindle torque, confirming its effectiveness and practicality in wind turbine control applications.

title: Optimal Dispatch for Transmission and Distribution Collaboration with Massive Electric Vehicles
authors: Hongyu Long, Zhengxin Chen, Yun Gao, Xiaorui Hu, Yi Long, Xin Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The optimal scheduling problem is studied for power system transmission and distribution association under large-scale electric vehicle access scenario in this paper. First, the transmission and distribution-associated multi-objective optimal scheduling model is constructed to solve the deficiencies of the existing studies. The model objective function includes the transmission grid-side power generation cost, the transmission and distribution system active power loss, exhaust emissions, and the distribution grid-side electric vehicle charging/discharging costs. The constraints cover various types of upper and lower bound constraints for transmission and distribution systems reliable operation and elaborate on the sources of wind and PV output data, as well as EVs charging/discharging energy consumption and tariff strategies. Second, a transmission and distribution correlation scheduling application scenario for largescale EV access is constructed, and the scheduling results under fixed tariffs, time-of-day tariffs, and peak-valley tariffs are analyzed, which show that peak-valley tariffs are advantageous in terms of both system costs and vehicle owner costs. Finally, a transmission-distribution correlation IEEE63 node test system consisting of improved transmission grid IEEE30 nodes and distribution grid IEEE33 nodes is designed, under which highperformance and diverse scheduling schemes for generators and electric vehicles are obtained, which provides a new way of thinking about scheduling of a new type of power system.

title: Triple-Active-Bridge Power Conversion in Hydrogen-Based DC Microgrids: Topology Design and Coordinated Control
authors: Qiyan. Wang, Kosala Gunawardane, Li Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The depletion of conventional primary energy sources and growing environmental concerns have accelerated the development of DC microgrids (MG) integrated with renewable energy sources. However, the traditional DC microgrid architectures face challenges of strong coupling between different ports and low operational scheduling efficiency when integrating diverse distributed energy resources. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a hydrogen-based DC microgrid architecture based on triple active bridge (TAB) converters, which incorporates photovoltaics (PV), battery energy storage systems (BESS), supercapacitors (SC), proton exchange membrane electrolyzers (PEMEL), and proton exchange membrane fuel cells (PEMFC). First, the power transfer mechanism of hydrogen-based DC microgrid is clarified through the establishment of TAB transmission power models and small-signal models. Then, a decoupled loop control method is developed for TAB converters through the introduction of a decoupling matrix, which enables independent operation among different ports. Furthermore, a rule-based energy management strategy for the proposed DC-MG is developed to realize effective aggregation of distributed energy resources, which achieves efficient utilization of different distributed energy sources. Finally, the proposed technical solutions are experimentally validated on an OPAL-RT platform. Finally, experimental validation conducted on the OPAL-RT platform demonstrates that the proposed hydrogenbased DC microgrid using the TAB converter achieves stable and efficient energy management under varying scenarios.

title: Enhancement of Cross-Differential Protection Schemes for Detecting Broken Conductor Faults in High-Voltage Transmission Lines
authors: Yohsafat Supono, Vigor Zius Muarayadi, Mohamad Fajar Rinaldi Utomo, Anam Malik, Kuthsav Thattai, Rishi Malyala
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Broken conductor (BC) faults present a unique challenge for conventional cross-differential protection (CDP) schemes in transmission systems. High probability of shunt fault (ShF) that occurs after BC often result in incorrect operation or failure to isolate the affected line. This paper proposes an enhanced CDP logic that integrates voltage start blocking, negative-to-positive sequence ratio, and bypass mechanisms to reliably detect and trip lines experiencing BC faults. The enhanced scheme is validated through simulated scenarios in PSCAD, demonstrating its ability to detect BC and trip the affected line. This successful isolation prevents the possibility of a subsequent ShF on the same line. However, since CDP's protection zone is reduced after one side trip, additional backup protection remains necessary to ensure comprehensive fault coverage.

title: SFD: Towards Segment Fusion Dataflow for Spatial Accelerators
authors: Fuyu Wang, Minghua Shen, Yufei Ding, Nong Xiao, Yutong Lu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Spatial accelerators are promising to satiate the growing demands for performance and energy efficiency in deep neural networks (DNNs). Due to the speed gap between onchip compute cores and off-chip memory bandwidth, common DNNs suffer from poor operational intensity and are increasingly memory-bound. While operator fusion has shown potential in alleviating this bottleneck, existing approaches suffer from two key limitations. They rely on predefined fusion templates before tensor mapping and impose tile constraints during mapping. As a result, they overlook the potential of fusing more operators and lead to sub-optimal performance. In this paper, we propose a segment fusion dataflow optimization framework called SFD. Central to this framework is the dataflow abstraction that enables template-free operator fusion after mapping and supports tile constraint relaxation through tile scheduling. Based on this abstraction, we first introduce a memory-centric mapper, which defines a design space and incorporates an algorithm to facilitate design space exploration (DSE). Then we propose an analytical network segmenter, which leverages mapping results to analyze tensor lifetimes and on-chip memory usage, fusing operators into variable-length segments. Finally, we introduce a dependency-aware tile scheduler, which develops a priority queue for each segment to ensure correct execution order. Extensive experiments with different DNNs demonstrate SFD achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.4 \times$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.2 \times$</tex> speedup for spatial accelerators over state-of-the-art fusion frameworks.

title: Robust Dual-Loop Nonlinear Control for a Two-Phase Interleaved Boost Converter in Fuel Cell Electric Vehicle Applications
authors: Ijaz Ahmed, Waqas Hassan, Michael Negnevitsky, Evan Franklin
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Regulating the DC bus voltage in fuel cell electric vehicles (FCEVs) is inherently challenging due to the highly variable output characteristics of fuel cells and the dynamic power demands of vehicular loads. This study discuss a robust dual-loop nonlinear control strategy for a two-phase interleaved boost converter (IBC) to address these challenges. An integral super-twisting sliding mode controller (ISTSMC) is employed, featuring an outer voltage control loop and an inner current control loop. The outer loop produces the reference current for the inner loop, enabling a coordinated and stable regulation of the DC bus voltage. The proposed control scheme is validated through detailed MATLAB/Simulink simulations under step changes in the desired output voltage and external output disturbances. Results demonstrate that the ISTSMC achieves fast transient response, low overshoot, reduced chattering, and strong robustness, making it well-suited for FCEV power management applications.

title: Implementation of Residual Dense Attention Net for Wildlife Species Detection and Identification using Multimodal Data Sources
authors: Suneetha Surapuraju, S. Maruthupermal
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The deep learning-aided approaches for recognizing the objects can support the ecologists in identifying vast specifies on their data and further estimating the populations. However, these approaches also encounter problems including only tiny areas captured by these techniques and allowing for habituating a minimum amount of wildlife species. Effectively employing the huge amount of data generated by these techniques lacks in utilization of distinct models needed for observing the biodiversity. To overcome these issues, a new model is implemented for identifying and detecting wildlife species. The novelty of the developed work is to accurately recognize the wildlife species with the support of multimodal data sources, which help to minimize the errors and the time of manual analysis. Moreover, this developed model helps to recognize the species even if they are in complex backgrounds. Initially, essential audio, image, and video data are collected from public websites. At first, the acquired audio is converted into 2D spectrogram images. Further, the converted images are fed as input to the Residual Dense Attention Net(ResDANet) for detection. Secondly, the raw images are given as input to ResDANet for identifying the species. Finally, the input video is subjected to the 3D ResDANet for detecting the species. After the process, the developed network is validated by utilizing various metrics and contrasted with the existing methods. Hence, the findings show that the developed network achieves the higher detection outcomes of the wildlife species.

title: Smart Shopping Trolley Using RFID Technology: An Iot-Based Approach for Enhancing Retail Checkout Efficiency
authors: Mohamed Imran Mohamed Ariff, Nur Munirah Nazam, Noreen Izza Arshad, Andry Chowanda
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: This paper presents the design and evaluation of a cost-effective, scalable, and fully automated Smart Shopping Trolley that integrates Radio Frequency Identification (RFID), Internet of Things (IoT) platforms, load-cell weight sensors, and a mobile application into a unified system. Conventional shopping faces persistent inefficiencies, including barcode dependency, long queues, and limited real-time tracking. The proposed system employs an ESP32 microcontroller to coordinate RFID-based item recognition, weight mismatch detection, and Firebase-enabled mobile synchronization, while enabling seamless digital payments. Functionality testing confirmed over 95 % accuracy in RFID scanning and weight verification, with checkout time reduced from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4}-\mathbf{6}$</tex> minutes to under 1 minute. Usability testing indicated strong shopper and administrator satisfaction, supported by intuitive interfaces and reliable performance. Findings are consistent with prior studies highlighting RFID-IoT integration and sensor-enabled retail automation, demonstrating that the Smart Shopping Trolley offers a practical, user-friendly, and scalable solution for nextgeneration retail environments.

title: Performance Evaluation of AI-Based Algorithms for 5G RAN Slicing
authors: Sakshi Somani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: The emergence of 5G networks has revolutionized communication based on the concept of network slicing, particularly in the Radio Access Network (RAN) dimension. Proper RAN slicing leads to efficient allocation of resources to applications like eMBB, URLLC, and mMTC. In this paper, we present a performance comparison of three Artificial Intelligence (AI) techniques — Decision Tree, Multilayer Perceptron (MLP), and Q-Learning for dynamic 5G RAN slicing. A pre-processed dataset was used to train and test all models based on parameters like Guaranteed Bit Rate (GBR), Delay, and Packet Loss. Comparative analysis is done based on classification accuracy, learning pattern, and adaptability based on different network conditions. Experimental outcomes reflect that, even though Decision Trees are efficient and simple, MLPs are accurate but are computationally intensive. Q- Learning, however, demonstrates excellent adaptability in dynamic conditions. This paper presents useful knowledge about each technique's pros and cons, which enables the selection of appropriate models for future intelligent RAN slicing schemes.

title: Skin Cancer Classification Using a Transfer Learning—Enhanced Hybrid Architecture with Multi-Scale Attention
authors: R. Durga Devi, M. Deepika
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: 抓取摘要失败: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='ieeexplore.ieee.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /document/11412718/ (Caused by ProtocolError('Connection aborted.', ConnectionResetError(104, 'Connection reset by peer')))

title: Zero-Knowledge Proof–Enabled Privacy Preservation for Secure Digital Payments in FinTech Blockchain
authors: Surya Teja Meesala, Devdas Gupta, Praveena Challa, Aashita Jain, Venu Sannamuri, Srikanth Mandru
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The explosive gains of FinTech-enabled digital payments have raised concerns about transaction privacy, the leaking of identity information and regulatory compliance on public blockchains. Existing privacy-preserving payment solutions either have a high computational overhead or do not offer controlled auditability as demanded by financial regulators. This research addresses the problem of ensuring good privacy of transactions while guaranteeing lawful transparency of decentralized payment systems. The goal is to create a blockchain payment framework that incorporates zero-knowledge proof (ZKP) which will ensure payer anonymity, transaction unlinkability and selective regulatory disclosure. The proposed method combines cryptographic identity commitments, private transaction circuits with zk-SNARK and gas optimized smart contract verification with controlled audit proofs. The framework is tested on the Private-FinPay data set that contains two million transactions. Experimental results indicate that the proposed ZKP-FinPay can achieve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 2 0 0}$</tex> transactions per second, verification latency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2 0}$</tex> ms, anonymity set of 50,000 users, and 0.5% probability of privacy leakage, which is better than the five state-of-the-art privacypreserving payment protocols. These findings confirm the viability of regulatory-compliant privacy preservation of FinTech blockchain systems as being technically feasible and practically scalable.

title: Fault Detection in AC Microgrid Using CNN, GRU and Attention Mechanism
authors: Rudranarayan Pradhan, Purbasha Padhi, Vivek Tripathy, N.S. Prabhupad Mohapatra, Ritesh Kumar Mangaraj, Devi Prasad Nanda
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Microgrids are localized energy systems that integrate resources such as solar PV, wind turbines, and load control, operating in coordination with the main grid. Their functions include improving efficiency and reducing losses. Fault detection in microgrid systems is challenging. A fault is any abnormal condition in the electrical network arising from various causes. It makes the system unstable. For better accuracy we need to detect the faults properly in a convenient method. We are adopting new techniques for fault detection using modernization, instead of conventional methods. Nowadays various techniques using machine learning for fault detection has been proved to be the best. In this paper we are going to combine CNN, GRU and attention mechanism to determine faults in microgrid. We have used binary classification method for the above technique. Our model is expected to meet future techniques of fault detection in microgrids (MGs).

title: Deep learning-Based Fault Detection and Classification in Multi-Bus Power Systems Using MATLAB Diagnostic Feature Designer and Bidirectional LSTM Networks
authors: Abhirup Sen, Shreya Sonakshi Rana, Jay Kumar Atal, Rudra Narayan Dash, Swati Smaranika Mishra, Nirnoy Mondal Ravi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Traditional fault detection systems in power networks face significant challenges in accurately identifying complex fault patterns, particularly in multi-bus configurations with varying impedance conditions. This research presents an innovative deep learning framework that combines MATLAB Diagnostics Feature Designer (DFD) with advanced neural network architectures implemented through Deep Network Designer. The methodology employs Multi-layer Perceptron (MLP) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) networks to enhance fault classification accuracy beyond conventional approaches. Time-domain features extracted using DFD serve as inputs to the deep learning models, enabling automatic pattern recognition and fault characterization. The system demonstrates exceptional performance in detecting and classifying five faults: single line-to-ground (LG), line-to-line (LL), double line-to-ground (LLG), three-line (LLL), and three-line-to-ground (LLLG) faults occurring on Bus 6 of the test system. Experimental results indicate superior classification accuracy of 95.92% for the validation dataset, significantly outperforming traditional machine learning methods. The deep learning approach exhibits robust performance across diverse operating conditions and demonstrates computational efficiency suitable for real-time power system protection applications.

title: A User -Centric Interactive System for Multi-model Communication Using Face Gestures, Hand Signs and Voice Commands
authors: Sangeetha R, Cibiraj M, Hema Priya N, Kishor T, Kanish R
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The virtual mouse is software that allows the user to interact with his or her computer in a more natural way than using a mouse. Virtual mouse software leverages computer vision technology to enable necessary interactions such as cursor movements, selections and scrolling. The two-stream model architecture allows for multimodal analysis and supports two streams. The first stream is a transformer-based multimodal fusion model (Transformer or Video Swin Transformer) to handle cross-temporal-action fusion. The second stream video-based transformer focuses on capturing head and hand movements. Leveraging the voice component of the system, audio embedding is created through transformer models (HUBERT or Wav2Vec 2.0) that demonstrate voice command accuracy ranging from 92.8% to 96.7% in different noise scenarios. Skeleton key points of head and hand movement are recovered from video based on Media Pipe or Open Pose, and structured into spatial-temporal graphs for ST-GCN processing, achieving gesture recognition accuracy of 94.3% for hand gestures and head movements at 91.2% accuracy. The fused visual-audio models had high recognition performance, showing multimodal intent recognition accuracy at > 97.7% with late fusion and confidence aggregation. These results demonstrate the capability of the system to facilitate immersive, intuitive, and contactless human-machine interaction

title: Enhanced Substations’ Earthing System Design Using Multi-Layer Soil Modeling for Reclaimed Ash Ponds
authors: Abir Kumar Majumdar, Nishant Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Reengineering of ash ponds at defunct thermal power stations into High Voltage (HV) substation construction or industrial infrastructure development, presents a unique earthing design challenge. Although the area has siting benefits such as a pre-existing transmission line corridor, the soil parameters distinctly differ from the natural ground. Conventional earthing design method creates grid with shallow and regular spacings, based on the assessment of a singular resistivity value at the surface in accordance with IEEE 80-2013, with the expectation of homogeneous to slightly stratified soils. Owing to the anisotropic and variable characteristics of ash pond strata, these processes require modification, often compromising grounding performance.This paper proposes a novel technique for multi-layer earthing system in composite sites, consisting of compacted natural soil over deeper deposits of fly ash. The soil model presents complete site-analysis incorporating multi-depth resistivity profile and geo-technically consolidated site characterization using Current Distribution, Electromagnetic fields, Grounding and Soil structure (CDEGS) software’s quasi-static modeling tool - MALZ module, which performs explicit modeling of anisotropic, multi-layered soils. The model considers the compacted soil stratigraphy and fly ash variability to analyze the behavior and reliability of earthing system. The framework provides a strong design technique that complies with IEEE 80-2013, combining operational reliability and cost-effectiveness for economically viable substation earthing on reclaimed ash ponds.

title: Game-Theoretic based Double Auction Framework for Optimal Allocation of Electric Vehicles and Charging Station Scheduling
authors: Manoj Saha, Sidhartha Sankar Thakur, Sajal Mukhopadhyay, Aniruddha Bhattacharya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The placement of Electric Vehicle (EV) Charging Stations (EVCS) and Battery Swapping Stations (BSS), along with the coordinated scheduling of Electric Vehicles (EVs), is essential to meet growing charging demands of EVs while minimizing their impact on the power distribution network. In this study, a comprehensive framework is developed on a radial distribution network integrated with a road network to strategically allocate EVs to different charging infrastructures. A double auction-based mechanism is proposed to allocate EVs between EVCS and BSS, ensuring mutual profit for both EV owners and EVCS/BSS operators. This work focuses on two primary objectives: (1) minimizing energy loss in the distribution network through optimal placement of EVCS and BSS, and (2) maximizing the profit of EVCS and BSS operators via optimal scheduling of EVs considering game theory-based compromised electricity prices. Real-time uncertainties related to EV travel behavior are modeled using the 2 m -point estimation method to improve reliability and practicality. The proposed problem is solved using a newly developed hybrid optimization technique combining Differential Evolution (DE) and Prairie Dog Optimization (PDO).

title: Enhancing Pothole Detection: A Deep Learning-Based Investigation comparing YOLO Variants with Validation on multi-weather pothole images
authors: Gautam Kothari, Amit Kumar
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Potholes pose a serious threat to the longevity of infrastructure and the safety of mobility, especially on Indian road networks. A real-time pothole identification framework based on sophisticated YOLO (You Only Look Once) object detection models is presented in this study. To represent real-world differences in lighting, weather, and pavement texture, six YOLO variants, chosen due to their proven speed and accuracy —YOLO11n, YOLO11s, YOLO11m, YOLO12n, YOLO12s, and YOLO12m—were trained on a varied and well-structured dataset. The trained models were tested on self-captured road photos from Dehradun, India, to assess generalization under real-world circumstances. With an mAP@50 of 0.817 and a recall of 0.753, YOLO11m outperformed all other versions in terms of accuracy and localization, resulting in the best overall performance. This detailed comparison reveals the real-world trade-offs between model size and performance, highlighting the greater deployment capabilities of YOLO11m. With AI-driven infrastructure management, the suggested system provides a very efficient automatic pothole detection solution, facilitating real-time monitoring, smart road maintenance, and increased road safety.

title: A Lightweight Detection Architecture for Real-Time Medical Pill Recognition
authors: B Srinivasulu, R Veeranjaneyulu, P Venkateswarlu, A Mounika, K Sowjan Kumar, E Mani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: This paper presents an efficient deep learning-based model for automatic medical pill recognition using streamlined convolution neural network architecture. The proposed model processes input pill images through a sequential stack of lightweight convolution layers, followed by a Spatial Pyramid Pooling Fast (SPPF) block that enhances multi-scale feature representation. The network architecture incorporates transpose convolutions and feature concatenation operations to fuse spatial and semantic information from different layers, facilitating precise pill localization. The model demonstrates high accuracy in detecting pills, even in challenging scenarios such as overlapping instances and reflective surfaces. Experimental results show that the model achieves a significantly high mean Average Precision (mAP) of 98.0%, an inference speed of 500 FPS, and a compact model size of only 4.2 MB, making it suitable for healthcare applications.

title: Secure Key Exchange and Wormhole Attack Prevention in Wireless Ad-Hoc Networks
authors: Ekta Chowdhury, Anindya Kumar Biswas, Monalisha Biswas, Ayush Mitra, Ashoke Maity, Ayush Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: For networks established in an ad hoc manner, this work presents secret key generation and share distribution using Asmuth bloom scheme among multiple recipients, considering thresholds. No single or less than appointed threshold recipient can recover the secret value. The proposed method enlightens how the hidden value is being encrypted and later on only the chosen number of threshold recipients can decrypt it securely. Asmuth bloom scheme is helping to keep the secret value hidden across the shares. The secret value is being encrypted through some cryptographic process and random integer by the forwarder but the value is not being publicly disclosed. If adversary node or hidden node/terminal enters into the connected network and performs Worm-hole attack then it can be detected using geographical leash & also can be prevented by the help of temporal leash. The security analysis section provides support to the claims made.

title: Robust Storage Sizing and Adaptive Power Flow Control for Renewable-Powered Residential Systems
authors: Saher Javaid, Iacovos I. Ioannou, Yasuo Tan, Jahangir Hossain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper presents a robust and adaptive framework for residential energy systems with optimal storage sizing and an adaptive demand power flow control (ADPF) algorithm to ensure stable system performance under fluctuating generation and demand. The storage sizing optimization model determines the minimum storage capacity needed to support critical loads, guaranteeing the Robustness Against Fluctuations (RAF) property of the power system. The novel Adaptive Demand-based Power Flow (ADPF) algorithm assigns available power from photovoltaic (PV) generation and energy storage systems (ESSs) to prioritized loads in real time while preserving the safety bounds of ESS. To evaluate the proposed storage sizing and ADPF algorithm, simulations are carried out on a residential renewable-powered system using real PV data from smart apartment and appliance-level demand profiles, including both critical and non-critical loads. Four connectivity configurations are considered to assess the impact of network flexibility on storage requirements and load-serving capability. Additionally, energy not served (ENS), loss of load probability (LOLP), curtailed energy per critical load, renewable utilization, storage throughput, and peak deficit are evaluated to assess system reliability, resilience, and efficiency, which are important for systems with high penetration of renewables. Results demonstrate that increased system interconnectivity reduces the required storage size and enhances the effectiveness of adaptive control. The proposed framework provides a scalable and efficient solution for designing and operating resilient, user-aware residential power systems.

title: Adaptive Reconstruction-Based Model Predictive Control for Networked Stochastic Systems Under False Data Injection Attacks
authors: Kai Ma, Ning He, Chao Shen
abstract: A resilient stochastic model predictive control (MPC) method based on an adaptive input reconstruction mechanism is proposed for networked stochastic systems under false data injection (FDI) attacks. To the best of our knowledge, this is the first stochastic MPC framework designed to address FDI attacks; it not only mitigates the conservatism of existing methods but also reduces system resource consumption. Particularly, an adaptive input reconstruction mechanism is introduced to relax the assumptions on FDI attack energy in existing resilient MPC methods by reconstructing feasible control inputs. In addition, the adaptive prediction horizon and terminal constraint are co-designed to reduce the computational complexity. Furthermore, the conservatism inherent in existing resilient MPC methods due to hard constraints is alleviated by transforming fixed hard constraints into stochastic constraints. Based on these designs, sufficient conditions are derived to guarantee the proposed method’s recursive feasibility and the closed-loop system stability. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through simulations on a DC–DC converter system.

title: User Sentiment Analysis of Solar Energy Monitoring Apps: Insights from Google Play Reviews
authors: Afnan Shobil, Salah AL-Hagree
abstract: With a focus on user reviews from the Google Play Store, this study investigates user perceptions of mobile applications designed for remote monitoring and control of solar energy systems. This study uses sentiment analysis, a powerful natural language processing tool, to extract valuable information from structured and unstructured text data and classify opinions and comments as neutral, negative, or positive to understand user experiences, identify significant areas of satisfaction and dissatisfaction, and provide application developers with relevant information by analyzing more than 17,100 user comments across 18 applications that were updated in 2024, using the Google-Play-scraper Python script. The reviews have been analyzed using the Valence Aware Dictionary and Sentiment Reasoner (VADER), to determine whether they are positive, neutral, or negative. The analysis revealed that a large majority of users (67.4%) expressed primarily positive sentiments about the ease of use and effectiveness of these apps in monitoring solar systems; there are also about (15.1%) negative reviews, and (17.5%) neutral reviews. The analysis leverages classical supervised Machine Learning (ML) models in combination with lexicon-based sentiment analysis (VADER); to classify the sentiment expressed in comments. Support Vector Machine (SVM) is one of the best supervised machine learning methods that can be used for sentiment classification tasks, which achieved 91% accuracy, indicating the effectiveness of SVM in sentiment classification when compared to other machine learning methods, such as: Decision Tree (DT), Logistic Regression (LR), RidgeClassifier, Random Forest (RF), and Naive Bayes (NB) which achieved 90%, 89%, 87%, 68%, and 40% accuracy, respectively. However, the study also identified areas for improvement, such as addressing technical bugs, improving response times, and enhancing the user interface. These findings provide crucial insights for developers to improve user experience, enhance app functionality, and ultimately improve user satisfaction with solar monitoring apps. The findings also show that sentiment analysis is a useful technique for classifying and assessing reviews and comments from other people's reviews and feedback.

title: Pyramid Vision Transformer-Based Multi-Scale Feature Fusion and CNN Decoder for Accurate Colon Polyp Segmentation
authors: Zakaria Kerkaou, Yassine Oukdach, Mohamed El Ansari, Lahcen Koutti, Mouna Salihoun
abstract: Polyp segmentation is essential in reducing the incidence of colorectal cancer by enabling the timely detection and removal of polyps, which are the precursors to most colorectal cancers. Accurate segmentation provides crucial information that aids in the early diagnosis and treatment of the disease. However, this task is challenging due to the considerable variation in polyp texture, color, and size. Additionally, some polyps closely resemble the surrounding healthy tissue in color, making it difficult to distinguish the boundaries between the polyp and its surrounding area. To address these challenges, we propose a deep learning model based on an encoder-decoder architecture. In the encoder section, we utilize a Pyramid Vision Transformer (PVT) to extract robust global feature representations across multiple stages. We introduce a multi-scale fusion module that combines features from different scales, allowing the model to capture diverse information. A custom convolution refinement attention module is employed to further refine the features, and it is integrated with the output of an efficient simple residual path, which preserves essential polyp features. Experiments are conducted on five publicly available polyp datasets. CVC-Clinic and Kvasir-SEG datasets are used for training, while CVC-ColonDB, Etis LaribDB, and CVC-300 datasets are used to evaluate generalization. The proposed approach achieves state-of-the-art performance, with a mean Dice coefficient of 93.9% and a mean Intersection over Union of 89% on CVC-Clinic, and 91.0% and 85.9% on Kvasir-SEG, respectively. Furthermore, our approach demonstrates the ability to adapt to new, unseen polyp features, achieving optimal results on the CVC-ColonDB, Etis LaribDB, and CVC-300 datasets. This adaptability makes it suitable for real clinical scenarios.

title: Evaluation of Magnetometer-less Inertial Sensors for Static and Dynamic Spatial Orientation Estimation
authors: Alejandro Castellanos Alonso, Gonzalo García Carro, Juan C. Álvarez, Diego Álvarez, Antonio M. López, Diego Valdés Tirado
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This study introduces a magnetometer-free, custom-built inertial measurement unit (IMU) combined with an opensource sensor-fusion algorithm and provides a systematic experimental comparison against commercial IMUs that integrate proprietary hardware and closed-source filtering software. The main innovation lies in demonstrating that an open and fully reproducible hardware and software architecture can achieve orientation estimation accuracy comparable to that of state-of- the-art commercial solutions under both static and dynamic conditions. Experimental results show that the proposed system achieves a mean orientation error of 2.10% (0.11°), while consistently outperforming proprietary filtering approaches in static scenarios and exhibiting competitive variability during dynamic motion. Furthermore, the study presents a comprehensive benchmarking framework that analyzes the influence of sensor noise characteristics and filtering strategy on estimation performance across multiple hardware and algorithm configurations. These findings establish that custom-built IMUs paired with open-source sensor-fusion algorithms constitute a reliable, cost-effective, and transparent alternative to commercial hardware and software solutions for biomedical and engineering applications.

title: The Human Bridge: How the Human Factor mediates the impact of Industry 4.0 technologies on supply chain resilience in the Industry 5.0 era
authors: Omar León, Begüm Kanat
abstract: Industry 4.0 technologies (such as Internet of Things, Artificial Intelligence, and Big Data) are redefining supply chain resilience paradigms. However, the emergence of Industry 5.0 introduces a fundamental shift by positioning the Human Factor as a central element in this technological equation. Using data from 125 managers in manufacturing and service industries, this study investigates this transition through a dual methodological approach: Canonical Correlation Analysis to identify key technological patterns and mediation analysis to elucidate the role of Human Factor. Confirmatory factor analysis was also applied to validate the measurement model, and all analyses were complemented with bootstrapping procedures to ensure methodological robustness and reliability. The Canonical Correlation Analysis results reveal strategic associations between specific Industry 4.0 technologies and critical supply chain resilience dimensions, highlighting optimal configurations for diverse operational contexts. The mediation analysis demonstrates that the Human Factor not only complements but also significantly channels the impact of technologies on resilience, acting as a bridge between technical capabilities and operational outcomes. Specifically, we show how human adaptability, problem-solving, and digital competencies condition the effectiveness of Industry 4.0 technologies, explaining why organizations that invest in both technological and human resources achieve higher resilience. These findings provide empirical support for the Industry 5.0 paradigm by demonstrating the mechanisms through which the Human Factor enhances technological value creation, and they offer actionable insights for organizations (particularly Small and Medium-sized Enterprises) seeking to balance technological and human capital to optimize resilience in increasingly complex supply chains.

title: Robust Smoothing of Wireless Ranging Errors using Tensor Fibered Rank in IoT-Enabled Positioning
authors: Yuhang Mei, Chengming Luo, Yongshuai Fei, Zizhuo Liu, Han Xu, Fantong Kong, Biao Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Wireless sensor networks (WSNs) that can provide essential location information have become the key driver for various industrial Internet of Things (IoT) applications. Time-varying and open wireless channels are susceptible to noise and non-line of sight interferences, which can cause the wireless ranging errors and even measurement outliers. Since similar sampling locations can generate similar wireless ranging, this paper proposes a tensor fibered rank based on the weighted total variation (TFR-WTV) method for smoothing wireless ranging errors. Considering the local correlation characteristics of wireless ranging, the sparsely measured wireless ranging data is mapped into a wireless ranging tensor, which can be further decomposed into a low-rank target tensor and a sparse noise tensor using the robust principal component analysis. Based on the multidimensional low-rank property, the tensor singular value decomposition (t-SVD) is extended to mode k-t-SVD, and a fibered nuclear norm of nonconvex logarithmic operators is used as the low-rank smooth approximation to enhance wireless global correlation. Meanwhile, a weighted total variation serves as the joint constraint term for capturing wireless local continuity and structural details. Finally, the proposed TFR-WTV algorithm is efficiently solved by the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM), which can obtain the smoothed low-rank target tensor. Simulation experiments are conducted to evaluate the smoothing performance of wireless ranging error with different noises and different outlier rates. With use of the networked measurement platform and publicly available data with obstacle interferences, the wireless ranging smoothing are evaluated using seven baseline algorithms, which can provide reliable location-based services for industrial IoT applications.

title: Sequential Convex Approximation Approach for Chance-Constrained AC Optimal Power Flow under Arbitrary Random Distribution with Mild Conditions
authors: Jiajun Chen, Jianquan Zhu, Haixin Liu, Tao Jiang, Yuhao Luo, Mingbo Liu
abstract: In this paper, a sequential convex approximation (SCA) approach is proposed for solving the chance-constrained AC optimal power flow (CC-ACOPF) under arbitrary random distribution with mild conditions. Unlike traditional approaches that are only theoretically applicable to transforming chance constraints to some tractable constraints under Gaussian distributions, SCA can complete the same task while not restricted by the forms of random distributions. This makes more sense because the uncertainties of power systems usually follow non-Gaussian distributions. However, the proposed SCA approach is time-consuming, since it needs to sample massive scenarios to simulate the random variables' distributions existing in chance constraints. To this end, a distribution projection (DP) technique is further proposed to directly describe these random distributions in the Bernstein polynomial space. In this way, the repeated calculations in massive sampling scenarios can be omitted, thereby improving the computation efficiency of the SCA approach significantly. Case studies in several test systems validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title:
Development of 2 km-class Carbon-doped MgB
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Wire with Uniform Critical Current Property
authors: Dong Gun Lee, Jun Hyuk Choi, Minoru Maeda, Seyong Choi, Jung Ho Kim
abstract: Kilometer-scale carbon-doped MgB<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> superconducting wires were fabricated using a three-dimensional vibrating powder-filling powder-in-tube process and systematically evaluated for uniformity and performance. The optimized process enabled fabrication of a 2.1 km-long 18 filament conductor with a critical current density (Jc) of 1.13 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup> A/cm² at 20 K and 2 T, showing only ∼2% variation along its length. Carbon substitution enhanced high-field performance primarily through impurity scattering, while the n-value demonstrated a strong proportionality with Jc across all measured conditions. This correlation establishes the n-value as not only a research parameter but also a practical quality-control metric for industrial wire production. The combined achievements of process innovation and transport uniformity highlight the feasibility of MgB<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> conductors for MRI magnets, high-field research magnets, and superconducting power transmission.

title: Automated Microwave Interferometric Sensor for Detecting Extracellular Vesicle-Mimicking Particles
authors: Sevda Seyyedmasoumian, Maede Chavoshi, Bart Nauwelaers, Tomislav Markovic, Dominique Schreurs
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: A compact automated microwave interferometric sensing setup for label-free detection of extracellular vesicle (EV)-like particles in microfluidic environments is presented. The proposed setup employs a programmable vector signal generator for automated amplitude and phase control, simplifying control and improving precision and sensitivity. An interdigital capacitor (IDC) resonator enhances field confinement and sensitivity to dielectric changes. Polystyrene (PS) bead suspensions (0.25%–1%) are used to emulate the dielectric behavior of large EVs (L-EVs) with 10-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m particle size. Measurements show distinct power and frequency shifts with concentration up to 36.2 dB and 4.65 MHz, respectively. The system achieves a limit of detection of 0.07%, demonstrating compact, sensitive, and label-free biosensing.

title: Clinically Oriented CNN–Transformer Architectures for Reliable Bronchoscopic Recognition of Lung Lesions and Anatomical Structures
authors: Rolyph Erwan Ntoutoume Nguema, Mohamad Forouzanfar, Ali Traore
abstract: Bronchoscopy is central to diagnosing central lung cancers but remains limited by reliance on operator expertise and variability in visual interpretation. In this work, we adapt and evaluate CNN–Transformer hybrid models for the classification and segmentation of bronchoscopic images, with a particular emphasis on clinically realistic patient-level evaluation. These models combine convolutional blocks, which capture fine-grained local features, with Transformer components that encode long-range dependencies and global context, yielding feature representations well suited to the complexity of bronchoscopic images. The primary objective of this study is to adopt CNN-Transformer hybrid architectures for bronchoscopic lesion and landmark recognition, while evaluating their performance under clinically relevant data partitioning conditions. We evaluate our methods on BM-BronchoLC, a publicly available dataset of 2,921 annotated bronchoscopic images, and present two complementary frameworks: MedViT, a convolution-enhanced vision transformer for multi-label classification, and FCB-SwinV2, a dual-branch design coupling a convolutional encoder with a SwinV2 Transformer U-Net decoder for semantic segmentation. To directly address the study objective, we compare the performance of both models under random image-level splitting and rigorous patient-level partitioning, which prevents leakage of visual patterns between training and testing sets and provides a more clinically realistic evaluation. MedViT achieves 94.7% accuracy (AUC 0.95) for anatomical landmarks under random splitting and preserves comparative performance with 93% (AUC 0.91) under patient-level separation. For lung lesions, results remain competitive at 82.3% (AUC 0.79) and 80% (AUC 0.69), respectively. FCB-SwinV2 yields Dice scores of 0.42 for landmarks and 0.33 for lesions with random splitting, which decline to 0.38 and 0.32 under patient-level evaluation. These results show that while the models maintain overall solid performance, they also exhibit a consistent drop under patient-level validation, underscoring the risk of overestimation when relying solely on random splitting. This controlled comparison between the two evaluation protocols demonstrates that despite the expected decrease in performance when removing data leakage, the proposed architectures remain competitive and generalize effectively to unseen patients. These findings indicate that our adapted CNN–Transformer architectures provide useful baselines for BM-BronchoLC and show encouraging signs of generalization to unseen patients, while also illustrating the performance differences between random and patient-level evaluation. They also reinforce that proper patient-level evaluation is central to the reliability of AI systems, and should be systematically adopted to avoid inflated performance estimates. All code and models are released to support reproducibility and foster future research.
