title: AnoDS: A Blockchain-Based Anonymous Small Donation System for IoT Devices_supp1-3665329.pdf
authors: Rui Shi
abstract: This article introduces the anonymous small donation system (AnoDS), a blockchain-based AnoDS for Internet of Things (IoT) devices in industrial environments. Traditional donation models often rely on centralized charitable organizations, which can cause delays and reduce transparency. AnoDS aims to address these issues by leveraging blockchain's decentralized and tamper-resistant nature, allowing direct interaction between donors and donees via IoT devices. The system integrates seamlessly with industrial IoT networks, incorporating digital identity management and efficient pointcheval-sanders signature (PS) signatures to ensure real-time tracking of donations while preserving donor anonymity. We implement the core cryptographic components of AnoDS and conduct micro-benchmarks of the underlying cryptographic operations to estimate its computational and communication overhead. The results indicate that AnoDS achieves high efficiency and low communication overhead under realistic parameter settings, making it suitable for deployment in industrial IoT scenarios. In addition, we formalize the system and threat models, analyze the security properties under clearly stated trust assumptions, and compare AnoDS with representative donation schemes in terms of functionality and efficiency. This system offers a secure, transparent, and efficient solution for anonymous donations in IoT-driven industrial environments.

title: Engineering a Robust Social Media Bot Detection Model Using Roberta, Time Series Patterns, and Graph Neural Networks
authors: Selvin Retna Raj Thavasimuthu, A. Thomas Paul Roy, Vinuja G, J. Relin Francis Raj, R. Karthik Ganesh, P. Bharathi
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: This study presents an engineering-driven multimodal deep learning framework for robust detection of social media bot accounts by integrating advanced natural language processing, temporal modeling, and graph-based learning. The system employs RoBERTa to generate rich semantic representations of textual content, enabling the detection of repetitive expressions, low linguistic diversity, and machine-generated phrasing. Behavioral activity patterns are analyzed through a Time Series Transformer, which captures temporal irregularities, burst posting behavior, and deviations from human circadian rhythms. Complementing these components, a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) evaluates the structural properties of user interaction networks, identifying abnormal connectivity, suspicious communities, and coordinated bot clusters. The engineered fusion of content, behavioral, and graph-based representations results in a scalable architecture capable of real-time operation on largescale social platforms. From an engineering standpoint, the model strengthens digital ecosystems by contributing to more resilient ICT infrastructure, ensuring that online platforms maintain reliable and authentic interaction channels. By mitigating automated manipulation and enhancing platform integrity, the framework also supports improved cybersecurity, offering early detection of malicious automated accounts. Experimental evaluations demonstrate high accuracy, strong generalization performance, and consistent robustness when compared to traditional machine learning approaches. Overall, this work provides a comprehensive engineering solution for automated bot detection and can be adapted for deployment across diverse social media environments.

title: Fuel Cell/Supercapacitor Hybrid Power Systems in Small Scale Unmanned Aerial Vehicles using Optimized Energy Management Strategy
authors: Sahan Neralampitiyage, Kosala Gunawardane, Manoj Ranaweera, Kasun Subasinghage
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The limitations of battery power systems in small scale Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), including weight and computational constraints, have significantly increased the demand for lightweight power systems with extended flight durations. This paper proposes a fuel cell/supercapacitor hybrid power system with a novel rule based Energy Management Strategy (EMS) that requires low computational resources, designed for small scale UAVs. The EMS is designed to operate the Fuel cell (FC) within its optimal efficiency region while dynamically managing the charge/discharge behavior of the supercapacitor (SC) based on instantaneous power demand and State of Charge (SoC) of the SC. A detailed MATLAB/Simulink model is developed using a 300W Proton Exchange Membrane Fuel Cell (PEMFC) and a 7F SC bank. Simulations are performed using a predefined 10-second UAV flight profile to evaluate the effectiveness of the proposed hybrid power system for small scale UAVs. The results demonstrate that the system maintains a stable 36V DC bus voltage, ensures efficient power sharing between the two sources, and significantly reduces excessive charge/discharge cycles of the SC while operating within the allowable SoC range, thereby enhancing system reliability and extending its lifespan.

title: GyRot: Leveraging Hidden Synergy Between Rotation and Fine-Grained Group Quantization for Low-Bit LLM Inference
authors: Sangjin Kim, Yuseon Chou, Byeongcheol Kim, Jungjun Oh, Hoi-Jun Yoo
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Low-bit quantization is essential for efficient LLM inference, and both rotation and fine-grained group quantization have shown individual promise. However, their combination often leads to accuracy degradation or hardware overhead due to a mismatch between the global nature of rotation and the localized behavior of group scaling. We propose GyRot, a quantization framework and hardware accelerator that bridges this gap through algorithm-hardware co-design. GyRot introduces Coarse Rotation, Fine Grouping (CoRFiG) and Harmonic-Aligned Permutation (HAP) to enable cooperative integration of rotation and group quantization, enhancing quantizability while relaxing scaling factor precision. To further reduce hardware cost, we reformulate asymmetric quantization and introduce a zero-point rounding strategy that enables fully integer dequantization. Implemented on an INT4-based tensor PE architecture, GyRot achieves state-of-the-art 4-bit accuracy across LLaMA-family models, while delivering up to 3.4× speedup and 3.6× energy efficiency over baseline LLM accelerators. These results validate GyRot's practical effectiveness for scalable and energy-efficient LLM deployment.

title: Automated Cardiomegaly Detection Using Deep Learning: A DenseNet121-Based Approach for Chest X-Ray Classification
authors: Jayapal Lande, Parul Nasra, Stuti Bhatt, Alankrita Joshi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Abnormal heart enlargement or cardiomegaly is a red flag of cardiovascular disease and it should be detected at an early stage to be treated promptly. Diagnosis of chest X-rays (CXR) is subjective and time-consuming done manually. In this paper, a suggestion is made to introduce an automated cardiomegaly detection system based on the DenseNet121 convolutional neural network (CNN) which will perform classification of the chest X-ray into either Normal or Cardiomegaly. The model takes advantage of transfer learning, image preprocessing, and data augmentation as a way of improving generalization. It has been trained using the Adam optimizer and binary cross-entropy loss with high accuracy in classification (94 percent) and high precision (0.96) and recall (0.94). A comparative study with the existent CNN models like VGG16, ResNet50, and InceptionV3 showed that DenseNet121 was a better model because of its high feature connectivity and gradient reuse. Also, analysis of activation functions indicated that Softmax was the best with binary classification. The suggested solution is a powerful, effective, and scalable clinical decision support solution to the automated cardiomegaly detection based on CXR images.

title: Effects of Harmonics on Distribution Transformer Loss and Its Mitigation: Bangladesh Grid Perspective
authors: S. M. Masud-Bin-Ali, Md. Minarul Islam, Imi Bintey Fariha Rahman, Abdul Hasib Chowdhury, Taha Selim Ustun, Kashem M. Muttaqi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Power quality (PQ) of a power distribution network is a critical factor that directly influences the efficiency and longevity of consumer appliances. Through a comprehensive analysis, this paper examines the detrimental effects of poor PQ on distribution transformers, highlighting increased operational costs, reduced lifespan of appliances, and frequent malfunctions. This paper proposes easy-to-implement solutions to address these challenges and enhance PQ in the power distribution network in Bangladesh. This work aims to provide actionable insights to aid policymakers, utility companies, and stakeholders in building a resilient and efficient power distribution system.

title: Research on Multi-Objective Optimization Method of Integrated Energy System Considering Time-of-Use Electricity Price
authors: Yang Liu, Pengtao Li, Hengyu Liu, Jiayang Liu, Sai Sun, Zuoxia Xing
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: To tackle the multi-objective optimization challenge of regional integrated energy systems in alignment with the “Dual Carbon” goals, this paper introduces an adaptive dynamic programming (ADP)-based optimization approach that integrates a time-of-use electricity pricing mechanism. A comprehensive multi-objective optimization model is developed, focusing on maximizing energy utilization efficiency, minimizing carbon emissions, and reducing operational costs. The GRUBERT hybrid neural network is employed to forecast distributed energy outputs and load demands, while a policy iteration-based ADP algorithm is utilized to facilitate collaborative multiobjective optimization. This study examines a typical RIES within a specific region and conducts simulation analyses for representative days during the transition season. The results indicate that the proposed algorithm significantly outperforms traditional particle swarm optimization methods in terms of energy utilization rates, carbon emissions reduction, and operational cost efficiency.

title: State of Health Estimation of Lithium-Ion Batteries Using the Least Squares Method for Accurate Performance Assessment
authors: Smaranika Mishra, Sarat Chandra Swain, Zefree Lazarus Mayaluri
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Lithium-ion (Li-ion) batteries have become increasingly important in many industries due to their high energy density, efficiency, and long cycle life. To ensure safe and reliable operation, Battery Management Systems (BMS) use specialized algorithms to monitor, control, and optimize battery performance. These algorithms are typically classified by their main functions, such as State of Charge (SoC) estimation, which includes methods like Coulomb Counting, Open-Circuit Voltage (OCV), and modelbased techniques. This paper focuses on estimating the State of Health (SoH) of Li-ion cells using the Least Squares Method - a mathematical approach that assesses battery degradation and remaining capacity by fitting experimental data to a predictive model. The goal is to reduce the error between observed battery behavior and model predictions, providing a precise evaluation of SoH. A MATLAB/Simulink-based simulation model has been created to analyze and validate the battery’s performance under different operating conditions.

title: A Deep Quantized Softmax Network-Based Pseudomeasurement Generation Model in Active Distribution Networks
authors: Shahabodin Afrasiabi, Sarah Allahmoradi, Xiaodong Liang, Junbo Zhao, C.Y. Chung
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Pseudo-measurements are artificially generated estimates derived from historical data to represent power system variables that are not directly measured in real-time. In active distribution networks (ADNs), where the limited sensor coverage and high uncertainty from distributed energy resources (DERs) exist, pseudo-measurements are essential for ensuring the system observability. This paper presents a deep learning model for the pseudo-measurement generation in active distribution networks. The model uses a Quantized SoftMax Network (QSN) to estimate conditional probability distribution functions (CPDFs) without assuming any predefined statistical model. To capture spatial and temporal patterns, the QSN is combined with convolutional neural networks (CNNs), gated recurrent units (GRUs), and an attention mechanism. The proposed model is tested on the IEEE 123-bus system using real residential load and renewable generation data. Results show that the proposed model provides more accurate and reliable pseudo-measurements compared to existing conditional kernel density estimator (CKDE) and deep mixture density network (DMDN)-based models.

title: Explainable AI for Chemotherapy Response: A SHAP-Integrated Random Forest Approach
authors: B. Sathiyaprasad, R. Vanathi, G. Uma
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: In our work, we propose an explainable AI framework to predict the response to chemotherapy drugs using gene-expression profiles. Our system employs Random Forest regression to simulate the response of 50 anticancer drugs across approximately 2,000 genes alongside SHAP based explanations of the gene-level effects. Using Dactolisib (928 samples) as a representative example, this study's model illustrates an MSE of 1.0406, with an MAE of 0.8049 and R2 of 0.2108. These results outperform Ridge Regression and XGBoost under 5-fold crossvalidation. The findings of the SHAP analysis are biologically meaningful, especially genes relevant to PI3K/mTOR signaling. We combine the model along with a Gradio interface for prediction and explainability in real-time. The framework shows that the model for drug-response is accurate, transparent, and scalable for precision oncology.

title: A Three-Port DC-DC Converter-Based EV Charger for Light-Duty Electric Vehicles
authors: Sameer Ahmed Khan Mojlish, Amal C Sunny, Kashem M. Muttaqi, Danny Sutanto
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The increasing adoption of Electric Vehicles (EVs) necessitates efficient and cost-effective charging solutions to support the transition from conventional fossil-fueled vehicles to environmentally friendly EVs. This paper proposes a charging infrastructure for simultaneously charging two-light-duty Electric Vehicles (EVs)-based on a novel Three-Port DC-DC Converter (TPC). The proposed charging scheme consists of two stages, where the first stage is a front-end rectifier connected to a single-phase grid (230V, 50Hz), and the second stage is the three-port converter with two 48 V output ports. The highfrequency transformer integrated in the TPC facilitates the isolation between the input and output ports. Unlike the conventional DC fast charging stations, where individual EV charging ports need dedicated DC-DC converters, the proposed solution can charge two light-duty EVs simultaneously using a single DCDC converter, thus offering a cost-effective solution with fewer semiconductor switches and eliminating the need for multiple DC-DC converters. The converter operation and waveforms are presented along with the control technique, where a combination of phase shift and duty cycle control is deployed to regulate two EV ports. The proposed charging solution's performance is validated using a detailed simulation in the MATLAB/Simulink platform.

title: A Performance and Applicability Assessment of Consensus Algorithms for Modern Blockchain Architectures
authors: G Sreenivasulu, Bathula Siva Nageswara Rao, Cettymalla Rama Krishna, Srikanth Lukka, Lakshmeelavanya Alluri, V T Ram Pavan Kumar M
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive comparative research of the significant blockchain consensus algorithms, such as Proof of Work, Proof of Stake, Delegated Proof of Stake, Practical Byzantine Fault Tolerance, Proof of Authority, and hybrid mechanisms, and the purpose of it is to assess the performance, safety and applicability of blockchain to the modern use of blockchain. The studies analyze the key metrics, including throughput, latency, resource consumption, finality behavior, and fault tolerance in various network conditions based on a quantitative, simulation-based methodology with the help of secondary datasets. The results indicate that there are high levels of performance differences among consensus algorithms, as permissioned and delegated algorithms show better efficiency, and low latency, whereas public mechanisms put more emphasis on decentralization, disregarding speed and energy efficiency. The paper identifies the trade-offs inherent to consensus design and points out that no one mechanism is optimal, instead it needs to be chosen based on applicationspecific factors to do with scalability, trust, security, and decentralization. These insights help gain better insight into the issue of consensus behavior and make future choices regarding building the blockchain system.

title: Hybrid Transformer-Driven Healthcare: BERT—T5 Model for Precision Diagnosis and Adaptive Care Recommendations
authors: S. P. Santhoshkumar, P. Satish Reddy, A. Geetha, S. Jyothi, J. H. Jaseema Yasmin, Gajalakshmi S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The categorization of sophisticated transformer architectures have written fresh doors for intelligent clinical decision support, specifically for disease analysis as well as personalized treatment plan. Although Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) provides classification of complex medical text with high precision and good contextual understanding, it does not provide the type of generative abilities required to devise a treatment recommendation specific to individual patients. On the other hand, the Text-to-Text Transfer Transformer (T5) is very good at natural language generation, but it is vulnerable to hallucinations, which limits its use in healthcare, where it is critical to know what the drug will do. To overcome these limitations, this paper proposes a novel approach Hybrid Transformer-Driven Healthcare, which is a unified BERT-T5 architecture that is dedicated to achieving the goal of offering precision diagnosis and adaptable care recommendations. The proposed hybrid system's T5 is used to generate logical and context-aware treatment recommendations and BERT to accurately classify symptoms and diseases. It also incorporates the use of a controlled form of decoding in order to reduce the number of hallucinations. The model is better than standalone transformer baselines in experimental evaluations on a multimodal clinical dataset with 94% accuracy in diagnostic classification and 92% accuracy in treatment generation with a hallucinations rate reduced to 3%. These reflect the capacity of transformer fusion to enhance reliability, constancy and clinical utility in computerized healthcare systems. The proposed framework is a major jump in AI-Assisted Clinical Decision Making, and offers scalable and robust architecture for the next-generation personalized health care applications.

title: A Novel Framework for Intrusion Recognition in Optimal Linear Kalman Filter Based FASE
authors: S. Kundu, Ark Dev, J. Banerjee, S. Gupta, B.K. Saha Roy, S.S. Thakur
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Power system state estimation serves as an essential component in modern energy management system operations. Accurate estimation of states serves as a fundamental prerequisite for all subsequent operations. However, attackers can inject malicious data into the measurements to instigate false data injection attack (FDIA) which eventually degrades the estimation accuracy of the state variables. This paper proposes a novel framework, which incorporates both predicted and filtered state variables determined through a forecasting-aided state estimation (FASE) technique employing the optimal linear Kalman filter, to detect FDIA. Furthermore, the deviation of predicted and estimated states is further determined through a novel detection index. In case of attack, the detection index will exhibit higher value in comparison to normal condition. The suitability of the suggested real time detection strategy is ascertained by implementing it on IEEE test network of 14 and 118 bus.

title: Dual Band Microstrip Patch Antenna Using Compact Structural Tuning for Smart Wireless Applications
authors: D. M. Kumbhar, S. S. Ittannavar, Roopalata S Hosamani, Ravi Hosamani, Bhavaling Alias Pratima Khot, A B Arabhanvi
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: This paper presents the comprehensive design, simulation, and performance analysis of a compact dual-band Microstrip patch antenna using capacitive coupling techniques. The antenna is designed to efficiently operate at 2.4 GHz and 5.2 GHz, making it highly suitable for modern wireless systems such as WLAN, Bluetooth, and IoT devices. To enable dual-frequency operation within a compact footprint, the antenna integrates a U-shaped slot in the patch and implements a defected ground structure (DGS) on the substrate's underside. Capacitive coupling is employed to enhance impedance matching and minimize return loss. The antenna is simulated using Ansys HFSS, and a prototype is fabricated to validate simulation results. Key performance parameters including return loss (S11), VSWR, gain, efficiency, radiation pattern, and current distribution are analyzed. The proposed antenna exhibits return loss better than –20 dB at both frequency bands, with VSWR values below 1.2, gain above 5.5 dBi, and radiation efficiency exceeding 85%. Surface current analysis confirms dual-mode excitation, validating the design’s frequency agility. These results confirm that the antenna is a reliable, cost-effective solution for next-generation wireless devices requiring dual-band operation.

title: FaceTrace: Leveraging Motion Patterns for Deepfake Video Authentication
authors: Ramya RS, Karuna Narayankar
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: This paper presents a deep learning system for deepfake video detection based on a hybrid spatio-temporal model that integrates ResNeXt-50 and Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. Spatial features are extracted from face regions by a pretrained ResNeXt-50 CNN, while temporal evolution between video frames is modelled by LSTM layers. The system features a web-based interface built with Flask to enable the user to upload videos and dynamically manage the frame sampling rate. The system analyses input videos by cropping and detecting faces, extracting frames, and classifying content as Real or Fake based on temporal face inconsistencies. Prediction confidence scores are produced, and outputs are visualized in frame-wise bounding boxes and heatmaps. Experimental results demonstrate the model's high accuracy of 98.5% and confidence for real-time deepfake detection. This approach demonstrates the effectiveness of combining convolutional and recurrent neural networks for tackling video-based forgery detection tasks.

title: Adaptive AI-KM Readiness in India’s Defense Ecosystem: A Multidimensional Benchmark and Roadmap
authors: A. Sharma, S. Sidharth
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: India’s defense agencies are exploring AI-driven Knowledge Management (KM) to accelerate indigenisation. However, organizational preparedness across stakeholders remains uncharted. This study benchmarks AI-KM readiness in tri-services, DRDO, DPSUs, academia, and industry, pinpointing gaps and crafting an actionable roadmap. A stratified mixed-methods design was deployed with 50 participants (10 per stakeholder group). A validated five-dimension instrument (Leadership, Human Expertise, Infrastructure, Data Governance, Culture) guided a Likert survey and thematic interviews. Quantitative analysis featured ANOVA, t-tests, and effect sizes; qualitative insights were extracted via dual-coder thematic analysis (κ = 0.82). Infrastructure leads (M = 3.13, SD = 0.44), while leadership lags (M = 2.89, SD = 0.39). Significant group disparities appear (F = 5.65–20.05, p < 0.001), with academia and industry outperforming defense bodies. A striking Human Expertise gap (Academia vs Tri-services: t = 6.30, d = 2.82) underscores critical skill deficits. Five core constraints emerged: strategic–operational misalignment, antiquated systems, security–sharing tensions, siloed operations, and training shortfalls. We recommend a phased roadmap—immediate capability sprints, mid-term infrastructure and governance reforms, and long-range culture transformation—to galvanize India’s AI-KM indigenisation agenda.

title: Enhanced Paddy Yield Prediction Utilizing a Hybrid Convolution Transform Framework Integrated with Soil-Weather Interaction Analysis for Precision Agriculture
authors: L. Febin Rani, Devi. T, N. Deepa
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: India is the world's second largest producer of rice, contributing about 20 % to global rice production. Global food security and related health issues require accurate, timely, and reliable paddy yield predictions in India. The existing approaches have two drawbacks, despite their ability to predict crop yields, namely inability to establish direct linear or nonlinear mapping among raw data to crop yield values and strong reliance on quality of extracted features. In this manuscript, Enhanced Paddy Yield Forecasting under Hybrid Convolution Transform Framework Integrated with Soil Weather Interaction Analysis Precision Agriculture (EPYP-HCTF-SWIPA) is proposed. Initially, paddy seed data are collected from Quantum Paddy Seed Selection India dataset. The collected data are pre-processed using Adaptive Tracking Dual Nested Kalman Filter (ATDNKF) to handle missing values and normalize dataset. From the pre-processed data, the features like Variety, State, Soil Type, Sand Percent, Silt Percent, Clay Percent, Soil pH, Nitrogen, Phosphorus, Potassium, Rainfall-mm, Temp-C and Yield-t-per-ha are extracted using Newton Time Extracting Wavelet Transform (NTEWT). The extracted features are given into Hybrid Convolution Transform Framework, where a Binarized Simplicial Convolutional Neural Network with Vicinity Vision Transformer (BSCNN-VViT) predicts paddy yield by combining data from each growth period. To enhance model performance, the Warthog Optimization Algorithm (WOA) is used to optimize the weight parameters of BSCNN-VViT, resulting in more accurate predictions and reduced error rate. The proposed EPYP-HCTF-SWIPA model is evaluated based on accuracy, precision and error rate, achieving 99.56% accuracy, outperforming existing models. Its performance is superior when compared to models like Advanced Prediction of Rice Yield Gaps under Climate Uncertainty under ML Techniques in Eastern India (APRY-CML-EI), Harnessing Multi Source Data and Machine Learning (ML) for Enhanced Rice Yield Estimation (HMSD-MLRYE), Deep Learning Enhanced Remote Sensing Integrated Crop Modelling for Rice Yield Prediction (DLERS-RYP).

title: Development of a Geospatial Strategy in Identifying the Optimal Electric Vehicle Charging Stations in Colombo, Sri Lanka
authors: Surath Gajanayake, Qingwu Zhao, Guohong Tian
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The rapid motorization has alarmed the scientific sector to perform more research inclined towards the sustainability aspect of the transportation sector. This study is a sub-part of the main study which focusses on the development of electrification model for the internal combustion engine (ICE) powered three-wheelers in Sri Lanka. This specific study focusses on the development of a scientific model for the identification of the optimal electric three-wheeler charging stations within Colombo district, Sri Lanka. In performing the research, data were acquired from 35 conventional ICE-powered three-wheelers based in Colombo, Sri Lanka. Data were collected throughout a continuous period of 1 month and over 3.5 million data points pertaining to the spatial data were collected. The acquired data is then cleansed and filtered to perform the algorithmic manipulations and K-means clustering was opted. The GoogleColab development environment was utilized in performing the algorithms on the collected data and the output was visualized using the GIS tools in terms of heatmaps and density maps. Finally, 12 optimal and feasible charging stations have been defined as a key deliverable of the study

title: VeloxGNN: Efficient Out-of-Core GNN Training with Delayed Gradient Propagation
authors: Yi Li, Tsun-Yu Yang, Zhaoyan Shen, Ming-Chang Yang, Bingzhe Li
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Training Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) on large-scale data is essential in various applications, e.g., transportation, and molecular biology. As graph sizes increasingly surpass main memory capacities, the out-of-core (OOC) GNN training system (OOC-based) has been proposed, a scheme that storing graphs on external storage, such as SSD or HDD, sequentially loading and processing smaller partitions. However, existing OOC-based systems face the key challenges: excessive data migration between storage and memory, as well as reduced model accuracy. In this paper, we theoretically and empirically analyze the limitations of state-of-the-art OOC-based systems and identify opportunities for optimization. Guided by our theoretical insights, we propose VeloxGNN, a novel system to improve data migration efficiency while maintaining high model accuracy. First, we introduce a novel algorithm, named Delayed Gradient Propagation (DGP), which specifically designed for OOC-based system. DGP leverages both historical node embeddings and unbiased gradients to achieve two key objectives simultaneously: minimizing the data migration (by ensuring that dataset is read at most once) while maintaining model accuracy. To support DGP, we then propose system-level optimizations: dynamic memory management, DGP-aware loading order, and a new graph partitioning method that separates labeled and unlabeled data. Experimental results show that VeloxGNN achieves memory-based accuracy while reducing training time by 17.7% to 73.3% across various datasets and GNN models, outperforming state-of-the-art methods. This result highlights VeloxGNN's potential for efficient and scalable GNN training on large-scale graph data.

title: DSAssassin: Cross-VM Side-Channel Attacks by Exploiting Intel Data Streaming Accelerator
authors: Ben Chen, Kunlin Li, Shuwen Deng, Dongsheng Wang, Yun Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Modern datacenter infrastructures increasingly integrate bespoke accelerators to offload specific workloads from CPUs. Among them, Intel's Data Streaming Accelerator (DSA), supported by Intel VT-d, is deployed in the latest Xeon processors. However, its security implications remain unexplored. In this paper, we propose DSAssassin, a novel side-channel attack launched from DSA. Although Intel's scalable I/O virtualization mitigates device-based threats, we show that DSA introduces a new attack surface that bypasses such protections. By reverse-engineering the Device TLB (DevTLB) introduced by DSA, we find that it is not isolated by different processes. We further analyze DSA's shared work queue (SWQ) and uncover a timer-free side channel via DMWr transaction. Building on these insights, we develop two attack primitives: a DevTLB-based timing attack and an SWQ-based contention attack. These enable cross-VM covert- and side-channel attacks. First, the cross-VM covert-channel can achieve a true capability of 17.19 Kbps with a 4.64% error rate—5× faster and 4× more accurate than the latest work. Second, we present a website fingerprinting attack on the top 100 websites, and the classification accuracy can reach 85.7%. Finally, we demonstrate a cross-VM keystroke inference attack with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$F_{1}$</tex> scores of 92.0% (DevTLB) and 98.4% (SWQ)—outperforming the state-of-the-art.

title: State of Walk-Through Weapons Detectors for Public Screening
authors: Nikita Ermolaev, Mert Karakaya
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: Walk-through weapons detectors are increasingly deployed in public venues to mitigate weapon-related threats. This paper presents a comparative analysis of four primary detection technologies: active magnetic, passive magnetic, active imaging, and passive imaging. We describe their underlying operating principles, assess their strengths and limitations, and evaluate deployment considerations. The paper highlights trade-offs in detection performance, throughput, and false alarm rates, offering guidance on technology selection for specific operational environments.

title: Day-Ahead Scheduling Method of Offshore Wind Power System Considering Typhoon Uncertainty
authors: Lujie Liu, Guoyou Shan, Lingling Huang, Shurong Wei, Xiaolin Ge, Xiangjing Su, Yang Liu, Zixu Ren, Peipei Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Over the past decade, typhoons have emerged as a primary natural hazard threatening the secure operation of power grids in coastal urban areas. Addressing the day-ahead dispatch challenge for power systems that integrate offshore wind generation, this paper presents a multi-scenario stochastic programming framework that explicitly incorporates typhoon-related uncertainties. Firstly, considering typhoon uncertainty, a typhoon scene set is constructed using the typhoon path, maximum wind speed error model, and probability circle. On this basis, a system loss risk cost model and an offshore wind power output uncertainty set under typhoon uncertainty are proposed. Then, the optimal scheduling model of power system with offshore wind power based on multi-scenario stochastic programming is constructed. With the goal of minimizing the total cost of load loss risk cost, scheduling cost and wind power operation cost under the influence of typhoon, solve the model to get day-ahead scheduling case. Finally, simulations are performed on a modified IEEE 39-bus example. The outcomes demonstrate that the presented method can more accurately reflect the actual uncertainty of typhoons, lessen the uncertainty and conservatism of day - ahead scheduling based on a single typhoon forecast situation, and demonstrate the validity of the proposed method and model.

title: Half-Bridge Configured 7L Inverter Topology with Triple Voltage Gain and Equal Voltage Rating of Components
authors: Marif Daula Siddique, Pooyan Alinaghi Hosseinabadi, Mehdi Seyedmahmoudian, Saad Mekhilef, Michael Negnevitsky, Alex Stojcevski
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper introduces a seven-level inverter design characterized by integrated voltage enhancement and fault tolerance. The proposed design utilizes a single DC input source, 12 power switches, four diodes, and two capacitors to achieve a voltage gain of three, while avoiding the use of large magnetic components such as transformers or inductors. This is accomplished through the implementation of strategic switching patterns and the management of capacitor charging and discharging sequences, facilitating the generation of seven distinct output voltage levels. This configuration improves power conversion efficiency and reduces the number of components, resulting in a more compact system. Fault analysis reveals that, 8 out of 12 events of a single open-circuit fault in the switch, the inverter can maintain at least 67% of its rated output voltage, thereby enhancing system reliability. Extensive simulation studies have been conducted using PLECS for single-phase systems under various operational conditions. The system employed phase disposition and nearest level control pulse width modulation (PWM) at a fundamental frequency of 50 kHz, effectively demonstrating its performance in terms of voltage quality and voltage gain.

title: Data-Enabled Optimal Tracking (DeeOT) Control Approach for Grid-Connected Inverters
authors: Said Al-Abri, Myada Shadoul, Hassan Yousef
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Recent advancements in data-driven control approaches provide effective solutions, especially when physical models are unavailable or when the system is posed to significant model uncertainties. In this paper, we present a Data-Enabled Optimal Tracking (DeeOT) algorithm for a Grid-Connected Inverter System (GCIS). The DeeOT relies on offline data obtained from a SIMULINK model of the GCIS system with random inputs to obtain an optimal tracking policy for the inverter electrical currents. In particular, in this paper, we extended the existing Data-Enabled Policy Optimization (DeePO) algorithm by adding an integral term to account for the tracking objective. The performance of the proposed method is analyzed via MATLAB/SIMULINK experiments under different conditions. The results suggest the efficiency of the proposed optimal data-driven tracking algorithm under various grid conditions.

title: A Compact Common Grounded 9-Level Switched Capacitor Multilevel Inverter for Grid Integration
authors: Ribhu Chakraborty, Tapas Roy
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: This work introduces a single phase nine-level common-grounded switched-capacitor multilevel inverter (CGSCMLI) intended for grid-connected operation. The configuration uses a single DC supply along with fewer switching devices, while the common-ground arrangement helps suppress leakage currents that usually arise in transformerless inverters. By incorporating a switched-capacitor stage, the circuit achieves voltage boosting directly within the inverter, removing the need for a separate DC-DC conversion stage. The capacitor voltages are maintained in balance naturally, without relying on sensors or extra control hardware. To manage the interaction of the grid, a proportional resonant (PR) controller is employed, which shapes grid current and provides reactive power compensation under different conditions of the power factor. A 2-kW proposed inverter is modeled in MATLAB/Simulink. The simulation results confirm that the inverter delivers a boosted nine-level output voltage with well-balanced capacitor voltages and a better harmonic spectrum. The results also demonstrate stable current injection to the grid under dynamic reference current change conditions, validating the practicality of the proposed inverter.

title: Early Prediction of Student Dropout in Higher Education Using Machine Learning Models
authors: Sarthak Dahiya, Aryan Singh, Ram Kishan Dewangan, Himanshu Suyal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Dropout in higher education remains one of the concerns that cut across both the students and the institutions involved. The ability to identify at-risk students early on can allow for intervention, but traditional models often rely only on pre-entry data. This study extends existing dropout prediction methodologies by integrating Learning Management System (LMS) data into demographic and academic performance data. We present a new studentship metric that aggregates measures of both cognitive and social engagement from LMS logs to enhance dropout prediction in early stages. We use the XGBoost and Neural Networks machine learning models to analyze data from 8,267 student records across multiple disciplines. Results indicate that while academic performance is a strong predictor at later stages, incorporating LMS-based studentship data enhances the accuracy of early-semester predictions. Moreover, predictor importance varies across fields; academic performance dominates in STEM, while engagement metrics are more significant in social sciences. This approach provides actionable insights for dropout prevention through early intervention.

title: DALA-XGBoost: An AI-ML Framework for Cross-Domain Maternal-Fetal Risk Assessment
authors: Seelwant Kaur, Jashanpreet Kaur, Urvashi Urvashi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: In the delicate balance of life before birth, even small maternal health issues can pose serious risks to the fetus. Recent reports indicate that over 260,000 women lost their lives in 2023 due to preventable pregnancy complications, which indicates the urgent need for proactive and automated methods to detect pregnancy risks early. The lack of clinically annotated maternal data sets, along with the problem of integrating maternal and fetal health records, limits the effectiveness of existing prediction tools. To address this, we introduce a new cross-domain classification framework that combines Dual Autoencoder Latent Alignment (DALA) with Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) classifier. In this approach, separate autoencoders are trained independently on the maternal and fetal datasets to learn domain- specific latent representations, and then aligned in a common space through unsupervised learning. Labeled fetal embeddings are used to pseudo-label unlabeled maternal data through distance clustering, thereby effectively transferring risk knowledge across domains. These maternal embeddings are then used to train an XGBoost classifier to predict the fetal risk categories Normal, Suspect, and Pathological exclusively based on maternal - indicators. The model obtained an overall accuracy of 95.8% and F1 scores of 98%, 89%, and 91% for the three risk classes. SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) are used to highlight the key maternal features that influence predictions, Principal Component Analysis (PCA) validates the latent alignment, and Local Interpre table Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME) explain the individual pathological predictions for clinical transparency. In summary, this research offers a scalable and potent solution for detecting fetal risks using only maternal data, thus enabling early intervention in low-resource environments where fetal monitoring is unavailable.

title: Fast Output Sampling Based Sliding Mode Controller with Disturbance Observer for Buck Converter
authors: Jagannath Samantaray, Rudranarayan Pradhan, Mukul Choudhury
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: This paper presents a novel fast output sampling (FOS)-based discrete-time sliding mode control (DTSMC) approach, augmented with a disturbance observer (DO), aimed at enhancing the robustness. The control strategy leverages the multirate output feedback (MROF) technique—an output feedback mechanism—to reduce reliance on state measurements, thereby simplifying implementation and lowering system cost. A new disturbance observer is proposed and integrated into the DTSMC framework, further improving robustness against external disturbances and model uncertainties. Simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed method, showing significant enhancements in both robustness and control efficiency for buck converter applications. This work contributes to the development of more reliable and cost-effective control strategies for power electronic systems.

title: HELPR: Helmet Enforcement and License Plate Reconnaissance Using YOLOv8 and EasyOCR for Real-Time Traffic Surveillance
authors: Kumar Manthri, S Sai Samay, T N Chandra Sahith, Anjali T
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Traffic accidents that result in death of people are mainly caused by motorcycle accidents, especially in those areas where wearing of helmets is not a mandatory practice. To solve the problem of manual surveillance by the traffic authorities, this paper suggests an automated, real-time possession to identify the helmet violation and motorcycle number plates on the roadside surveillance streams. The framework incorporates the YOLOv8 object detector model to localize riders, helmets, and number plates, and uses the EasyOCR to do strong optical character recognition on plate regions extracted under a variety of lighting and traffic conditions. Experimental results on controlled datasets and on real-world video footage show that the system can be characterized by a high detection rate, achieving 93% accuracy in helmet detection, 96% accuracy in rider detection, and 88% accuracy in number plate detection, whereas EasyOCR gets a maximum of 96% accuracy in character-level OCR. a highquality text extraction rate, and a high generalization ability to a variety of complex traffic scenes, which means that the system has an opportunity to provide scalable, data-driven traffic enforcement within the smart city environment.

title: Design and Development of Real-Time Microgrid Control Simulation with IEC 61850-Enabled Substation Automation System
authors: Mohammad Hasanuzzaman Shawon, Arindam Ghosh, Ehsan Pashajavid, Murilo S. Baptista
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper presents the development and implementation of a multi-agent system (MAS)-based microgrid (MG) control strategy, integrated with an IEC <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{6 1 8 5 0}$</tex>-compliant power system on the OPAL-RT platform. An overview of real-time simulation methodologies, namely Software-in-the-Loop (SIL), Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL), and Rapid Control Prototyping (RCP), is first provided to contextualise the work. A detailed MG model is then developed, and the validation of its control strategy is carried out using the SIL approach on an OPAL-RT system. Within the IEC 61850 framework, the simulation incorporates the architecture and communication protocols through a systematic modelling approach on the OPAL-RT platform. Various data exchange mechanisms at the process bus and bay bus levels are examined to verify protocol functionality. The outcomes of this research demonstrate the practical feasibility and interoperability of IEC 61850-based communication protocols in MAS-enabled microgrid interconnection control, offering a validated framework that can be directly leveraged to advance resilient, standard-compliant smart grid operations

title: Intelligent Question Paper Generation System for Academic Assessments
authors: P. Chinnasamy, Harrish M, HarishKumar T, Hari Sudhan S, Selva Mathan M
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The manual preparation of question papers is a time-consuming and difficult task for the teachers, as it involves lots of efforts on the part of the teachers to ensure that proper coverage of the syllabus, proper level of cognitive difficulty, and proper preparation of answer keys. The paper will briefly describe, implement and evaluate AIScribe, an intelligent web based product that automatically generates the question papers and key answers using Artificial Intelligence (AI).The system provides a structured workflow where authorized staff can upload a syllabus document, configure examination parameters (type and difficulty distribution and receive a complete, formatted question paper alongside an answer key containing essential evaluation keywords. The core intelligence is driven by the integration of Large Language Model(LLM) API, specifically the google Gemini API , which is guided by meticulously engineered prompts to generate pedagogically relevant content. The system shows the significant reduction in the time to create a set of question papers from several hours (manual) to less than two minutes automatically. While the need for expert review to ensure factual accuracy does exist, AIScribe appears to be a very helpful aid for standardizing assessment creation, minimizing faculty workload, and increasing efficiency within the academy. The paper ends with a discussion of limitations and suggestions for future improvements, which include having multiple choice questions and working with Bloom's Taxonomy for better difficulty calibration.

title: Reconfigurable CLLC Converter Design and Optimization for Wide-Voltage Battery Charging from Universal Grid–Derived High-Voltage DC Link
authors: Nguyen Tan Tung, Man-Hay Pong, Huang-Jen Chiu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The CLLC resonant converter is widely employed in battery charging applications due to its high efficiency, bidirectional capability, and soft-switching operation. Modern electric vehicle (EV) chargers are increasingly required to operate flexibly, adapting to various AC grid levels and a broad range of battery voltages. In a universal three-phase AC system, with line-to-line voltages ranging from 342 V to 528 V, the resulting DC-link voltage remains within a narrow but elevated range. This characteristic poses unique challenges to CLLC converter design, particularly in meeting gain and efficiency requirements across the entire operating envelope. This paper presents a systematic design and optimization methodology that integrates the flexibility of Python-based automated constraint verification with the simulation capabilities of the SIMBA platform for performance evaluation. The proposed approach is validated through the development of a 10 kW CLLC prototype, achieving a peak efficiency of 98.26%, thereby demonstrating the effectiveness of the design and optimization process.

title: SimuCare: Intelligent Health and Performance Simulation
authors: Vikas Kamra, Aryan Negi, Rishabh, Devanshu
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: This paper introduces SimuCare which is a simulation project that we are developing will utilize a 2D visual world to provide customized health advice. It is not merely a monitoring tool, but it is a way of creating space as a dynamic simulator of what-if situations. The system can model and predict performance and health complex health and performance paths by digital twin generation of an athlete using live data. SimuCare is an amalgamation of large language models, quantitative predictive models and a GraphRAG knowledge base to provide transparent and explainable decisions. Based on the Actor-Network Theory, the framework displays the interconnectivity among the human users along with the non-human system components. An athlete health use case is where we have begun but our engine is domain independent, hence it may be applied in anything including finance, logistics or any other area. In this paper, we describe our original design that will be the foundation of truly intelligent, adaptive, and predictive digital technology.

title: Modeling of 4H-SiC MESFET by Including Thermal Effect, Surface and Substrate Trappings Using Botox Optimization Method
authors: Nagalakshmi Yarlagadda, Satti Sudha Mohan Reddy, N. Karthikeyan, M. Sai Veerraju, S. Senthilkumar, G. B. Santhi, Sampada Viraj Dravid, Ajay Sudhir Bale, Kandi Bhanu Prakash
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: MEtal Semiconductor Field Effect Transistors (MESFETs) of $\mathbf{4 H}-\mathbf{S i C}$ configurations are used for many applications in our daily life. The model of MESFETS is very complex to analyze under direct current characteristics. Three primary effects: surface trapping, thermal effects, and substrate trapping, to explain the direct current characteristics of the MESFET are considered in this paper. The presented model of I-V characteristics incorporates the Caughey-Thomas configuration, which accounts for trapping of substrate electron through various deep-level trapping methods, field-dependent electron mobility, and provides an analysis of the twodimensional distribution of charge beneath the gate. This suggested approach is thorough, taking into consideration all major thermal impacts and material imperfections, such as traps. However, it is very difficult to solve complex mathematical equations of the $\mathbf{4 H}$-SiC MESFET to obtain accurate I-V characteristics under different regions due to its complexity. Hence, Botox Optimization Algorithm (BOA) is utilized to solve various complex equations of $\mathbf{4 H}$-SiC MESFET to obtain I-V characteristics accurately. As a result, the performance of the proposed analytical model is very similar to that of a real-time $\mathbf{4 H}$-SiC MESFET. Different behavioral traits are acquired and assessed through the use of the MATLAB programming package.

title: Challenges and Mitigation Technique in Overcurrent Relay Coordination for Low Voltage Radial Systems
authors: Megha Jaiswal, Shanker D. Godwal, Akhilesh A. Nimje, Sarika S. Kanojia, Siddharth Mehta, Maitry Patel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Overcurrent protection in low voltage and radial distribution systems often faces challenges, particularly in ensuring proper coordination between primary and backup relays. One significant issue is the miscoordination that arises despite appropriate selection of plug settings for both relays. This miscoordination is primarily due to the uncertain pickup range inherent in overcurrent relays. In this paper, this problem is thoroughly investigated. A solution is proposed to address the issue of uncertain pickup ranges in overcurrent relays. To evaluate the proposed approach and validate, one radial feeder is considered. Further, three various relay characteristic of over current relays, normal inverse, very inverse and extremely inverse have been considered. Furthermore, Harmonic Search Algorithm, Genetic Algorithm, and electromagnetic field optimization are employed to achieve the optimal settings for both plug setting multipliers (PSMs) and time dial settings (TDSs), ensuring reliable relay coordination.

title: Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Models to Identify Skin Cancer
authors: Divya Yadav
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
