title: An Explainable Federated Learning Framework for Privacy-Preserving Medical Diagnosis on Edge Devices
authors: Parasdeep Parasdeep, Mukesh Kumar Verma, Simarpreet Singh, Dharmesha Upadhaya, Ayushi Ayushi, Shilpa Mahajan
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The incorporation of artificial intelligence (AI) into healthcare diagnostics has raised critical issues of model interpretability, patient data protection, and deployment feasibility on resource-constrained devices. Clinical decision-making requires transparency and data confidentiality, both of which are frequently compromised by traditional centralized machine learning techniques. This paper suggests a novel explainable federated learning (FL) framework designed for edge-based medical diagnostics in order to address these issues. The system allows for decentralized model training across institutions without exchanging raw patient data by embedding lightweight explainable AI (XAI) modules, specifically SHAP and LIME, at each client node. These modules enhance clinical trust by providing patient-specific, easily comprehensible explanations. Among our contributions are: (i) designing a privacy-preserving training protocol that combines differential privacy and secure aggregation; (ii) optimizing lightweight deep learning models for low-power edge environments; and (iii) introducing a quantitative Explainability Index (EI) to evaluate interpretability in federated settings. When tested on benchmark datasets (the ECG5000 and brain MRI scans), the framework demonstrates high classification accuracy while maintaining interpretability and privacy. By bridging important gaps between privacy, performance, and transparency, this work provides a reliable and scalable AI solution for healthcare systems of the future. Additionally, the results pave the way for multimodal data fusion and federated transfer learning, which will enhance real-world deployment and increase clinical utility.

title: Cross-Dataset Transferability Analysis of Neural Networks for Battery SOH Prediction Using Synthetic and Experimental Data
authors: Alexander Popp, Utz Spaeth, Sahar Qaadan, Heiko Fechtner, Benedikt Schmuelling
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This study investigates the effectiveness of artificial neural networks for lithium-ion battery State of Health estimation using both synthetic and experimental datasets. The research evaluates model performance across different datasets and examines the transferability of models trained on synthetic data to real-world experimental conditions. Results demonstrate excellent predictive accuracy for models trained and tested on matching datasets, with synthetic models achieving RMSE values around 0.001 and experimental models reaching RMSE of 0.023. However, significant limitations emerge in cross-dataset transferability, with synthetic models showing poor performance on experimental data (RMSE: 4.965). The findings highlight the critical need for hybrid modeling approaches that effectively combine synthetic and experimental data to bridge the gap between physics-based simulations and real-world battery behavior in practical applications.

title: Data-Driven HRM: Using AI to Improve Strategic Workforce Allocation in the Healthcare Industry
authors: Ghousia Jabeen, Rajyalaxmi M
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The healthcare sector is measuring the work issues such as staff shortage high employee turnover and disconnected between the availability of patient and person needed these challenges affect the quality of healthcare services add to operational stress. This study analysis Artificial intelligence and data analytics are revolution human resource management by improving workers planning and affection. Machine learning natural language processing protective analytics facilitate more precious recruitment, performance, evaluation and employer attention strategy in health sector .Technology can anticipated staffing recruitment project turnover rate and announcing scheduling ultimately reduce burnout and boosting workforce productivity in the healthcare field. AI role in workforce allocation ensure that staffing level match changing patient number particularly during emerging like pandemic this paper aim is to explore artificial intelligence announcing workforce management in healthcare HRM discussing advantages challenges and opportunities while highlighted ethical consideration is application.

title: Integrating Artificial Intelligence with Conventional Yoga for Posture Recognition and Wellness Improvement
authors: Anita Kumari, Deepshikha Tonk, Avinav Pathak, Shikha Chaudhary, Vineet Vishnoi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Incorporating yoga into daily habits enhances overall wellness by increasing physical flexibility, muscular strength, body equilibrium, and emotional resilience. Integrating contemporary artificial intelligence (AI) with this time-honored practice can make it more accessible and flexible for a wider audience. Yoga has evolved as an instrument for physical and psychological improvement of health or simply self-improvement with the final aim of tying the singular consciousness with the universal. Yoga and meditation were advanced as a perfect combination of physico-philosophical rituals during ancient times on Indian land. In its actual sense, yoga means to yoke together the distinct spirits and consciousness. ‘Yoga Sutra,’ a magnificent work of sagacious ‘Patanjali,’ mentioned ‘Ashtangic-yoga,’ which undoubtedly highlighted the main destination of yoga and meditation as to impart the mind mainly through calmness and physical exercises that yoke the physical body with the mind and the spirit. Nowadays, yoga and meditation practices are generally used as therapeutic ingredients, either unaccompanied or as accessories to other therapies, in a wide range of physical and mental illnesses. This research presents an all-encompassing framework for identifying and categorizing yoga postures through the use of machine learning strategies and computer vision methods. At the heart of this endeavor lies the implementation of body posture detection algorithms, utilizing tools such as Media Pipe and Pose Net, along with Open-Pose, to detect essential points on the human body in a solitary image or video frame. This study stands out due to its comprehensive assessment of different traditional classifiers applied to numerous yoga postures. This study emphasizes the promise of associating pose estimation models with ML classifiers to develop cognitive systems that can aid individuals engaged in yoga, representing a groundbreaking advancement in fusing traditional practices with cutting-edge technology.

title: Adaptive Deep Learning Frameworks for Intelligent Fraud Prevention Strategies in Dynamic Digital Ecosystems
authors: Sandeep Yadav, Shubham Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: As digital ecosystems grow in complexity and scale, fraudsters are increasingly leveraging automation, adversarial manipulation, and synthetic identities to evade detection. Traditional fraud prevention systems largely rule-based or dependent on shallow machine learning struggle to keep pace with these evolving threats. In this work, we propose an adaptive deep learning framework for intelligent fraud prevention, capable of ingesting multi-source data, modeling contextual relationships, estimating uncertainty, and continuously learning from new fraud patterns. The framework integrates a hybrid encoder (CNN + BiLSTM), a Graph Neural Network for relational fraud detection, and a Bayesian uncertainty-aware classification head. It also incorporates a continual learning loop to adapt to zero-day fraud strategies. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets such as IEEE-CIS and PaySim demonstrate that our framework outperforms existing methods in terms of accuracy, robustness to adversarial attacks, and inference latency, making it highly suitable for real-world deployment in financial, telecom, and ecommerce platforms.

title: Analysis of Series Reactive Power Compensation Results Under Hybrid Operation of Cross-Linked Polyethylene (XLPE) and Oil-Filled Submarine Cables
authors: Shoushui Han, Liang Zou, Zhiyun Han, Fanbo Meng, Guochang Li, Qingsong Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Submarine cables play an increasingly important role in the current power system as a key part of the trans-regional transportation of electrical energy in the power system. In a specific project, two single-core oil-filled submarine cables were originally running in parallel, and a third XLPE submarine cable was planned to be added in the future. However, due to the damage of the second oil-filled submarine cable in a phase of the cable, the second cable can not be run under regular operating conditions, so it is proposed to put a three-core XLPE submarine cable in the second back, the use of three-core XLPE submarine cable in a core instead of the oil-filled submarine cable in the damage of a phase, the formation of cross-linked polyethylene (XLPE) and oil-filled submarine cables mixed operation of the working conditions, due to the difference in the two kinds of submarine cable parameters, it is necessary to add a series connection in the system. Due to the different parameters of the two cables, it is necessary to add a series reactive power compensation device to the system. After series compensation, it is found that the maximum power delivered under mixed operation is increased and the current-voltage imbalance is improved. An overvoltage analysis of the series-compensated system was required and found to be in compliance with safe operation standards.

title: Hyperlocal Temperature and Humidity Prediction Using Supervised Machine Learning
authors: Lyuboslav Gigov, Narasimha Rao Vajjhala, Anton Stoilov
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Machine learning offers computationally efficient alternatives to traditional numerical weather prediction (NWP) models and global forecasting systems (GFS), which struggle with high computational costs and coarse spatial resolution. This study focuses on hyperlocal weather prediction, comparing linear regression (LR), polynomial regression (PR), decision tree regression (DTR), and random forest regression (RFR) for forecasting average temperature and relative humidity. Using ten years of historical data (2015-2025) from Sofia, Berlin, and Tokyo, we achieved substantial predictive accuracy across climatically diverse regions. For temperature prediction, linear regression and random forest demonstrated statistically equivalent performance (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> > 0.994), with linear regression achieving faster execution times (0.001s vs 0.022-0.040s). For humidity prediction, linear regression demonstrated clear superiority (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> > 0.940) over random forest (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> 0.913-0.933), while decision trees showed the poorest performance (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> 0.741-0.814). Decision trees performed competitively for temperature (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> 0.992-0.993) but struggled significantly with humidity's nonlinear dynamics. These results demonstrate that model selection should prioritize the relationship structure in data rather than algorithmic complexity, with linear regression offering a balance of accuracy and computational efficiency. The findings of this study challenge the assumption that complex ensemble methods are necessary for accurate weather prediction, offering practical applications in precision agriculture, smart city infrastructure, event planning, and resource-constrained IoT deployments where real-time predictions are essential.

title: Efficient Classification of Fish Diseases from Skin Images Using ResNet50 and Transfer Learning
authors: Gotte Ranjith Kumar, Ritu Rani, Sonal Malhotra, Kamlesh Kukreti
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Aquaculture is seriously threatened by fish illnesses, which also cause large financial losses and lower fish output. Effective disease control and sustainable aquaculture management depend on timely, accurate diagnosis of these diseases. Large-scale or real-time monitoring is not suited for conventional diagnostic techniques such visual inspection, histological analysis, and molecular testing since they are often time-consuming, expensive, and dependent on expert intervention. In this work, a deep learning-based algorithm is suggested to use skin pictures to automatically classify fish diseases. Using a publicly accessible Kaggle dataset of 1,750 photos equally distributed among seven classifications Bacterial Red Disease, Aeromonas, Bacterial Gill Disease, Saprolegniasis (fungal), Parasitic Disease, White Tail Disease (viral), and Healthy Fish was used. The backbone model came from pretrained ImageNet ResNet50 convolutional neural network. A custom classification head was included, and a transfer learning method helped to refine the model. Model generalization was enhanced using image pre-processing methods including resizing, normalizing, and augmentation. Accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and confusion matrix were used in evaluation of the model. With a classification accuracy of 97.14%, the proposed ResNet50-based model proved rather successful in spotting fish diseases from skin photos. The outcomes validate the possibility of our deep learning method as a dependable, scalable, real-time tool for automated fish disease diagnostics in aquaculture systems.

title: RLHF-Driven Household Appliance Scheduling with Dynamic Comfort Windows
authors: Alaa Selim, Huadong Mo, Hemanshu Pota, Daoyi Dong
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Residential energy management must coordinate shiftable appliances and behind-the-meter storage under time-varying tariffs and evolving occupant comfort windows. Conventional formulations (e.g., Mixed Integer Linear Programming) depend on precise forecasts and careful manual tuning, limiting robustness to model error and preference drift, while standard Reinforcement learning (RL), though adaptive, is hampered by reward misclassifications and sample inefficiency. We present an end-to-end reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) framework that warm-starts from expert schedules via supervised fine-tuning, learns a preference-aligned reward model from trajectory comparisons to capture cost-comfort-battery trade-offs, and employs potential-based shaping within proximal policy optimization to stabilize and accelerate learning. Evaluated on realistic tariff scenarios with shifting comfort windows, the learned policy yields operationally feasible schedules that preserve comfort, reduce operating costs, create export opportunities, and temper battery cycling while meeting practical runtime constraints; it also converges rapidly and generalizes across preference changes and tariff realizations, indicating resilience to model-plant mismatch and distributional shift. Empirically, our RLHF-trained agent outperforms a strong PPO baseline by over 60% in cost-adjusted reward, while achieving full comfort satisfaction and zero unmet runtime. By unifying prescriptive optimization with RLHF, the framework advances user-centric residential energy control and provides a reproducible path to robust, preference-aware scheduling under uncertainty.

title: A High Gain Restructured DC-DC Quadratic Boost Converter (RDQBC) Applicable for Fuel Cell
authors: Subrat Behera, Ranjeeta Patel, Byamakesh Nayak, Tapas Roy, Vinod Khadkikar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Fuel Cells (FC) are the clean sources of energy and therefore are required by automation industries for their efficient operation. For doing so it is mandatory to have suitable Dc converters for achieving wide range of voltage operation as FC generates low voltage. This paper proposes a restructured DC-DC quadratic boost converter (RDQBC) for achieving a wide range of voltage gain sufficing the need of fuel cell electric vehicles (FCEV) for smooth operation. Here a network of inductor and capacitor replaces a specific inductor highlighted in the circuit to enhance the voltage output across the resistive load. The proposed converter works in synch with FC and EV. A detailed analysis of the proposed converter for 100 W has been demonstrated in the MATLAB simulation environment. The switching frequency considered is 20 kHZ. The results are observed in the steady state reason. The voltage gain of RDQBC has been compared with other converters as well.

title: Cross-domain Sentimental Analysis in Healthcare using Explainable AI
authors: Shivangi Tayal, P Chandra Shaker Reddy
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Cross-domain analysis is a key issue in the domain of natural language processing. Most of the model working in this domain fails to work across multiple domains. This limits the reliability of the model in examining the patient reviews, clinical notes, or social media engagement on health-related topic. Through this paper, we proposed a model using different types of deep learning methodologies like Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT), and Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) for performing cross-domain sentiment analysis in the field of healthcare. Results demonstrate that the BiLSTM has performed best among the models by getting an accuracy of 0.8524. Also, for better interpretability, we integrated various Explainable AI methods like Local Interpretable Model Agnostic Explanations (LIME) for LSTM and BiLSTM, and Shapley Additive explanations (SHAP) for BERT. Both LIME and SHAP provided valuable insights in which LIME showed semantically coherent, domain independent features influencing predictions while SHAP highlighted token-level contributions in BERT.

title: A LightGBM-SHAP Framework for Accurate and Interpretable Crop Yield Prediction
authors: Abhishek Dangi, Deepak Kumar Rathore
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Accurate crop yield prediction plays a vital role in agricultural planning and food security, especially in the face of erratic weather and soil conditions. This paper presents an AI-based platform that leverages historical meteorological and soil datasets to forecast crop yield using machine learning techniques. The model integrates key agronomic variables such as rainfall, temperature, nitrogen-phosphorus levels, and crop phenology to train a LightGBM regressor. The system also incorporates explainable AI (XAI) methods to enhance transparency and decision- making utility for farmers. Results show that our approach achieves a coefficient of determination (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) of 0.94 and an RMSE of 0.72, outperforming traditional statistical models. The system’ s interpretability, facilitated by SHAP values, empowers stakeholders to understand how environmental factors affect yield outcomes. This study demonstrates the potential of data-driven agriculture to address productivity challenges, especially under climate uncertainty.

title: Energy-Efficient Target Coverage in Wireless Visual Sensor Networks Using LSTM-Based Trajectory Prediction and Grey Wolf Optimization
authors: Navedha Evanjalin R, Mohaideen Pitchai K, Dhanush Ragavendar S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The resource-intensive nature of visual data processing presents significant challenges for Wireless Visual Sensor Networks (WVSNs) in balancing target coverage and energy consumption. The integrated framework presented in this paper combines Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks for trajectory prediction, YOLOv8n(nano)-based object detection, and Grey Wolf Optimization (GWO) for energy-conscious camera node scheduling. The proposed work uses a Pareto-optimal solution space exploration to solve the multi-objective optimization problem of maximizing target coverage while minimizing energy consumption. According to experimental results, our approach reduces energy consumption by 30% compared to baseline methods while achieving up to 95% target coverage. Through chunked inference, the framework effectively processes 10,000 images, produces balanced scheduling decisions across distributed camera nodes, and produces accurate trajectory predictions with MSE loss below 0.01.

title: A Hybrid Feature Selection and Ensemble Learning Framework with Optimized Preprocessing for High-Accuracy Lung Cancer Detection
authors: Sony Tripathi, Abhay Shukla, Apoorv Mishra
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Early and correct diagnosis of lung cancer is the only way to enhance survival rates, but due to the prevalence of small and imbalanced datasets, predictive models are highly limited. The current paper presents a new hybrid intelligent methodology that combines a two-step feature selection pipeline with an ensemble classification model that is confirmed on the Kaggle Lung Cancer Dataset. Filter-based mutual information ranking coupled with wrapper optimization via Genetic Algorithm and Elastic Net regularization was used to select features (reduced a 12-dimensional feature set to 5 key attributes (Age, Smoking, Coughing, Chest Pain, Shortness of Breath)) with a dimensionality reduction of 58.3%. Missing value imputation, normalization of data through one-hot encoding, and ADASYN balancing were performed to deal with class imbalance and maximize the generalization capabilities. The experimental findings show that the given method performs much better than baseline models. The hybrid prediction achieved 94.8% accuracy, 94.5% precision, 94.2% recall, and 94.37 F1-score, with a model AUC of 0.962. This outperforms the no-feature-selection benchmark by 6.2 in accuracy and 5.9 in recall, despite the unbalancing preprocessing options. The Confusion matrix reported 285 true positives and 290 true negatives, with minimal values of 10 and 15 false positives and false negatives, respectively, indicating strong reliability. Despite the pilot data set being small, survey-based, and using a test set with 95.9599 accuracy labeling, the proposed method achieves competitive and reliable performance, as noted through comparative analysis with the existing literature. These findings assure that the optimization of preprocessing, hybrid feature selection, and ensemble learning can be effective both in reducing overfitting and increasing diagnostic performance. As such, this technique may serve as a practical method in data-limited settings to screen patients to detect lung cancer at an early stage.

title: AI-Driven Innovations in Autonomous Drones: A Thorough Review of Security, Navigation, and Multi-Sensor Technologies
authors: Soukaina Dadi, Mohamed Lachgar, Hamid Hrimech, Ali Kartit
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Networking, Intelligent Systems &amp; Security (NISS)
abstract: 抓取摘要失败: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='ieeexplore.ieee.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /document/11415477/ (Caused by ProtocolError('Connection aborted.', ConnectionResetError(104, 'Connection reset by peer')))

title: Secure Convergence Model for Distributed Interactions
authors: Balapu Vishnu Vardhan, Pindiboyana Reethika, Sankar Ganesh Karuppasamy, Sajeev Ram Arumugam, Senthil Murugan V, Gopi S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: In modern digital ecosystems, distributed architectures such as cloud computing, multi-agent systems, and the Internet of Things (IoT) are facing challenges in ensuring a secure and reliable coordination of heterogeneous nodes in the modern digital ecosystem. The current solutions typically cover only one aspect of security, e.g., encryption, trust management, or anomaly detection, without being able to cover the whole spectrum of dynamic networks. This study introduces SCMDI to overcome such drawbacks and it is a secure convergence model for distributed interactions. SCMDI combines hybrid encryption, situational risk assessment, adaptive anomaly detection and policy based access control. It compares the reliability of the nodes with respect to past data and the actual behavior through machine learning models that observe network activity in real time. The framework guarantees confidentiality and integrity by using a combination of symmetric and asymmetric cryptography as well as automatically changing access policies based on the evolving network situations. As well as it is a low weight audit logging system offers tamper free traceability without adding excessive weight to computation. Simulation environment experiments with distributed environments show that SCMDI is effective in terms of detecting malicious activities, imposing trust ratings, and enforcing security policies. In general, the proposed model can provide a high performance, high scalability and intelligent yet secure and collaborative protection to distributed computing environments.

title: Real-Time Fault Detection in Ocean Observing Platforms
authors: Omprakash Gurrapu, Sunny Solmen Edelli Padmarao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Ocean observing platforms (OOPs) comprising moored buoys, gliders, autonomous surface vehicles, and fixed coastal stations - provide untold value for climate science, marine safety, and resource management. Persistence in the harsh marine environment exposes both sensors and platforms to mechanical degradation, biofouling, communication loss, and power faults, which decrease data quality and operational uptime. This article describes a full-fledged approach to realtime fault detection, developed specifically for OOPs, that combines edge-based pre-processing, multi-sensor fusion, statistically-based anomaly detection, and lightweight machinelearning classifiers designed for constrained computing environments. Robustness to missing data, asymmetric sensor reliabilities, and intermittent communications have been prioritized in the pipeline. The benchmarked approach has demonstrated faster detection latency, higher true positive rates (TPR), and lower rates of false alarms within realistic disturbance scenarios, versus traditional threshold-and-rule, or centralized post hoc analytics approaches. Guidelines are provided for implementation covering data conditioning, target selection (e.g., which models to choose given constrained compute footprints), adaptive thresholds, and remote update mechanisms.

title: The Role of Artificial Intelligence in Shaping Human Life
authors: Priya Kate, Sachin Dagadkar, Aryan Sontakke
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence is becoming a major part of our everyday life, and its influence is growing even more in 2025-2026. AI is helping people in many areas such as education, healthcare, communication, and work. It makes tasks faster, improves decisionmaking, and offers new ways to solve problems. At the same time, AI also brings challenges like loss of jobs, privacy issues, and the need for new skills. This abstract explains how AI is changing human life, the benefits it provides, and the responsibilities we must follow while using it. The goal is to understand how AI can support society in a safe and positive way.

title: The Silent Threat: A Deep Dive into Audio Deepfakes and Detection Techniques
authors: Ravi Kumar Kushwaha, Prathmesh Durge, Shivansh Lohani, Pratik K Agrawal, Nitin Rakesh
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Audio deepfakes (ADs) are a type of emerging voice cloning technologies initially developed to be used in other applications like audiobooks. They are now being used more often to undermine privacy and security, thus raising the need to conduct research on AD detection. The resulting literature has led to the creation of machine-learning (ML) and deep- learning (DL) approaches. The research provides an overview of the modern methods of AD detection, categorizes the imitation and synthetic attack paradigms systematically, and provides the comparison of the existing fake-audio datasets. Unlike previous studies, which focus on specific approaches, this study engages in a critical synthesis of recent ML and DL approaches on heterogeneous datasets, finding a correlation between the performance indicators, such as the equal-error rate (EER) and tandem detection cost function (t -DCF ), and the properties of the data sets. The results provide a systematic guide to measuring detection strength and embark on the future research on dataset-free, noise-resistant, and accent-sensitive AD detection models.

title: AI-Driven Brain Tumor Detection: Optimizing PHOGF and SCHF Features with Bayesian, Gaussian, and Boosting Classifiers
authors: Radhika Sethuraman, Gururama Senthilvel P
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The identification and categorization of brain tumors remain vital because they lead to prompt, accurate diagnosis along with appropriate treatment strategies. This research examines various machine learning systems that utilize a combination of Pyramid Histogram of Oriented Gradients (PHOGF) Filter and Simple Color Histogram Filter (SCHF) for feature extraction, along with Bayes Net, Gaussian Process, and AdaBoost classifiers. Assessment of these models involved evaluating their accuracy and precision, as well as recall, ROC, and PRC metrics, and execution time to determine the optimal brain tumor prediction. PHOGF + Bayes Net was picked as the best model because it produced 95.71% accuracy while delivering optimal ROC (0.99) and PRC (0.99). Test results showed SCHF + Bayes Net achieved a high accuracy rate of 95.49% but its 33.63 seconds of execution time made it considered inefficient regarding computational performance. The combination of PHOGF with Gaussian Process achieved 94.92% classification accuracy, along with quick execution times of 0.12 seconds. This was followed by SCHF combined with Gaussian Process, which reached 95.23% accuracy while running within 0.43 seconds. The classification accuracy from AdaBoost-based models was the lowest at 87.43% when implementing PHOGF + AdaBoost. AdaBoost-based models operated as the fastest solutions among all models, given their 0.06 seconds execution time when using SCHF + AdaBoost. The data indicates that Bayes Net and Gaussian Process classifiers deliver remarkable performance outcomes, but AdaBoost models operate effectively for time-sensitive applications.

title: Frequency Nadir and RoCOF Prediction Using Predisturbance Data and Artificial Neural Networks
authors: M. Ramirez-Gonzalez, F. R. Segundo, P. Korba
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Power system frequency stability is vital to keep a reliable electricity supply. However, the ongoing transition to cleaner electrical networks is giving place to system dynamics characterized by faster/pronounced frequency deviations and higher rates of change of frequency (RoCoFs), which impose significant challenges to grid operators and increase the risk of blackouts. Although traditional time-domain simulations have been widely used for frequency stability analysis, they are computationally intensive and in general not suitable for real-time applications. Therefore, a machine learning based alternative using artificial neural networks (ANNs) is proposed in this paper. By using predisturbance data, an ANN model is trained to predict the frequency nadir and RoCoF of a sample system before the occurrence of a given generation loss. Besides, in order to identify the most influential information (as related to the measuring points for the task), permutation feature importance is applied to the trained network. Then, a new ANN model with a reduced set of input features is built and implemented in a real-time environment for further performance verification. Offline and real-time simulation results reveal the capability of the proposed approach to support the anticipation of considered disturbances and provide relatively fast and accurate predictions of the concerned frequency metrics.

title: Synergistic Integration of Agrivoltaics and Electric Vehicle Charging: A Sustainable Energy Framework
authors: Al-Amin, SM Ferdous, Md Shoeb, GM Shafiullah
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: To alleviate the growing energy demand and voltage instability challenges in modern power distribution networks, this study proposes an integrated framework combining agrivoltaics (AV) systems with electric vehicle (EV) charging infrastructure. The proposed AV system is designed to enable simultaneous crop cultivation and photovoltaic energy generation by employing elevated and well-spaced panel structures. Recently, the authors conducted a techno-economic assessment to examine the financial and operational implications of directing surplus AV energy toward EV charging. The authors also developed a novel Weighted Average Power Estimator (WAPE) controller to support stable EV integration. Thus, a dynamic framework is proposed in this paper on a distribution network to show the efficacy of the AV and EV integrated system. The results show that the proposed AV–EV integration framework enhances renewable energy utilization, improves voltage stability, and supports decarbonized sustainable transport solutions.

title: CNN-Based Rice Leaf Disease Classification Applying Augmentation Techniques for Improved Accuracy: A Deep Learning Approach
authors: Joann G. Perez, Eugene S. Perez
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Early detection of rice leaf diseases is critical for crop protection and yield optimization. This paper presents an automated classification system for three prevalent rice diseases—Bacterial leaf blight, Brown spot, and Rice—blast using Inception V3 convolutional neural network with comprehensive data augmentation. A dataset of 330 RGB images collected from Central Luzon rice fields was partitioned 80:20 for training and testing. Data augmentation techniques (rotation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 20^{\circ}$</tex>, flipping, zoom <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.9-1.1 \times$</tex>, brightness <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 20 \%$</tex>) expanded the effective training set threefold. The Inception V3 transfer learning model achieved 96% classification accuracy after 50 epochs, compared to 93% at 30 epochs, with training loss reduced from 0.19 to 0.10 and validation loss from 0.54 to 0.40. Correct predictions exhibited confidence scores <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$>0.99$</tex>, while misclassifications showed notably lower confidence (0.61-0.79), enabling uncertainty-aware decision support. The model's 87 MB footprint and 0.15 s inference time enable mobile deployment for field use. Results demonstrate that transfer learning with systematic augmentation provides an efficient, accurate solution for rice disease diagnosis in resource-constrained agricultural settings.

title: Artificial Intelligence in Social Media: A Comprehensive Review of Models, Applications, and Ethical Issues
authors: Rupesh Dabbir
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The convergence of artificial intelligence (AI) and social media has redefined how information is created, consumed, and moderated across digital ecosystems. From real-time content recommendation to misinformation detection and mental health analysis, AI powers the operational core of modern platforms. This paper presents a comprehensive review of foundational AI models, emerging applications, and the socio-technical challenges associated with their deployment in social media. We explore deep learning architectures, transformer-based NLP systems, computer vision models, and self-supervised frameworks shaping content personalization, moderation, and interaction. Additionally, the paper investigates rising trends—such as multimodal AI, federated learning, and generative synthetic media—alongside ethical concerns related to fairness, surveillance, algorithmic manipulation, and regulatory compliance. Through real-world case studies and a cross-platform comparison, we distill critical insights into the capabilities and limitations of current systems. We also identify open research problems that must be addressed to ensure the responsible evolution of AI in social media. Our findings highlight the urgent need for transparent, accountable, and user-aligned AI frameworks that prioritize both technical excellence and societal well-being.

title:
BISen: A Robust Framework for Efficient CNN Inference on
                    <u>B</u>
                    attery-Free
                    <u>I</u>
                    ntelligent
                    <u>S</u>
                    ensory Nodes
authors: Sepehr Tabrizchi, Shayan Gerami, Justin Feng, Nader Sehatbakhsh, David Pan, Arman Roohi
abstract: We present BISen, a framework for efficient and reliable convolutional neural network (CNN) inference on battery-free, energy-harvesting IoT sensor nodes. Battery-powered deployments suffer from limited lifetimes, high replacement costs, and environmental impacts, problems that will intensify as IoT scales to billions of devices. Energy-harvesting nodes remove batteries but face intermittent power, resulting in frequent failures that corrupt the intermediate CNN state, require costly checkpointing and rollback, and amplify non-volatile memory (NVM) traffic under tight on-chip memory constraints, leaving little harvested energy for useful sensing and inference. BISen introduces a reactive intermittent execution model for CNN workloads on off-the-shelf ultra-low-power microcontrollers. An energy-aware state machine with a safe-stop mechanism halts execution before brownout, while selective checkpointing preserves only the minimal CNN state needed for forward progress. This enables seamless resumption across power cycles while sharply reducing NVM reads/writes and memory-access overheads. Across two commercial MCU+radio platforms, three real harvested power traces, and nine CNNs, BISen cuts NVM operations by up to 86.4%, reduces standby/load/store operations by up to 94.1%, 94.5%, and 90.7%, and improves sensing throughput by about 1.3−1.4× compared to a state-of-the-art reactive baseline under the same energy budget, enabling long-lived, battery-free, carbon-aware IoT deployments.

title: Retraining Gastrocnemius Muscle Coordination Reduces Late-Stance Knee Contact Force in Individuals with Knee Osteoarthritis_supp1-3669842.pdf
authors: Michelle R. Joyce

title: An Interference Suppression Method Incorporating Sound Field Characteristics for Depth Estimation
authors: Guangyu Gong, Kunde Yang, Rui Duan
abstract: Accurate target depth estimation in deep-water Reliable Acoustic Path (RAP) environments is challenging due to the impact of strong interference on the energy of beam outputs from vertical line arrays (VLAs) in the target direction. This paper presents a sound field characteristics-based generalized sidelobe canceller (SFC-GSC) method. The method applies sidelobe cancellation by assigning weights to interference directions, creating nulls in multiple interference paths. These weights are designed based on the multipath arrival structure of interference, effectively distinguishing weak targets from interference components. The resulting nulls enhance target trajectory visibility, enabling automatic extraction. The refined target energy is further processed using spatial domain Fourier transform (SpFT) to achieve precise depth estimation. Simulation results indicate that the method achieves a depth estimation RMSE of less than 5 meters under a -40 dB signal-to-interference ratio (SIR). Experimental data from the South China Sea strongly interfered environment further confirm its feasibility, with the estimated depth deviating by 2 meters from interference-free results.

title: Some classes of punctured codes
authors: Daotong Qiu, Jian Gao, Fanghui Ma, Jiafu Mi
abstract: For i ∈ {1, 2}, let Ci be an [n, ki, di] cyclic code over the finite field Fq. The (C1, C1 + C2)-construction produces [2n, k1 + k2]q quasi-cyclic (QC) codes with index 2, which have three types of codewords, i.e., (c1, c1), (0, c2) and (c1, c1+c2). In this paper, we firstly investigate the weight distributions of the QC codes (C1, C1+C2). By deleting coordinates on C1 and C1+C2, we obtain several classes of linear codes with the forms (CTp, C1+C2) and (CTp, (C1 +C2)Ts), where Tp and Ts are sets of coordinates, and determine their weight enumerators in some cases. All these codes are optimal with respect to the Griesmer bound, and can produce new few-weight linear codes after comparing with some recent works. Finally, we analyze the minimum locality of the duals of some punctured codes. Two classes of d-optimal and k-optimal locally recoverable codes are constructed.

title: Hi-CIoTNet: A CNN-Based Hierarchical Model for IoT and Critical IoT Traffic Classification
authors: Shahbaz Rasheed, Farrukh Zeeshan Khan
abstract: The growing number of Internet of Things (IoT) deployments has brought significant network heterogeneity and elevated traffic levels which make it difficult to identify and rank IoT network flows accurately. The current methods fail to identify IoT traffic from non-IoT traffic and they cannot detect essential IoT streams which need absolute latency and reliability standards. The research presents Hi-CIoTNet as a hierarchical deep learning system which conducts two-stage traffic identification through the RAD-CNN model that extends baseline CNN with Residual, Attention and Dropout layers. The system consists of two stages which first detects IoT traffic from non-IoT traffic and then separates IoT traffic into Critical and Non-Critical categories. The research used UNSW-IoTraffic dataset to test the system because it contains extended IoT network recordings from various devices. The RAD-CNN system combines residual learning to maintain gradient stability with attention mechanisms for feature enhancement and dropout regularization for model stability. The proposed model achieved 99.90% accuracy for IoT/Non-IoT classification and 99.92% accuracy for Critical/Non-Critical flow identification. The fine-tuned model achieved 97.0% accuracy when applied to the IoT-23 dataset for evaluating its ability to adapt between different IoT environments. The results show that Hi-CIoTNet operates effectively as an SDN network controller for real-time critical IoT traffic management.

title: STAND-Net: A Spiking Temporal Attention autoeNcoDer Network for Efficient EEG Artifact Removal
authors: Ruizhi Zhang, Xiaoyu Guo, Yu Pan, Shuo Gao
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Electroencephalography (EEG)-based brain computer interface (BCI) systems hold significant promise across diverse applications; however, their performance is compromised by pervasive physiological artifacts that degrade signal fidelity. While current deep neural networks (DNNs) improve artifact rejection, their high computational cost precludes deployment in wearable BCIs systems. Here, we introduce STAND-Net (Spiking Temporal Attention autoeNcoDer Network), a neuromorphic architecture that leverages event-driven spiking neurons to achieve ultra-efficient, high-fidelity EEG artifact removal. STAND-Net combines a spike-convolution encoder-decoder with leaky integrate-and-fire neurons to model spatiotemporal EEG dynamics, a dilation-enhanced residual backbone capturing long-range dependencies, and a spike-rate attention mechanism dynamically localizing artifacts via neuronal firing patterns. The system demonstrates >3.7 dB improvement in signal-to-distortion ratio over state-of-the-art methods across diverse artifacts while consuming 97.98% less power than comparable DNNs. Crucially, downstream BCI classification accuracy increased by 6.64% using STAND-Net-processed signals. This work establishes a neuromorphic framework for low-power and high quality EEG artifact removal in wearable BCI systems.

title: Improving Counting Accuracy of Post-Disaster Visual Question Answering for Remote Sensing
authors: Bianca-Cerasela-Zelia Blaga, Sergiu Nedevschi
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: In post-disaster damage assessment, visual question answering (VQA) systems are essential in identifying the severity and scope of damage. However, counting-related tasks, such as determining the number of vehicles and flooded buildings, remain a significant challenge for current deep learning models. To address this issue, we propose DeVANet (DeBERTa Vision Attention Network), a novel architecture aimed at enhancing counting accuracy in VQA for post-disaster scenarios. We leverage DeBERTa for language modeling and introduce an innovative image embedding module, where local-global attention guides Vision Mamba features to achieve precise extraction of both small and large objects. Our fusion mechanism employs self-attention for both text and image data, followed by bidirectional cross-attention and co-attention to enhance multimodal integration. We tackle VQA as both a classification and regression problem by employing separate MLPs for each task: one handling discrete class predictions and the other generating continuous values for counting tasks. A joint loss function, combining weighted cross-entropy and negative binomial loss, ensures optimized performance across both tasks. Extensive experiments on the FloodNet and RescueNet datasets demonstrate that DeVANet achieves significant improvements in counting accuracy and overall VQA performance compared to state-of-the-art works, supported by detailed ablation studies that validate the effectiveness of each component in the architecture.

title: Information Bottleneck Theory-Guided Dimension Reduction for Large-Scale Multi-Objective Optimization_supp1-3669468.pdf
authors: Xingyi Zhang
abstract: Dimension reduction has notably emerged in solving large-scale multi-objective optimization problems (LSMOPs). Nevertheless, the determination of the latent dimension after dimension reduction remains a critical challenge, where inappropriate dimensions risk the failure to find the Pareto optimal set. To address this issue, an information bottleneck theory-guided dimension estimation method is proposed, which theoretically derives the optimal latent dimension of LSMOPs. Specifically, the latent dimension is established in a quantitative relationship with the importance of decision variables based on information bottleneck theory. Then, the importance of decision variables with respect to convergence and diversity is quantified to determine the optimal latent dimension, and the high-dimensional decision space is subsequently projected into a low-dimensional latent space via dimension reduction techniques. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method on various problem suites, i.e., LSMOP, WFG, and ZCAT, when integrated with different dimension-reduction techniques. Furthermore, the version integrated with the weighted optimization framework also outperforms five representative competitors with superior performance on most of test problems.

title: SIGS: A Synergistic Information-Guided Sparse Framework for Battery SOH Estimation
authors: Qian Xu, Mingyao Ma, Liping Mo, Tingzhi Jiang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: To address challenges in battery State of Health (SOH) estimation, which stem from the isolated optimization of feature engineering and model construction stages and the high computational cost of conventional methods, this paper introduces SIGS, a Synergistic Information-Guided Sparse framework. This multi-level collaborative framework first employs our novel Simplified Information Bottleneck (SIB) with a smoothness term to extract robust features and their importance scores. These scores then synergistically guide the subsequent stages: a new importance-weighted aging prototype selection (IW-APS) method identifies representative inducing points from the aging trajectory, and an importance-weighted kernel enhances the sparse Gaussian Process Regression’s focus on critical indicators. Comprehensive validation on four public datasets confirms the framework’s superior accuracy, with prediction root mean square error (RMSE) below 1% and outperforming state-of-the-art benchmarks across all scenarios. Furthermore, a Pareto analysis demonstrates its practical value by providing a clear guide for navigating the trade-off between prediction accuracy and computational cost.

title: Spatial Multiple Importance Sampling for Real-time Irradiance Probes_supp1-3669877.mp4
authors: Shi-Min Hu
abstract: Real-time global illumination rendered with low variance remains a persistent challenge. Many engines employ irradiance probes as a relatively cheap technique, but constrained computational budgets often lead to flickering artifacts in rendered images. In this paper, we propose spatial multiple importance sampling, which reuses ray–surface intersection data among real-time irradiance probes to significantly reduce flicker caused by variance, enabling efficient computation in complex scenes under limited ray tracing budgets. Moreover, our approach incorporates a probe selection mechanism to enhance reuse efficiency and a visibility estimation method to mitigate bias. Experimental results demonstrate that our method significantly reduces variance at a fixed ray tracing cost, delivering high-quality, stable outputs in real-time scenarios.

title: Performance Analysis of a Rare-Earth-Free Axial Flux Permanent Magnet Motor Using a PCB Stator_supp2-3669789.mp4
authors: Shahin ASGARI

title: Kalman Filter–Based Power Management for Railway Wireless Power Transfer Systems with EDLC Integration
authors: E. Sato, R. Ota, K. Kondo
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Power Electronics
abstract: Dynamic wireless power transfer (DWPT) enables power transfer only while the onboard and ground coils are magnetically coupled, necessitating high peak power and a steep power ramp-up. In addition, for several-hundred-kilowatt reception, using a lithium-ion battery (LiB) alone results in an excessively high C-rate and accelerates degradation, whereas implementation constraints in this power range limit the switching frequency of power semiconductors to only a few kilohertz. This study proposes a power management scheme that connects an electric double-layer capacitor (EDLC) in parallel with the LiB and controls the EDLC charging current through feedforward based on transmitted power estimated via a Kalman filter. The proposed scheme suppresses the LiB C-rate while enabling the system to follow steep power variations with a limited number of switching actions. Furthermore, by extracting the high-frequency component of the battery current and using it for damping control, current oscillations during power transients are suppressed. Compared with a conventional disturbance-estimation-plus-feedback method employing a low-pass filter, the proposed method improves command-tracking performance and closed-loop controllability, and its effectiveness in reducing DC–DC converter current ripple is experimentally demonstrated using a 2-kW downscaled setup.
