title: Realization of an In-Band Reconfigurable Metasurface for Nonreciprocal/Reciprocal Transmission
authors: Ninghui Liu, Wenhao Wang, Qi Sun, Zhonglei Mei, Tiaoming Niu
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: Reconfigurable nonreciprocal electromagnetic devices are critical communication components in areas such as electromagnetic isolation, co-frequency co-time full-duplex single-antenna communication systems, and reconfigurable signal routing. In this letter, we design a reconfigurable nonreciprocal metasurface by utilizing the gyromagnetic property of ferrite and the switching capability of a diode with four independently reconfigurable operation modes: 1)bidirectional transmission; 2)nonreciprocal transmission(+z); 3)nonreciprocal transmission(−z); and 4) bidirectional blocking. By adjusting the operating states of the diodes, the functions are achieved in the same frequency band and can be freely switched between these functions. Numerical simulations show that the transmission coefficient can achieve about −3 dB, when the metasurface works in the corresponding modes. The experimental results validate the proposed concept, demonstrating that the device successfully fulfills the predesigned functionality. The proposed design delivers fresh insights and mindsets for manipulating EM waves arbitrarily, holding possibilities for applications in electromagnetic isolation, full-duplex single-antenna communication systems, and reconfigurable signal routing.

title: Rubato: Efficient Post-Quantum Asynchronous Distributed Randomness Beacon with Integrated Consensus
authors: Linghe Yang, Tonghong Chong, Jian Liu, Jingyi Cui, Guangquan Xu, Yude Bai, Lei Zhang, Tao Luo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Distributed randomness beacons are essential for distributed systems (e.g., blockchain and MPC), providing un biased and unpredictable shared randomness. However, implementations in asynchronous networks often suffer from poor scal ability, low throughput, and high resource consumption when deployed as independent protocols. The state-of-the-art HashRand (CCS'24) achieves high throughput and low computational in tensity using only lightweight post-quantum cryptographic primitives for an independent asynchronous beacon. Building further on this, we propose Rubato, a low-overhead, high-throughput beacon protocol that leverages lightweight batched Asynchronous Complete Secret Sharing with Byzantine Atomic Broadcast-based state machine replication (via our tailored RubatoSMR). Rubato reduces communication complexity by an O(clogn) factor compared to HashRand and resolves the circular dependency between beacon and BAB-SMR in asynchronous settings. Experiments on AWSdemonstrate that Rubato achieves ideal overall performance in scalability, resource usage, and throughput; for instance, at n = 121nodes, it produces an average of 174 beacons per minute, with RubatoSMR further optimizing memory and bandwidth consumption.

title: Report on the 1st Workshop on Human-Centered Recommender Systems (HCRS 2025) at TheWebConf 2025
authors: Kaike Zhang, Yunfan Wu, Yougang Lyu, Du Su, Yingqiang Ge, Shuchang Liu, Qi Cao, Zhaochun Ren, Fei Sun
abstract: None

title: Towards Detection of Deep Fake Videos
authors: Mohammed Muddasir N, Abhishek M M, Chethan Chirag S J, Dinakar M K
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The emergence of deep fake technology has generated significant concerns across society about risk and misinformation. While deep-fake detection has been studied extensively, finding low-quality deepfakes and differentiating their qualities is a major challenge. This study looks at the rapidly evolving field of deep fake detection, specifically within the video realm related to identifiable facial transformations. The primary dataset for this study = Celeb-DF. As a large dataset of high-quality deep fake videos of celebrities, Celeb-DF presents a large challenge to current detection efforts. The study provided a state-of-the-art Quality-Agnostic Deep Fake Detection (QAD) framework for identifying different qualities of deep fakes in concurrent ways, along with results that surpassed all established results across many datasets. The paper discusses issues addressed in ongoing studies to improve detection, including developing new advanced models such as Stable Diffusion, and using a interpretability-through-prototypes combination of fine-tuned Vision Transformers with Support Vector Machine(s). Finally, we showed a front endbased web application for detecting deep fake videos from a model based on a new deep learning approach based on Long Short-Term Memory trained on frame-level characteristics extracted from a ResNext Convolution neural network.

title: PSO-Tuned Predefined-Time Sliding-Mode Control of a Grid-Forming Inverter
authors: Pooyan Alinaghi Hosseinabadi, Marif Daula Siddique, Michael Negnevitsky, Saad Mekhilef, Mehdi Seyedmahmudian
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper introduces a novel predefined-time sliding-mode control (PSMC)-based cascaded approach. Two PSMCs are designed separately to regulate the capacitor voltage and inverter current of an islanded grid-forming (GFM) inverter with an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$L C$</tex> filter. Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) is employed to optimally tune the PSMC parameters, thereby ensuring optimal controller performance. In the proposed design, a new sliding surface and control law are formulated based on the concept of predefined-time stability (PTS), ensuring that the tracking errors converge to the origin within a predefined time. A variable exponent is introduced in both the sliding surface and the control law, guaranteeing PTS while adaptively adjusting the control effort depending on whether the states are far from or near the equilibrium, thereby ensuring fast convergence, robust performance, and avoiding additional chattering. Furthermore, chattering is mitigated by incorporating the integral of the switching term within the control law. Finally, simulation results confirm the effectiveness of the proposed method compared to a commonly used PI controller from the recent literature, particularly under system disturbances such as load variations and reference voltage changes.

title: TEMP: A Memory Efficient Physical-Aware Tensor Partition-Mapping Framework on Wafer-Scale Chips
authors: Huizheng Wang, Taiquan Wei, Zichuan Wang, Dingcheng Jiang, Qize Yang, Jiaxin Liu, Jingxiang Hou, Chao Li, Jinyi Deng, Yang Hu, Shouyi Yin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) demand significant memory and computation resources. Wafer-scale chips (WSCs) provide high computation power and die-to-die (D2D) bandwidth but face a unique trade-off between on-chip memory and compute resources due to limited wafer area. Therefore, tensor parallelism strategies for wafer should leverage communication advantages while maintaining memory efficiency to maximize WSC performance. However, existing approaches fail to address these challenges. To address these challenges, we propose the tensor stream partition paradigm (TSPP), which reveals an opportunity to leverage WSCs' abundant communication bandwidth to alleviate stringent on-chip memory constraints. However, the 2D mesh topology of WSCs lacks long-distance and flexible interconnects, leading to three challenges: 1) severe tail latency, 2) prohibitive D2D traffic contention, and 3) intractable search time for optimal design. We present TEMP, a framework for LLM training on WSCs that leverages topology-aware tensor-stream partition, trafficconscious mapping, and dual-level wafer solving to overcome hardware constraints and parallelism challenges. These integrated approaches optimize memory efficiency and throughput, unlocking TSPP's full potential on WSCs. Evaluations show TEMP achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.7 \times$</tex> average throughput improvement over state-of-the-art LLM training systems across various models.

title: Investigating Video Impression of Real Time Conversational Agent for Remote Sessions Using LLM
authors: Sasirekha R, Jaswant S, Janarthanan M
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have rapidly advanced in their ability to simulate intelligent, adaptive agents across a wide range of domains. While much of the existing work focuses on task-specific chatbots or standalone AI assistants, the integration of LLM-driven agents into real-time video conferencing remains largely unexplored. In this project, we present AIVA (AI-based Virtual Avatars) a multi-client video conferencing system that enables users to initiate live sessions with AI agents capable of adopting dynamic roles such as teacher, coach, or mentor based on user-provided descriptions. The system architecture combines LLM-powered reasoning for conversational intelligence with 3D avatar rendering to deliver a visually embodied, interactive agent experience. WebRTC is used to maintain uninterrupted real-time communication between clients and AI agents. When a user initiates a session with a prompt like “Act as personal fitness coach,” the system employs targeted prompt engineering to shape the agent's tone, behavior, and domain knowledge to match the requested role. The platform also includes essential features such as session control, avatar behavior customization, and conversation history management. Preliminary evaluations across academic tutoring, emotional support, interview preparation, and latency testing indicate that AIVA can produce highly engaging, human-like interactions in a live multimedia environment. These results high- light the system's strong potential for scalable, intelligent, and personalized virtual communication technologies, particularly in educational, advisory, and training-oriented applications.

title: Compression-Aware Gradient Splitting for Collective Communications in Distributed Training
authors: Pranati Majhi, Sabuj Laskar, Abdullah Muzahid, Eun Jung Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: While distributed training is crucial for scaling deep learning models, it incurs significant overhead due to the collective communication of gradients. To alleviate the burden, compression techniques are commonly used to improve network bandwidth utilization. However, compression poses challenges for synchronized AllReduce collective communications, even more so in scalable systems. Non-uniform data sizes resulting from compression can cause bandwidth under-utilization, as faster nodes remain idle while waiting for slower nodes to complete data exchanges, increasing overall communication and consequently, training time. However, the inherent similarity in gradients across consecutive batches presents an opportunity to mitigate these inefficiencies. By leveraging the quantization of gradients and consistent distribution of zeros, the gradients can be partitioned logically to speedup communication. Splitting them into groups with and without zeros can allow different compression approaches for both. The bandwidth under-utilization due to nonuniform data size can also be solved by partitioning the gradients into variable-sized chunks, leading to more balanced compressed data sizes and reduced idle waiting time. We propose two novel strategies in Oscar, where gradient splitting is designed to improve communication and training. Oscar-SW is a novel software-based technique supporting direct AllReduce that splits gradients into probable zeros and non zeros to apply count sketch compression. Oscar-HW, a novel hardware/software codesigned gradient splitting technique is proposed with ASC (Adaptive Stepwise Coding), an encoding technique for gradient compression in distributed training. Oscar-HW dynamically splits fixed-point quantized gradients for AllReduce communications and maximizes bandwidth utilization for state-of-the-art hardware compression techniques. ASC is a variant of Adaptive Arithmetic Coding (AAC) that generates a distinct probability table for each timestep of AllReduce to adapt to its unique value ranges and avoids sending the probability table during the communication of gradients. Our experimental results show that Oscar-SW achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.22 \times$</tex> speedup and 7 % better accuracy over the SOTA CountSketch algorithm. Oscar-HW achieves an average AllReduce speedup of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.77 \times$</tex>, and an average end-to-end training speedup of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.38 \times$</tex>. ASC achieves an average AllReduce speedup of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.05 \times$</tex> over Atalanta and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4.66 \times$</tex> over no compression.

title: An Innovative Framework Combining CNN and BiGRU for Monitoring and Forecasting Water Stress in Smart Farming Systems
authors: Bayyarapu Sai Rajya Lakshmi, Ch V Raghavendran
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Water stress has recently become one of the most important challenges related to agriculture since it constricts both productivity and sustainability. This work describes a deep learning-enhanced framework designed to classify and predict drought-specific stress on tomato crops. Previous work applied conventional classifying algorithms (Decision Tree, Random Forest) and RNNs (LSTM); for our work, we added Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and a more complex model of CNN + BiGRU + Batch Normalization for greater accuracy. With drought dataset publicly available as a substitute for Bioristor sensor data, the models are benchmarked against a multitude of performance metrics. The hybrid model proposed in this study attained 98.31% accuracy as compared to competing models demonstrating its promise within precision agriculture applications aimed at predicting drought enabling automated irrigation systems.

title: Severity Detection of Chronic Fatigue Syndrome Using XGBoost and Graph Neural Networks
authors: Sarah Thomas, Samvrudha R. R, Sashank Abburu, T. Pushkar Reddy, Susmitha Vekkot
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome (CFS) is a complicated and exhausting condition that poses challenges for diagnosis because of its subjective symptoms and overlapping clinical signs. This study presents a hybrid model that combines Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and XGBoost to predict CFS severity across seven outcome classes using longitudinal patient data from the University of Bristol CFS dataset. By capturing relational structures and high-dimensional features, the model achieved an accuracy of 97.77%, a precision of 97.83%, a recall of 97.77%, and an F1-score of 97.76%. These results demonstrate strong classification ability and clinical significance. Our method helps improve tracking of patient outcomes and supports the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly SDG 3(Good Health and Well-being) and SDG 9(Industry, Innovation, and Infrastructure) by promoting accessibility of good health and well-being for all sections of society through AI-based disease prognosis. The use of AI in healthcare diagnostics shows a scalable and sustainable approach for managing chronic diseases, especially in areas with limited data or resources.

title: Smart Attendance and Emotion Monitoring System using Face Detection with CCTV Integration
authors: B. Lavanya, A. Yaswanth Sai, K. Gangadhar Rao, A. Revanth Reddy, P. Ashok Reddy
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: In this study, the authors offer a proposal of an AI-based system that is used to track and resolve the problem of student attendance and classroom attendance in schools. CCTV cameras are where students are captured by the cameras placed at the entrance of the school and at the inside of the classes; after the deepface and FaceNet model the images of the faces of the students are captured; these images are processed automatically by the FaceNet model and automatically identify the attendance. Entry-point captures make a success as compared to classroom captures in detecting proxy attendance and also indicate dropout of classes. Moreover, the system has a facial emotion recognition module that assesses the engagement level by evaluating the expression of attentive, confused, or bored expressions and therefore offers real-time feedback on the classroom dynamics to the teachers. Here, the attendance record and engagement metric visualization and alerts on an interactive dashboard are shown that allow appropriate involvement in time. Python, OpenCV, and SQLite/csv are the selected languages that are used to develop the proposed system.

title: A Decoupled Optimization Framework for Battery Energy Storage System Scheduling with Non-Linear Electrothermal Dynamics
authors: Yuhang Wu, Canbing Li, Yu Cheng, Jun Chen, Chen Fang, Qing Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The penetration of renewable energy makes the battery energy storage system (BESS) a crucial component for the power grid. The BESS scheduling, however, is inherently non-convex due to the non-linear characteristics such as charge/discharge complementarity constraints and battery degradation. To overcome this, we develop a decoupled optimization framework that separates the optimization problem into the linear and non-linear parts. The non-convex complementarity constraints of charge/discharge are rebuilt, producing a BESS convex hull optimization. The model further integrates the electrothermal effect via an iterative process, where the BESS's feasible region is tightened using updated aging cost estimations. This method balances computational efficiency with model accuracy. The results demonstrate that the proposed method can efficiently optimize the BESS operation with the non-linear complementarity constraints and electrothermal effect.

title: Real-Time Sentiment analysis of Multimodal Data streams – A Deep learning-based framework in Social Media Platforms
authors: Aarti, Manzura Shermatova, Anant Deogaonkar, Chetna Vaid Kwatra, M. Thangamani, Akanksha Chauhan
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The companies of social media have exploded exponentially and generated enough multimodal data, such as text, photos, videos, and audio. Sentiment analysis of such different data streams is highly challenging due to the difficulty of processing the volume, velocity, and variety of such data streams. In the proposed research, a solution to real-time sentiment mining of multimodal messages in interactive social media platforms is suggested, based on deep learning. It stitches multiple structures of deep neural networks into one system and comprises audio processing models to identify a sentiment in the speech (RNNs and transformers to process the text-based input and CNNs to do image analysis). To efficiently integrate the learnt features from several modalities, a multimodal fusion layer is added. To guarantee low-latency sentiment classification, the system uses Apache Kafka and Apache Flink for real-time stream processing. The robustness and scalability of the system are demonstrated through evaluation on real-time Twitter and Instagram data streams as well as benchmark datasets. The suggested approach performs better in terms of accuracy and reactivity than conventional unimodal sentiment analysis models, offering insightful information for use in crisis management, political analysis, brand monitoring, and consumer feedback systems. This study emphasises how crucial real-time processing and multimodal integration are to thorough sentiment analysis in dynamic online settings.

title: Operational Analysis of Multi-Carrier Energy Systems Under Uncertainty and Integration of Renewable Energy and Storage
authors: S.S. Sahoo, Ankit Garg, Md Fahim Ansari, K. R. Niazi, Debasish Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The creation of Multi-Carrier Energy Systems (MCES), which link several energy carriers like gas, heat, along with electricity, was prompted by the growing demand for integrated along with environmentally friendly energy alternatives. Nevertheless, managing the operations of these systems is intrinsically complicated, and the incorporation of renewable energy sources (RES), such as wind and sunshine, which are distinguished by their erratic and unpredictable output, adds to this complex. Energy storage technologies are essential for balancing supply as well as demand variations in order to solve these issues and preserve system resilience. An operational evaluation of MCES under uncertainty is presented in this work, with special attention paid to the effects of storage systems and renewable variability. In order to factor weather unpredictability, demand loads, as well as price of power uncertainties into account, a hybrid modeling framework that included deterministic and stochastic optimization approaches to modeling was created. The structure of the system integrates a number of MCES components, which includes as resource hubs, distributed power production, as well as storage systems, as well as is put into practice as a series of case studies to show how well it works. The findings demonstrated how crucial predictive monitors, a practically adaptable energy routing network, along with the use of advanced forecasting methods are to maximizing energy efficiency and lowering operating costs. In the global transition to cleaner and more dynamic fuel, these findings will open up opportunities for versatility in the design of dependable and flexible electricity systems that enable a cleaner as well as stronger energy ecosystem.

title: Reliability Assessment of Integrated Energy Systems Accounting for the Uncertainties in Energy Sources and Loads
authors: Zuoxia Xing, Zhi Zhu, Hao Sun, Jiayao Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper proposes a short-term reliability assessment method based on sequential Monte Carlo simulation to address the challenge of evaluating the operational reliability of integrated energy systems (IES), which arises from the tight coupling and coordinated operation of multiple energy systems on the transmission and demand sides. Firstly, a multi-dimensional assessment framework incorporating equipment failure models, the stochastic nature of wind and solar power, and the dynamic response of energy storage is constructed. Secondly, based on the equipment output plans generated by an optimization model, the fault states and supply-demand balance are simulated on a time-step basis to quantify the reliability indicators over a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{2 4}$</tex>-hour period. Finally, with the economic operation of IES as the objective, the operational plans are issued following the sequential Monte Carlo simulation-based reliability assessment process for electricity and heat. The results of the case study show that this method has a positive effect on the optimal scheduling of IES in ensuring the continuous supply of critical loads and the secure and reliable operation of system. It can accurately capture the impacts of short-term equipment failures and sudden load changes. The hourly resolution enables rapid identification of supply gap periods, providing decision support for the dynamic adjustment of energy storage strategies.

title: Automated Thyroid Ultrasound Classification Using Cnn
authors: Ms. Akilandeswari. T, M.Siva Santhosh Kumar, L. Supraja, V. Thirumalesh, K.Sai Charan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: This work focuses on developing an end-to-end approach in automatically classifying thyroid ultrasound images by using a compact convolutional neural network and metadata-driven labelling. The processing starts with unpacking an archive of ultrasound images together with XMLbased annotation files into the working directory. From such XML records, the case identifiers and TIRADS scores were extracted, then converted to simple binary labels that reflect whether an image does or does not show thyroid pathology. After organizing the images in class-specific directories, a TensorFlow data pipeline was built, which handled some essential preprocessing steps, including resizing, intensity normalization, caching, and prefetching to ensure efficient GPU utilization in training. The model used is a small, sequential CNN consisting of three convolution stages with 32, 64, and 128 filters, each followed by pooling, and ended with fully connected layers for prediction. For training, the model employed the Adam optimizer with a binary cross-entropy objective, while the performance was monitored on the separate validation subset. A simple inference module has also been included that preprocesses a given frame of ultrasound and displays the model's prediction alongside the visual input. In general, the design emphasizes practical aspects of implementation suitable for smaller datasets of medical images, while remaining reproducible and capitalizing on structured XML annotations for accurate label generation.

title: Experimental Analysis of LFENCE-Based Mitigation Against Speculative Execution Side Channels
authors: Neeraj Krishnan Mullakkara, Sreeraj Rajagopal Nair, Rajesh Kannan Megalingam
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Speculative execution increases the performance of the processor by predicting and executing instructions out of order, allowing for faster computation in modern CPUs. However, it introduces critical vulnerabilities that enable transient attacks to leak sensitive data through covert hardware channels, exploiting mechanisms like branch misprediction and out-of-order execution. This project studies the time taken in different cases, both with and without LFENCE, with detailed analysis. LFENCE acts as a speculation barrier to halt risky predictions and mitigate timing leaks. Our results highlight the differences in execution times, providing insights into how speculation influences cache behavior and creates exploitable gaps. Using Python-based visualizations, we map these timing patterns to offer clear insights into vulnerabilities and defenses. By incorporating various defense mechanisms, including LFENCE and other hardware security strategies, this study demonstrates their role in reducing transient leaks, offering a practical understanding of execution timing impacts and performance trade-offs. This accessible demonstration bridges theoretical hardware security concepts with real-world observations, emphasizing effective countermeasures without requiring extensive hardware modifications.

title: Sensor-Minimized-FCS-MPC-Controlled Transmitter Architecture for Misalignment-Tolerant Wireless EV Charging
authors: Tabish Imtiaz, Mohammad Anas Anees, Saad Mekhilef, Marizan Binti Mubin, Tey Kok Soon, Ahmed Elsanabary
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper presents a Finite Control Set Model Predictive Control (FCS-MPC) strategy for regulating output power in a dynamically misaligned wireless electric vehicle charging system utilizing a double-D coil configuration. Unlike existing approaches that implement the DC-DC converter on the receiver side, this work introduces an FCS-MPC-controlled buck converter on the transmitter side. This architecture significantly reduces the hardware footprint on the vehicle—where space is limited-by eliminating the need for additional onboard power electronics. Furthermore, the system employs only a single voltage and current sensor, both positioned on the transmitter side, enhancing compactness and simplifying sensing requirements. The results demonstrate that the proposed FCS-MPC maintains the output voltage and current within 5% of their nominal values for lateral misalignments up to 56.25% of the coil length. The controller exhibits robust steady-state and transient performance under varying load conditions. These findings confirm the effectiveness of the proposed transmitter-side control architecture for compact, efficient, and reliable wireless charging of electric vehicles.

title: Estimating ESS Revenue from Multiple Market Services in Finland's Electricity Market
authors: Md Arafath Uzzaman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This study explores the potential revenue generation of energy storage systems (ESS) in the Nordic electricity markets, particularly in Finland. By utilising historical market data and the open-source QuEST valuation platform developed by Sandia National Laboratories, the study quantifies the profitability of ESS across day-ahead, intraday, and frequency regulation markets. Two scenarios are analyzed: arbitrage-only operations and a combined strategy incorporating arbitrage and frequency regulation services. The results highlight the significant role of ESS in capitalizing on price fluctuations and enhancing grid stability. Cross-border trading occurs as the dominant revenue source, followed by frequency regulation, while arbitrage offers a smaller yet critical contribution. The proposed methodology integrates optimization modeling with real-world market dynamics, offering a replicable and adaptable framework for assessing ESS profitability. This study provides practical insights for grid operators, investors, and policymakers aiming to enhance energy system flexibility and resilience in the Nordic context.

title: RATM: Reinforcement Learning for Co-Optimized CPU Scheduling and NUMA Memory Management in Compiler Design
authors: T.C. Manjunath, Sreerama M. P., Shrivatsa K. S., Tanzila Khanam, Nandini Modi, Balakrishna R.
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The current OS uses the stationary heuristic methods & are set-up systematically while designing the system for handling of the CPU schedules & allocating of memories. These heuristics fundamentally fail under the different dynamically shifting workloads characteristic of current data centres, where batch processing, real-time analytics, and interactive applications coexist. This paper introduces RATM (Resource-Aware Adaptive Task Manager), an innovative”Authoritative Controller“architecture developed in Rust that substitutes static policies with a Deep Q-Network (DQN) reinforcement learning agent capable of optimising kernel behaviour during runtime. Our system has a rigorous Policy-Mechanism Separation. The model-free DQN agent watches the system state all the time and chooses actions. The VRRP (Varying Response Ratio Priority) Scheduler and NAAT (NUMA-Aware Adaptive Tiered) Allocator, on the other hand, are passive, configurable mechanisms that carry out commands. The RATM controller acts as a middleman between these layers, making sure that safety rules are followed and turning abstract actions into real API calls. The experimental results show that our RL-driven kernel cuts the average wait time in calibration scenarios by more than 70% compared to the static baseline, while still being very fair. The RL agent learns to proactively activate NUMA page migrations during workload phase changes, which “flattens the curve” of latency spikes that are common in traditional schedulers. Results shows the power of methodology presented here.

title: An Interpretable Hybrid CNN Framework for Severity Classification of Cardiovascular Diseases Using ECG Data
authors: Aparna Baboo, Sachikanta Dash, Rabinarayan Panda, Ramarao Nayapamu, Athinti Anusha
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases (CVD) are a foremost reason of death due to blocked arteries that impact blood circulation. Early medical detection can identify CVD, but predicting future risks remains a challenge, often resulting from irregular heart rhythms and poor blood circulation. Early and precise detection is essential to avert life-threatening complications. This work examines the utilization of deep learning (DL) methodologies to automate and enhance CVD prediction utilising electrocardiogram (ECG) images. Using a Kaggle-based arrhythmia ECG dataset (split 80% for training and 20% for testing), For enhance diagnostic performance, the study proposes a hybrid model combining ResNet101 and VGG19 with an attention mechanism and dual-stream feature extraction. Focal Loss and the AdamW optimisers are utilized to improve the model's capability to hold class imbalance. Grad-CAM and SHAP are examples of interpretability technologies that are used to make important ECG areas stand out, which makes things more clear. This approach shows strong potential for real-time CVD detection and IoT-based healthcare integration, proposing a capable tool for initial diagnosis and better patient outcomes.

title: Tempranillo: Non-Speculative Early Register Release
authors: Carlos Escuin, Paolo Salvatore Galfano, Davide Basilio Bartolini, Leeor Peled, Mehdi Alipour
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Limited by the breakdown of technology scaling, CPU architects are looking for creative solutions to deliver performance improvements while unable to traditionally scale up microarchitectural structures. One promising approach is engineering a microarchitecture that uses resources more efficiently, for example, by recycling them faster to relieve pressure on critical structures and making them look bigger than they are. The physical register file (PRF) is a key structure that faces severe area and power constraints that limit its (and the whole CPU's) scalability. Based on this observation, previous work proposed solutions to reduce the pressure on the PRF by reducing the time each register remains allocated. In this paper, we corroborate earlier findings that the early release of registers is a promising approach to reduce the occupancy of the PRF and we identify novel tight conditions for safely releasing registers. Based on this analysis, we design Tempranillo: an aggressive, non-speculative microarchitecture to release registers as early as possible without requiring additional recovery mechanisms. Tempranillo delivers up to 3.3 % and 11.8 % performance improvement over conventional release on singlethreaded and a 2 -way SMT CPUs, respectively. Additionally, Tempranillo requires modest storage overheads, translating into a performance improvement per KiB of storage of up to 2.6 % and 9.3 % for single-thread and 2-way SMT, respectively. Our evaluation shows that Tempranillo improves over both the state-of-the-art non-speculative and speculative proposals.

title: XGBoost-Based Detection and Localization of Electricity Pilferage in Low-Voltage Distribution Networks With Rooftop PV and Net-Metering
authors: Neo Nimrod DJ. Castillo, Zoe D. Pasiliao, Simon Alab E. Tiotangco, Michael Angelo A. Pedrasa, Ronald R. Cabaoig
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)

title: A Statistically-Grounded Attention Mechanism for Early Detection of Alzheimer’s Disease Using Pattern Recognition on Clinical Data
authors: Harika Mahapatra, A. V. Sriharsha
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Early detection of Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is critical; current diagnostics often rely on costly and inaccessible neuroimaging. To address this, we propose a multiview Attention-Guided Multimodal Fusion (MAGF) framework that uses deep learning for early detection of AD using accessible clinical data.Our framework introduces a parameter-free attention mechanism, an approach in machine learning to weight information. This mechanism is grounded in the coefficient of variation (CV), which measures the variability of the relative data. We posit that a modality’s diagnostic utility is inversely proportional to its relative data dispersion. MAGF implements this by using specialized encoders, neural network components that process various types of data. These encoders integrate four types of data: cognitive assessments, cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) biomarkers (measurable indicators in spinal fluid), genetic factors, and demographics.When evaluated in a large ADNI cohort (N = 1,641), MAGF achieves 89.7% accuracy and a 0.92 AUC. It significantly outperforms strong baselines, including standard multihead attention, with an 11.3% absolute accuracy gain. Ablation studies validate the design. An interpretability analysis using SHAP confirms that biomarker ratios and cognitive assessments are key diagnostic indicators, aligned with established clinical knowledge.Overall, this work demonstrates a statistically grounded fusion framework for accessible, accurate, and reliable AI-assisted AD diagnosis, directly addressing barriers to clinical deployment.

title: Conversion of Indian Sign Language into Text and Multiple Languages Using Machine Learning
authors: Surya Nivash B, Srinikethan D, Gowri Vidhya N
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: This project proposes a lightweight and efficient system that detects Indian Sign Language(ISL) gestures in real time and translates them into text and speech. The detection of hand landmarks was made easier due to the MediaPipe framework's tools for simple and accurate detection of hand landmarks. The Random Forest Classifier was used as a model for detecting images of different ISL gestures that were captured via a webcam. As soon as a signal reached 100, the detected object/gesture was converted into English text from which it can be translated via the Google Translate API into a variety of different international or indian languages. The translated text then transformed into speech using text-to-speech tools such as gTTS and pyttsx3. The platform is also available in a desktop interface and web-based interface to add accessibility to the user that can use it in any location with internet access and does not require any specialized hardware. The project attempts to ease making communication accessible for users with speech or hearing loss. to encourage participation in the community.

title: Fork vs Pthreads: A Quantitative Microarchitectural Comparison for Real-Time CPU-Based ML Inference
authors: Suvansh Gupta, Sumit Kumar Jindal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: This paper presents a refined and in-depth microarchitectural evaluation of two concurrency paradigms used in linux systems, POSIX threads and fork () -based multiprocessing—for real-time, CPU-bound machine learning (ML) inference workloads. With the increase in demand for on-device inference in CPU-only environments such as embedded and edge systems, selecting an efficient parallelization model becomes critical. We implement actual representative ML workloads including matrix multiplication, multilayer perceptron inference(MLP), and also a prime sieve for raw compute capability, and parallelize them using both threading and forking strategies. Using rigorous benchmarking and performance counter analysis using Linux perf, we evaluate execution time, instructions per cycle (IPC), and branch miss-rate across varying input sizes and core counts. Our results show that thread-based parallelism consistently outperforms process-based approaches in terms of scalability, instruction throughput, and cache efficiency, particularly under high concurrency. Fork-based models incur greater overhead due to memory duplication and context switching. These findings offer practical guidance for optimizing CPU-only ML inference pipelines in latency-sensitive and resource-constrained environments.

title: IoT and ML Based Smart Irrigation System for Crop Prediction and Water Usage Optimization
authors: Midun kumar. H, Navin Khumar. S, Muthukumaran. N, Nikhilesh. S, Maher Shalal. J
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The project proposes a Smart Crop Recommendation System implemented by the use of IoT that can help farmers choose the most appropriate crop based on the real-time analysis of the environmental data. Conventional forms of farming are usually on experience and few observations and this can make them take less than optimal decisions. To overcome this, the system will be combined with ESP32 microcontroller and a combination of sensors which will include DHT11 temperature and humidity sensor, soil moisture sensor, soil pH sensor and rain sensor to continuously measure the important parameters of agriculture. The gathered information is passed to the ThingSpeak cloud platform through Wi-Fi, which also gives a possibility to access the information remotely and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\log$</tex> the data. Through cloud storage, farmers are able to monitor the status of fields in the long run and have access to the past that can be used in longterm planning. The dataset on ThingSpeak is received with the help of MATLAB and a Decision Tree classifier is used to predict the crops. According to the environmental conditions such as temperature, humidity, soil moisture, pH, and rainfall, the system suggests the most appropriate crops such as rice, wheat, maize and chickpea based on hard-coded thresholds and trained data trends. The solution will improve precision farming by making decisions based on data and make them more resource-efficient and productive. The system does not only enable farmers to have the right information in a timely manner, but it also enhances sustainable agriculture through efficient use of water, fertilizers, and other agricultural inputs. Internet of Things with machine learning thereby offers a reliable, efficient and scalable instrument in contemporary farming.

title: Spatio-Temporal Land Cover Change Detection using Machine Learning and Google Earth Engine
authors: J. Chinnakamma Devi, R. Mahalakshmi
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The combination of Google Earth Engine ARIMA time-series exploration with Random Forest, a powerful machine learning technique, to offer a novel hybrid solution for spatio-temporal land cover change detection. The proposed method aims to address the challenges of identifying variations in land cover accurately and efficiently over extensive periods. To produce accurate categorization results, the Random Forest model is conditioned on past land cover data during the first step of the hybrid approach. The second section examines the temporal patterns of different land cover classes using Google Earth Engine ARIMA time-series analysis to determine trends and patterns in land cover changes. The new approach, which marries Random Forest with ARIMA time-series analysis, leverages both methods' strengths to provide a more accurate and robust spatio-temporal detection of changes. The combined approach can be employed for large regional and also global surface area monitoring programs because of its ability to be expanded and its efficacy. This approach has real-world applications in environmental management, conservation, city planning based on an intimate understanding of land cover change and their consequences. In studies of spatiotemporal LST variations, the MODIS land surface temperature (MOD11A1) component has been used widely. However, it has a limited suite of uses, partly due to the low resolution of space. To transcend this, the paper uses the proposed ARIMA-RF approach. It achieves accuracy of approximately 99.5%.

title: Fire Hazard Analysis of Faults in LVAC Enclosures: Experimental Comparison
authors: Michael Graeme Gibson, Andre N. Cuppen, Yuan Liu, Nirmal-Kumar C Nair
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Electrical fires pose a serious hazard in low-voltage AC and hybrid AC/DC networks. In New Zealand, a small fraction of underground LV distribution enclosures (pillars) catch fire annually due to internal component failure, posing risks to people and infrastructure [1]. This research investigates how connection overheating leads to fire, emphasizing the need for fire testing to enhance safety standards. It details key experimental procedures, including objectives, methodology, instrumentation, and data collection. By examining LVAC faults and their ignition processes in plastic LV pillars, the findings inform equipment specifications and safety measures to reduce failure rates and associated risks in electrical networks.

title: Verilog-Enabled Simulation and RTL Synthesis of SPECK Algorithm for Robust and High-Speed Cryptographic Operations in VLSI
authors: Pravin A. Dwaramwar, Ashish Avasthi, Somu Chinnusamy, Prasanna Kumar Sistla, Chitranjan Dwivedi, T.C. Manjunath
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The article displays an comprehensive approaches for the $h / w$ realizing of the SPECK based lighter weighted block-cipher based verilog simulated & the RTL synthesized applications in the VLSI depended cryptographical systems. The proposed method will consists of the designing of the SPECK also & its functional evaluations, thus aiming very higher through puts & the robust type of encryptions which are suitable for the resources based constrained environment. The results of experimentation demonstrates the algo’s suitable parameters for their $h / w$ realization or the implementations, thus exhibiting very lesser area in the overheads, having higher speed of operations & very much secured strength in the cryptographic features, thus making this algo very much ideal for current technological VLSI depended solutions for the security needs. Apart from this, to prove its efficiency, the verilog dependent RTL implementations of the SPECK algos is very much ideal for the constrained resourced VLSI environment, where the performance & the security of the algo matters a lot. In order to leverage this algo’s simple concepts, such as the ARC, Addition, Rotation, XOR operation, the designed model will minimize the $h / w$ complexity in design aspects. The Speck’s flexibilities also allow the performances in the scaling concept across a wide range in the $h / w$ target, that too from the FPGA’s to the ASIC’s, in the sense not too much sacrifice is done in the robustness to the cryptographic need. Therefore, the conducted research will not only do the validation of the feasibility in the practical aspects of the higher speeds SPECKs deploying in the current technological cryptographical circuit, which also will demonstrate the potential for a wider spreading in the adoption w.r.t. the demonstration of the potentials in a no. of applications such as IoT, embedded systems, next generation secured communications frameworks where there is a $\mathbf{h} / \mathbf{w}$ efficiency & protection of the datas which are more critical in priority.

title: ZK-AVS: Zero-Knowledge Address and Spend-Limit Proofs for Real-Time Payment Systems
authors: Vimal Teja Manne
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Address verification and spend limit checks in card and instant payment systems expose cardholder ZIP codes and velocity histories to gateways and intermediaries, which increases the privacy impact of breaches. At the same time, issuers rely on these checks to control fraud within strict two to three hundred millisecond authorization budgets. This paper presents ZK-AVS, a design that replaces plaintext AVS and spend limit evaluation with zero knowledge proofs produced on the customer device and verified at the payment gateway. The issuer stores commitments to the cardholder ZIP and per window spend counters, and the device proves that its ZIP matches the committed value and that the proposed transaction keeps cumulative spend within the configured limit, without revealing the underlying values. We instantiate a synthetic workload of fifty thousand transactions and show that AVS mismatch and limit pressure carry useful fraud signal that can be exposed as proof outcomes. The design is structured to fit within sub three hundred millisecond budgets on commodity devices, while removing direct exposure of address and spend history data at the gateway and keeping changes largely at endpoints.

title: LF-SA Based Solar Cell Parameter Extraction for Floating Photovoltaic Systems
authors: V Abhinav Kumar, Anik Goswami, Pradip Kumar Sadhu, Sagnik Bhattacharya
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The floating photovoltaic (FPV) systems installed in saltwater areas have distinct difficulties with solar cell parameter recovery owing to the corrosive effect of salt-mist, changing temperature, and dynamic operating circumstances that distort IV characteristic. Conventional metaheuristic methods of single-diode model (SDM) fitting can be accused of premature convergence and low accuracy with noisy and time-varying data. In this paper, a hybrid optimization technique combining Levy flight (LF) exploration with Simulated Annealing (SA) exploitation is proposed in order to obtain better accuracy and robustness of the parameter extraction. The LF-SA model is mathematically modeled to fit SDM, tested on artificial and benchmark I-V data, and compared to Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) and standalone SA. Findings indicate that there is an increase of $\mathbf{1 5}-\mathbf{2 5 \%}$ in the estimation accuracy and minimized RMSE with no decrease in the computational efficiency that is compatible with embedded monitoring. The method also identifies the indicators of degradation, including resistance building up due to salt intrusion, and assists with predictive maintenance in FPV systems. Such results demonstrate that LF-SA is an encouraging optimization tool towards dependable model of the solar cells and real-time health of offshore PV systems.

title: AI-Driven Behavioral and Motion Based Control of Electrical Appliances for Physically Challenged Individuals
authors: Hariharasudhan T, Kalaivani K, Santhosh S, Sree Raksha K A, Madhan M, Naveenkumar S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The aim of this project works towards the design of a smart system that utilizes behavior and motion for controlling electrical appliances within the house. The system is designed to assist physically disabled individuals in making their lives easier. The system incorporates the use of sensors that do not have to come into contact, such as Passive Infrared sensors, ultrasonic sensors, and Inertial Measurement Units. The system requires computer programs to analyze the input provided by the sensors in real time for the system to be able to determine what the individual wants to do next. The system can turn on light, fan, or air conditioners using special switches known as relay modules. The system can analyze data instantly, meaning it reacts quickly and functions even when the internet connection is off. Through its ability to sense the actions performed by the individual and the environmental aspects, the system makes life more accessible for people and allows individuals to be more independent. Additionally, the system reduces energy by only using appliances when they are necessary.

title: Quantum Cryptographic Techniques for Secure Genetic Data Sharing: Mutual Information Feature Ranking vs PCA and LASSO
authors: Sultan Alam, Daksh Verma, Arinjay Jain, Tejeshwar Singh
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The rapid progress of genomic sequencing has transformed biomedical research, enabling large-scale studies and personalized medicine. However, the highly sensitive and immutable nature of genetic information demands strong privacy safeguards, particularly against emerging quantum computing threats. This work presents a post-quantum secure framework for genetic data sharing that integrates lattice-based encryption (CRYSTALS-Kyber) with hash-based signatures (XMSS). To address the high dimensionality of genomic datasets, we compare mutual-information-driven feature selection methods—minimum redundancy maximum relevance (mRMR) and maximal information coefficient (MINE)—with principal component analysis (PCA) and least absolute shrinkage and selection operator (LASSO). Graph entropy is employed to quantify the interdependence of selected features, and the efficiency of secure graph traversal is analyzed using Breadth-First Search (BFS) and Depth-First Search (DFS) under encryption. Experimental evaluation on gene expression and text datasets, implemented in Python with scikit-learn, demonstrates that MI-based methods achieve higher classification accuracy while reducing encryption overhead by up to 45% compared to PCA and LASSO. Theoretical analysis further establishes provable security and computational efficiency guarantees for the proposed approach.

title: MIRZA: Efficiently Mitigating Rowhammer with Randomization and ALERT
authors: Hritvik Taneja, Ali Hajiabadi, Michele Marazzi, Kaveh Razavi, Moinuddin Qureshi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: In-DRAM Rowhammer mitigation requires three resources: space (to track aggressor rows), time (to perform mitigation), and energy (to refresh victim rows). An ideal in-DRAM mitigation must minimize all three overheads. Recent randomized trackers, such as MINT, can perform tracking with negligible storage overheads. However, they perform mitigation proactively and frequently, which incurs significant performance and energy overheads at low thresholds. Recently, JEDEC introduced Per-Row Activation Counters (PRAC) and ALERT Back Off (ABO) protocol to obtain the time for mitigation reactively, as needed. While PRAC+ABO minimizes the time and energy overheads of mitigation, PRAC incurs significant changes to the DRAM array and significant performance overhead (6.5% on average) due to increased memory timings to update the PRAC counters. Our goal is to develop an efficient in-DRAM mitigation that has low storage, performance, and energy overheads. Our paper proposes MIRZA, the first low-cost reactive in-DRAM mitigation. MIRZA relies on MINT to track aggressor rows. However, instead of proactively doing mitigation at regular intervals (via REF or RFM), MIRZA uses ABO to reactively obtain the time required for mitigation. To avoid frequent ABO, MIRZA employs Coarse-Grained Filtering to disable mitigations if the activation count is below a certain Filtering Threshold. To tolerate a threshold of 1K, MIRZA requires a storage overhead of only 196 bytes of SRAM per bank. Compared to MINT, MIRZA reduces the mitigation overheads by 28.5×. Compared to PRAC, MIRZA has 45× lower area overheads and negligible slowdown (0.36% average slowdown vs. 6.5% for PRAC).

title: Predicting Mental Health Conditions from Survey Data
authors: Mahesh Babu Gujjarlamudi, Swapna Bommanaboina, VenkataMohan Porumamilla, Harish Sharma Thurlapati
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Mental health disorders in the workplace, especially in the technology sector, affect an employee’s performance, job satisfaction, and overall health of the organization. This research uses machine learning techniques to predict mental health conditions within the workplace using data collected from the technology sector. We trained various classification algorithms, namely K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Random Forest, Support Vector Machine (SVM), Decision Tree, and evaluate their performance in identifying individuals with mental illness. Comprising survey responses, the dataset measures attitudes towards mental health and frequencies of mental health disorders in tech workplace environments. Our study results show that the model with the highest prediction accuracy is the Random Forest Classifier, at 73.81%. It is followed by SVM (70.63%), KNN (68.52%), and Decision Tree (64.29%). The research will help in early intervention strategies through the development of a reliable prediction framework which will help the healthcare professional and organizations determine the employees who need the required support services. The results show that ensemble learning methods for mental health prediction are effective and offer a basis for implementing early mental health screening in workplaces. This study furthers the use of AI in health and provides helpful insights into the mental health workings of technology-oriented workplaces.

title: Evolutionary State and Dual Sub-Swarm Assisted Particle Swarm Optimization for Image Thresholding Problem
authors: Kuldeep Gautam, Shubham Gupta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Particle swarm optimization (PSO) is one of the fundamental and widely used swarm intelligence-based meta-heuristic algorithms to solve continuous optimization problems. Its performance is highly sensitive to its parameters and the velocity rule. To address this challenge, an evolutionary state and dual sub-swarm assisted PSO named EDS-PSO is proposed in this paper. In the EDS-PSO, the evolutionary state of the particles and the time-varying parameter are adopted to construct two sub-swarms to improve the exploitation and exploration abilities in the algorithm. Secondly, a weighted center scheme and historical experience are used to define the velocity rules of particles in these sub-swarms with time-varying rules for inertia weight and acceleration coefficients. Later, to specifically solve multilevel thresholding, a rounding-off procedure is applied to transform the search space from continuous to integer space. To validate the search procedure of the EDS-PSO algorithm, it has been applied to solve the image threshold segmentation problem for 100 images. Comparison with other metaheuristic algorithms using several performance metrics verifies the effectiveness of the proposed EDS-PSO algorithm.

title: Noise Reduction in Underwater Acoustic Signals Using a Hybrid Wavelet Transform and Graph Neural Network (GNN) Model
authors: Pavaimalar S, Nivethitha S, Vinusha V, ReenaSri K K, Rubadharshini G, Karuppasamy L
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Underwater acoustic communication is the backbone of applications such as submarine navigation, autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs), environmental monitoring, and defense systems. However, reliable signal transmission remains a major challenge due to severe noise interference caused by multipath propagation, Doppler shifts, marine life, turbulence, and ship engine noise. Conventional denoising methods, including Wiener filtering, spectral subtraction, and deep learning approaches such as Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), are limited in capturing the highly dynamic and graph-like structure of underwater acoustic channels. This paper proposes a novel Hybrid Wavelet Transform and Graph Neural Network (GNN) model for noise reduction in underwater acoustic signals. The Hybrid Wavelet Transform (HWT) decomposes the noisy signal into multiple subbands, efficiently separating useful low-frequency components from noise-dominated high-frequency components. These subband features are then mapped into a graph structure, where nodes represent wavelet coefficients, and edges encode multipath and Doppler correlations. A GNN-based denoising framework, equipped with graph attention mechanisms and physics-guided priors, is employed to suppress incoherent noise while preserving essential acoustic features. The proposed model is unique, domain-specific, and tailored to underwater environments. Preliminary analysis shows that it can outperform existing CNN-based methods, achieving improved Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) and Perceptual Evaluation of Speech Quality (PESQ) by 1–3%, while offering robustness against highly non-stationary underwater noise.

title: Co-Optimization of Electricity and Gas in Islanded Hybrid Systems Through an Adaptive Risk-Cost Frontier Analysis
authors: Mehrdad Ghahramani, Daryoush Habibi, Asma Aziz
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Isolated hybrid power systems combining electricity and gas networks offer an effective solution for reliable and resilient energy supply in off-grid communities. The increasing share of renewable resources, especially wind power, along with demand forecast errors, poses significant operational challenges. This paper presents a day-ahead co-optimization framework that links electricity and gas networks with unit commitment and employs a p-robust optimization method to manage uncertainty in both wind generation and load. Unlike deterministic or traditional robust models, the proposed approach provides a controllable balance between cost and reliability through the robustness parameter, while maintaining computational efficiency. Demand response is incorporated into both electricity and gas systems, enhancing flexibility and reducing the need for costly network upgrades. To demonstrate the model's effectiveness, two case studies are included. Results show that the p-robust formulation clearly defines a risk-cost frontier, keeping unserved energy at zero with only moderate cost increases. Compared with deterministic and Monte Carlo methods, the proposed model improves both reliability and resilience, offering a practical and efficient scheduling strategy.

publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
