title: Analysis of Undamped Oscillations and Blackout Risk in Inverter-Driven Energy Grids with Key Control Strategies
authors: Md Shamim Hasan, Nayeemur Rahman, Sukumar Kamalasadan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The increasing penetration of inverter-based resources (IBRs) is fundamentally reshaping the dynamics of bulk power systems. With synchronous machines replaced, natural inertia and damping are significantly reduced, leaving grids vulnerable to poorly damped or sustained oscillations. This paper investigates a critical failure mode in systems where more than two-thirds of the required power is supplied by IBRs. It is demonstrated that undamped voltage and frequency oscillations, once actuated by a disturbance, can persist and even amplify rather than decay. These oscillations interact with ride-through and protection thresholds, causing sequential IBR tripping and potentially initiating cascading blackouts. Time-domain simulations on both a single-IBR infinite bus system and a modified IEEE 39-bus network illustrate how a localized disturbance can escalate into an area-wide blackout. The results demonstrate how sustained oscillations under grid-following (GFL) inverters can destabilize the system and propagate disconnections, while gridforming (GFM) control suppresses oscillations, ensuring stability. The findings underscore oscillatory instability as a hidden but severe blackout risk in high-IBR systems and highlight the effectiveness of GFM inverters as a mitigation measure.

title: Comparative Analysis of Transformer Models in Disaster Tweet Classification for Public Safety
authors: Sharif Noor Zisad, N. M. Istiak Chowdhury, Ragib Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: Twitter and other social media platforms have become vital sources of real time information during disasters and public safety emergencies. Automatically classifying disaster related tweets can help emergency services respond faster and more effectively. Traditional Machine Learning (ML) models such as Logistic Regression, Naive Bayes, and Support Vector Machines have been widely used for this task, but they often fail to understand the context or deeper meaning of words, especially when the language is informal, metaphorical, or ambiguous. We posit that, in this context, transformer based models can perform better than traditional ML models. In this paper, we evaluate the effectiveness of transformer based models, including BERT, DistilBERT, RobERTa, and DeBERTa, for classifying disaster related tweets. These models are compared with traditional ML approaches to highlight the performance gap. Experimental results show that BERT achieved the highest accuracy <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(91 \%)$</tex>, significantly outperforming traditional models like Logistic Regression and Naive Bayes (both at 82%). The use of contextual embeddings and attention mechanisms allows transformer models to better understand subtle language in tweets, where traditional ML models fall short. This research demonstrates that transformer architectures are far more suitable for public safety applications, offering improved accuracy, deeper language understanding, and better generalization across real world social media text.

title: An Adaptive Compensation Strategy for Concurrent Current Harmonic Suppression and Power Ripple Mitigation for Grid- Tied Solar PV System
authors: Manash Kumar Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper presents an adaptive compensation strategy for harmonic suppression and power ripple elimination in grid-tied solar photovoltaic (GTSPV) systems operating under harmonically distorted and weak grid voltages. An adaptive dual multi-resonant (ADMR) current controller is employed to mitigate grid current harmonics and power ripples under these adverse grid conditions. The adaptive functionality is realized through an integrated adaptive enhanced reduced-order generalized integrator frequency-locked loop (AERO-FLL), which simultaneously provides grid synchronization with minimized steady-state oscillations and eliminated DC offset. Also, the AERO-FLL provides the estimated frequency to the ADMR controller. The entire strategy is implemented directly within the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\alpha} \boldsymbol{\beta}$</tex> reference frame, eliminating the need for a dedicated phase- locked loop (PLL) and thereby reducing computational complexity on the digital microcontroller. Experimental validation using an OPALRT OP4510 real-time simulator demonstrates the superior effectiveness of the proposed compensation strategy compared to conventional compensation strategy.

title: SEADGAT: A Heterogeneous Graph Attention Network with Cross Distillation for Encrypted Traffic Classification
authors: JiaZhong Lu, Kun Yu, YuanYuan Huang, Zhitan Wei, JiaLi Yin, Xiaolei Liu
abstract: With the increasing severity of network security threats, encrypted traffic identification has become a core challenge in the field of network security. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have demonstrated significant potential in capturing the complex association patterns among encrypted traffic byte units, owing to their powerful structural modeling capabilities. However, traditional homogeneous graph modeling methods struggle to fully characterize the multidimensional heterogeneous relationships between headers and payloads in encrypted traffic. Although Heterogeneous Graph Neural Networks (HGNNs) can address such complexity, they suffer from parameter redundancy and a sharp increase in training overhead due to independent edge-type modeling, while lacking a mechanism for collaborative knowledge transfer across edge types. To address this, this paper proposes a Cross-Type Distillation mechanism, which constructs a unified structural representation path to enable bidirectional knowledge transfer between heterogeneous edge types. This approach enhancing the feature expression capability of weak semantic edges. Building on this foundation, we introduce the malicious traffic detection model SEADGAT, which employs an edge-type weight-sharing mechanism to compress the propagation weights of multiple edge types into a unified representation space and integrates them into graph attention computation. This substantially reduces training time while preserving the ability to perceive structural differences. Based on a heterogeneous graph framework, SEADGAT accurately characterizes the complex dependencies between byte units and between headers and payloads, combined with a dynamic fusion mechanism to generate comprehensive traffic representations. DFUSE is enhanced with gated cross-interactions for adaptive multimodal fusion. Experiments on packet-level and flow-level classification across multiple encrypted traffic datasets demonstrate that SEADGAT outperforms existing methods in classification accuracy, training efficiency, and model parameter scale.

title: Spectral Feature Gating Adapter for Hyperspectral Object Tracking
authors: Wanlin Zhao, Yunhe Zhang, Songyuheng Gao, Gang He
abstract: Hyperspectral object tracking has garnered significant attention due to its ability to leverage rich spectral information for robust performance in challenging scenarios. However, existing methods often rely on transferring RGB-based pre-trained networks, which suffer from spectral information loss during band selection or computational inefficiency in band regrouping strategies. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel spectral feature gating adapter for hyperspectral object tracking (SGAT). SGAT introduces a lightweight adapter-based architecture to optimize band regrouping, enabling efficient and adaptive fusion of spectral features. The proposed Multiple Band Adapter (MBA) module extracts discriminative spectral representations from regrouped band subsets, while the Dynamic Fusion Gate (DFG) module dynamically weights and integrates these features based on their relevance to the tracking task. By jointly leveraging the MBA and DFG, SGAT effectively preserves critical spectral information while minimizing computational overhead. Extensive experiments on HOTC dataset demonstrate that SGAT achieves state-of-the-art performance, outperforming existing trackers.

title: High-Performance 3D Liquid-Metal Tactile Sensing System with Data-Driven Signal Decoding for Dynamic Force Estimation
authors: Shuang Wang, Ziyi Song, Liangkai Chen, Xin Wang, Le Xie
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Tactile sensing plays a crucial role in human-robot interaction, surgical assistance, and intelligent operation. However, achieving stable and accurate perception of multidimensional contact forces under dynamic contact conditions still presents challenges in structural design and signal decoding. To address this issue, this paper proposes a three-dimensional tactile sensing system based on gallium-indium (GaIn) liquid metal for acquiring and estimating triaxial force information during dynamic contact. This sensor employs a multi-layered flexible structure and a distributed sensing unit design, enabling simultaneous sensing of normal and tangential force components. Considering the strong coupling characteristics of liquid metal tactile signals in both spatial and temporal dimensions, this paper introduces a data-driven decoding method combining convolutional neural networks and long short-term memory networks (CNN–LSTM) to reconstruct continuous triaxial contact forces from multi-channel tactile signals, achieving prediction accuracy superior to various baseline regression models. Experimental results demonstrate the feasibility of this liquid metal tactile sensing scheme in dynamic three-dimensional force estimation tasks, providing an effective tactile sensing means for precise robot manipulation and safe human-robot collaboration.

title: Visual-in-Visual: A Unified and Efficient Baseline for Image Restoration_supp1-3669720.pdf
authors: Yuning Cui
abstract: Recent years have witnessed remarkable progress in image restoration, yet achieving both high performance and efficiency remains a persistent challenge. To address this issue, we present VIVNet, a strong and efficient unified baseline designed to balance accuracy and practicality. Drawing inspiration from the high efficiency of the human visual system, VIVNet embeds a biologically inspired micro visual module into each block of a macro U -shaped vision architecture. This module mimics key perceptual processes such as retinal encoding, lateral inhibition, and high-order processing by combining lightweight depth- wise convolutions for multi-receptive-field feature extraction, a similarity-aware weighting mechanism to emphasize informative signals, and high-order interactions implemented via iterative element- wise multiplication to capture complex dependencies. This design enhances the model's representational capacity while maintaining computational efficiency. Unlike most existing methods that are limited to narrow task settings, we evaluate VIVNet across a wide range of scenarios, including general, all-in-one, and composite degradation tasks, as well as ultra-high-definition (UHD), underwater, medical, and remote sensing datasets. Extensive experiments show that VIVNet delivers competitive performance with high efficiency.

title: SASG-DA: Sparse-Aware Semantic-Guided Diffusion Augmentation For Myoelectric Gesture Recognition
authors: Chen Liu, Can Han, Weishi Xu, Yaqi Wang, Dahong Qian
publish: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Surface electromyography (sEMG)-based gesture recognition plays a critical role in human-machine interaction (HMI), particularly for rehabilitation and prosthetic control. However, sEMG-based systems often suffer from the scarcity of informative training data, leading to overfitting and poor generalization in deep learning models. Data augmentation offers a promising approach to increasing the size and diversity of training data, where faithfulness and diversity are two critical factors to effectiveness. However, promoting untargeted diversity can result in redundant samples with limited utility. To address these challenges, we propose a novel diffusion-based data augmentation approach, Sparse-Aware Semantic-Guided Diffusion Augmentation (SASG-DA). To enhance generation faithfulness, we introduce the Semantic Representation Guidance (SRG) mechanism by leveraging fine-grained, task-aware semantic representations as generation conditions. To enable flexible and diverse sample generation, we propose a Gaussian Modeling Semantic Sampling (GMSS) strategy, which models the semantic representation distribution and allows stochastic sampling to produce both faithful and diverse samples. To enhance targeted diversity, we further introduce a Sparse-Aware Semantic Sampling strategy to explicitly explore underrepresented regions, improving distribution coverage and sample utility. Extensive experiments on benchmark sEMG datasets, Ninapro DB2, DB4, and DB7, demonstrate that SASG-DA significantly outperforms existing augmentation methods. Overall, our proposed data augmentation approach effectively mitigates overfitting and improves recognition performance and generalization by offering both faithful and diverse samples.

title: Performance of M -ary Pulse Position Modulated Underwater OWC Links with Turbulence and Path Loss Effects
authors: Sajid Mumtaz, Mohammad Furqan Ali, Mohd Fadzli Mohd Salleh, Faezah Jasman, Imran Shafique Ansari, Mubeen Aslam, Dushantha Nalin K. Jayakody, Luís Miguel Campos
abstract: Recent years have seen a surge in the usage of optical signals for real-time wireless data transfer in a wide range of underwater applications because of their large bandwidth, higher data rates, low latency, security, power, and cost efficiency. However, underwater optical wireless communication (UOWC) is substantially impaired by absorption, scattering, and turbulence effects that induce scintillations during optical signal propagation. A potential modulation scheme can be a promising solution to overcome such scintillations. The pulse position modulation (PPM) may address the fading and optical irradiance across the underwater channels while providing the power efficiency to the wireless sensor networks (WSNs). To access the link performance under the proposed modulation scheme, the closed-form expressions for outage probability and BER metrics are typically required. Unlike horizontal and single-layered models, this work evaluates the performance of vertical cascaded links in the North and Baltic oceans affected by the combined effects of absorption, scattering, and turbulence by utilizing an M-ary PPM scheme. The unified expressions for the probability density function (PDF) and cumulative distribution function (CDF) are obtained by integrating depth-dependent layered Gamma-Gamma (GG) turbulence with absorption- and scattering-based path-loss (PL) attenuations. The closed-form expressions for the outage probability (OP) and the average bit error rate (ABER) are derived by following the derived PDF and CDF to evaluate the end-to-end (E2E) performance underM-ary PPM. The analytical results are validated using experimentally extracted channel parameters and rigorous Monte-Carlo (MC) simulations.

title: SPEX: A Vision-Language Model for Land Cover Extraction on Spectral Remote Sensing Images
authors: Dongchen Si, Di Wang, Erzhong Gao, Xiaolei Qin, Liu Zhao, Jing Zhang, Minqiang Xu, Jianbo Zhan, Jianshe Wang, Lin Liu, Bo Du, Liangpei Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Spectral information has long been recognized as a critical cue in remote sensing observations. Although numerous vision-language models have been developed for pixel-level interpretation, spectral information remains underutilized, resulting in suboptimal performance, particularly in multispectral scenarios. To address this limitation, we construct a vision-language instruction-following dataset named SPIE, which encodes spectral priors of land-cover objects into textual attributes recognizable by large language models (LLMs), based on classical spectral index computations. Leveraging this dataset, we propose SPEX, a multimodal LLM designed for instruction-driven land cover extraction. To this end, we introduce several carefully designed components and training strategies, including multiscale feature aggregation, token context condensation, and multispectral visual pre-training, to achieve precise and flexible pixel-level interpretation. To the best of our knowledge, SPEX is the first multimodal vision-language model dedicated to land cover extraction in spectral remote sensing imagery. Extensive experiments on five public multispectral datasets demonstrate that SPEX consistently outperforms existing state-of-the-art methods in extracting typical land cover categories such as vegetation, buildings, and water bodies. Moreover, SPEX is capable of generating textual explanations for its predictions, thereby enhancing interpretability and user-friendliness. Code will be released at: https://github.com/MiliLab/SPEX.

title: Suppression Method for Stochastic Line Errors in Grating Interferometers Based on Spatial Wavefront Phase Analysis Theory
authors: Wenyuan Zhou, Yujia Sun, Zhaowu Liu, Yuhang Cui, Lin Liu, Wenhao Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Grating interferometers have been extensively adopted in modern precision manufacturing industries. Diffraction grat-ings are the core components of grating interferometric displace-ment measurement systems. Notably, stochastic line errors of the grating introduce additional phase errors into the phase infor-mation of diffracted beams, ultimately degrading measurement precision. To eliminate the interference from stochastic line errors, this study establishes the spatial wavefront phase analysis theory (SWPAT). The adverse effects of stochastic line errors on the phase of diffracted beams are investigated, and a method of using large-diameter beams is proposed to suppress these errors. To val-idate this method, a heterodyne Littrow grating interferometric experimental setup is built, and controlled experiments with four different beam diameters are designed using the control variable method. Experimental results demonstrate that when the meas-urement beam diameter increases from 1 to 4 mm, the maximum displacement error is reduced by 34.3%, and the 3σ value is re-duced by 45.9%, thus verifying the correctness of SWPAT. This work enriches the theoretical framework for error compensation in grating interferometric displacement measurement systems and holds significant importance for improving displacement meas-urement precision.

title: Handling Traffic Uncertainty in Failure-Tolerant Traffic Engineering_supp1-3670142.pdf
authors: Jiaqi Zheng
abstract: Network failure uncertainty and traffic demand uncertainty pose a series of challenges to efficiently solve network traffic engineering (TE) problems in the production wide-area networks (WANs). Prior works especially predict-then-optimize paradigm in TE systems encounter a significant mismatch between the upstream prediction task and the downstream optimization task, leading to potential demand loss risk and further degrading the performance of customer-facing applications in the cloud. In this paper, we present TUFTTE, a risk-driven TE optimization framework, where the objective function and constraints can be flexibly specified by the network operators. We employ a differentiable optimization layer to guide the prediction model with the objective of minimizing the network risks. When the network risk is defined as the demand loss, our approach can provably converge to the global optimum. Experimental results on real-world topology demonstrate that TUFTTE reduces the demand loss by 11.59% on average compared to the state-of-the- art algorithm. We believe that our paper deeply explores the potential of prediction and promotes the understanding on how to develop and deploy a general learning module into the commercial TE optimization.

title: Accelerating Inter-App Communications for Time-Critical O-RAN Control Loops
authors: Lorenzo Rosa, Andrea Garbugli, Domenico Scotece, Luca Foschini
abstract: The Open Radio Access Network (O-RAN) is reshaping cellular architectures through disaggregation, openness, and programmability, enabling intelligent control across modular RAN components. A growing ecosystem of user-defined control-plane applications, namely rApps, xApps, and the emerging dApps, operates at different timescales and unlocks advanced control loop capabilities, but introduces diverse and stringent Quality of Service (QoS) requirements for communication. Current implementations typically rely on cloud-based messaging systems, which privilege transparency and ease of use for developers and limit support for time-sensitive workloads. In this paper, we propose that O-RAN control loops base their internal communication on INSANE, a cloud-native, data-centric middleware that supports multiple networking stacks, including kernel-bypass option such as eBPF XDP, DPDK, and RDMA. Our preliminary evaluation shows that INSANE achieves nearly 2× higher throughput than widely used alternatives, while reducing 99.9th-percentile latency of over an order of magnitude for small messages. At the same time, INSANE preserves a uniform and easy-to-use programming interface. These results highlight INSANE as a promising foundation for faster, more predictable control loops, a significant step toward the ultimate goal of enabling AI-driven RAN optimizations in O-RAN systems.

title: A 55 MHz Bandwidth and Sub- μ V Offset Precision Amplifier With Two-Step Dynamic Offset Calibration and Noise Suppression Techniques
authors: Yang Zhang, Xinyan Xue, Zi Wang, Mingyi Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: A fully differential offset-stabilized amplifier with a two-step dynamic offset calibration achieving sub-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>V offset and a 55 MHz gain-bandwidth product (GBW) is presented. The two-step offset calibration combines digitally-assisted auto-calibration with dynamic offset compensation (DOC) that maintains ultralow residual offset. The slow-settling auto-zeroing (AZ) technique is integrated with a proposed chopping scheme, which suppresses noise across the entire bandwidth, enabling higher bandwidth operation with reduced ripple and noise aliasing. Fabricated in a 180 nm BCD process, the prototype occupies an area of 1.32 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>. The measurements show that the input-referred offset standard deviation is reduced by 99.97%, from 0.8 mV to 206nV. Furthermore, the noise spectrum is nearly flat with the high-frequency ripple effectively suppressed. A noise floor of 7.8 nV/<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\surd }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>Hz has been achieved, corresponding to a noise efficiency factor (NEF) of 15. These results demonstrate its potential to be widely adopted in high-accuracy and high-bandwidth applications.

title: An Ecosystemic Approach for the Seamless Integration of Terrestrial and Non-Terrestrial Network Connections
authors: Claudio Sacchi, Carmen D’Andrea, Elisa Conti, Tommaso Foggi, Amina Piemontese, Alessandro Ugolini, Armando Vannucci, Camilo Rojas, Nour Badini, Fabio Patrone, Mario Marchese, Fulvio Babich, Massimiliano Comisso, Alberto Carini, Francesco Adamo, Simone Pauletto, Henok Berhanu Tsegaye, Petro Mushidi Tshakwanda, Michael Devetsikiotis
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: An unrestricted, global, and ubiquitous connectivity, available to anyone, everywhere, and anytime, is a key objective of the 6G vision. A true, seamless integration of terrestrial (T) and non-terrestrial (NT) networks is mandatory to reach such an ambitious target. From this perspective, the three-year ITA-NTN project (2023-2025), funded by the European Union within the framework of NextGenerationEU work program, aims to investigate advanced solutions for the seamless integration of Terrestrial and Non-Terrestrial networks. This paper considers the project aspects concerning the provision of seamless connectivity across the heterogeneous T and NT layers by proposing an integrated ecosystem, which enables transparent information exchange among the various communicating nodes belonging to each layer. The ecosystem relies on the software-based management of fully reconfigurable network architectures based on disruptive concepts like cell-free multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO), full software-defined radio (SDR)-based multi-waveforms physical layer design, use of aggressive capacity-oriented radio resource management strategies based on Non Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA), extended use of Software-Defined Networking (SDN), and Artificial Intelligence (AI) to monitor and manage the entire network architecture. The discussion of the achieved results validates the proposed ecosystemic approach both in terms of viability and quantitative performance improvement.

title: Message from the General Chairs: NISS 2025
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Networking, Intelligent Systems &amp; Security (NISS)

title: Proceedings
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Networking, Intelligent Systems &amp; Security (NISS)

title: Contextual Embedding Fusion for Quick-Commerce Reviews: Rating, 5-Class Classification, and Sentiment Analysis Across Classical ML, DL, and Transformer Models
authors: Monica Ranka, Amit Malhotra, Bhupendra singh hada, Samiran Jana, Abhishek Maheshwari, Md Shaik Amzad Basha
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Quick-commerce platforms depend on accurate reading of customer feedback to steer pricing and service quality. We study a dataset of delivery-agent reviews, and build two rating predictors and a text-only classifier suite. On the rating regression task (1–5), we benchmark seven models (Ridge, Lasso, ElasticNet, SVR-RBF, Gradient Boosting, Random Forest, XGBoost). The best test performance is achieved by RandomForestRegressor with MAE = 0.527, RMSE = 0.973, and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> = 0.555; cross-validation places XGBoost and Gradient Boosting close behind. For 5-class rating classification, we compare seven TF–IDF baselines (LogReg, LinearSVC, two SGD variants, RidgeClassifier, ComplementNB, XGBClassifier) and find SGD (hinge) strongest (Accuracy = 0.817, Macro-F1 = 0.389), yet mid-rating classes (2–4) remain difficult. To address label ordinality, we fine-tune DistilBERT with ordinal heads: CORAL and CORN. These achieve Accuracy = 0.837/0.819 and Macro-F1 = 0.439/0.441, improving Macro-F1 by +5–6 points over TF–IDF baselines, mainly by rescuing classes 2–4. We further map regression outputs to 5 classes via optimized cutpoints, raising Macro-F1 from 0.329 (rounding) to 0.380. Paired, fold-wise comparisons using Wilcoxon signed-rank and bootstrap 95% CIs show consistent gains; with 5 folds the discretized p = 0.0625 indicates all folds favor the better model, suggesting statistical significance with more folds. Overall, ordinal transformers and learned thresholds provide measurable, reproducible improvements for short, noisy e-commerce reviews, delivering higher fidelity on mid-range ratings while preserving high accuracy.

title: A framework for personalized treatment and symptom-based diagnosis of neurological and cardiovascular disorders using machine learning
authors: Atul Kumar, Harsh Sharma, Thipendra P Singh, Mahendra Kumar, Ashish Baiswar
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: One of the most significant tasks in modern medicine is the timely identification of cardiovascular and neuro-related pathologies. The article additionally examines the transformative ability of deep learning (DL) and subsequent machine learning (ML) in enhancing the accuracy and efficiency of diagnostics in conditions such as Alzheimer's and Parkinson's diseases, epilepsy, brain tumours, etc, and cardiovascular diseases. We provide a general outline of the state-of-the-art ML techniques, including state-of-the-art classification algorithms, feature extraction techniques, and significant biomedical data. We have demonstrated that AI-based models may be applied in the setting of the early development of the disease, an individual approach to treatment, and expense savings in healthcare. They will be more applicable in the screening and the real-time monitoring phases. We also observe the principle of data mining, specifically, the entropy predictive models, besides the decision trees. The current article identifies the need to have effective, scalable AI tools in the clinical setting because it stresses the need to reduce the burden of threatening diseases globally in life, with assistance provided by early intervention that relies on ML. The insights obtained are the path to the future of predictive healthcare analytics and intelligent systems for managing diseases.

title: A Comprehensive comparative study of Supervised Machine Learning Models for Network Threat Detection
authors: Vijaya Ramineni, Purushottam Kumar, Arun Kumar B.T, Prakash Kuppuswamy, Suresh Gorantla, Ahamed Ali Shaik Meeran
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Network anomaly detection has become a critical component of cybersecurity due to the increasing scale and complexity of modern network traffic. Traditional supervised machine learning methods often face limitations when applied to high-dimensional datasets, as redundant and noisy features can reduce their accuracy and efficiency. To address this issue, a hybrid model combining Principal Component Analysis (PCA) with Random Forest (RF) is employed. PCA serves as a dimensionality reduction technique, extracting the most relevant components of traffic data and improving model scalability. The Random Forest classifier then operates on this reduced feature space, enabling robust classification of normal and anomalous behaviors. This integrated approach enhances detection accuracy, lowers false alarm rates, and offers improved computational performance compared to conventional supervised models. The hybrid PCA–RF framework demonstrates strong potential for application in large-scale, dynamic network environments.

title: Self-Supplied Power Type and Capacity Calibration Technique Based on CNN-TCN Algorith
authors: Hengyu Liu, Qingqi Zhao, Zuoxia Xing, Qitong Fu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: To solve the problem of mismatch between the actual self-supplied power configuration of customers with power supply and the registry of grid companies, a selfsupplied power calibration technique based on CNN-TCN is proposed. The method first establishes the corresponding output probability model by collecting the power generation and usage data of existing photovoltaic, self-supplied power plants, and electric vehicle charging and discharging equipment, and applying Newton's interpolation method and improved normalization for data processing. Next, a convolutional neural network fully extracts the spatiotemporal features of power generation and utilization data, and verifies the types of power sources in the self-provided power supply. Finally, the full connection layer outputs the final prediction results for self-provided power supply configurations. Experimental analysis is conducted using actual power generation and consumption data from a specific region. The case study indicates that the model achieves an average type verification accuracy of 99.405%, with capacity verification accuracy ranging from 2.213% to 10.57% across different power supply types.

title: A Unified Control Scheme to Facilitate Grid-Forming and Grid-Following Services in Microgrids with Hydrogen Energy Storage and Electric Vehicles
authors: Kuldeep Kumar, Kashem M. Muttaqi, Danny Sutanto
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper proposes a unified control scheme that facilitates both grid-forming and grid-following services using hydrogen energy storage and electric vehicles in microgrids. The contribution of the present study is that required power-sharing is achieved by implementing the unified control scheme at the AC side of the inverters. Due to the difference in the output response of the hydrogen technology, i.e., fuel cell and electric vehicles (battery), different control schemes for grid-forming inverters are proposed. During the grid-forming applications for fuel cell, inertia-based control is used for inverters in which the fuel cell inverter compensates constant power fluctuations, while a supercapacitor addresses high power fluctuations. A droop-based control scheme is proposed for grid-forming applications with electric vehicles. Grid-forming inverters provide the required active and reactive power to the load by controlling their voltage and frequency. In grid-following mode, the inverter injects the required amount of current into the grid using the fuel cell or electric vehicle. The proposed unified control strategy is validated in simulation and the hardware-in-the-loop experiments, and it is found that it satisfactorily supplies the required active and reactive power at the AC bus of microgrids.

title: Capacity Optimisation in Renewable-Rich Power Grid Using Grid Enhancing Technologies
authors: Jiatong Wang, Zhiquan Zhou, Phanindra Ganivada, Wenyuan Bai, Kashem Muttaqi, Danny Sutanto
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The increasing penetration of renewable energy sources presents a critical challenge to power system operation, specifically in balancing generation intermittency with grid stability and minimizing energy curtailment. Existing grid enhancing technologies are often applied in isolation and lack the system-level coordination required for maximum effectiveness. This paper proposes a coordinated control strategy that integrates three complementary technologies: soft open point (SOP), dynamic line rating (DLR), and dynamic operating envelope (DOE). The proposed framework assigns specific functions to each technology. DLR determines real time transmission capacity from meteorological and operational data. DOE converts system security constraints into dynamic power limits for end users. SOP manages power flows within the distribution network to adhere to the established DOE boundaries. The synergy between these technologies creates a closed-loop system based on quantification, control, and utilization. An optimal power flow model is employed to simultaneously enhance grid stability margins and maximize the integration of renewable energy. Mathematical models of the proposed strategy are developed, and their effectiveness is validated through multiple scenarios. The results indicate that the coordinated strategy substantially reduces energy curtailment and maintains grid stability during disturbances.

title: Topology-Aware vs Topology-Agnostic Deep RL for Microgrid SoC Balancing
authors: Iacovos I. Ioannou, Saher Javaid, Yasuo Tan, Vasos Vassiliou
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Maintaining a balanced State of Charge (SoC) across distributed Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) is essential for reliable microgrid operation. We compare three deep reinforcement learning (DRL) controllers for SoC balancing: two topology-agnostic baselines (MLP-PPO and SAC) and a topology-aware Graph Attention PPO (GAT-PPO). On a modified IEEE 13-bus feeder with realistic PV/load profiles, MLP-PPO achieves the best balancing with the lowest mean absolute SoC deviation <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{1 7. 7 6 \%})$</tex> and lowest energy losses <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(673.52 \text{kWh})$</tex>, while also being fastest to train and infer (26.35 s training; 2.75 s/episode evaluation). SAC delivers the highest average reward and markedly higher load-serving efficiency (92.11%) at the cost of weaker balancing (21.78% mean absolute deviation) and higher losses (928.17 kWh). The topology-aware GAT-PPO attains 100% load-serving efficiency but exhibits pathological behavior-severely degraded balancing (47.70%) and the highest losses (1806.15 kWh) -consistent with a reward-compliant yet task-misaligned policy. These findings indicate that (i) strong balancing and low losses can be achieved without topology encoding (MLP-PPO), (ii) Soft Actor-Critic (SAC) offers a practical efficiency-reward compromise, and (iii) topology awareness can amplify reward loopholes unless the objective tightly aligns with the operational goal.

authors: J Gladson Maria Britto, Anantha Raman G R, Sravanthi K, Sateesh Nagavarapu, Sandela Naga Sharanya

title: A Modular Machine Learning Framework for Personalized Health Monitoring and Risk Prediction
authors: Sonukumar Pandit, Mohamad Yasin Thara, Aswathy RNair, Shilpa Singhal, Monisha Mohan
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: A modular machine learning scheme to model disease risk and track personal health is introduced in this paper, with five scaled, algorithms designed to compute diet suggestion, illness forecasting, ECG pattern, fitness analysis, and health risk evaluation. Every module is domain optimized in accuracy and interpretability. The Diet Module using Random Forest has achieved the hyperparameter of 89.2 accuracy, whereas the Disease Module with the hyperparameter of 86.7 and the value of F1-score of 0.84. The ECG Module with ensemble and embedded learning as Random Forest, XGBoost, LightGBM, and Gradient Boosting had accuracy of 93.5 percent and AUC of 0.96. Fitness Module (Linear Regression) gave an R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> score of 0.82 and the Risk Module (XGBoost) got an accuracy of 91.3 percent with an F1-score of 0.89. High quality of inputs was achieved by strict preprocessing steps such as denoising, normalization, encoding and segmentation. The framework has significant potential of personalized, data-based health information and may be utilized in further clinical integration and digital health. This work is novel because it integrates five different modules (diet, disease, ECG, fitness, and risk) into one modular ML architecture to enable monitoring of holistic health.

title: Harnessing Computing Power for Machine Learning and Deep Learning: An In-Depth Review of Hardware, CUDA Core Architectures and their Impact on Performance
authors: Md. Hasib Hasan, A.F.M Mahmud Ullah, Mohammed Anwer, Mahady Hasan, Md. Sadekur Rahman, Md. Tarek Habib
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) have emerged as transformative paradigms in modern computing, underpinning applications from natural language processing to autonomous systems and medical diagnostics. Central to their success is the computational acceleration provided by Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), particularly those based on NVIDIA's CUDA architecture. This study presents an in-depth analysis of CUDA-enabled GPU architectures spanning from Fermi to Hopper and their impact on ML/DL performance, scalability, and energy efficiency. Through detailed comparisons with traditional CPUs and specialized Tensor Processing Units (TPUs), we highlight the evolution of CUDA cores, memory hierarchies, and profiling tools that enable high-throughput, low-latency AI computation. Empirical studies, including convolution-heavy CNN tasks and real-time inference on edge devices, demonstrate substantial speedups and efficiency gains. We also explore GPU-specific optimizations such as kernel fusion, warp scheduling, and memory coalescing, emphasizing their role in accelerating training and inference. This study offers valuable insights into the hardware-software co-design strategies essential for scaling future AI workloads.

title: The Linear Algebra Foundations of Artificial Intelligence: A survey from Theory to Applications
authors: Namrata Karandikar, Tilottama Barhate
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Mathematical concepts are largely entangled in expanding models, analysing data and creating learning algorithms in Artificial Intelligence (AI). The most popular AI techniques such as Neural Networks, Natural Language processing and computer vision are based on concepts of Linear Algebra. This paper surveys the dependency of AI algorithms on Linear algebra concepts like vectors and matrices, singular value decomposition, Principal Component Analysis. A matrix decomposition to represent real world AI applications is discussed through a numerical example. Various challenges in applying Linear algebra operations for large datasets are also discussed in this paper.

title: AI-Based Real-Time Stress Monitoring in Autism Students: Instant Caregiver Alerts and Support Recommendations
authors: Mynavathi R, Rajendran P, Kanimozhi V, Buvaneswaran G, Mahalakshmi S
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The paper presents a real-time, camera-based intervention for stress detection and response for students with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). Due to their emotional sensitivity and communication difficulties, students with ASD might find it hard to express their feelings of stress, which could lead to meltdowns, withdrawal, or behavioral issues. Early detection becomes vital for their overall well-being and learning. The system targets facial expressions, eye movement, and postural information through a non-contact camera, meaning none of this monitoring is done through the use of a wearable device. AI-assisted image analysis gives 92.3% stress detection accuracy under classroom settings. On stress detection, caregivers are notified of the context and suggested calming strategies based on relaxation visuals or music, which decreased stress for 87% of the time. The custom interface logs incursions into the classroom and generates reports that can be used to track behavior trends. Such a passive approach to system integration can increase classroom engagement and personalized support by about 40%.

title: Early Detection of Sepsis Using Grid Search-Tuned XGBoost on ICU Clinical Data
authors: Mohit
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: In this research, we used the MIMIC-III clinical database to construct a machine learning model for predicting early-onset sepsis. Multiple clinical characteristics, such as vital signs and laboratory examinations, were compared. Characteristics significantly associated with sepsis by chi-square test were selected. Imputation of missing data was performed on group-computed (median) values by sepsis status. The XGBoost model was used and optimized with Grid search cross-validation for hyperparameter tuning. The model was trained and tested using stratified train-test split. We obtained a high level of performance with F1-score at 0.9995 and showed rare wrong classification cases. These results indicate that sepsis can be predicted early accurately using structured clinical data and preprocessed models.

title: Real-Time Energy Optimization in IoT Microgrids Using AI-Based Prediction Models for Solar Power Integration
authors: Mainak Biswas, Rajeswari Swain, Arnab Ghosh
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: As the necessity for clean energy rises, there is an extensive opportunity for solar-powered microgrids to function as a sustainable means of implementing decentralized power systems, particularly in remote applications. The transitory characteristics of solar irradiance generate issues of energy forecasting and realtime optimization. The present paper presents a real-time, artificial intelligence-based optimization framework, for IoT-enabled microgrids utilizing only solar energy. Utilizing a hybrid model incorporating a Multi-Head Attention (MHA) Deep Autoencoder and a XGBoost regressor for forecasting solar output, the proposed system was developed as part of a larger edge-fog-cloud architecture. Furthermore, it increased forecasting accuracy, decreased the latencies, and produced long-term energy forecasting to effectively load balance for overall.

title: Coordinated Control of a Data Center for Demand Response Participation
authors: Pam Paul Gyang, Pratyush Chakraborty, Lasantha Meegahapola, Xinghuo Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Data centers are increasingly seen as ideal candidates for demand response programs due to their operational flexibility, automation capabilities, and ability to manage workloads without compromising service quality. Their integration into demand response schemes not only enhances grid reliability but also reduces operational cost and environmental impact. This study develops an electrical and thermal dynamic model of a data center, aimed at evaluating its power consumption and thermal flexibility for safe participation in demand response programs. The model captures real-time interactions among server workloads, heat generation, and cooling system dynamics. A coordinated control framework is proposed to manage both server utilization and Heat Ventilation and Air Conditioning (HVAC) operations in response to external demand response signals. Three demand response strategies, load curtailment, load shifting, and coincidental peak management, are used to investigate the 1 MW data center response. Simulation results over 12 hours demonstrate the framework's effectiveness in controlling power demand and maintaining thermal stability, without compromising operational reliability. The findings highlight the potential of coordinated server and cooling control to unlock significant load flexibility, offering valuable insights for the design and operation of grid-interactive data centers.

title: A Novel Improvement of Wind Power Supply by Using the RBFN Algorithm
authors: Ch Hussaian Basha, S Resmara, S Venkateswarlu Reddy, Fini Fathima
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The negative impacts of power-producing plants that utilize fossil fuel on the environment have led to efforts of more on alternative sources of energy (AES) in the production of electricity. Deposits of energy in the form of fossil deposits are also depleting, thus making the transition to a cleaner one relevant. Solar, wind, hydro, and tidal energy, also known as Renewable Energy Resources (RER), are gaining popularity. Among the systems that have been facing rapid development are wind and PV systems (solar photovoltaic). These are natural resources that cannot be controlled due to the change of seasons and weather conditions. Such variability may cause shifts in energy production, which affects the power system stability. This would be handled through the application of the Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) methodology. MPPT enables renewable energy to be harvested with maximum energy in different situations. A Radial Basis Function Controller (RBFC) can be used to bring about an improvement in the efficiency of MPPT with a wind energy system. In a situation where wind turbines are equipped with a well-designed boost converter and RBFC simulated data show that it could render a constant flow of power.

title: PV-assisted EV Charging Station with G2V/V2G/V2V Features Utilizing Fuzzy Controller
authors: Subhojit Das, Sanjeev Kumar Pandey, Suman Maiti, Naba K. Peyada
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Most of the conventionally available EV chargers in the market are not capable to provide DC charging along with other features like G2V, V2G and V2V in the absence of a utility grid. Conventional chargers with V2V capability typically route power through the grid or central bus. This paper proposes a solution featuring G2V, V2G, and V2V capabilities integrated into a renewable-assisted charging station that functions as a Microgrid and can operate without the utility grid. Note that the BESS of the Microgrid makes it feasible to provide all the aforesaid features under the absence of utility grid. The V2V feature is implemented using an isolated DC-DC converter with a single magnetically coupled multi-winding transformer featuring multiple ports where input of the DC-DC converter is connected across the dc-bus of Microgrid. The EV charging ports can exchange power even in the absence of utility grid or Battery Energy Storage System (BESS). The control of the Microgrid along with the mutually coupled DC-DC converter has been developed and simulated in MATLAB/Simulink. The energy exchange between vehicle to BESS and vice versa under the absence of utility grid is also explored and necessary control methods have been proposed. The results demonstrate satisfactory performance of the overall EV charging system, with various modes of operation.

title: Performance Optimization of Big Data Analytics through Machine Learning
authors: Subhajit Roy, Jayashri Deb Sinha, Subir Gupta, Bibhuti Bhusan Dash, Utpal Chandra De, Sudhansu Shekhar Patra
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The massive expansion of digital data in healthcare, finance, and social media has both challenged and empowered the ability to extract meaningful insights from vast amounts of data. Traditional processing frameworks are not adequate due to the very large scale, high speed, and drastic heterogeneity of data. This article proposes a new framework that combines machine learning models with distributed big data platforms to deal with predictive accuracy and system scalability simultaneously. This work is new due to its dual focus on improving supervised, unsupervised, and ensemble learning models in large-scale computing environments such as Hadoop and Spark. The main aim is to assess to what degree machine learning-based methods can surpass the conventional drawbacks of big data analysis, such as long training time, inefficiency in clustering and classification, and handling heterogeneous datasets. The approach uses Random Forest and Support Vector Machines for classification and K-means clustering for unsupervised learning, with strict preprocessing and evaluation steps for ensuring robustness. Experimental outcomes prove Random Forest's best accuracy of 92%, which surpasses Support Vector Machines with 88%, and K-means with a silhouette coefficient of 0.74. Additionally, distributed execution lowered training time by almost 40%. All these outcomes confirm the fact that the proposed integration provides scalable, efficient, and accurate solutions for real-time data-driven decision-making.

title: Prediction of Lithium-Battery State of Charge Under Dynamic Maritime Environmental Conditions Using Machine Learning
authors: Mahmoud Elsisi, Shao-Hang Tsao, Yong-Zhe Huang, Cho-Han Hu, Chun-Lien Su
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Accurate State of Charge (SoC) prediction is critical for ensuring the safe and efficient operation of lithium-battery energy storage systems (BESS) in maritime environments. Unlike stationary or land-based systems, maritime batteries operate under rapidly changing environmental conditions as ships traverse different latitudes and climates. This study investigates SOC prediction under three representative dynamic variationsdry heat, cold, and humid heat-following the international DNVGL-CG-0339 certification guidelines. Controlled experiments were conducted to replicate these environmental transitions and collect operational datasets. Machine learning models were then trained to dynamically estimate SoC in real time, accounting for the effects of temperature and humidity fluctuations. Results show that the proposed approach maintains high prediction accuracy under variable conditions, enabling more reliable energy management, reducing the risk of unexpected power loss, and extending battery life. The findings provide a foundation for robust SoC monitoring in all-electric ship propulsion systems, supporting maritime certification compliance and advancing the integration of intelligent battery management within smart grid-enabled vessels.

title: Smart IoT- Enabled Portable Health Monitoring System for Real-Time Medical Applications
authors: Devunipally Hareeshwar, Gunti Vyshnavi, Gundu Jayanth, M. Srilatha
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: The rapidity of medical intervention is critical in the world today, larger still in underserved and remote com munities. The current project presents a small, portable health monitoring system with IoT characteristics for continuous real time applications. The use of 5 inexpensive off-the-shelf sensors attached to an ESP8266 NodeMCU microcontroller provides a small real-time health monitoring system that can monitor physiological parameters such as heart rate, body temperature, blood oxygen saturation (SpO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>), air quality, and fall detection. The monitored data from the sensors is presented locally on an LCD while simultaneously relayed wirelessly to a mobile application for remote health workers or caregivers to access the same information remotely. The device works through USB powering, which enables con sistent power supply and thereby usable across indexed locations such as emergency or rural settings. Our testing confirmed low error rate and consistent sensor measurement accuracy, typically less than 0.4% compared with commercial devices. The health monitoring system provides active health management via the home — for a number of medical conditions — while relieving health dichotomies in the primary health care system through monitoring. The utilization of embedded systems with wireless commu nication to transmit data to the Cloud for Health monitoring applications demonstrates advancements in telemedicine and provides an insight into the value of IoT for the future of healthcare.

title: Predictive Modeling of Body Fat Percentage with Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Bhagavant K Deshpande, Rajani Kodagali, V. Hemamalini, Justin Z, N. Ponnarasi, S D Prabu Ragavendiran
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: This study presents a comprehensive anthropometric body fat assessment dataset comprising records from 381 volunteers, each including frontal and lateral body images, biological data (age, sex, height, and weight), and anthropometric measurements. Body fat percentage (BFP) was recorded for 220 participants using laboratory-validated methods. Machine learning models—including linear regressors (Bayesian Ridge, Ridge Regression, Orthogonal Matching Pursuit) and ensemble methods (Random Forest and XGBoost)—were developed to estimate BFP. The Bayesian Ridge regressor achieved MAE = 4.14 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\pm 0.62)$</tex> and R2 = 0.580 <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\pm 0.203)$</tex> using image-derived features combined with biological data, while XGBoost achieved the lowest MAE and highest R2 across all models. These results demonstrate that accurate BFP estimation is possible without manual measurements, highlighting the feasibility of scalable, non-invasive, image-based assessment methods.

title: SpotCC: Facilitating Coded Computation for Prediction Serving Systems on Spot Instances
authors: Lin Wang, Yuchong Hu, Ziling Duan, Mingqi Li, Chenxuan Yao, Feifan Liu, Xiaolu Li, Leihua Qin, Dan Feng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: The growing adoption of prediction serving systems (PSSes) has made cost-saving deployment on preemptible spot instances crucial, yet frequent preemptions severely harm availability. While coded computation (CC) can keep availability cost-effectively by encoding original jobs into parity ones, its direct application to spot instances incurs prohibitive decoding overhead and tail latency under frequent preemptions. We identify two findings for optimization: (i) decoding asymmetry (only original job failures require decoding); (ii) preemption unevenness (variation in preemption rates across cloud regions). Leveraging these findings, we propose SpotCC, a new CC framework that strategically dispatches parity jobs to high-preemption (volatile) regions and original jobs to low-preemption (stable) regions. SpotCC designs locality-based and fine-grained volatility identification to reduce decoding operations and mitigate job congestion, respectively, and adaptively tunes configurations for decoding minimization. Experiments show that SpotCC improves P99 latency by 83.9% over state-of-the-arts, while maintaining ultra-low monetary costs.

title: Coordinated Optimal Scheduling of Source-Grid-Load-Storage Considering Joint Electricity-Carbon Operation in Distribution Networks
authors: Li Mingxin, Wang Yifeng, Shao Lituo, Li Na, Wu Ting, Xia Shiwei
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: To address the untrackable and high carbon emissions in new power systems, this paper proposes an optimized scheduling approach for active distribution networks that accounts for source-network-load-storage (SNLS) interactions within an integrated electricity-carbon framework. A carbon flow tracing model is established to visualize nodal carbon potential, and a stepwise carbon emission cost calculation method is introduced. An SNLS optimal scheduling model is constructed with objectives including thermal power operation, wind and photovoltaic curtailment penalties, load shedding, energy storage operation, and stepwise carbon costs, aiming to coordinate carbon reduction with economic performance. The improved IEEE 33bus network is used for validation. Results show that the model enables visualization of nodal carbon potential, and comparative analysis under three scenarios verifies the model's effectiveness in promoting carbon reduction while achieving a better balance between emission reduction and system economy.

title: A Scientometric Review of Machine Learning and Deep Learning Applications in Breast Cancer Detection: Trends, Challenges, and Future Directions
authors: Baljit Kaur, Renu Popli
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: One of the most prevalent and fatal illnesses in the world, breast cancer poses a serious threat to public health. Recent developments in deep learning (DL) and machine learning (ML) have greatly enhanced the detection and diagnosis of breast cancer by providing quicker findings, increased accuracy, and assistance with clinical decision-making. As per Scopus data from 2012 to 2025, we can see a thorough evaluation of ML and DL applications in breast cancer detection. It examines the contributions from different countries and organisations, as well as publishing trends, citation metrics, and keyword distribution, to provide better insights. With time, there is a change in analysis due to time, and interest in AI-based diagnostics is expanding. This paper focuses on significant advancements, developments, problems, including model transparency and data quality, issues, and potential avenues for further research. By highlighting existing trends and limitations in the area, the results hope to direct academics and practitioners and encourage further innovation in AI-driven healthcare.

title: AutoHAAP: Automated Heterogeneity-Aware Asymmetric Partitioning for LLM Training
authors: Yuanyuan Wang, Nana Tang, Yuyang Wang, Shu Pan, Dingding Yu, Zeyue Wang, Mou Sun, Kejie Fu, Fangyu Wang, Yunchuan Chen, Ning Sun, Fei Yang
