title: A Comprehensive Study on Driving Cycle for Electric Vehicle Application
authors: Arghya Mukherjee, Shantanu Chatterjee, Sudipta Banerjee, Pranendu Manna, Samit Changder, Chinmoy Chakraborty
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Real-world driving cycles are a crucial factor for accurately determining the performance, range, and energy consumption of electric vehicles (EVs). Standard test-driving cycles such as the NEDC, FTP-75, and WLTP were originally designed for internal combustion engine vehicles earlier and do not fully reflect specific characteristics of electric vehicles, such as regenerative braking, high initial torque, and location-dependent traffic behavior. In recent years, researchers have introduced a range of methods for developing EV-aligned driving cycles. These include micro-trip analysis, road segment-based construction, kinematic sequence modeling and stochastic synthesis techniques such as Markov chain and Monte Carlo simulation. This review explores key studies from multiple regions, considering both international and city-specific cycles, with examples from Dublin, Xi’an, Florence, and Gwacheon. Attention is also given to how factors such as road type, traffic congestion levels, and driver behavior influence velocity–acceleration profiles, which in turn affect estimation of the energy demand and emissions. Comparative findings show that location-specific cycles can produce energy consumption values that deviate by 20–30% from results obtained using generic legislative procedures. The review concludes with an evaluation of prevailing constraints, considering the deficiency of extensive electric vehicle datasets, and deliberates on prospects for the development of more representative and transferable driving cycle frameworks.

title: Enhancing Federated Learning Security Using Homomorphic Encryption and Zero-Knowledge Proofs
authors: Anvith S G, Nithish Kushal Reddy, Rushank Tripathi, Kavitha C.R
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Federated learning enables multiple clients to collaboratively train a shared model without exchanging raw data, but it raises privacy and integrity concerns when model updates traverse untrusted channels. In this project, we develop a secure federated learning pipeline that combines the CKKS homomorphic-encryption scheme with Groth-16 zero-knowledge proofs to protect client updates during transmission and to verify that each update stays within an agreed-upon norm bound. We benchmark CKKS parameters (poly_modulus degree, coefficient_moduli, and scale) on real-world model vectors to identify an optimal setting—8192-degree with two primes (60-bit and 40-bit) at a 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">32</sup> scale—that offers sub-100 ms encryption, minimal error, and moderate ciphertext sizes ( 3.3 MB). Clients train a small convolutional network on disjoint partitions of the MSTAR SAR dataset, generate succinct ZK proofs for each 128-element weight chunk, encrypt those chunks under the selected CKKS context, and submit both ciphertexts and proofs to dedicated servers. The homomorphic-aggregation server sums encrypted updates, while the ZKP server enforces correctness by rejecting any proof that violates the norm constraint—demonstrated by catching an intentionally malicious client. End-to-end testing confirms that the combined CKKS+ZKP pipeline preserves model accuracy and ensures both confidentiality and integrity of federated updates.

title: Leveraging AI/ML for Self-Optimizing 5G RAN: A Cloud-Integrated Framework
authors: S. Saravanan, T. Thanigasalam
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The increasing complexity of 5G Radio Access Networks (RAN) necessitates intelligent automation to ensure optimal performance, resource efficiency, and Quality of Service (QoS). Traditional static optimization methods are inadequate for handling dynamic traffic patterns and network heterogeneity. This paper proposes a cloud-integrated AI/ML framework that enables self-configuration, self-regulatory, self-repairing, and self-optimization in 5G RAN. Leveraging Machine Learning (ML) techniques such as Reinforcement Learning (RL), Deep Learning (DL), and Anomaly Detection, the framework enhances network automation, predictive maintenance, and real-time decision-making. The integration of cloud-native architectures and Multi-Access Edge Computing (MEC) reduces latency and improves scalability. Key challenges, including data overload, inference latency, and diverse network conditions, are addressed through distributed AI processing and adaptive learning models. Simulation results using NS-3, TensorFlow, and MATLAB demonstrate improved throughput, latency, and energy efficiency over traditional methods. The proposed AI-driven framework advances autonomous network management, paving the way for scalable, self-optimizing next-generation networks.

title: Multi-Stage Wide Frequency Range Optimization of High-Frequency Magnetic Links for IGBT, SiC MOSFET, and GaN-Based Isolated Power Converters
authors: Mahbubur Rahman Kiran, Md. Rabiul Islam, Jiatong Wang, Kashem M. Muttaqi, Danny Sutanto
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: In isolated power converters such as dual active bridge (DAB), the high-frequency magnetic links (HFMLs) are excited by the high-frequency inverters. The electromagnetic performance of the HFMLs significantly depends on the type of excitations and the switching characteristics of the power semiconductor devices, IGBT, SiC MOSFET, and GaN, used in the converters. According to a recent literature review, the primary obstacles in the design optimization of HFMLs involve accurate Multiphysics studies for diverse situations, which often necessitate a significant computational effort utilizing the finite element method (FEM). Furthermore, precise non-linear surrogate replicas are necessary for the optimization procedure. In this paper, a 10 kW HFML has been optimized using a multi-stage optimization method considering the characteristics of power semiconductor devices and a wide frequency range. The proposed optimization methodology includes the estimation of initial design parameters of HFML, 3D FEM-based optimization of electromagnetic performances, the Bayesian optimization algorithm, and the Gaussian process surrogate model. The scaled-down prototype of the optimized HFML rated at 2.5 kVA, with different frequency conditions, has been successfully tested and characterized in the laboratory.

title: GeoViTMamba: A Hybrid Vision Transformer and Transformer Decoder for Semantic Remote Sensing Image Captioning
authors: Inisha Rastogi, Prashant Hemrajani, Rohit Sharma
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Remote sensing image captioning (RSIC) links high resolution aerial imagery with naturallanguage descriptions for urban analysis, environmental monitoring, and autonomous planning. We present GeoViT Mamba, a lightweight encoder decoder framework coupling a Vision Transformer (ViT) encoder with a Transformer decoder, regularized by DropBlock, MixUp (image only), and token level masking to improve robustness under limited data. On the UCM Captions setting, GeoViT Mamba attains a METEOR score of 64.98, indicating strong semantic alignment while trading some ngram precision (BLEU) for semantic fluency. Qualitative analyses show context aware captions with coherent spatial references. These results suggest that combining global self attention with targeted regularization effectively enhances semantic caption quality for remote sensing applications.

title: Federated Learning and Blockchain Integration for Secure IoT Intrusion Detection
authors: Prince Tiwari, Sushant Yadav, K Geetha
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The fast-growing adoption of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has come with some important issues of secure and privacy-preserving interrelated systems. As cyberattacks are increasingly referred to the IoT environment, the problem of designing an efficient and scalable Intrusion Detection System (IDS) becomes urgent. Current solutions do not meet the challenge of optimizing spatial and temporal feature extraction and are not scalable to large quantities of edge devices. To circumvent these shortcomings, we introduce a federated learning (FL)-based IDS that uses a hybrid model of a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) and Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM). The CNN identifies spatial intrusion patterns whereas the BiLSTM represents temporal aspects in network traffic. In the context of a zero-trust environment, the raw data is kept at edge, and only the model parameters are sent to a central FL server in a secure manner. Our experimental findings on the CICIDS2017 and Edge- IIoTset datasets prove that the suggested IDS is effective in terms of accuracy, scalability, and detection power compared to centralized and traditional deep learning models.

title: Medipath: An Intelligent Emergency Medical Service System with NLP and Real-Time Coordination
authors: M. Jayalakshmi, B S Vikash, Divya Dharshini M, Kishore Kumar R, N Kathirmathi
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: This paper discusses the creation and assessment of MediPath, a smart web-based emergency medical assistance system. It connects patients, hospitals, and ambulance services in one digital platform. The system uses technologies like Natural Language Processing (NLP) to match hospital specialties with patient symptoms. It also uses real-time location services and smart route planning to address gaps in emergency medical response. MediPath has a three-part structure that supports logins for patients or attendants, hospital management portals, and navigation systems for ambulance drivers. The platform uses machine learning to link patient symptoms with hospital specialties, Firebase Realtime Database for easy data syncing, and mapping APIs for better traffic-aware routes. Performance tests show average response times of under 30 seconds for hospital matches and 95% accuracy in matching symptoms to specialties. There is also a significant drop in ambulance dispatch delays. User studies at different healthcare facilities indicate improved coordination, shorter emergency response times, and better use of resources. Although there are challenges with highly specialized medical conditions and connectivity in rural areas, MediPath greatly enhances emergency medical service delivery by creating a unified ecosystem that connects all parties in real time. This research presents a scalable, smart solution that addresses disconnected emergency medical services. It uses modern web technologies alongside healthcare knowledge to help save lives through quicker and more coordinated emergency responses.

title: XAI-Based Document Verification System for Government Services and Job Applications
authors: Amey Raut, Chetan Puri
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: This paper proposes an XAI-based document verification system to automate the authentication of identity documents for government services and job applications, addressing the need for efficient and transparent verification in the digital era. The system integrates optical character recognition (OCR) tools, including EasyOCR and Tesseract, with machine learning models—text-based logistic regression using TF-IDF and image-based random forest classifiers—to process PDF uploads, extract text and images from documents like Aadhar Cards and PAN Cards, identify types, and verify authenticity. Explainability is enabled via Local Interpretable Model-agnostic Explanations (LIME), offering insights into model decisions to enhance trust. Trained on datasets of valid and fake documents, the system achieves 90% accuracy in identification and 80% confidence in detecting fakes, validated by a dual-model approach that tackles uncontrolled environment challenges, such as poor image quality, through preprocessing (grayscale, adaptive thresholding, rotations). Utilizing Python libraries like easyocr, pdf2image, and scikit-learn, it supports multilingual processing (English, Hindi) and concurrent verification, reducing processing time. It is tailored for public sector applications, such as caste certificate issuance and Peon/Office Attendant recruitment, aligning with global XAI trends in forensic and employment support systems. Future enhancements, including Structural Similarity Index (SSIM) for signature verification, are suggested to boost accuracy and scalability.

title: Multimodal Emotion Recognition from Face, Body Posture, and Text Using a Self-Guided Single-Head Quantum Attention GAN with Snow Ablation Optimization
authors: Ch. Venkateswara Rao, S. Nagaraju
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The rapid advancement of multimodal emotion recognition has created new opportunities for enhancing clinical diagnosis, human-computer interaction, and affective computing; nevertheless, current models tend to be plagued by feature fusion limitations, attention deployment, and optimization, resulting in compromised recognition performance. To counter this shortcoming, the current research proposes Multimodal Emotion Recognition with Face, Body Posture, and Text Using a Self-Single Guided Head Quantum Snow Ablation Generative Adversarial Attention Network (3SGH-QG2AN) that is capable of highly accurate and trustworthy emotion classification. The dataset, “Multimodal Emotion Recognition with Face, Body Posture, and Text”, was built from 146 subjects gathered at the Shandong Mental Health. The 3SGH-QG2AN proposed here combines a Self-Guided Quantum Generative Adversarial Network (SG-QGAN) with Single-Head Self-Attention (SHSA), and its hyperparameters are adjusted by the Snow Ablation Optimization (SAO) algorithm to facilitate improved convergence stability and classification robustness. Experimental evidence shows that the 3SGH-QG2AN proposed in this work attains an impressive recognition accuracy.

title: A SRCVE-TCT Voltage Equalizer for Power Recovery in PV Arrays under Partial Shading
authors: Priya Ranjan Satpathy, Thurga Roobine Radha Krishnan, Vigna Kumaran Ramachandaramurthy, Renu Sharma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Solar PV arrays are highly susceptible to nonuniform irradiance caused by partial shading, resulting in mismatches among modules, significant power losses, distorted power curves, and inefficient maximum power point tracking. Conventional mitigation strategies exhibit drawbacks of high cost and complexity, unreliable operation, limited scalability, poor adaptability, energy loss, and suboptimal performance during dynamic shading scenarios. Hence, to address this gap, a smart row-coupled voltage equalizer with a total-cross-tied (SRVCE-TCT) approach is proposed in this paper to transfer energy from unshaded modules to shaded ones, thereby mitigating shading in arrays effectively without bypassing the shaded modules. The proposed approach enhances the power generation of PV arrays and ensures convex power curves during diverse and unpredictable partial shading scenarios. The proposed approach is tested using a $5 \times 5 \mathrm{PV}$ array under shading in the MATLAB/Simulink platform and benchmarked against existing mitigation strategies. The analysis reveals that the SRVCE-TCT can enhance the power generation of the PV array, with an average conversion efficiency of 99.37% during partial shading conditions.

title: Design and Simulative Testing of High-Gain Zeta Converters with an Adaptive P&O Algorithm
authors: Ch Hussaian Basha, S Resmara, Kotakonda Chakravarthi, Fini Fathima
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: This paper has explained the development and creation of a smaller, less expensive, and sophisticated Zeta-Zeta converter, capable of operating in a solar Photovoltaic (PV) energized Induction Motor (IM) drive system. The proposed converter system has a Communal Duty based Controlled (CDC) Source Series Load Parallel (SSLP) connection, and hence, effective sharing in the supply voltage and consumer sharing of current appears in the output. To make it either efficient and hardware-reduced, a technique of Perturb-Observe (P&O) based Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) comes into force. The switching of the Zeta converters is possible without using a Voltage-Current sensor, also to facilitate the smooth start-up of the IM. Additionally, the system also integrates the Voltage Source Inverter (VSI) and the technique of Sine Pulse Width Modulation (SPWM) in the generation of the intended switching signals. This is especially applicable to continuous headwater pumping, such that the pump will be able to keep its operating speed constant at all times without inducing the dynamic upheavals. The simulation outcomes of this solar PV-based water pumping system are calculated concerning simulations performed in the MATLAB/Simulink environment, since it is driven by the proposed system.

title: Power Quality Enhancement Using Deep Learning Technique Based Dynamic Voltage Restorer
authors: Soumya Ranjan Das, Devi Prasad Acharya, Nidan Gupta, Rupsa Pramanik, Debani Prasad Mishra, Jayanta Kumar Sahu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: This research is about improving the electrical power quality of the network that feeds a nonlinear load by reducing the total harmonic distortion and the neutral conductor current of the network and thus improving the overall performance of the system. To implement adaptive harmonic compensation for dynamic voltage restorer under changing load conditions, a deep learning, based control framework is proposed. The proposed approach employs recurrent learning architectures, including gated-recurrent units and LSTM networks, to optimize the operation of the DVR. The recital of the deep learning models is compared with the conventional pq0 theory for harmonic identification and compensation. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method satisfies international harmonic distortion limits while effectively minimizing neutral current. In addition, the controller maintains stable DC-link voltage regulation in the presence of small voltage disturbances. The effectiveness of the proposed deep learning framework is validated using performance metrics such as accuracy, computational complexity, and loss function. The results indicate high prediction accuracy with reduced computational burden. The developed strategy is applicable to three-phase, four-wire distribution networks and is suitable for both residential and commercial power quality enhancement applications.

title: Boosting Texture-Guided Deep Network Accuracy for Brain Tumor MRI Classification via EB0-Bor-RF Framework
authors: Richa Golash, Ankush Goyal
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: MRI-based brain tumor classification(MRI-BTC) is a critical task in biomedical engineering, facilitating accurate diagnosis and treatment planning. In this work, we propose a hybrid of machine and deep learning(ML/DL) approach using EfficientNet-B0 for feature extraction from the penultimate layer, followed by Boruta algorithm for optimal feature selection, and Random Forest as the final classifier for brain tumor classification. The proposed framework EB0-Bor-RF (EfficientNet-B0 + Boruta + Random Forest) further enhance class-wise transparency through SHapley Additive exPlanations(SHAP) values. This comprehensive approach provide a clear interpretable insights into the model’s decision-making process, which can help to foster trust among medical practitioners. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework significantly improves recall and agreement in four-class MRI-based BTC, outperforming baseline methods while offering reliable, doctor-trusted explanations. This scalable and interpretable approach holds promise for advancing clinical decision support systems in brain tumor diagnosis.

title: BotInferAttack: An LSTM-Based Framework for Membership Inference Attack on Bot Dataset
authors: Akash Shah, Monica Mehrotra, Shri Kant
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Malicious bots pose a serious danger on online social networks (OSNs) by altering public perception, propagating fake news, and deceiving users. Mitigating bot detection techniques help relieve some of this concern, however, they are vulnerable to Membership Inference Attacks (MIAs). MIAs refer to situations when an attacker is able to deduce whether certain data points were included in the training dataset of a particular model, which can lead to severe privacy breaches. This can expose sensitive information that can be misused. This research builds off our previous work on MIAs with depression dataset [1] by applying them to bot detection datasets with the creation of a specific MIA framework called BotInferAttack. The base model consists of a Random Forest (RF) classifier, Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier, and Artificial Neural Network (ANN) classifier. The shadow model implements Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) networks to identify trends in sequential behavior. In the attack model, the membership inference is conducted by utilizing a random forest, while the shadow model uses a Gaussian distribution to create the synthetic data. The research results show that detection systems faced 90% inaccuracy. This points towards the ever-increasing risk that bot detection systems face against MIAs, indicating a need for stronger privacy policies in place.

title: Deep Learning-Based YOLO Framework for Accurate Detection and Coordination of Autonomous Vehicles
authors: V Shobhan Reddy, Dhruva R Rinku, Tarun Kumar Juluru, Sridevi Chitti, Ramchandar Rao P, R. Ramadevi
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Now a days the autonomous vehicles are gaining a lot of attention as technology is increasing day by day. Many researchers have gone through several experiments to develop autonomous vehicles but failed in the accuracy and the smartness. With the smart work of the vehicles, there should be coordination between the vehicles to make the decisions. The Vehicle Communication helps in the collection of the data regarding the Vehicles. Accordingly, necessary information regarding vehicles such as direction, speed, position, identity and some of the future behaviors of the vehicle can be obtained with high accuracy. This paper contains a deep learning-based YOLO (You Only Look Once) proposed for detection and collection of autonomous vehicles. This experiment results with the high accuracy compared to that of the previous methodologies.

title: Deep Learning with Vision Transformers for Classifying Intracerebral Hemorrhage in CT Images
authors: Amlan Anurag, Sukanta Kumar Sabut, Jigyansha Jeevan Rout, Laxmi Prasad Mishra
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Intracerebral Hemorrhage (ICH) represents a crit-ical condition involving bleeding within the skull that functions as a complex pathological state which threatens human well-being. The traditional diagnosis method of CT scan interpretation through manual work is challenging due to high time requirements and risks of human mistakes. Vision Transformers (ViT) serve as the main focus of this research because they strengthen ICH classification by leveraging their advanced ability to handle distant relationships versus Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs). A deep learning model based on ViT has been designed in this study to analyze brain CT images in a specific dataset to classify the presence of hemorrhage. The utilization of self-attention mechanism in ViT is used to investigate the operations on images together with data expansion techniques and model refinement activities. Testing indicators show the model achieves high classification performance through the measurements of accuracy and precision alongside ROC-AUC. We achieved an accuracy of 96.25% which outperforms CNN based models and demonstrates competitive results with recent ViT based approaches. Professionals may find Vision Transformers to be an effective diagnostic tool for medical imaging based ICH detection.

title: Advancing Health Care Systems with AI Supported by Cloud and Wireless Networks
authors: P. Sathish Kumar, M. Vedaraj
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Modern computing has been changed rapidly by the development of internet technologies and cloud computing is a solution that is scalable, flexible, and cost-effective. Cloud platforms remove the necessity of managing physical infrastructure, which means that there is easy access to software, data, computing capabilities, and storage. Their decentralized data centers and their flexible service formats enable them to be applicable in so many industrial settings. This research examines the combinations of cloud computing technology and wireless communication technology in enhancing financial control in hospitals where budgeting, allocation of resources, cost management, and generally financial operations are major challenges to the hospitals. In order to solve these problems, an artificial intelligence (AI) driven hybrid cloud-based optimization framework is suggested. The structure uses cloud computing services and wireless network connectivity to automate everyday activities and simplify financial operations, which enhances the efficiency of operations. The design is centered on an integrated Fuzzy Neural Network (FNN) model which was compared to independent neural networks and the traditional fuzzy logic methods. It has been proved through experiments that the hybrid model is able to handle hospital financial processes successfully with low computational overhead and high accuracy of 98.01.

title: A Robust PLL Technique for Grid Synchronization Under DC Offset and Harmonics
authors: Shuvra Prokash Biswas, Rabiul Islam, Arindam Ghosh, Stephen Martin, Mahdi Dargahi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper introduces a hybrid PLL framework for synchronizing with three-phase grids, designed to suppress the adverse effects of DC offsets and voltage harmonics. The proposed PLL integrates a third order generalized integrator (TOGI) based quadrature signal generator (QSG) with a harmonic attenuator (HA), enhancing the accuracy and dynamic performance of conventional double second-order generalized integrator (DSOGI) based PLL (DSOGI-PLL). The TOGI-QSG improves orthogonal signal generation, while the HA suppresses low-order harmonics and DC offset components before the synchronous reference frame (SRF)-PLL stage. A comparative performance analysis of the proposed PLL and the DSOGI-PLL is carried out in MATLAB/Simulink software environment under polluted grid voltage conditions, including DC offset injections and harmonic distortion. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed PLL significantly reduces ripples in the dq-axis voltages, lowers the frequency and phase errors, and achieves reduced total harmonic distortion (THD) compared to the DSOGI-PLL. Thus, the proposed technique offers a robust and reliable solution for grid synchronization in modern inverter-dominated power systems.

title: Coordinated Energy Management and Reactive Power Control for Enhancing Microgrid Voltage Stability Using Electric Vehicles
authors: Pratibha Hurkadli, D. B. Kulkarni
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: This paper proposes a coordinated control paradigm that couples Energy Management Strategies (EMS) with a Microgrid Voltage Stabilizer (MGVS) for enhancing voltage stability and operational robustness in electric vehicle (EV)-integrated microgrids. The EMS uses a game-theoretic algorithm to optimise the EV charging schedules, whereas the MGVS maintains dynamic control over voltage charges through the distribution of the reactive power according to a voltage deficiency index. The proposed method is tested on a 21-bus microgrid model on MATLAB/Simulink and DIgSILENT Powerfactory. The results of the simulation show the decrease of peak demand by 18%, enhancement of the voltage profiles in the range of nominal values by 5%, increase of Transient Voltage Stability Margin Index (TVSMI) by 24%, and EV charging satisfaction by 26 percent. The system recovery time is also slashed by an implied 50% by the framework. These results define the suggested EMS-MGVS coordination as a scalable and efficient tool towards the active and reactive power management in future smart and robust microgrids.

title: Transforming Healthcare Through Data Analytics: Toward a Safer, Smarter, and More Secure Health System
authors: Sajidul Islam Khan, Md Majedur Rahman, Emran Hossain, Md Shihab Rahman, Abdullah Al Mahmud Ashik, Safiul Islam
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Predicting key clinical outcomes like mortality, sepsis and a 30-day readmission is important for improving patient care and the use of clinical resources in intensive care units (ICUs). Here we present a bifurcated approach that includes both centralized and federated learning approaches to establish predictive models utilizing real world healthcare data-MIMICIV; Synthea data. In the centralized context, we modeled mortality prediction, sepsis prediction and 30-day readmission prediction using a CNN-LSTM architecture that is designed to learn complex temporal and sequential patterns of patient data. We mostly used tabular data in our implementation, but the architecture was set up to allow the model to learn temporal and sequential records. The architecture allowed a model to learn relationships among features using pseudo-sequence data. The predictive model achieved very good performance; achieving a mortality prediction accuracy of 97.45%, detection accuracy of sepsis at 98.45%, and forecasting accuracy of 30-day readmission prediction at 98.47%. These improved results outperformed previous and traditional classifiers Logistic Regression and Random Forest & SVM, supporting the consideration of deep learning solutions in clinical prediction tasks. In the federated learning context, we proposed a distributed training frame work based on LightGBM that utilizes the Synthea nodes for distributed training, while preserving confidentiality, this will allow Synthea nodes to contribute to a model at a global level while preserving patient privacy. Through the federated model, we reached a global accuracy of 98.92% in predicting hypertension, all while maintaining minimal communication overhead and strong distribution performance across the nodes. These comparisons showed that our proposed federated method could deliver a high predictive accuracy, and manage class imbalance, a topical viewpoint in the real healthcare. Finally, this research paper shows the importance of integrating intelligent deep learning systems and privacy-sensitive mechanisms. We find that these hybrid solutions can have a significant effect and add value to clinical decision support, especially in the situations where information sharing is limited due to privacy issues.

title: A Data-Driven Digital Twin Formation with TCN for State Prediction of Grid-Tied PV Systems
authors: Rituvic Pandey, Nishant Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: 抓取摘要失败: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='ieeexplore.ieee.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /document/11411224/ (Caused by ProtocolError('Connection aborted.', ConnectionResetError(104, 'Connection reset by peer')))

title: A Framework for Automated Web Service Discovery and Selection in Service-Oriented Architectures
authors: B. Bikku, D. Ramesh, P. Praveen
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Service-oriented architectures often employ large scale service registries, in which manual service discovery becomes very costly and prone to human error. The current techniques frequently do not have automated processes for evaluating functional relevance and quality of service (QoS) parameters during service selection which causes high time latency and not optimal service binding. The framework presented here is one that combines semantic service annotation, ontology-based matching, and multi-criteria decision analysis in a fully automated process. The framework was tested on a registry of 4,567 public webservices, the semantic filtering was able to pruned the candidates list from 4,567 services down to 912 services, and the ranking algorithm finally retrieved the top 25 possible services based on throughput, response time, availability and reliability. Experimentally verified, they show a decrease in average discovery time from 920 ms to 640 ms and service relevance scoring from 0.62 to0.83 MRR. Results show an improvement in service discovery automation in terms of precision and speed.

title: Impact of an Optimal SoC-Aware EV Charging Strategy on the Voltage Regulation of Distribution Networks
authors: Mohammad Kamran Ikram, Mehdi Seyedmahmoudian, Gokul Thirunavukkarasu, Saad Mekhilef, Alex Stojcevski
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The uncoordinated charging of electric vehicles (EVs) creates volatile and unpredictable load profiles, posing a significant challenge to the Distribution System Operators (DSOs). Building on a previously developed optimization framework, this paper presents a detailed analysis of its impact on the voltage regulation of a distribution network. The framework, which uses a Genetic Algorithm (GA) to hierarchically manage an EV fleet based on State-of-Charge (SoC), is applied with an objective function to minimize the total cost of grid energy. Simulation results on an enhanced IEEE 33-bus system demonstrate that the strategy not only reduces the peak demand but also successfully eliminates all voltage violations caused by uncontrolled charging, confirming its effectiveness as a critical tool for maintaining grid stability.

title: Performance Analysis of Synchronous Machine and Grid-Forming Converter Configurations in Low-Inertia Power Systems
authors: Akash Shenoy, Lekshmi S, Rashmi M. R, Hoong Pin Lee
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The evolving landscape of modern power systems is increasingly characterized by reduced system inertia due to the widespread integration of inverter-based resources. Synchronous Machines (SMs) provide larger inertia where as Grid-Forming Converters(GFCs) provide faster control capabilities. This paper presents a comprehensive comparative analysis of four generator configurations comprising different combinations of Synchronous Machines, and Grid-Forming Converters.The configurations considered are SMs alone, GFS alone and two hybrid models with varying proportions of SMs and GFCs as generators. The frequency response, voltage stability, and overall resilience of each setup under typical grid disturbances are evaluated for an IEEE 9-bus system using Matlab/Simulink. The Results indicate that hybrid architectures offer an optimal trade-off between the fast control capabilities of GFCs and the natural inertial support of SMs. The findings provide practical insights into designing robust low-inertia grids and highlight the importance of coordinated operation between legacy and inverter-based generation units for future power systems.

title: Real-Time Hardware Implementation and FFT Analysis Approach of Different PWM Techniques for Five-Phase Two-Level VSI using F28335 DSP
authors: Jeeban Kumar Nayak, Ranjan KumarBehera
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Multiphase voltage source inverters (VSIs) are commonly employed for the purpose of delivering multiphase variable voltage and variable frequency power supply, utilizing pulse width modulation (PWM) control techniques. This paper presents an experimental comparison of three modulation strate-gies-Sinusoidal Pulse Width Modulation (SPWM), Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation (SVPWM), and Discontinuous Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation (DSVPWM) for a five-phase twolevel Voltage Source Inverter (VSI). A real-time control platform was developed on the TMS320F28335 digital signal processor (DSP) to generate gating signals for the inverter, ensuring precise timing and flexibility in modulation strategy selection. Hardware tests with a five-phase induction motor (FPIM) were conducted, and output voltage phase current were analyzed using Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) to determine harmonic components and Total Harmonic Distortion (THD). Results show that SVPWM offers better DC bus utilization and lower THD than SPWM, while DSVPWM reduces switching losses with a slight increase in low-order harmonics. SPWM, though simpler, produces higher THD and lower voltage utilization. The findings provide practical guidance for selecting modulation strategies in high-performance five-phase VSI applications.

title: Blockchain Enhanced Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) for Real Time Credit Card Fraud Detection
authors: Ayush Kushwaha, Aashutosh Kumar Pandit, U Muthaiah
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Credit card fraud has become a fast-moving, complex threat that traditional detection systems have a hard time stopping. This paper takes a new approach by combining Explainable AI (XAI) with blockchain’s unbreakable trust layer to make a fraud-detection system that is not only quick and accurate, but also clear and impossible to hack. Our solution makes each alert easier to understand for analysts and stakeholders by using a group of modern classifiers (XGBoost, LightGBM, and CatBoost) and adding clear and easy-to-understand explanations from SHAP and LIME. A lightweight smart-contract framework on Ethereum logs every flagged transaction and its explanation digest at the same time. This makes an unchangeable audit trail that regulators and auditors can trust which not only keeps fraud alerts safe and clear, but, also helps people follow the rules by keeping a permanent and verifiable record of detection decisions. Blockchain removes concerns about data tampering or manipulation, which are common problems with centralized fraud detection systems. This design is built into a real-time streaming pipeline and gives verdicts in less than a second. This allows banks and other financial institutions to respond quickly to new fraud schemes and limit their losses. The system’s design uses modern stream processing tools to handle a lot of transactions quickly and accurately, without losing any of the information’s meaning. In today’s fast-paced digital economy, where fraudsters take advantage of every millisecond of delay in detection, this ability to respond quickly is very important. The resulting framework establishes a new benchmark for next-generation financial security systems by discouraging fraudulent activity and strengthening stakeholder confidence through indisputable evidence of integrity and explainability.

title: A Portfolio-Level Optimization Framework for Coordinated Market Participation and Operational Scheduling of Hydrogen-Centric Companies
authors: Seyed Amir Mansouri, Kenneth Bruninx
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The vision of electrolytic hydrogen as a clean energy vector prompts the emergence of hydrogen-centric companies that must simultaneously engage in electricity, hydrogen, and green certificate markets while operating complex, geographically distributed asset portfolios. This paper proposes a portfolio-level optimization framework tailored for the integrated operational scheduling and market participation of such companies. The model co-optimizes asset scheduling and market decisions across multiple sites, incorporating spatial distribution, technical constraints, and company-level policy requirements. It supports participation in the electricity market, physical and virtual Power Purchase Agreements (PPAs), bundled and unbundled hydrogen markets, and green certificate transactions. The model is applied to three operational scenarios to evaluate the economic and operational impacts of different compliance strategies. Results show that centralized, portfolio-level control unlocks the full flexibility of geographically distributed assets, enabling a 2.42-fold increase in hydrogen production and a 9.4 % reduction in daily operational costs, while satisfying all company policy constraints.

title: Deep Learning for Spectral Processing in Microbottle Resonator Humidity Sensors
authors: Huda Adnan Zain, Khurram Karim Qureshi
abstract: This work demonstrates a deep learning–assisted microbottle resonator (MBR) humidity sensor coated with agarose and coupled to a tapered fiber. The convolutional neural network (CNN) processes barcoded spectral data, enabling joint analysis of wavelength shifts, intensity variations, and linewidth changes. The sensor exhibits a CNN classification accuracy of 95%. The results confirm the suitability of deep learning for multidimensional spectral processing in whispering gallery mode (WGM) humidity sensing.

title: A Novel Optimization and Yield Analysis Method for MEMS Devices Combining Bi-Fidelity Deep Neural Network and Active Subspace
authors: Lin-Feng Zhao, Gong-Zeng Liu, Yun-Cong Mo, He Wang, Qing-An Huang, Zai-Fa Zhou
abstract: The development of high-performance MEMS devices necessitates efficient design optimization and yield analysis in the presence of unavoidable process variations. The existing algorithms are not able to integrate the different requirements of optimization and uncertainty quantification (UQ), highlighting the necessity for a comprehensive algorithmic framework. To this end, a novel algorithm framework is proposed for the optimization and UQ of MEMS devices. The deep neural network (DNN) and polynomial chaos expansion (PCE) are respectively leveraged to build the global surrogate model for the nondominated sorting genetic algorithm II (NSGA-II) and the local surrogate model for the UQ analysis. The DNN is refined by the bi-fidelity data training scheme to reduce the training data demanded, and the active subspace dimension reduction (AS) is employed to fully utilize the information within the global surrogate model, thereby minimizing the computational resources needed for the local surrogate model construction. This algorithm is applied to a kind of MEMS thermal flow sensor. The proposed algorithmic simultaneously increases sensor sensitivity by 14.77% while reducing computational expenditure by 44.4% during the optimization phase. Besides, the yield analysis is also derived accurately, with computational cost reductions of 73.0%. These results indicate that this novel algorithm supplies an effective, accurate, and efficient technique for the optimization and yield analysis for MEMS devices. [2025-0231]

title: Novel Configuration of an Elliptic Waveguide Filter Using TE 103 Mode for the Q-Band OMUX
authors: Naeun Shin, Changsoo Kwak, Dong-Pil Chang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This letter presents a three-channel Q-band output multiplexer (OMUX) based on pseudo-elliptic <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$T\!E_{103}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-mode waveguide filters with diagonal cross coupling. The elliptic-function condition is derived using a coupling-matrix formulation, and the proposed structure is shown to satisfy the required coupling relationships. A folded resonator configuration is introduced to realize the cross-coupling while maintaining a compact structure. The OMUX operates with three channels centered from 37.9 to 39.5 GHz, each providing a 700 MHz bandwidth. An efficient design procedure combining equivalent-circuit optimization and full-wave 3-D electromagnetic (EM) simulation is employed, significantly reducing the design time. Measurements of the fabricated prototype show good agreement with simulations, achieving an in-band return loss better than 20 dB, out-of-band rejection exceeding 18 dB at a 100 MHz offset, and a minimum insertion loss of 0.5 dB. The proposed approach provides a compact and thermally efficient Q-band OMUX solution suitable for satellite payload applications.

title: Comparative Study of Deep Learning Models for Nail Disease Classification
authors: Diya Agnihotri, Bhavpreet Kaur, Vinay Dhiman, Amit Chauhan, Rajni Mohana
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Nail diseases requires specialist evaluation as the visual manifestations are subtle and also there is coexistence of various features. This paper investigates the promise of deep learning, specifically Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), to classify nail diseases automatically. We compare four cutting-edge CNNs namely DenseNet121, ResNet50, EfficientNetB0, and MobileNetV2 on a hand-collected dataset of 3,744 images in six categories of nail diseases. The four models were trained on the same conditions with data augmentation and transfer learning. DenseNet121 had the highest accuracy (97%), followed by ResNet50 (90%), EfficientNetB0 (85%), and MobileNetV2 (77%). Elaborate metrics and confusion matrices unveil strengths and weaknesses of every model. The results prove CNNs to be viable for detecting nail disease, with DenseNet121 providing the greatest accuracy and MobileNetV2 appropriate for resource-limited deployments. The research provides the foundation for real-world clinical decision support in AI-based dermatological diagnostics.

title: The Sedpgram: A Novel Optimal Demodulation Frequency Band Selection Framework for Multi-type Gearbox Early Fault diagnosis
authors: Laixing Li, Jingbu Wei, Yuhang Wang, Wanming Ying, Khandaker Noman, Yongbo Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Early fault detection in multi-type gearbox remains a critical challenge, as weak fault-induced signals are frequently overwhelmed by strong ambient noise and operational interference. Existing methods are primarily tailored for fault characteristics of a single gearbox type, severely limiting their applicability and robustness in complex industrial gearboxes. While Optimal Demodulation Frequency Band (ODFB) analysis is a potent feature extraction technique, its performance is fundamentally constrained by frequency band segmentation strategies and the design of optimization metrics. To address this issue, this paper proposes a novel ODFB selection framework on the Spectral Energy Distribution Probability (SEDP) gram to robustly extract multi-type gearbox fault features. Its framework prioritizes identifying the most informative band center frequency using the SEDP metric—which quantifies the concentration of characteristic energy. Subsequently, a binary tree algorithm is employed to adaptively segment the frequency spectrum, recursively approximating the identified center frequency. Crucially, the Demodulation Sideband Retention (DSR) ratio is used to autonomously determine the optimal segmentation level, ensuring maximal retention of fault information while suppressing noise. This framework enables effective multi-type gearbox fault diagnosis solely through preset parameter fine-tuning. Validation using the NWPU multi-type gearbox datasets demonstrates that proposed method outperforms traditional ODFB methods in enhancing early fault features.

title: Joint Registration and Fusion Network for Dual-Frequency SAR Images Based on Semantic Information
authors: Mengfan Ge, Mingjun Huang, Hongtu Xie, Bin Xu, Haozong Liu, Yuanjie Zhang, Kaiwen Qiu, Nannan Zhu
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) provides the high- resolution imaging capability and can obtain the ocean and land target information under complex observation conditions. Dual-frequency SAR images exhibit the complementary properties in the resolution and scattering characteristics, thus fusing dual- frequency SAR images can combine these advantages to provide the richer and more comprehensive information for downstream semantic segmentation tasks. However, existing fusion algorithms typically assume preregistered images and focus on the good visual effects while neglecting the semantic requirements of high-level vision tasks. To address this problem, a joint registration and fusion network for the dual-frequency SAR images based on the semantic information is proposed, which can achieve the high-precision cross-frequency registration and high-quality image fusion. First, in the image registration stage, the microwave vision data (MVD) semantic pseudo-color maps are introduced as the auxiliary information, and the semantic-consistency loss is used to guide the registration process, finally the fusion loss is fed back to the image registration as an optimization signal. Second, in the image fusion stage, the global spatial attention mechanism is used for the feature extraction and fusion, and then the semantic segmentation loss is incorporated to guide the image fusion so that the results can better conform to the high-level semantic structures. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness and correctness of the proposed method, which can outperform state-of-the-art approaches in the image registration accuracy, image fusion quality and downstream semantic segmentation performance.
