title: Low-Complexity Compensation for IQ Skew-Induced Mirror-Subcarrier Crosstalk in High-Speed Coherent DSCM Systems
authors: Yongchao Jin, Muqi Liu, Qian Xiang, Siyu Gong, Chen Cheng, Linsheng Fan, Pengxi Yang, Qun Zhang, Sen Zhang, Tianjian Zuo, Yanfu Yang
abstract: Digital subcarrier multiplexing (DSCM) has emerged as a promising candidate for future ultra-high-baud-rate transmissions. However, DSCM signals are susceptible to transmitter IQ skew. Traditionally, this issue has been mitigated using 4× 4 real-valued multiple-input multiple-output (RV-MIMO) equalizers. Nevertheless, such equalizers suffer from high computational complexity, making them unsuitable for low-power applications. In this paper, we propose and experimentally validate a low-complexity scheme for compensating transmitter IQ skew in coherent DSCM systems. Specifically, for each subcarrier pair, the mirror subcarrier is first processed through an adaptive FIR filter bank to estimate the image crosstalk induced by IQ skew. This estimated crosstalk is then subtracted from the subcarrier to be compensated, effectively mitigating IQ skew. Compared to the conventional 4× 4 RV-MIMO scheme, which requires 16 FIR filters, the proposed scheme utilizes only 8 FIR filters to compensate a subcarrier pair, thereby reducing the number of multipliers by 50%. Experimental results on a 4-subcarrier DSCM system, in which each subcarrier operates at 12 Gbaud, show that the proposed scheme performs comparably to the conventional 4× 4 RV-MIMO scheme over an IQ-skew range of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\pm$</tex-math></inline-formula> 6 ps (7.2% of the subcarrier symbol period). Specifically, under an IQ skew of 2 ps, the received-optical-power (ROP) penalty of the proposed scheme relative to the conventional 4× 4 RV-MIMO scheme is only 0.19 dB on the high-frequency subcarrier at a BER of 3.8e-3. These findings validate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme as a promising low-power solution for future high-capacity coherent optical interconnects.

title: PerC-SimAM-BLSTM: Position Perception Circular Convolution With Simple Attention Mechanism Based on BLSTM for Bundle Branch Block Detection
authors: Jibin Wang, Bo Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks and Learning Systems
abstract: Left bundle branch block (LBBB) and right bundle branch block (RBBB) are two typical types of arrhythmias. Due to their similar electrocardiogram (ECG) features, the conventional visual inspection ECG strategy has always been challenging. Motivated by MetaFormer, we investigate a novel MetaFormer-like model that is composed of both position perception circular convolution (PerC) as a new Token Mixer and simple attention mechanism with bidirectional long short-term memory (SimAM-BLSTM) as a new Channel MLP called PerC-SimAM-BLSTM for automatic LBBB and RBBB detection. This framework not only inherits the global feature extraction ability of Transformer but also enhances global–local feature acquisition capability of PerC. Experiment results demonstrate better improvements than the existing strategies and also strike a better balance between performance and complexity from an empirical experiment perspective. Mathematical convergence proof ensures stable convergence of model training to the theoretical optimal solution from a theoretical perspective. Our PerC-SimAM-BLSTM achieves an accuracy of over 99.1% across the China Physiological Signal Challenge 2018 and MIT-BIH arrhythmia databases (MADBs), demonstrating great potential in lightweight devices.

title: Learning Globally Stable Neural Imitation Policies via Reshaped Energy Functions
authors: Haohui Huang, Wenxu Zheng, Junda Duan, Kailun Huang, Jing Guo, Chenguang Yang
abstract: Energy function-based DSs hold tremendous potential for developing point-to-point motion learning models with stability and accuracy. Nevertheless, a commonly encountered issue is that most methods struggle to strike a balance between stability and accuracy. This article proposes a novel adaptive modulation scheme that aims to learn a DS based on neural energy functions to alleviate the constraints of traditional methods. In this method, we decouple and modulate the radius and angle of the motion position in a polar-like space through coordinate transformation to ensure global stability and learning accuracy for point-to-point motion learning and generalization. Both simulations using a public benchmark and real-world evaluations of ultrasound robot scanning demonstrate that the proposed method exhibits superior generalization and higher accuracy for motions compared to existing motion learning methods.

title: Improving Conversational Literature Retrieval Quality via Personalized Profile-Based Re-ranking_supp1-3669741.pdf
authors: Zhenping Xie
abstract: Academic literature retrieval is constrained by the paradox of “information overload” versus “evidence scarcity”, a tension that deepens when researchers iteratively refine their queries in multi-turn conversational settings. To address this challenge, we propose Conversational Literature Personalized Re-ranking (CLPR), a personalized framework that unifies dense semantic retrieval with personalized user profiling. CLPR first performs a broad high-recall retrieval to collect candidate documents, then compresses conversational history into a concise textual profile that encodes sequential continuity, immediate focus, and long-term research background via a large language model. The generated profile serves as a pseudo-query for a neural cross-encoder to produce the final ranking. Cross-domain testing on the public LitSearch (computer science) benchmark confirms its robust generalization, yielding an NDCG@10 of 0.4793. On MedCorpus, a new multi-turn biomedical conversational retrieval benchmark constructed for this study, CLPR attains state-of-the-art performance with P@1 = 0.9497 and NDCG@10 = 0.9271, surpassing the strongest baseline by substantial margins. Ablation shows long-term background cues contribute most, and maintaining a short, up-to-date profile across turns outperforms a static one. CLPR therefore delivers accurate, personalized literature retrieval and can accelerate evidence synthesis across scientific domains.

title: Simulated Artifacts and Data Augmentation for Real-World Video Motion Deblurring
authors: Sota Moriyama, Kaito Kira, Koichi Ichige
abstract: This paper proposes a data augmentation method that simulates artifacts specific to real-world videos as a preprocessing step for applying a deep learning-based video deblurring method to real-world videos. Conventional methods in video deblurring using deep learning have suffered from poor generalization performance. Even if a video deblurring method shows high accuracy on a test dataset in the same domain as the training dataset, it will show less accuracy when inferring real-world test videos. Therefore, we assume that real-world videos contain compression noise and image processing artifacts not included in training deblurring datasets. We introduce a data augmentation method that applies data transformations simulating these real-world video-specific degradations during training. In this study, we prepare a real-world test dataset with no ground truth videos using video captured by a commercially available smartphone. Then, we aim to improve the estimation accuracy of deblurring in real-world videos by performing inference using our data augmentation method.

title: Hybrid Graph-RL for Beam Management in 6G Satellite Networks with Heterogeneous Service Requirements
authors: Zhu Jincheng, Pan Zhiwen
abstract: To meet heterogeneous IoT service requirements in the era of ubiquitous connectivity, flexible and real-time beam management and power allocation have become a critical challenge for sixth-generation (6G) non-geostationary orbit (NGSO) satellite networks. Conventional model-driven approaches, although analytically tractable, exhibit limited adaptability and scalability in large-scale, dynamic satellite environments. Reinforcement learning (RL)-based solutions offer great flexibility, but still face scalability and robustness challenges due to the massive decision space, complex system dynamics and interference conditions. Moreover, most existing RL frameworks overlook model-based priors essential for facilitating policy training and improving generalization. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a Graph-embedded Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (Graph-DDPG) beam management framework that jointly optimizes the Quality of Service (QoS) for delay-sensitive (DS) and throughput-sensitive (TS) services while ensuring satellite load balancing. By incorporating model-informed guidance into the learning process, the proposed method substantially enhances scalability and convergence. Simulations verify that the proposed framework achieves up to 56.12% throughput satisfaction increase and at least 19.47% queuing latency reduction over baseline approaches, underscoring its potential for practical and effective beam management in large-scale NGSO satellite networks.

title: Impact-aware Maneuver Decision with Driving Style Tuning for Autonomous Vehicle
authors: Yuyang Xia, Shuncheng Liu, Yan Zhao, Han Su, Xiaofang Zhou, Kai Zheng
publish: IEEE Transactions on Knowledge and Data Engineering
abstract: Autonomous driving is an emerging technology that has developed rapidly over the last decade, with decision-making remaining a critical challenge, particularly due to its significant role in traffic congestion. In this paper, we propose a novel perception-and-decision framework, called HEAD, which consists of an enHanced pErception module and a mAneuver Decision module to address this challenge. In the enhanced perception module, a graph-based state prediction model with a strategy of phantom vehicle construction is proposed to address incomplete vehicle features and predict future states in parallel. Then in the maneuver decision module, a deep reinforcement learning-based model is designed to learn a driving policy based on a parameterized action Markov decision process. A hybrid reward function takes into account aspects of safety, efficiency, comfort, and impact to guide the autonomous vehicle to make optimal maneuver decisions. To make our framework applicable to more scenarios, we further propose an improved HEAD (HEAD++) framework that makes the autonomous vehicle adapt to various road structures, such as lane merging and diverging scenarios. Besides, we develop a style tuning module in HEAD++, which supports personalized driving style tuning. To mitigate high training costs, an efficient style tuning method with approximate gradient descent is proposed to reduce the number of training iterations. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our framework. Compared to state-of-the-art methods, HEAD++ reduces overall traffic disturbance by 23.3%-40.9%, lowers collision risk by 4.5%-17.8%, and improves passenger comfort by 13.1%-30.5%, while maintaining high traffic efficiency.

title: Evolving Intelligent Network Attack Classifier Under Label Distribution Shift_supp1-3669948.pdf
authors: Minhae Kwon
abstract: The next-generation internet is being reshaped by the growing intelligence and connectivity of artificial intelligence of things (AIoT) devices. This evolution emphasizes the importance of AI-enabled network intrusion detection systems (NIDS) in intelligent network environments. Two major technical challenges in developing such systems are addressing class imbalance and adapting to label distribution shifts after deployment. Class imbalance, caused by the dominance of normal traffic and the scarcity and uneven distribution of attack types, often results in low performance for minority attack type classes. In addition, evolving attack patterns necessitate efficient and continuous model post-training, despite the limited and unlabeled data available in the post-deployment phase. This study proposes a unified training framework consisting of a two-step pre-training scheme and an adaptive post-training scheme. In Step I of pre-training, balanced contrastive pair selection is performed to account for class imbalance, while Step II refines the representations of hard-to-distinguish samples located near decision boundaries using latent features. In the post-training phase, the framework leverages both model outputs and latent representations to generate reliable pseudo-labels for learning. A key insight of this work is that a well-structured latent representation space enables reliable pseudo-labeling during post-training. Extensive simulations across eight online label shift scenarios with three datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves up to an <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\bf {8.6}\%$</tex-math></inline-formula> improvement in accuracy and F1-score over eight state-of-the-art approaches, along with a significant reduction in post-training complexity.

title: Improved Traffic Sign Detection with ECA Attention and Augmentation
authors: Nyamathulla Shaik, Shashavali Dodiyam, Padmaja Vemala, Pallavi Ragula, Prakash Rao Chagalamarri
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Traffic sign detection plays a vital role in Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS) and Unmanned Vehicles (UVs), as it enables machines to perceive and interpret the road environment for safe and autonomous navigation. Accurate classification of traffic signs enhances driving safety, eliminates human dependence, and strengthens the decisionmaking capability of Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS). While traditional deep learning models such as YOLOv3 and YOLOv7 have shown strong performance in detecting and localizing road signs, they are often affected by challenges like lighting variations, motion blur, and adverse weather conditions. Moreover, existing attention mechanisms like Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) introduce additional computational overhead. To address these limitations, the proposed study introduces an improved Traffic Sign Detection model based on YOLOv8, integrated with Efficient Channel Attention (ECA) and advanced data augmentation techniques. The system enhances generalization through synthetic weather degradation, varied intensity, and motion blur effects. Replacing SE with ECA significantly reduces inference time and model size without compromising accuracy. Trained with the AdamW optimizer and a Cosine learning rate scheduler, the model achieves an impressive mAP of about 99% at 0.5 IoU on the German Traffic Sign Detection Benchmark (GTSDB), surpassing baseline results in both accuracy and speed. Its lightweight and robust architecture ensures scalability, efficiency, and real-time performance, making it ideal for embedded automotive applications.

title: Battery Scheduling and Smart Inverter Volt/Var Control for Voltage Stability in Microgrids
authors: Bilal Khan, Saifullah Shafiq, Caroline Hachem Vermette, Ursula Eicker
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: As distribution grids decarbonize and electrify, maintaining voltage quality under integrated inverter-based distributed resources has become challenging due to the localized voltage sensitivities. These dynamics call for adaptive, data-driven coordination of battery storage and smart inverters that can act on fast timescales while respecting network limits. This paper presents a microgrid control and optimization framework that coordinates battery energy storage with smart-inverter volt/var functionality to improve voltage regulation and operating costs in a building-centric microgrid (MG). The test system adapts the IEEE 13-node feeder to represent building load profiles, integrating solar photovoltaic (PV), smart inverters, and grid-connected battery energy storage. The proposed method schedules battery charging/discharging while enabling autonomous volt/var support at PV nodes, thereby flattening voltage profiles, reducing technical losses, and shifting energy procurement away from pricey periods. Case studies on the modified IEEE 13-node feeder show that coordinated battery dispatch and volt/var control maintain bus voltages within acceptable limits under variable load and solar PV generation, while lowering total energy costs relative to baseline system operation.

title: Point-of-Care Diagnostics for Infectious Diseases: A Novel Approach using Microfluidics and Machine Learning
authors: Raghav Garg, Kuvanch Roximovna Bekchanova, Shivangi Kumari, Sunil Semwal, S.Tamizharasu, Aniket Adarsh
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Infection conditions add greatly to the worldwide illness load. Sepsis is a potentially fatal organ malfunction caused by an abnormal reaction to infection. Obstacles in diagnosing possess significant effects on life. Sensitive and precise diagnostic techniques are among the most successful approaches to detecting and managing infection origins. Conventional diagnosis procedures for infectious illness identification necessitate counter tools that are not suitable for point-of-care (POC) diagnostics. Modern microfluidics, an extremely compressed, automated, and interconnected technological advance, has the possibility to replace established techniques for conducting rapid, cost-effective, precise, and local diagnosis. The most effective methods for infection diagnosis in microfluidic gadgets are molecular diagnosis. At this point, a six-gene level authorization of immunized cell modification, called Sepgroup, is defined using blood specimens from 587 in-house individuals with alleged sepsis, machine learning (ML), and cross-validation. This authorization is employed for predicting medical decline within the initial twenty-four hours (h) of medical display. 3179 cases from current autonomous groups have had their prognosis accuracy confirmed. In 249 individuals, a Sepgroup diagnostic assay depending on RT-PCR demonstrates a 95% sensitivity in anticipating deterioration of the serial organ dysfunction evaluation values over the first 24 hours. An independent centrifuge microfluidic device that detects whole-blood Sepgroup classifiers is evaluated; it demonstrates a 93% sensitivity and 90% specificity in determining the likelihood of medical deterioration in individuals with alleged sepsis. Microfluidic devices support studies on sepsis and offer resources for upcoming virus epidemics.

title: An Intelligent System for Graduate Admission Prediction
authors: Anishaa Chaudhuri, Saisha Goel, Nidhi Mishra
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Many graduates face difficulty understanding the varying admission criteria across universities and often remain unsure whether their profiles meet institutional requirements, making it challenging to select suitable universities within limited application windows. To address this, the paper proposes a Graduate Admission Prediction System that evaluates an applicant's profile and estimates their likelihood of admission using data-driven methods. It compares baseline ML models with ensemble techniques to identify the most effective approach, integrating a stacking ensemble model that analyzes academic and application parameters to predict admission chances. An LLM-based SOP evaluator powered by Gemini 1.5 Flash automatically assesses the quality of Statements of Purpose, while SHAP provides interpretability and personalized insights for applicants. The system also includes a university rating calculator and a guidance mechanism to help users improve their profiles, offering a reliable and transparent way for students to assess and enhance their graduate admission prospects.

title: Design and Implementation of Enhanced Multiplier-Free Architecture for VLSI Signal Processing
authors: Veera Boopathy E, Raghul G, Madhusudan Kulkarni, Syed Nageena Parveen, Thamilselvi R M, Vignesh M
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: High-throughput Datapath architecture is a critical issue in modern VLSI system design, especially high-performance Digital Signal Processing (DSP) systems. This paper presents an effective and compact 4-tap programmable Finite Impulse Response (FIR) filter architecture that uses a multiplexer-logic-based architectural design that is based on the Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) methodology, in which the traditional multipliers are replaced with shift and add operations. Moreover, architecture presents a new Multiplexer-Based Full Adder (MBFA) using a transmission gate to perform arithmetic functions with lower complexities. Further, a simplified Carry Select Adder (CSLA) is introduced, which comprises a single Ripple Carry Adder (RCA) enhanced with MBFA units, which provides significant area and delay savings as redundant adder paths are removed. A carry save adder (CSVA) is optimized, and also the delay elements are synthesized in the form of transmission gates and placed in the filter pipeline. The final implementation is analyzed by the TANNER EDA tool, displaying area savings of up to 16% and a reduction of up to 22% delay compared to that of a conventional FIR filter implementation. In addition, the suggested CSLA possesses impressive area and delay reduction of 49% and 65.5% respectively, compared to traditional CSLAs, confirming its suitability in low-power, high-speed DSP cores of modern VLSI systems.

title: Employing Blockchain to Provide Safe and Expandable Authentication Techniques: Decentralized Personal Identification Management for Internet of Things Devices
authors: M. Sukanya, V. Samuthira Pandi, J Salomi Backia Jothi, Khaled Tawfiq Al-Assaf, Amit Dutt, M. Dinesh
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The increasing number of Internet of Things (IoT) devices are imposing new security challenges on secure communication, identity management, and reliable authentication across heterogeneous networks. Traditional centralized identity systems have been increasingly challenged because of a single point of failure, lack of scalability, and susceptibility to data breaches. To mitigate these drawbacks, in this work, we present a new decentralized authentication framework based on blockchain for safe, scalable and verifiable authentication in IoT. Based on the characteristic of both decentralization and immutability, as well as the automatic executing features of smart contract, the method give every IoT device their unique verifiable digital identity and confirm their individual verifiable digital identity autonomous without the third institutions. The platform leverages a permissioned blockchain network design that ensures superiority in terms of transaction speed and resource utilization with respect to the resource constrained nature of most of the IoT devices. Identity verification requests got processed through consensus-driven protocols, secured and validated by smart contracts and it is guaranteed that they are tamper-proof, transparent and not vulnerable to identity spoofing or unauthorized access. Furthermore, cryptography techniques, such as PKI and DIDs, will provide more data’s confidentiality and a strong authentication among a large number of distributed nodes. The proposed architecture also introduces hierarchical trust models and role-based access policies to accommodate different levels of device importance and sensitivity. Experimental study on a simulated IoT environment shows that the blockchain based authentication scheme shows better performance than the traditional central entity-based system in scalability, fault tolerance & security robustness. The findings demonstrate authentication that is fast and unconstrained, with a decreasing likelihood of identity compromise and coherent interworking of disparate device manufacturers and network technologies. This research demonstrates that blockchain-based IoT decentralized identity and access management not only enhances cybersecurity but also paves the way for secure machine-to-machine communication at scale.

title: Deep Learning Approach for Accurate Detection of Common Leaf Diseases in Crops
authors: K Kiran, Rajkumar, Stuti Bhatt, Alankrita Joshi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The present article is an automatic study based on deep learning to detect and categorize plant leaf diseases on Common rust in Corn, Early blight in Potato, and Bacterial spot in Tomato. The proposed model makes use of a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture toward the maximization of features extraction and classification rates. The proposed CNN model has three convolutional layers whose filter depths expound on each other with max-pooling layers in between and a fully connected dense layer with dropout to avoid overfitting the models. Multi-class classification is implemented in the last output layer where softmax activation is done. The data is extracted from kaggle and methods like resizing, normalization, and augmentation are used to improve generalization of the model. Model performance is shown to be exceptional as experimental results indicate an overall accuracy of 99 percent across the test dataset. The classification report shows precision, recall and F1-scores approximating 1.0 in all three categories with a low rate of misclassification being recorded only in Early blight in Potato subset. The practical effectiveness of the model is supported by the demonstration in the confusion matrix since the classes are practically separated by the model without any mistake.

title: Secure File Transmission using Cryptography and Blockchain
authors: Ajay Vikram, Singh Sarika Jain, Bhasker Bachheti
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Digitally interconnected environment heavily relies on the secure transmission of files through digital channels. Organizations must implement robust programming solutions alongside secure encryption mechanisms to safeguard data during transfer. This work combines traditional symmetric encryption algorithms—Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) and Data Encryption Standard (DES)—with blockchain technology to ensure secure and verifiable file transmission. The proposed setup integrates the efficiency and security of AES/DES encryption with the immutability and transparency of blockchain, resulting in a data protection system that maintains confidentiality while offering auditability. According to test data, the combined AES/DES encryption and blockchain-based validation led to improved results in file transmission security and traceability.

title: Nonlinear Electrochemical Impedance and Harmonic Response of a 26650 LFP Battery Under Varying Current Amplitudes
authors: Umair Afzal, Ashley Fly
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper investigates the influence of perturbation amplitude on the electrochemical impedance response of lithium iron phosphate (LFP) cylindrical batteries across different states of charge (SOCs). Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy (EIS) was conducted on a 26650 format (2.6 Ah) LFP battery at 80%, 50%, and 30% SOCs. A wide range of current perturbation amplitudes, from 20 mA to 2.12 A, was applied using a Gamry Reference 3000 over a frequency range of 10 kHz to 10 mHz. The results show that higher amplitudes cause significant distortion in the impedance spectra, with nonlinear responses becoming more pronounced at lower SOCs. The presence and magnitude of the second and third harmonic voltage components indicate and quantify nonlinear distortion. The findings demonstrate the potential diagnostic value of harmonic analysis in identifying early signs of nonlinear electrochemical response in LFP batteries.

title: Improving Credit Decision with an Interpretable EBM-Based Model
authors: Prasanna R, John Bruno J, Saranya D
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Supply Chain Finance has been vulnerable to supplier default risks and opaque credit analysis. This paper proposes a credit risk model that evaluates CatBoost, Random Forest Variables, and Explainable Boosting Machine. Ablation tests confirm that CatBoost has 90% accuracy with AUC 0.90, followed by 89% for RF with AUC 0.90, while EBM has been found to be superior with 92% accuracy and AUC 0.93. EBM has been found superior to classical approaches of logistic regression with 82% and AUC 0.84, and decision tree with 85% and AUC 0.86. The model encompasses supplier payments, discrepancies, network dependencies, as well as macroeconomic variables. SHAP values reveal that late payments, liquidity, and order quantities are of prime significance. The model also ensures regulatory compliance with SR-11-7 model risk management practices, which are used to provide early warnings to sub-standard suppliers with algorithmic transparency.

title: Robust Speaker Verification Using Deep Embeddings in Noisy and Adverse Conditions
authors: Rajesh Maharudra Patil, Chaithanya D J, Vinodkumar P More, Iranna Makarabbi
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Biometric authentication Speaker verification systems are finding more and more applications in real-world biometric authentication, where noise resistance and channel sensitivity are essential. Most conventional methods, including GMM-UBM and i-vectors with PLDA, tend to deteriorate when the conditions are unfavorable acoustically. In the proposed research, we suggest a powerful speaker verification system, which is grounded on deep embeddings, utilizing computing structures, like ECAPA-TDNN, and noise-sensitive training methods. Generalization was improved by introducing massive data augmentation by using the MUSAN noise corpus and artificial room impulse responses (RIRs). Besides that, domain adversarial training was incorporated in order to reduce the variability due to unseen conditions. Results of the experimental on the IITG Multi-Variability Speaker Recognition Database have shown that the Equal Error Rate (EER) and the minimum Detection Cost Function (minDCF) are highly improved than the baseline systems. The discriminative capability and strength of the learned speaker embeddings were further demonstrated by visualization using t-SNE and heatmaps of similarity using cosine. The solution proposed is a good and scalable solution to the problem of speaker verification in real world situations.

title: A Multi-Objective Game Environment for Evaluating AI Agents
authors: Vamshidhar Reddy Parupally
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)

title: AI-Driven Energy Cost Optimization for Mixed-Use Buildings Using Machine Learning and Renewable Integration
authors: Furquan Nadeem, Mohamed Zaery, Syed Muhammad Amrr, S.M. Suhail Hussain, Mohammad A. Abido
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper presents an AI-enabled energy management framework for a mixed-use residential-commercial building, designed to minimize grid electricity costs while maximizing the utilization of on-site renewables. A modified IEEE-69 distribution model is adapted to represent the case study, integrating aggregated domestic and commercial loads with solar photovoltaic (PV), biogas generation, and a battery system at the point of common coupling (PCC). Short-term forecasts of cost drivers are obtained using machine learning models, among which the Random Forest regressor achieved the highest accuracy with a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of $4.11, Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of $5.83, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{R}^{2}$</tex> score of 0.97. These forecasts guide an optimization strategy that aligns flexible demand with renewable availability and tariff variations. The monthly energy cost was reduced from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\$10,742.51$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\$8,102.63$</tex>, yielding a 24.6% saving. The framework demonstrates how predictive analytics combined with cost-aware scheduling can achieve meaningful economic and sustainability gains, offering a compact and transferable solution for future smart city applications to achieve affordable and clean Energy.

title: Quantum-Resistant Cryptographic Models for Next-Gen Cybersecurity
authors: Navin Chhibber, Amber Rastogi, Ankur Mahida, Vatsal Gupta, Piyush Ranjan
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Another threat is the development of large quantum computers, which have a high likelihood of breaking the high popular security protocols because it can use both Shor and Grover algorithms. In order to fix this looming threat, quantum-resistant cryptographic systems, otherwise known as post-quantum cryptography (PQC), are being formulated to protect cybersecurity systems of the future. The current paper presents the state of the art in designing, realizing, and testing the security of robust quantum-resistant algorithms, paying attention to lattice-based, code-based, multivariate polynomial and hash-based cryptography. We discuss their resistance to classical and quantum attackers, distributed system scalability properties, and their deployment in practice (secure communications, blockchain, cloud computing infrastructures). Also, we study a hybrid cryptographic model that integrates the classical efficient cryptography scheme and a quantum-resilient cryptographic scheme to achieve a backward-compatible solution and simultaneously improving the forward security properties. With the experimental findings, it is evident that performance with reasonable computational footprint of the proposed framework succeeds to install amplified security fortitude which successfully harbours prolific cybersecurity systems of the future.

title: Design and Modelling of a robust type-3 Fuzzy controller for frequency stabilization enhancement of AC Microgrid
authors: Prakash Chandra Sahu, Sunil Kumar Bhatta, Sarat Chandra Swain, Buddhadeva Sahoo, Subhransu Ranjan Samantaray
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Due to their technological, financial, and environmental advantages, micro-grids are widely integrated into the power systems of modern electric networks. The intermittent nature of renewable energy resources and the stochastic behaviour of loads cause frequency oscillations in micro-grids, particularly in the islanded manner of functioning. This paper proposes an intelligent type-3 fuzzy logic controller (T3-FLC) for load-frequency regulation in islanded micro-grids made up of many energy resources. The T3-FLC approach’s gains are scheduled optimally by adopting an improved Sinh Cosh algorithm. The T3-FLC approach guarantees that the system’s frequency profile stabilizes smartly under any adverse conditions. The recommended T3-FLC has been selected for its advanced three-dimensional membership structure and broad control action. This intelligent T3-FLC control action sharply dominates the load dynamics and uncertainties and facilitates stable frequency performance within minimal time intervals. Further, the potential attribute of the recommended T3-FLC controller is compared to the efficacy of the type-2 FLC and standard fuzzy PID controller. It is concluded from the simulated frequency response that, intelligent T3-FLS smartly diminishes the settling time frequency error $(\Delta \mathrm{F})$ by 64% and 314% as related to that of T2-FLC and FPID approaches, respectively.

title: Performance Analysis of a Generalized Card Trick Algorithm: A Case Study in Algorithmic Optimization
authors: Pinak Salunke
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Self-working mathematical card tricks, while appearing as simple recreational puzzles, are demonstrations of robust deterministic algorithms. This paper presents an algorithmic analysis of the generalized N-card trick, framing it as an interactive permutation algorithm. We designed and implemented two distinct C++ versions: a “Naive Algorithm” that directly simulates the physical shuffling of cards by performing extensive data copying, and an “Optimized Algorithm” that uses a mathematical function based on logical indexing to calculate an item's final position without any data movement. A comparative benchmark was performed for various dataset sizes (N). The results demonstrate a significant performance difference, with the naive approach showing runtime growth consistent with its high memory overhead, while the optimized version's runtime remains nearly constant. This work showcases how a change in algorithmic paradigm from physical simulation to logical calculation can yield substantial performance gains and provides a foundation for developing efficient interactive selection systems.

title: A New Design of 5L Active Neutral Point Clamped Inverter Topology with Double Voltage Gain
authors: Marif Daula Siddique, Pooyan Alinaghi Hosseinabadi, Mehdi Seyedmahmoudian, Saad Mekhilef, Jose Moreira, Michael Negnevitsky, Alex Stojcevski
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: A five-level inverter topology with ANPC structure with reduction in switch count, and inherent voltage boosting capability has been developed in this paper. The developed design has a single DC input source, ten power switches, along with five capacitors arranged to achieve a voltage gain of two, thereby eliminating the need for bulky magnetic components such as transformers or inductors. This voltage gain is achieved through strategic switching and capacitor charging/discharging sequences that allow the inverter to generate five distinct voltage levels at the output. Its structure is inherently symmetrical and scalable, making it exceptionally appropriate for incorporation into both single-phase and three-phase medium-voltage utilization. Comprehensive simulation studies were conducted in PLECS software for both single-phase and three-phase implementations under various operating conditions, utilizing different modulation techniques.

title: Optimized Control Strategies for Microgrid Dynamics: LQR and ILQR Tuned via Metaheuristic Optimization Algorithms
authors: Khidr AK Mohamed, Muhammad Majid Gulzar, Md Shafiullah, Khalil Agail Mohamed
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Microgrid stability is critical with the increasing integration of renewable energy sources. This study investigates Linear Quadratic Regulator (LQR) and Integrator-Augmented LQR (ILQR) controllers to improve transient response, reduce steady-state errors, and enhance robustness against disturbances. Metaheuristic algorithms: Genetic Algorithm (GA), Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), and Differential Evolution (DE) are used for optimal parameter tuning. Results show that ILQR significantly outperforms classical LQR, with DE providing the fastest convergence and best overall performance. Simulations performed in Python confirm the practical effectiveness of integral action and optimized tuning in improving microgrid stability and accuracy.

title: XAI-MigNet: An Explainable AI Model for Migraine Prediction Using Feedforward Neural Networks and SHAP Analysis
authors: Debyanshu Tiwari, Gaganjot Kaur, Niyaz Ahmad Wani, Jatin Bedi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Leveraging Artificial Intelligence in clinical care can provide strong assistance in areas like forecasting diseases and diagnosing patients. But still many individuals think AI is too complicated to understand for making decisions, particularly in the healthcare field. This concern brings attention to the value of designing AI tools that bring precise predictions and interpretable insights. Migraine is a neurological illness that affects millions of people around the world, often resulting in intense pain and influencing the standard of living. In earlier times, doctors used to treat the migraine based on patients reported symptoms, which were not always accurate and clear. Detecting and diagnosing migraine in a timely manner can significantly enable tailored treatment strategies. In the last few years, deep learning models have emerged as powerful tools for finding out the subtle patterns and hidden relationships that were not easily captured by conventional statistical methods for the prediction of migraine disease. In this study, we have proposed a deep learning approach strategy for migraine prediction utilizing a Feedforward Neural Network with an overall accuracy of 91.25%. Moreover, approaches like Explainable AI such as SHAP (SHapley Additive Explanations), including summary plots and local and global explanations, have also been integrated to enhance the model’s interpretability and trust in clinical applications. The result demonstrates that the proposed approach not only achieves high accuracy but also provides valuable insight into the root cause of the migraine episodes, establishing it as a valuable solution for real-world health care applications.

title: Video Retrieval Using GAT Enhanced CLIP Model
authors: Mohd Adil, Nadeem Akhtar, Haris Shoaib, S. M. Shadab Khan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Video retrieval, the task of retrieving relevant videos given textual queries, is a critical component of modern multimedia systems and applications. This paper presents two CLIP-based video retrieval models evaluated on the widely-used MSR-VTT dataset: a baseline CLIP model with feedback (CLIP-Feedback) and an advanced model incorporating Graph Attention Networks (GAT) and scene-aware keyframe extraction with Rocchio feedback (CLIP-GAT). Both models leverage the CLIP framework but differ significantly in their feature extraction strategies, temporal aggregation techniques, and feedback mechanisms for refining retrieval. We fine-tune both models on a 7,000-video training split and evaluate on a separate 3,000-video test split, reporting Recall@K, Median Rank (MdR), Mean Rank (MnR), and Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR) metrics. The CLIP-GAT model achieves superior performance overall, with R@1 of 29.40% and MRR of 0.8678, compared to CLIP-Feedback's R@l of 25.39% and MRR of 0.7678, thereby demonstrating the efficacy of GAT-based temporal modeling, scene-aware processing, and feedback-enhanced retrieval performance.

title: Real-Time Offside Detection in Football Using Wearable Sensors and Embedded Ball Tracking
authors: Vibha Rao, Avni Jain, Bhuvana Prabha B, Kirankumar Manivannan
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Offside detection in football is a crucial yet challenging task that requires extreme precision and real time on field decision making to ensure fair game play. This conference paper suggests a dedicated Offside Detection System (ODS) designed to accurately identify offside rule violations using cost efficient sensor based technology. The system relies on compact, embedded sensors integrated into players' shoes and the football, continuously collecting real time positional data. A real-time processing unit analyses this data to decide offside occurrences with minimum latency, ensuring that gameplay remains uninterrupted. Due to the timing constraints of football matches, the ODS is optimised for low power consumption which extends the battery life of its own sensors and communication modules. Moreover, the system is highly portable and light which makes it very adaptable to the professional matches without affecting player performance. The real time processing capacity along with precise sensor data and efficient computational capability can help the referee in making immediate decisions through notifications thereby reducing human error in offside decision making. This research highlights our proposed system's potential to enhance accuracy and fairness in football officiating through innovative embedded sensor technology.

title: Islanding Detection of Distributed Generation using Rotation Double Random Forest
authors: Anasuya Roy Choudhury, Ranjan Kumar Mallick, Pravati Nayak, Sairam Mishra
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel method for islanding detection in a multi-distributed generator (multi-DG) based microgrid using Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) and Rotation Double Random Forest (RDRF). With the goal to properly capture non-linear and non-stationary properties, VMD has been employed to extract intrinsic mode functions (IMFs) from voltage signals at the point of common coupling (PCC). An RDRF classifier, employing dual-layered ensemble learning to improve accuracy and decrease variance, receives the statistical properties of features. Simulation studies under diverse scenarios, including normal and switching events, demonstrate high detection accuracy, low false detection rate, and a significantly reduced non-detection zone (NDZ), outperforming conventional classifiers

title: A Switched-Capacitor Based Midpoint-Clamped Multistage Inverter for Sustainable Power Applications
authors: Dipanwita Ganguly, Ranjit Kumar Barai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: This paper proposes the design, mathematical modeling, and performance evaluation of a three-phase switched-capacitor multilevel inverter (SCMLI) using a capacitor-grounded switched cell in a transformer-less (CGSCTL) arrangement. The proposed topology improves output voltage quality while reducing component count, voltage stress, and system complexity, making it well-suited for renewable energy systems, electric mobility, and grid-connected applications. The $\mathbf{d}-\mathbf{q}$ reference frame uses a proportionalintegral (PI) controller for accurate current regulation, a phase locked loop (PLL) for grid synchronization, and an LC filter to suppress harmonics. The simulation results show wellsynchronized sinusoidal voltage and current waveforms with minimal distortion. FFT analysis reveals a Total Harmonic Distortion (THD) of 0%, suggesting high power quality. The inverter achieves 96% peak efficiency at a rated power of 10 kW and optimized capacitor sizing. Comprehensive performance assessment covering THD, efficiency, and transient response validates the effectiveness of the proposed system, which further features soft-switching and a modular framework supporting scalability and reliability.

title: Applying Machine Learning to Identify Optimal Locations for Wind Energy Generation Based on Regional Suitability
authors: Aniket Mahesh Vyavhare, Dr. Chaitali Shewale, Sandeep Kadam, Dr. Veena Kadam, Sonali Shirke, Prof. Mansi Namose
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)

title: Review and Implementation of Digital Twin for MPPT Techniques in Grid-Connected PV Inverters
authors: Prateek Shrivastava, Nishant Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The Integration of single-phase, single-stage gridconnected solar photovoltaic (PV) systems is becoming more popular for use in residential and distributed energy applications. Although effective Maximum Power Point Tracking remains a challenge under rapidly changing irradiance and temperature conditions. Conventional MPPT techniques such as P&O, INC, and ANN often suffer from delayed response, local convergence, and oscillations. This paper presents a comprehensive review of MPPT algorithms for grid-connected single-stage PV inverters and introduces a Digital Twin (DT) framework to enhance MPPT performance. The available literature for the DT system enables real-time synchronization with the physical PV setup, adaptive control strategy selection, and dynamic model updating. Also, the available survey presents its results on simulation-based DT that indicate the DT-assisted MPPT achieves over 98% tracking efficiency, reduces convergence time to under $\mathbf{1 5 0 ~ m s}$, and improves system stability under variable environmental conditions. The study highlights the DT’s potential to transform MPPT control in single-phase solar PV systems by offering intelligent, flexible, and resilient operation. The objective of this review is to highlight the innovation in the development of a cohesive, scalable DT-based MPPT control framework, particularly designed for single-phase PV systems, hence integrating conventional control techniques with intelligent, adaptable PV benefits.

title: Hybrid Resampling-Driven Deep Learning Architecture for Cardiovascular Risk Stratification
authors: Dodda Venkata Reddy, Thogati Adi Lakshmi, Sunkara Harini, Sattenapalli Sadarani, Aadi Nagender Babu, Swapna Raghunath
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: cardiovascular diseases (CVD) are a significant health issue around the world, which gives the reason why an accurate and interpretable predictive system should be developed. This is a study that proposes a deep learning method that considers a compound resampling technique to consider data skew and enhance the risk prediction of cardiovascular disease. The framework contains five neural network models: TabNet, Attention-LSTM, a mix of CNN and BiLSTM, multilayer perceptron (MLP), and 1D CNN. SHAP explainability was applied to the TabNet, The MLP, and the CNN+BiLSTM models to increase the explained Ness of the model, and thus it revealed many new things regarding feature prominence. TabNet has been the model to perform the best out of all models that have been tested with an accuracy of 96.19%, but it provided good results on F1 and AUC as well. Albeit the results of the Attention-LSTM and 1D CNN performing slightly worse, the MLP and CNN+BiLSTM models also gave comparative results. Each of the models was trained on a common preprocessing pipeline. The findings indicate that aggregating multiple models of deep learning with focused explainability and balanced data strategies are capable of generating predictive tools of cardiovascular risks that are reliable and interpretable. This approach has a possibility to become a part of clinical support systems.

title: Synchrophasor Data Anomaly Detection Using Unsupervised Transformer Autoencoder
authors: Purna Kukadiya, Hussain M Mustafa, Cosmas Ifeanyi Nwakanma, Anurag K Srivastava, Trapti Jain, Neminath Hubballi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Detecting anomalies in synchrophasor measurement data is critical for improving situational awareness and supporting better decision-making in power grid operations and control, especially as cyber-physical threats continue to grow. This work proposes an unsupervised Transformer Autoencoder (TAE) for anomaly detection in Phasor Measurement Unit (PMU) data with spatio-temporal characteristics. The model leverages a self-attention mechanism to capture complex temporal dependencies and integrates an autoencoder structure for unsupervised learning. Unlike conventional autoencoder-based methods that require entirely clean training data, the proposed TAE can be trained on datasets containing mostly normal measurements with a small fraction of anomalies, making it more practical for real-world applications. In addition, a block-wise Z-score normalization scheme with moving window is introduced to improve robustness, enabling the model to better identify spatio-temporal variations in PMU data. The model is validated using a high-resolution PMU dataset generated from a cyber-power testbed that includes a wide range of realistic cyber and physical anomalies. Extensive experiments show that the proposed TAE delivers consistent accuracy and strong generalization across different thresholds and window sizes when applied to realistic datasets. Results indicate performance of the proposed TAE algorithm with precision, recall and F1-Score of 97.20%, 98.58%, and 97.89%, respectively.

title: Analysis of Risk Factors on the Stability of Grid-Following Inverter-Based Power Generation System
authors: Md Minarul Islam, Kashem M. Muttaqi, Danny Sutanto, Md. Moktadir Rahman, Oscar Alonso, Abdul Hasib Chowdhury, Taha Selim Ustun
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper presents the integration of a four-stage power command control system with an inverter-based power generation system (IBPGS) and its associated controller. A fifteenth-order small-signal state-space model has been developed to represent the dynamic behaviors of the IBPGS and its control architecture. To evaluate system robustness, variations in LCL filter parameters and grid impedance, both of which fluctuate during operation, have been systematically analyzed to determine their critical thresholds beyond which system instability may occur. The impact of weak and strong grid conditions on the stability of the IBPGS has been investigated. Eigenvalue trajectory analysis with root locus has been used to assess the stability limit of the IBPGS. The results of the analysis highlight the important role of LCL filter characteristics and grid impedance in governing the stability of inverter-based power systems.

title: zkPHIRE: A Programmable Accelerator for ZKPs over HIgh-degRee, Expressive Gates
authors: Alhad Daftardar, Jianqiao Mo, Joey Ah-kiow, Benedikt Bünz, Siddharth Garg, Brandon Reagen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKPs) have emerged as a powerful tool for secure and privacy-preserving computation. ZKPs enable one party to convince another of a statement's validity without revealing anything else. This capability has profound implications in many domains, including: machine learning, blockchain, image authentication, and electronic voting. Despite their potential, ZKPs have seen limited deployment because of their exceptionally high computational overhead, which manifests primarily during proof generation. To mitigate these overheads, a (growing) body of researchers have proposed hardware accelerators and GPU implementations of kernels and complete protocols. Prior art spans a wide variety of ZKP schemes that vary significantly in computational overhead, proof size, verifier cost, protocol setup, and trust. The latest, and widely used ZKP protocols are intentionally designed to balance these trade-offs. A particular challenge in modern ZKP systems is supporting complex, high-degree gates using the SumCheck protocol. We address this challenge with a novel programmable accelerator to efficiently handle arbitrary custom gates via SumCheck. Our accelerator achieves upwards of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1000 \times$</tex> geomean speedup over CPU-based SumChecks across a range of gate types. We include this unit in zkPHIRE, a programmable, full-system accelerator that accelerates the HyperPlonk protocol. zkPHIRE achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1486 \times$</tex> geomean speedup over CPU and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$11.87 \times$</tex> geomean speedup over the state-of-the-art at iso-area. Together, these results demonstrate compelling performance while scaling to large problem sizes (upwards of 2<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">30</sup> constraints) and maintaining small proof sizes (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4-5$</tex> KB).

title: Characterizing Cloud-Native LLM Inference at Bytedance and Exposing Optimization Challenges and Opportunities for Future AI Accelerators
authors: Jingwei Cai, Dehao Kong, Hantao Huang, Zishan Jiang, Zixuan Ma, Qingyu Guo, Zhenxing Zhang, Guiming Shi, Mingyu Gao, Kaisheng Ma, Minghui Yu
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: As a major provider of LLM inference services, ByteDance has continuously explored diverse accelerator options to meet the rapidly growing inference demands of various heterogeneous LLM scenarios with higher cost-effectiveness, thereby enabling LLMs to serve more people worldwide. However, during this process, we have found that the complexity and opacity of cloud scenarios and corresponding cloud accelerators make it difficult for academia and many innovative chip startups to fully understand the real demands and challenges of these scenarios, which in turn severely restricts innovation and application potential in this field. To bridge this gap, we first present and analyze the data and characteristics of the ByteDance Doubao LLM app across multiple dimensions, helping the community understand real-world cloud scenarios, and detail the challenges and opportunities we have identified. Second, we propose and plan to open-source our multi-level evaluation framework, XPU-Perf, which includes benchmarks spanning instructions, operators, and models. This framework improves interpretability and trustworthiness, and helps promising new accelerator architectures gain wider adoption and development. Finally, we present comparative results of four typical accelerators, summarize their shortcomings and challenges, conduct in-depth analysis, and highlight numerous architectural and scheduling innovation opportunities we have observed.

title: Exploration of LLM Workload Reliability Based on di/dt Effects and Voltage Droops
authors: Zhixing Jiang, Justin Garrigus, Allison Seigler, Ethan Syed, Yan-Lun Huang, Mehdi Sadi, Tawfik Rahal-Arabi, Lizy Kurian John
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Large language model (LLM) inference workloads have emerged as a critical reliability challenge for cloud GPU systems. Unlike traditional workloads, the highly structured execution of LLMs creates large power oscillations. These oscillations become a vulnerability when their frequency aligns with the resonant modes of a GPU's power delivery network (PDN), leading to excessive voltage droops and unreliable operation. In this work, we present the first comprehensive profiling of LLM-induced power oscillations, revealing that many workloads generate oscillatory patterns in the MHz range-critically aligning with typical GPU PDN resonant frequencies and leading to excessive voltage droops. To systematically investigate this phenomenon, we developed a novel stressmark framework that generates workloads with controllable, high-frequency power oscillations and voltage droops. Our evaluation shows that operating at a resonant frequency induces voltage droops up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times$</tex> larger than conventional workloads, exceeding critical noise margins. Critically, we find that real LLM workloads operating even near these frequencies generate significant voltage droops greater than 100 mV. Based on these findings, we propose a kernel staggering technique that mitigates this threat by shifting power oscillation frequencies away from resonance frequency, successfully reducing voltage droops and reducing reliability concerns. This work provides the first systematic understanding of LLM-PDN resonance and offers a practical solution to improve GPU reliability in AI cloud environments.

title: FractalCloud: A Fractal-Inspired Architecture for Efficient Large-Scale Point Cloud Processing
authors: Yuzhe Fu, Changchun Zhou, Hancheng Ye, Bowen Duan, Qiyu Huang, Chiyue Wei, Cong Guo, Hai Helen Li, Yiran Chen
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Three-dimensional (3D) point clouds are increasingly used in applications such as autonomous driving, robotics, and virtual reality (VR). Point-based neural networks (PNNs) have demonstrated strong performance in point cloud analysis, originally targeting small-scale inputs. However, as PNNs evolve to process large-scale point clouds with hundreds of thousands of points, all-to-all computation and global memory access in point cloud processing introduce substantial overhead, causing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$O\left(n^{2}\right)$</tex> computational complexity and memory traffic where <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n$</tex> is the number of points. Existing accelerators, primarily optimized for small-scale workloads, overlook this challenge and scale poorly due to inefficient partitioning and non-parallel architectures. To address these issues, we propose FractalCloud, a fractal-inspired hardware architecture for efficient large-scale 3D point cloud processing. FractalCloud introduces two key optimizations: (1) a co-designed Fractal method for shape-aware and hardware-friendly partitioning, and (2) block-parallel point operations that decompose and parallelize all point operations. A dedicated hardware design with on-chip fractal and flexible parallelism further enables fully parallel processing within limited memory resources. Implemented in 28 nm technology as a chip layout with a core area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.5 ~\text{mm}^{2}$</tex>, FractalCloud achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$21.7 \times$</tex> speedup and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$27 \times$</tex> energy reduction over state-of-the-art accelerators while maintaining network accuracy, demonstrating its scalability and efficiency for PNN inference. The code for FractalCloud is available at https://github.com/Yuzhe-Fu/FractalCloud.

title: Nugget: Portable Program Snippets
authors: Zhantong Qiu, Mahyar Samani, Jason Lowe-Power
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Evaluating architectural ideas on realistic workloads is increasingly challenging due to the prohibitive cost of detailed simulation and the lack of portable sampling tools. Existing targeted sampling techniques are often tied to specific binaries, incur significant overhead, and make rapid validation across systems infeasible. To address these limitations, we introduce Nugget, a flexible framework that enables portable sampling across simulators, hardware, architectural differences, and libraries. Nugget leverages LLVM IR to perform binary-independent interval analysis, then generates lightweight, cross-platform executable snippets, nuggets, that can be validated natively on real hardware before use in simulation. This approach decouples samples from specific binaries, dramatically reduces analysis overhead, and allows researchers to iterate on sampling methodologies while efficiently validating samples across diverse systems.

title: Research on personalized learning path optimization of electrical control and PLC courses based on machine learning algorithm

title: Integrating Advanced AI Models and Blockchain for Robust Fake News Detection
authors: Pinisetti Sowmya, Anil Kumar Muthevi
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The objective of the study is to explore cutting edge methods in machine learning and blockchain in managing and detecting fake news. The research enhances known models such as ANN and LSTM by applying Bi-directional GRU and XGBoost and this improves performance on classification tasks in fake news detection. It uses blockchain technology to secure data with XGBoost parallel processing SHA-256 transactions to improve processing speed and transaction volume. Its framework demonstrates 98% accuracy from XGBoost, signifying power of the framework. This integrated method presents a safe and accurate frontier for misinformation solving. The application of these technologies improves performance in classification and provides guarantees of trust in the system, thus solving the fake news problem at a system level.

title: A Hybrid AI-ML Framework for Predictive Maintenance and Load Forecasting in Smart Grid Infrastructures
authors: Vijayanand Selvaraj
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The transformation of traditional power systems into intelligent smart grids necessitates advanced data-driven solutions for operational reliability and energy efficiency. This research presents a hybrid artificial intelligence and machine learning framework that integrates Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) with Random Forest (RF) for predictive maintenance and combines Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks with XGBoost for short- and long-term load forecasting. The proposed architecture utilizes multivariate data from smart meters, sensors, and weather APIs, enabling real-time analysis of equipment health and demand patterns. Experimental results demonstrate that the hybrid CNN-RF model achieves over 94% classification accuracy in fault detection, while the LSTM-XGBoost forecasting model yields a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) as low as 2.5%. Comparative evaluations with traditional models such as ARIMA, SVM, and standalone LSTM confirm the superiority of the proposed dual-model strategy. This framework can serve as a modular foundation for intelligent grid automation, maintenance scheduling, and demand-side management in nextgeneration power infrastructures.

title: Predicting Mental Health Conditions Across Countries Using Lifestyle Factors: A Machine Learning Approach
authors: Epifelward Niño O. Amora, Evangeline N. Olandria
