title: Forecasting with Open-AI Transformer Model S and P 500 Index
authors: Ritu Kothiwal, Manoj Kumar, Harshit Bihani, Madala Sasidhar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Financial markets have been the focal point of economic inquiry for decades, evolving from simple barter systems to complex, global exchanges. Recent advances in AI-Powered deep learning have motivated researchers to apply neural networks for stock prediction. This paper applies AI- driven Open-AI Transformer model to forecast S&P 500 index by using historical S&P 500 data from 2021 to 2024. The model integrates macroeconomic variables such as USA GDP, Inflation rate and Bond yield and sentiment data. Results shows that the Open-AI Transformer model outperforms forecasting accuracy, with an RMSE of O. 018, MAPE of 3.9% and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex> of 0.85. The paper contributes to financial econometrics by demonstrating the benefits of integrating AI methodologies into forecasting complex financial markets.

title: Neuromorphic Computing and AI-Driven Risk Assessment: Toward Ultra-Low Latency Financial Decision Systems
authors: Mohd Shahid Ali, Mohd Atif, Naveen Siriman, Afzalur Rahman, Taufeeque Ahmad Siddiqui
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: As financial systems mature, decision-making systems that strike a beneficial spot between accuracy and ultralow latency are required. Although traditional AI approaches deliver state-of-the-art performance, they rely on computational ‘brute-forcing.’ These models are energy-intensive and computationally costly relative to real-time learning about high-dimensional financial time series. Additionally, high energy costs result in non-real-time adaptive systems based on trading. The current work proposes a novel approach, consisting of the neural and AI components, integrated with the Spiking Neural Networking, Reinforcement learning, and Deep Ensemble modules for financial risk assessment. Financial data time series are cast into spike trains and accumulated into Eligibility Trace on-chip neuro-memories to track the volatility and detect the anomalies. Experiments on barbarity financial bits including ctypto-currencies, stocks and quantitative-credited show the systems achieve low inference latency of ~35 micro-second, 30 x faster compared to baseline deep learning models with well-competitive accuracy power than 0.91. We also experiment back-testing and achieve enhanced 12% Sharpe ratio. Moreover, the neuromorphic hardware demonstrates almost two orders of magnitude lower in energy. These results in the potential of such neuromorphic-AI structures towards real-time high-frequency trading, fraud detection, and sustainable risk monitoring.

title: A Deep Learning Approach for Binary Classification of Bone Fractures in Multi-Region X-ray Images
authors: Srinivas Aluvala, Bhoomika, Stuti Bhatt, Alankrita Joshi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Medical practice often provides bone fractures as the most consistent injuries therefore appropriate diagnosis must occur immediately to avert extended medical complications. Radiological diagnosis of X-ray images via trained radiologists remains effective but becomes slowed with addition of human error especially during peak patient exam hours. The research creates an automatic bone fracture detection system through the use of the Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) deep learning architecture which is excellent for image classification tasks. The data for the research was gathered from Kaggle's open-source database consisting of 10,580 X-rays that are either fractured or not fractured versions. The suggested CNN model functioned across the various subsets recording a 96% overall classification accuracy on training as well as evaluation. Both diagnosis classes performed strongly with accurate assessments so the model obtained 0.96 values for F1-score, recall, and precision which confirmed its generalized and reliable detection capability. The study validates that models developed on CNN technology offer great assistance to doctors for timely and accurate bone fracture detection that enables informed patient treatment choices.

title: Resource Allocation in Cloud Computing: A Review of Scheduling Algorithms and Optimization Techniques
authors: Sesha Sai Sravanthi Valiveti
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Intelligent resource management solutions are becoming more important in distributed computing settings due to the rising demands for efficiency, scalability, and flexibility. In this setting, optimising strategies and scheduling algorithms are crucial for managing diverse resources and dynamic workloads in the cloud. Focussing on heuristic, metaheuristic, and machine learning-based approaches, this review offers a thorough analysis of state-of-the-art methodologies for resource allocation. Performance parameters including cost efficiency, energy consumption, response time, and resource utilization are assessed using techniques like deep reinforcement learning, supervised learning, genetic algorithms, and particle swarm optimization. By categorizing and comparing these methods, the study highlights their respective strengths, limitations, and applicability across various cloud scenarios. The paper concludes by identifying critical research gaps and proposing future directions aimed at improving real-time decision-making, supporting hybrid and multi-cloud systems, and advancing adaptive, intelligent resource orchestration frameworks.

title: An Attention-Driven CNN-GRU Framework for Handwritten Character Recognition
authors: Sunkara Revathi, Buddaraju Revathi, Seethalam Surya Prakash, Pandu Siva Satyanarayana, Shaik Baba Vali
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Optical Character Recognition (OCR) is a process that is used to convert the handwritten text into a digital format so that computers can understand. Telugu, one of the widely used languages in South India, has more complex and similar characters, which bring challenges to the OCR systems. To overcome these challenges, this study introduced a hybrid deep learning approach that combines Convolution Neural Network (CNNs) for the extraction of the pictorial features, Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Units (Bi-GRU) for understanding sequential information and an additive attention Mechanism to high-light important features. This hybrid model is trained and tested on well-known datasets and achieved an accuracy of 97.396% with a test loss of 0.1050. This shows how this method makes OCR systems more precise and reliable when working with challenging scripts such as Telugu.

title: Bi-Level Siting and Sizing Optimization Method for Energy Storage Systems with Source-Grid Coordination
authors: Yu Cheng, Canbing Li, Dawei Chen, Yuhang Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The volatility of wind and photovoltaic (PV) power poses significant challenges to grid stability, where energy storage systems (ESSs) serve as critical flexibility resources. However, the existing ESS configuration methods lack the source-grid coordinated optimization considering the distinct frequency characteristics between wind and PV fluctuations. In this paper, a source-grid coordinated optimization method for ESS siting and sizing is proposed. Based on empirical mode decomposition (EMD), the renewable energy outputs are decomposed into grid-connected component and source level energy storage allocation tasks. An improved multi-objective particle swarm optimizer (IMOPSO) is utilized to site and size grid level energy storage. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Cognitive AI for Proactive Cyber Defense: A Rule-Based Multi-Agent Approach
authors: Vijayanand Selvaraj
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: As cyber threats evolve in complexity and frequency, traditional defense mechanisms struggle to keep pace. This paper introduces a cognitive Artificial Intelligence (AI) driven framework using a rule-based multi-agent system (MAS) for proactive cyber defense. The architecture simulates human-like decision-making and collaborative threat response by leveraging knowledge bases, behavior rules, and autonomous agents. Each agent specializes in tasks such as threat detection, risk assessment, mitigation planning, and adaptive learning. Through a hybrid integration of symbolic reasoning and real-time situational awareness, the system anticipates, interprets, and reacts to cyber events. Experimental simulations demonstrate significant improvements in detection accuracy, response speed, and adaptability, making the approach suitable for dynamic and large-scale cybersecurity environments.

title: Designing Intelligence at the Edge: Smart Laboratory Model for Remote & Safe Power Electronics Education
authors: Khushi Mukeshsingh Bhadouriya, Adityasingh Shashikantsingh Rajput, Smit Dharmeshkumar Parmar, Prashant Dineshkumar Tiwari, Ashish P. Patel, Nirav D. Mehta
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Hands-on laboratory work remains the backbone of learning in power electronics, but conventional setups still struggle with persistent safety risks, limited scalability, and almost no provision for remote access. These shortcomings have become more apparent with the growth of hybrid and distance education. To address them, this study details the creation of a Smart Power Electronics Laboratory that combines real-time sensing, predictive analytics, and automated fault prevention. The system follows a four-layer architecture built around low-cost embedded hardware, most notably the ESP32-S3 microcontroller paired with voltage, current, and thermal sensors linked to an AI-based decision layer. At its core, a compact Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model forecasts thermal conditions up to 30 seconds in advance with 93 % mean accuracy (±2.1 % standard deviation across trials, 95 % CI: 91.2–94.5 %), enabling early PWM shutdown and relay isolation before unsafe limits are reached. Complementing this, an Isolation Forest detector identifies short circuits, load imbalances, and waveform distortions in under 1.2 s. A secure, cloud-connected dashboard allows users to view live waveforms, issue control commands, and receive automated alerts, while logging all experimental data for later analysis. When compared with conventional laboratory setups, the proposed system achieved a 40 % improvement in uptime and recorded zero device failures during extended testing. The design is modular, affordable, and easily replicable, making it adaptable for a range of academic courses and providing safer, more flexible, and engaging laboratory experiences.

title: Survey of Task-Offloading Mechanisms in Mobile Edge Computing Environments
authors: Sandeep Gupta, Kuldeep Narayan Tripathi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The growing demand for real-time, data-intensive applications like autonomous driving, video streaming, and smart IoT services, Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) has become a key enabler for low-latency and high-efficiency computing at the network edge. This survey explores recent research efforts focused on intelligent task offloading, efficient resource allocation, and service management in MEC-enabled systems. This work reviews and analyzes influential research works that span a variety of environments, including vehicular networks, UAV-assisted systems, IoT devices in multi-hop settings, and high-speed mobile scenarios. These works leverage approaches such as deep reinforcement learning, federated learning, trajectory optimization, and game-theoretic approaches to tackle challenges like network dynamics, energy constraints, task heterogeneity, and caching dependencies. By comparing their methods, tools, and findings, it highlight how these solutions elevate system performance, minimize latency, and enhance user experience. This work also discusses open challenges related to scalability, mobility, and real-time adaptability. This survey aims to guide researchers and practitioners in understanding current trends and shaping the next generation of edge computing systems for intelligent, responsive, and sustainable networks.

title: VisualArt: Adaptive Art Tools for Paralyzed Individuals
authors: Sahana M S, Tejaswini S, Ramya B
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Sign language is a vital communication method for deaf and non-verbal individuals, yet interactions with those unfamiliar with sign language present significant challenges. Traditional methods like written communication or human interpreters can be time-consuming and may not always be available. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes an innovative device designed to translate hand gestures into text output. The Sign to Text Conversion System aims to address these limitations through an integrated approach combining gesture recognition, machine learning, OpenCV package, Media Pipe package which has Hand Tracking module, numpy package and natural language processing. By converting these gestures into real-time voice and text, it facilitates seamless communication between sign language users and those who do not understand it, ensuring a more inclusive interaction. This application leverages the OpenCV library to handle real-time data from the webcam. By translating hand gestures into universally understandable outputs, this device represents a significant step toward creating a more inclusive and connected society.

title: A Hybrid Solar-Biodiesel Submersible Water Pump System for Providing Fresh-Water in Residential Area: IKN Nusantara Case Study
authors: Prabowo, Burhanuddin Halimi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: IKN Nusantara, in East Kalimantan Province, was officially decided as the new capital of the country based on Law No. 3 of 2022 on January 18, 2022. However, several villages in the zero-point location of IKN Nusantara still have trouble in clean water supply. This study aims to design a hybrid power generation system to supply submersible and suction pumps system for clean water utilities in IKN Nusantara area. In this paper, it proposes two alternative scenarios to provide clean water by adopting some potential local renewable energy resources, namely solar-wind hybrid system and solar-biodiesel hybrid system. The analysis focuses on Bumi Harapan Village and Bukit Raya Village locations. Both villages are in the central government of IKN Nusantara. The analysis results show that the solar-biodiesel hybrid system provides the best solution. This system can produce <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$5,510 \text{kWh} /$</tex> year, with an effective power absorption by the pump load of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$4,109.9 \text{kWh} /$</tex> year during operating hours from 07:00 to 15:00 local time. The levelized cost of electricity and fresh-water production are <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\operatorname{Rp3.987,85} / \text{kWh}\left(4,757.51 \mathrm{m}^{3} /\right.{}$</tex> year) and Rp3.987, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$81 / \text{kWh}\left(7,348.29 \mathrm{m}^{3} /\right.{}$</tex> year) for Bumi Harapan and Bukit Raya, respectively. This power generation system can reduce <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{CO}_{2}$</tex> emissions significantly from positive <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2,267 \text{kg} / \text{yr}$</tex> to negative 22.2 kg/year.

title: Predicting DRAM Failures at Scale: A Two-Stage Approach for Heterogeneous Systems
authors: Chenglin Wang, Shouxin Wang, Zhirong Shen, Lu Tang, Shuyue Zhou, Ronglong Wu, Min Zhou, Jialiang Yu, Yiming Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Memory failures in large-scale production environments pose critical threats to system reliability and service availability. While existing studies have conducted in-depth analyses of the temporal and spatial correlations of memory errors, differences in characteristics across architectures remain largely unexplored. To uncover these overlooked correlations, this paper conducts an extensive analysis of over 130,000 DDR4 DIMMs collected from large-scale heterogeneous production clusters over a nine-month period. Through systematic spatial and temporal analysis across two Intel x86 architectures and four major DRAM vendors, we uncover five new findings and propose a novel twostage training strategy. This strategy addresses sample quality issues by applying temporal weighting to positive samples and adaptive reweighting to negative samples. It also incorporates comprehensive multi-dimensional feature engineering, covering static, spatial, temporal, and micro-level characteristics. Finally, it integrates dual-driven sampling strategies and adaptive prediction timing to balance prediction accuracy and operational efficiency. Extensive evaluation shows that our CatBoost-based model achieves F1-scores of 49.9% on Intel x86v5 and 57.6% on Intel x86v6, substantially outperforming existing methods. This cross-architecture validation demonstrates the robustness and generalization of our approach across different hardware platforms. To the best of our knowledge, our work presents the first large-scale cross-architecture analysis of memory error patterns and provides new insights for production-scale memory failure prediction systems.

title: RetinoAI: Smart Screening for Diabetic Retinopathy with EfficientNetV2
authors: C Aishwarya, K. Asika, A. Geerthana Silviya
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a persistent ophthalmic obstacle of diabetes that, if undetected, can result in irreversible vision loss. This paper presents an automated DR classification system using EfficientNetV2, an up-to-date Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architecture optimized for accuracy and efficiency. The model is trained on high-resolution retinal images grouped into five levels of severity: No_DR, Mild, Moderate, Severe, and Proliferative. EfficientNetV2 incorporates progressive learning and compound scaling, achieving faster convergence and superior classification performance compared to traditional CNN models. Experimental evaluation confirms the model’s robustness across all DR stages. The offered system provides a secure, non-invasive, and an adaptable way out for early DR detection, supporting ophthalmologists in clinical diagnosis and contributing to improved patient outcomes.

title: A Cybersecurity - Driven Software Metrics Framework for Continuos Risk Quantification and Anomaly Detection
authors: Arun Kumar Elengovan, Nandagopal Seshagiri, Isan Sahoo, Kathiresan Jayabalan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Modern systems running on software are in an increasingly hostile cyber environment where the traditional methods of risk assessment are not sufficient to provide continuous, context aware information of vulnerabilities and anomalous behavior. The root issue solved in this research is the lack of an integrated framework unifying the software engineering metrics and security telemetry to risk quantification for real-time use. This work is an attempt to develop a cybersecurity-driven software metrics framework to continuously measure, analyze, and predict security risk with the help of hybrid machine learning, graph-based dependency modeling, and attack path simulation. The proposed method combines static code indicators, runtime traces, threat intelligence and vulnerability scores into a multi-domain feature representation which is processed by supervised, unsupervised and graph neural models. Quantitative evaluation shows some huge improvements in comparison to top 5 baselines with an F1-score of 0.87, an AUC-ROC of 0.94, and reduces the mean time to detect incidents to 3.5 minutes. These results provide validation and proof of effectiveness for adoption of multi-modal feedback-based analytics pipeline for improving operational security posture. The study concludes that the combination of engineering metrics and security signals opens up the possibility of more accurate and pro-active risk management and to scale a path for the adoption of next generation DevSecOps automation.

title: A Comprehensive Computer Vision Framework for Brain Tumor Classification and Explainable Analysis from MRI Scans
authors: Aaron Xavier. S, M. Akhil Reddy, Naveen. S, Radha. D
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Early and precise detection of brain tumors from Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) is crucial for treatment planning and improving patient outcomes. This work presents a computer vision system for classifying brain tumors, specifically glioma, meningioma, and pituitary tumors, along with a ’no tumor’ category. The method starts with preprocessing MRI scans using Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE) to enhance image features. Next, we apply data augmentation to strengthen the dataset. The system uses five popular pre-trained Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) architectures: VGG16, ResNet50, EfficientNetB0, InceptionV3, and DenseNet121, following a transfer learning approach. We concentrated on feature extraction by training a custom classifier head on top of frozen base layers, which allows for quick and effective model development. A comparison of these models on the preprocessed dataset revealed that InceptionV3 and DenseNet121 performed best, with InceptionV3 reaching a validation accuracy of 85.20% and a weighted F1-score of 0.8499. We used GradCAM to visualize key areas in the images that influenced model predictions. The complete system is available as an interactive Gradio web application. It provides not only tumor classification but also detailed visual and quantitative analyses to help users understand how the model makes decisions. This work showcases a practical method for brain tumor classification while focusing on explainability through integrated computer vision techniques, offering a valuable tool for education and research.

title: Smart Pill Dispenser for Real-Time Elderly Care Through IoT, AI Insights, and Emergency Alerts
authors: Snehal Bankatrao Shinde, Harsh Yadav, Nileshchandra Pikle, Satyam Gupta, Siddarth Khandelwal, Tausif Diwan
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: This paper presents an IoT-based solution designed to enhance medication management and adherence, particularly for the elderly and individuals managing chronic conditions. The background highlights the growing need for reliable medication adherence systems due to challenges faced by patients in managing complex medication routines, leading to missed doses and potential health risks. The proposed work focuses on the design, implementation, and evaluation of a smart dispenser that automates pill delivery at user-defined intervals. The novelty of the work is the combination of features: high-precision dispensing using a stepper motor (768 steps/revolution), real-time schedule management via a Wi-Fi-enabled web interface, precise timing synchronized with NTP servers, enhanced safety through IR hand detection for dispensing, a push-button emergency alert system with Telegram notifications, and AI-driven insights into the consequences of missed doses. Experimental results demonstrate the system’s effectiveness, achieving a delivery success rate of 98.5% and an emergency notification response time of less than 3 seconds.

title: Enhancing PV Array Performance Under Partial Shading Using Logic Shifting Array Reconfiguration
authors: Asit Kumar Parida, Alivarani Mohapatra, Rudra Narayan Dash
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: With the growing global demand for renewable energy, Improving the operational efficiency and reliability of photovoltaic (PV) systems. has become increasingly critical. Although PV arrays offer a sustainable energy solution, their performance can be significantly impacted by factors such as partial shading, system expansion, and sub optimal configurations. To address these challenges, PV array reconfiguration,encompassing changes in both physical layout and electrical interconnection has emerged as a key strategy for enhancing energy output and overall system effectiveness. The motivations for reconfiguration include shading mitigation, system scalability, optimization of tilt and orientation, and fault correction. A wide range of methods has been proposed, from manual orientation adjustments to advanced dynamic tracking systems and intelligent algorithms that leverage parameters like solar irradiance and panel characteristics to improve performance. The effectiveness of these reconfiguration techniques is typically evaluated based on improvements in energy yield, reliability, and cost efficiency. In addition to reviewing existing strategies, this paper introduces a novel approach known as the Logic Shifting (LS) technique. Integrated within a Total-Cross-Tied (TCT) interconnection framework, the LS technique enables the physical rearrangement of PV panels using a distinctive numbering sequence, thereby enhancing overall energy generation under varying operating conditions..

title: High-Frequency Modeling of Electrical Machines Using Physics-Constrained Neural Network
authors: Ravneel Prasad, Bingwei Jing, Guoyu Chu, Rukmi Dutta
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: High-frequency modeling of motor drives is essential for accurate electromagnetic interference (EMI) analysis, requiring precise estimation of parasitic parameters such as resistances, inductances, and capacitances. Traditional parameter extraction methods rely on analytical derivations that introduce uncertainties due to simplifying assumptions, particularly in high-frequency regimes where impedance behavior is highly nonlinear. In this work, a Physics Constrained Neural Network (PCCN) based approach is proposed that leverages measured frequency response data to extract parasitic parameters with high accuracy while ensuring compliance with the underlying impedance equations. The PCNN architecture integrates domain knowledge by embedding the governing equations of differential and common mode configuration into the loss function. The model is trained using measured frequency response data and optimized through a hybrid approach that combines neural network predictions with physics-constrained optimization. Results demonstrate that the PCNN-based method provides improved parameter estimation accuracy, ensuring better generalization across varying frequency ranges.

title: Modeling SLA Breach Probability for Critical Fintech Data

publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
