title: Virtual Educational Dashboard for Children with ASD and ID
authors: Ekta Ukey, Sampriti Dogra, Tanvi Gharat, Sourabh Gokave
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Numerous children suffering with Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) also have Intellectual Disorder (ID). But the conventional way of education for children with ASD and ID are inadequately Structured. The Virtual Dashboard, is a technology driven platform designed to support the logical, emotional and social development of ASD and ID. It includes the features like visual signs, interactive literacy modules, audio-visual communication tools and physical activity guides to take care of individual requirements. This model integrates these structured learning process which will address emotional and behavioral challenges and creates a safe, accessible and engaging platform. The paper aims to promote lifelong learning and emotional self-development, ensuring that every child feels confident to achieve their dreams.

title: Equation-of-Motion-Based Assessment Method for Device Grid-Forming Capability Under Large Disturbances
authors: Xin Li, Wei He, Hui Yang, Yunpeng Zhou
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: In recent years, grid-forming (GFM) achieved through various power electronic devices has become a major research focus. Clearly defining the grid-forming capability of different devices is a prerequisite for optimizing their characteristics. Based on the concept of motion equations, this paper proposes a method to evaluate the grid-forming capability of devices under large disturbances. Firstly, utilizing a unified modeling approach based on motion equations, nonlinear motion equation models for both grid-following (GFL) and grid-forming devices were constructed. Secondly, a method for handling nonlinear blocks considering the upper and lower limits of state variable variations is introduced, detailing the process to obtain clusters of response curves used for assessing grid-forming capability. Finally, this method is applied to quantify the gridforming capability of GFM/GFL devices under large disturbances, investigates the impact of virtual synchronous grid-forming control parameters on this capability, and verifies the effectiveness of the proposed method through time-domain simulations.

title: A Secure and Intelligent Data Transfer System for Cloud Environments using Hybrid Cryptography and Deep Learning-based Intrusion Detection
authors: Rachana P, Lohit Mahadev Patgar, Mohammed Farhan, Pranam J, Ravikumar Ravikumar
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: With the rapid evolution of cloud computing, ensuring data security during transmission has become more critical than ever. Modern cyberattacks often stay a step ahead of conventional security mechanisms [1], [2]. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a multi-layered security framework that integrates advanced cryptographic techniques with intelligent intrusion detection.The framework employs Elliptic Curve Diffie–Hellman (ECDH) with Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) for secure key exchange [6], AES-256 in Galois/Counter Mode (GCM) for strong encryption and authentication [9], [11], and digital signatures for validating trusted entities [7]. A comprehensive threat model and well-defined security assumptions are provided to outline the guarantees of the proposed system.In addition to encryption, the model integrates an intrusion detection system powered by a Deep Neural Network (DNN) that continuously monitors network and system behavior to detect anomalies in real time [16]. We detail the DNN architecture, characteristics of the training dataset (approximately 100,000 cryptographic anomaly instances), and the evaluation methodology.Experimental results show that the architecture achieves outstanding cryptographic performance with only a 2.6% increase in latency for 100 MB files, while maintaining a 98.5% detection accuracy and an F1-score of 97.1% on benchmark datasets [15], [19]. Furthermore, an in-depth analysis of performance overhead and scalability demonstrates the framework’s practicality in real-world cloud environments. By combining advanced cryptography with adaptive intelligence, this work presents a robust and realistic approach to securing cloud data transfers against Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attacks, data tampering, and sophisticated intrusion attempts.

title: Hardware in Loop Validation for Dfig Control Under Normal and Abnormal Grid Conditions
authors: Jitendra Kumar Mahawar, Gururaj Mirle Vishwanath, Saikat Chakrabarti
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Doubly fed induction generator (DFIG)-based wind turbines contribute significant power to power systems. These networks frequently experience both balanced and unbalanced load variations, which can impact system stability and performance. This paper presents a hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) validation of a DFIG control under balanced and unbalanced load conditions and different operating conditions of DFIG. The HIL setup consists of a real-time digital simulator, a 2.5 kW DFIG, and an FPGA-based controller that controls the DFIG in real time. Unbalanced load changes may result in voltage unbalance at the point of common coupling, leading to oscillations in operating quantities of the DFIG. To minimize these effects, a negative-sequence rotor current minimization technique is implemented in the rotor side control of the DFIG. This approach effectively reduces the magnitude of oscillations in the DFIG's operating quantities, enhancing performance under unbalanced grid conditions.

title: Distributed Synchronous Condenser Excitation Control for Voltage Stability Enhancement in Power Systems with High Renewable Energy Penetration
authors: Qiufei Xu, Hongming Yang, Hanbin Diao, Luyu Liu, Jian Zhao, Archie James Johnston
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: In areas with high renewable energy penetration, the declining proportion of thermal power units reduces system short-circuit capacity (SCC) and weakens reactive power support capability, thereby increasing the risk of transient voltage instability. To address this issue, this paper proposes an excitation control strategy for distributed synchronous compensators that enhances voltage stability through rapid dynamic reactive power support. A reactive power output model of the distributed synchronous condensers is developed, and a voltage-reactive power coordinated excitation control strategy is designed. By leveraging their overexcitation capability, the proposed strategy dynamically compensates for the reactive power deficit at the point of common coupling. Using operational data from a regional power grid, shallow voltage sag and deep voltage sag fault scenarios are established under renewable energy penetration levels of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3 0 \%, ~} \mathbf{6 0 \%}$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 0 \%}$</tex>. Simulation results demonstrate that deploying distributed synchronous condensers enhances the system's voltage sag ride-through capability by 20 % to 30 % and reduces the voltage recovery time by approximately 20 %. Under an 80 % renewable energy penetration scenario, the proposed approach maintains the voltage sag nadir above 0.2 p.u., thereby effectively mitigating the risk of system voltage instability.

title: RPU – A Reasoning Processing Unit
authors: Matthew Joseph Adiletta, Gu-Yeon Wei, David Brooks
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Large language model (LLM) inference performance is increasingly bottlenecked by the memory wall. While GPUs continue to scale raw compute throughput, they struggle to deliver scalable performance for memory bandwidth bound workloads. This challenge is amplified by emerging reasoning LLM applications, where long output sequences, low arithmetic intensity, and tight latency constraints demand significantly higher memory bandwidth. As a result, system utilization drops and energy per inference rises, highlighting the need for an optimized system architecture for scalable memory bandwidth. To address these challenges we present the Reasoning Processing Unit (RPU), a chiplet-based architecture designed to address the challenges of the modern memory wall. RPU introduces: (1) A Capacity-Optimized High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM-CO) that trades capacity for lower energy and cost; (2) a scalable chiplet architecture featuring a bandwidth-first power and area provisioning design; and (3) a decoupled microarchitecture that separates memory, compute, and communication pipelines to sustain high bandwidth utilization. Simulation results show that RPU performs up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$45.3 \times$</tex> lower latency and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$18.6 \times$</tex> higher throughput over an H100 system at ISO-TDP on Llama3-405B.

title: Scaling Graph Neural Network Training via Geometric Optimization
authors: Fangzhou Ye, Lingxiang Yin, Hao Zheng
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Wafer-scale computing has emerged as an alternative solution to sustain performance scaling in the post-Moore era, driven by recent technology advancements such as chiplet integration. This enables considerable computing and storage capabilities on a single chip, making it capable of accommodating large machine learning models and datasets. Recent efforts have heralded the promise of wafer-scale architectures for deep learning inference and training. However, scaling the training of Graph Neural Networks in wafer-scale architecture remains a challenge and is relatively unexplored due to irregularities in gradient propagation as well as physical constraints from flat on-chip topologies. In this paper, we propose Aster, a topology-aware framework designed to efficiently support GNN training on arbitrary wafer-scale architectures. The proposed framework, as opposed to the current application or topology-specific heuristics, can be generalized to support any network topology and irregular GNN datasets. Specifically, we mathematically formulate commonly-seen network topologies in their geometric representation and prioritize communication efficiency during GNN workload partitioning and mapping. Based on the geometric representation, we propose a quadratic assignment problem solver to efficiently map irregular dataflows to a flat topology with reduced communication distance. The simulation results show that Aster can achieve performance speedup by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.91 \times, 1.50 \times, 1.84 \times$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.58 \times$</tex> in Mesh and speedup by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.84 \times, 1.56 \times, 2.05 \times$</tex>, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.49 \times$</tex> in Torus on average compared to Mini-cut [1], ScalaGraph [2], ChunkV [3], and Chunk-E [4], respectively.

title: Breaker Failure Backup Protection Scheme for MTDC Network
authors: Praveen Kumar Gupta, S.R. Samantaray
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Multiterminal High Voltage Direct Current (MTDC) networks are expected to become more complex in future, necessitating a robust protection scheme similar to those employed in AC transmission systems. In response, this paper introduces a communication-based breaker failure backup protection scheme, that employs change in current along with Exponential Moving Average (EMA). The use of EMA improves the schemes adaptability and efficiency by leveraging both previous and present samples. The scheme operates in parallel with primary protection scheme, enhancing network resilience, ensuring precise fault detection. The scheme operates without any dead zone, and requires only line current for an efficient operation. Demonstrating satisfactory performance, the proposed scheme achieves an operating time of less than 4 ms across the range of fault resistances varying from solid faults to $500 \Omega$ across the line.

title: Secure and Efficient Management of EV Charging in Microgrids Using LSTM Networks
authors: Manish Bharat, Ritesh Dash, Sarat Chandra Swain, Ranjeeta Patel, Jaya Krishna
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The demand for electric vehicles (EVs) has increased significantly in recent years, making it necessary to accurately forecast the amount of charging that EVs will require. When assessing its effects on the electrical system, which includes peak demand management and power management, this forecast is essential. For a 15 -minute resolution, this research study provides a new LSTM based deep neural network method to forecast the demand to charge the electric vehicle in the charging station. We also use AES-128 for secure user communication and station quantisation. In comparison to LSTM, our suggested approach delivers a 13.01% improvement in demand accuracy and a 9.2% decrease in both the mean absolute error and the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE). The suggested methodology provides a details comparison analysis between Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) and Mean Percentage Error (MPE). This research also provides 12 months forecast report on EV charging unit using python programming tools for prediction analysis.

title: LiLo: Harnessing the on-Chip Accelerators in Intel CPUs for Compressed LLM Inference Acceleration
authors: Hyungyo Kim, Qirong Xia, Jinghan Huang, Nachuan Wang, Younjoo Lee, Jung Ho Ahn, Wajdi K Feghali, Ren Wang, Nam Sung Kim
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: The ever-growing sizes of large language models (LLMs) introduce significant infrastructure challenges due to their immense memory capacity demands. While the de facto approach has been to deploy multiple high-end GPUs, each with a limited memory capacity, the prohibitive cost of such systems has become a major barrier to the widespread deployment of frontier LLMs. As a result, CPU-based inference has become an appealing and cost-efficient alternative, since a CPU can offer an order of magnitude larger memory capacity at a fraction of the cost while providing competitive throughput for matrixvector multiplication with the latest Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). It not only broadens accessibility for users without multiGPU setups but also enables hyperscalers to leverage underutilized CPU servers to accommodate temporarily surging inference demand. Nevertheless, even CPU's large memory capacity has become insufficient to serve LLMs with hundreds of billions of parameters. Under the memory capacity constraint, we may offload parameters to storage devices and fetch them on demand, but doing so significantly degrades inference performance due to the high latency and low bandwidth of storage devices. To address this challenge, we propose LILO, an LLM inference framework that leverages In-memory Analytics Accelerator (IAA) in the latest Intel CPUs, to accelerate inference under memory capacity constraints. By storing model parameters in a compressed format and decompressing them on demand using IAA, LILO enables significantly reduced storage access during inference under memory capacity constraints while preserving the model accuracy and behavior. LILO orchestrates the concurrent execution of on-chip accelerators, i.e., IAA, Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX), and AMX, to facilitate high-throughput decompression alongside inference computation. Furthermore, LILO implements selective compression, a Mixture-of-Expert (MoE)-aware optimization that reduces the decompression overhead by up to 1.9×. We demonstrate that LILO reduces inference latency by up to 4.9× and 4.3× for Llama3-405B and DeepSeekR1, respectively, under memory capacity constraints compared to the baseline inference solely relying on storage-offloading without compression.

title: Optimizing Operational Costs in Resource-Limited Environments via Linear Programming
authors: Vikalp Kumar Singh, Atma Prakash Singh, Vineet Kumar Verma, Rana Vikram Pratap Singh Yadav, Sunil Kumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) deployments typically generate substantial data volumes from devices situated across diverse geographical areas. To effectively manage these data, processing near the source, using fog computing, is often more advantageous than transmitting all data to centralized cloud servers. This work addresses the inefficiency of placing publish/subscribe (pub/sub) brokers only in the cloud for Internet of Things (IoT) systems by proposing a smarter placement of both data processing components and brokers across the network—from edge to cloud—based on the location of devices and users. By modeling the problem to minimize system costs like delay and network usage, and introducing two simple heuristics for optimal placement, the study shows that fog-based placement strategies can significantly improve performance, especially when devices and users are clustered, achieving near-optimal results within 2.5× of the best possible solution.

title: Enhancing Apple Leaf Disease Classification via VGG-16 with Convolutional Block Attention and Explainable AI
authors: Mohd Shuaib, Uday Chhabra, Vanshika Garg, Aditya Sharma, Aditya Joshi, Manoj Diwakar
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Apple farming has major importance all over the world however, its harvest is often damaged by leaf diseases such as Apple Scab, Black Rot, and Cedar Apple Rust. Routine inspection of crop diseases techniques are mostly dependent on human observation , which is both time consuming process and likely to be inaccurate. To overcome this issue, we introduce a deep learning model that uses the VGG16 architecture combined with the Convolutional Block Attention Module (CBAM) to Enhance feature identification and attention towards affected area related to disease in apple leaf images. The suggested model has a Classification accuracy of 98.96%, which shows better performance over than traditional methods. To further ensure clarity of model decisions, the Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) technique was used to highlight the infected areas of the leaf that contributed model predictions. The combination of VGG16, CBAM, and Grad-CAM not only increased diagnostic accuracy but also provides transparency, making the framework Robust and suitable for practical use in precision agriculture and plant disease management.

title: A Review of Physical Contact Methods for IGBT Junction Temperature Sensing
authors: Yizhu Wang, Lin Liang, Liuyu Tu, Zhiyuan Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Insulated Gate Bipolar Transistor (IGBT) junction temperature sensing is of vital importance for the health management of modern power grids. However, with the increasing integration of IGBT modules, the physical contact method faces new challenges in junction temperature sensing. To address the gap in the theoretical framework of physical contact methods, a novel and more systematic classification framework based on the sensor's integration position is proposed in the paper, dividing physical contact methods into packaging-level integration and chip-level integration. Furthermore, these integration positions are further evaluated against six key criteria, including accuracy, universality, performance impact, long-term stability, interference factors, and scalability. Finally, to address the inherent intrusiveness and connection challenges of existing integration positions and methods, several novel and potential positions for sensor integration beneath the soldered chip and in the uncovered areas of the press-pack IGBT chip are proposed and evaluated, providing an outlook for future research.

title: Fuzzy–Decision Tree Model for Online Fraud Detection Using Psycholinguistic and Personality Features
authors: K. Pushpa, N Sunil Kumar, Sreenivas Madupu, Anuradha Parasar, A. Bharathi, S. Mohanasundaram
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: 抓取摘要失败: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='ieeexplore.ieee.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /document/11411302/ (Caused by ProtocolError('Connection aborted.', ConnectionResetError(104, 'Connection reset by peer')))

title: A Multiclass Classification Method for PV Array Fault Diagnosis Robust to Label Error
authors: Guodong Qi, Mengyuan Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Machine learning based photovoltaic (PV) array fault diagnosis methods have attracted wide attention, but most of them do not consider the label error in training data, which can lead to overfitting of the fault diagnosis model and seriously affect the accuracy. This paper proposes a PV array fault diagnosis method robust to label error based on distributionally robust multiclass logistic regression. First, the steady state electrical signal data processing method is introduced. Then, fault diagnosis of PV array is modeled as a multiclass classification problem. Multiclass fault diagnosis model is established based on distributionally robust multiclass logistic regression. Finally, the proposed method is compared with the commonly used methods and the fault diagnosis method based on binary distributionally robust logistic regression under typical label error scenarios. The experimental results show that, the proposed method exhibits stronger robustness and higher fault diagnosis accuracy in label error scenarios, outperforming the comparison method by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.5-16.3 {\%}$</tex> at label error rate of 20%.

title: Design and Implementation of a Totem-Pole Power Factor Corrector with Light Load Efficiency Improvement
authors: Yi-Cheng Wu, Tsorng-Juu Liang, Kai-Hui Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The bridgeless totem-pole power factor corrector has been widely applied in high efficiency converters. When the synchronous rectifying switches are kept conducted, the inductor current will be reversed during the interval of low input current conditions, leading to circulating energy losses. A control method for improving efficiency in the totem pole power factor corrector is adopted. By using reverse current prevention(RCP) control, the corresponding synchronous rectifying switch will be turned off to avoid reverse inductor current, improving light load efficiency. Finally, a laboratory prototype with an input voltage of 220 V rms, output voltage of 400 V, and rated power of 4 kW is implemented to verify the theoretical analyses. The experimental result shows that the highest efficiency is 98 % at a load power of 3.5 kW. At a rated power of 4 kW, the efficiency and power factor are 97.7 % and 0.993, respectively. At an output power of 200 W, the system efficiency is improved by 1.85 % with reverse current prevention control.

title: Optimizing PV-Based Vehicle-to-Grid Systems with Intelligent Control: A Comparative Study of Neuro-Fuzzy and ANN-Based Approaches
authors: Mazen Mohamed, Slman Mohammemd, Mohamed MohamedAhmed, Md Shafiullah, Muhammad Mysorewala
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: With the growing need for renewable energy sources, this study investigates advanced control techniques for optimizing photovoltaic (PV) systems and integrating Vehicles to the Grid (V2G). The neuro-fuzzy controller is used to enhance Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT), which guarantees efficient solar energy capture under many circumstances. This controller blends fuzzy logic with the powers of neural networks. Fuzzy logic enables the regulation of energy flow in both directions for electric vehicle (EV) batteries in applications like vehicle-to-grid (V2G). Voltage Source Converters (VSC) are a fundamental part in ensuring grid compatibility when converting direct current (DC) to alternating current (AC). A comparative analysis has been done between neuro-fuzzy controller and artificial neural networks (ANN) for MPPT. Fuzzy Logic Controller used for the management of energy flow between electric Vehicle and grid.

title: Graphite-Enhanced Accurate Forced-Air Cooling for Magnetic Components
authors: Limbu Pratik, Ponnuvelu Bhoopal, River Tin-ho Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: An Accurate Forced-air Cooling (AFC) solution for magnetics components is presented. The proposed cooling solution can cool the magnetic cores with theoretical minimum cooling fan power and extra space consumptions. It also avoids the reliability issue caused by dusty in general forced-air cooling. Magnetic components such as inductors and transformers are integral parts of a power converter system. At high operating temperatures, these components are prone to saturation, which adversely affects losses and overall system reliability. Therefore, effective thermal management of magnetic components is essential. Design and performance of the proposed AFC have been evaluated analytically. Experimental verification is performed on a gapped EQ5032 inductor running at 8 kHz/90 A<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">p-p</inf>. Graphite-enhanced AFC can address limitations of typical designs by reducing peak temperature and thermal stress, improving thermal uniformity, and providing substantial benefits in thermal performance.

title: A Comparative Analysis of Data Mining Algorithms in Predicting the Academic Performance of University Students
authors: April Rose A. Zaragosa, Jenny Rose V. Wenceslao
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: This study evaluates the efficacy of Educational Data Mining (EDM) techniques in predicting the academic performance of Information Technology Education students at Northern Iloilo State University (NISU). Utilizing a quantitative retrospective design, the research analyzes historical data from 591 students to determine the predictive relationship between Senior High School (SHS) profiles, specifically School Category, Strand, a General Average, and collegiate General Weighted Average (GWA). Three supervised machine learning algorithms were comparatively assessed: Multiple Linear Regression (MLR), Support Vector Regression (SVR), and Random Forest Regressor (RFM). The models were trained on a dataset of 571 records and validated against 20 unseen test samples using Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), and the Coefficient of Determination (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${R}^{2}$</tex>). The results indicate that the Multiple Linear Regression (MLR) model achieved superior performance, demonstrating the lowest error rates (RMSE =1.3082; MAPE =1.33 %) and the highest goodness-of-fit <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\left({R}^{2} = {0. 2 9 1 9}\right)$</tex>. Conversely, the Random Forest model exhibited significant overfitting and poor generalization on the test data. The findings suggest that student academic performance in this context follows a linear trajectory, making MLR the most robust tool for early academic intervention and policy formulation at the university level. This study contributes to the field by demonstrating that traditional linear models remain more resilient than advanced ensemble methods for localized educational datasets, providing a stable foundation for early academic intervention.

title: A Performance Study of the Combined Implications of Synchronous Condensers and Battery Energy Storage Systems Supporting Grid Stability and Blackout Resilience
authors: Ashok Ghosh, Md. Rabiul Islam, Raad Raad, Shuvra Prokash Biswas, Dipti Fouzder
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Complete shutdowns of largely isolated power grids, which require a black start, are infrequent but can have significant impacts by disrupting entire regions. As modern power systems become more complex and incorporate diverse energy sources with different characteristics, the chances of such shutdowns are rising. This complexity, along with lower system inertia, increases the vulnerability of power grids to significant disturbances and total blackouts. Restoring these systems to regular operation in such events is technically challenging, especially for standalone grids lacking external connections. Key difficulties include restarting generation, energizing transmission lines, and managing critical dependencies like substation batteries. Additionally, severe weather events can worsen these risks by causing widespread damage, making restoration even more difficult. This paper introduces a hybrid solution to combine synchronous condensers and battery energy storage systems to enhance grid stability and improve system restoration and black start capability during high renewable energy integration periods. The model combines synchronous condenser technology for fault current contribution and real inertia with a battery energy storage system for active power and frequency support. The model is tested within a simplified network model to evaluate its performance, with results regarding bus voltage, active power, and reactive power for each bus presented in the model. The paper performs the simulation study in the DigiSILENT software environment.

title: Anomaly Detection for Secure Power System Digital Twins Updates
authors: Zhiwei Shen, Felipe Arraño-Vargas, Georgios Konstantinou
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Digital twins (DTs) are virtual representations of physical systems that are continuously updated using real-time data. In power systems, DTs can significantly enhance operational efficiency and decision-making across various applications. However, the accuracy of DT updates may be compromised due to measurement errors or communication disruptions in the data acquired from the physical twin (PT). This paper proposes a practical anomaly detector to secure DT updates against such compromised PT data. A modified Transformer model is employed to evaluate raw PT measurements prior to their integration into the DT. The development and offline testing of the detector are conducted using data generated by a virtual PT of a power system DT to demonstrate its effectiveness and utilisation. Results show that the detector accurately identifies PT changes, while successfully filtering out most anomalous data. Through ensuring secure and reliable updates, the proposed approach enhances the accuracy of DTs, supporting their broader adoption in power system applications.

title: Design and Implementation of an Advanced Continuous Monitoring System for Real-Time Performance Analytics
authors: Anjali J Kumar, Swadi Korattiparambil, Prof. B. Thangaraju
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: With the increasing complexity of modern computing environments, continuous monitoring of system and container metrics is essential for maintaining optimal performance and operational stability of the system. This paper presents a continuous monitoring tool designed to track and visualize essential system and container metrics in real-time. Built using open-source technologies, the tool integrates modules for data collection, storage, and visualization, thereby addressing the needs of modern Development and Operations (DevOps) environments. The system effectively tracked the Central Processing Unit (CPU), memory, network, and Input/Output (I/O) metrics along with the Docker container's performances by using the System Activity Report (SAR) for Linux metrics, InfluxDB for time-series data storage, and Grafana for visualization. The tool's comprehensive dashboard offers fine-grained insights into system health and re-source utilization, facilitating proactive performance optimization and streamlining troubleshooting. Future enhancements include predictive analytics and expanded DevOps Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to further support operational efficiency and application reliability.

title: Optimization of Multi-DC Systems Based on Combined Linear Power Flow Analysis
authors: Jiwoong Choi, Hojun Lee, Donghun Lee, Seyeong Kang, Umar Fitra Ramadhan, Minhan Yoon
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The increasing share of renewables in the Jeju power system has intensified supply-demand imbalances and voltage stability issues, emphasizing the need for effective HVDC operation strategies. While AC power flow offers high accuracy, its computational complexity limits real-time large-scale applications. DC power flow and PTDF-based optimization improve efficiency but neglect voltage and reactive power, often leading to unrealistic results. This paper proposes a Combined Linear Power Flow (CLPF) method that integrates the strengths of the DLPF and Reduced Jacobian models. Case studies on the Jeju system demonstrate that CLPF achieves both improved voltage and angle estimation and enhanced computational efficiency.

title: A Scoping Review of the Use of DSL in Software Applications: The Case of Chatbot Development
authors: Charaf Ouaddi, Adnane Souha, Lamya Benaddi, Abdeslam Jakimi, Rachid Saadane, Abdellah Chehri
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Networking, Intelligent Systems &amp; Security (NISS)
abstract: Domain-Specific Languages (DSLs) have garnered considerable attention in software development for their capacity to expedite the creation of diverse applications, including mobile applications, chatbots, websites, and recommendation systems. By offering high-level abstractions tailored to specific domains, DSLs simplify development tasks and make software creation more accessible to users, thereby reducing the need for extensive programming expertise. This paper aims to examine the use of DSLs in software development through a comprehensive literature review, analyzing existing DSLs, the types of applications they support, and the technologies employed in their construction. Additionally, this study proposes a DSL specifically designed to accelerate chatbot development. This DSL provides a graphical interface for modeling conversations and includes code generation templates for chatbot source files.

title: CrisisGuideGPT: an Agentic-Ai for Crisis Response Planning
authors: Saeed H. Al-Shamrani, Ragib Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: Natural hazards are continuously posing threats to people's lives. When a crisis happens, people seek reliable and actionable information to ensure their safety. With many sources online, some information can be static and generalized, while others have incomplete or contradictory information. This paper introduces CrisisGuideGPT, an agentic Artificial Intelligence (AI) system designed to generate personalized, location-specific crisis response plans. By integrating real-time data from the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) and the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) with established emergency guidelines via Google's Gemini large language model (LLM), CrisisGuideGPT addresses the limitations of static emergency plans. The system employs a multi-stage prompting strategy, including knowledge base grounding, self-correction, and interactive refinement, to produce structured, context-aware guidance for individuals facing crises like hurricanes, floods, wildfires, or earthquakes. We present the system's architecture, methodology, and prompting techniques, and discuss its strengths (e.g., personalization and real-time integration), limitations (e.g., LLM unpredictability and API dependencies), and implications for transparency and accountability in high-stakes AI applications.

title: An Ensemble Voting Model for Phishing Website Detection Using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Madhukar Dubey
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: A comprehensive analysis of ensemble approaches based on Artificial Intelligence (AI) for phishing attack recognition is presented in this study. The study focuses on five machine learning models (ML): Random Forest (RF), Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), XGBoost (XGB), Gradient Boosting (GB), and an ensemble Voting Classifier combining RF, XGB, and GB. The dataset used consists of 10,000 instances, separated into two categories—legitimate and phishing websites—each containing 50 features. Preprocessing was done using Min Max scaling to normalize the feature values. The individual models were trained and evaluated, with RF, XGB, and GB achieving perfect classification metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score of 100%), while the MLP reached slightly lower metrics (accuracy: 99.7%, F1-score: 99.6%). The proposed Voting Classifier, aggregating the predictions of RF, XGB, and GB, achieved consistent 100% performance across all metrics, demonstrating the advantage of combining strong individual classifiers in an ensemble model. In comparison, a previously proposed CNN-BiLSTM model achieved an F1-score of 98.8%. The results confirm that ensemble models, particularly the Voting Classifier, offer superior performance for phishing detection tasks compared to individual models and neural networks. Future work will explore real-time phishing detection, model interpretability, and advanced feature engineering to further enhance system robustness and adaptability to evolving cyber threats.

title: Pediatric Cancer LLM for Symptoms and Side-Effects Prediction with Explanation
authors: Ramyashree P M, Sonal Shirke, Rajalakshmi R, Sonia Maria D'Souza
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)

title: Kidney Stone Identification Using a Two-Stage ResNet and SVM-Based Ultrasound Image Analysis System
authors: Venkata Kiran Kumar Ravi, Satwik Chatterjee, Prakash Nagapillai, S. Devi, K. N. Sharma, Kishan Singh
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The identification of kidney stones with ultrasound imaging presents difficulties due to noise, low resolution, and structural complications. Conventional deep learning algorithms frequently struggle to achieve consistent accuracy in diverse image contexts. The proposed study introduces a two-stage ResNet-based deep feature extraction system integrated with a Support Vector Machine (SVM) classifier to overcome these limitations. The initial ResNet stage acquires low- and mid-level spatial data, whereas the subsequent stage enhances high-level semantic representations. These attributes are subsequently utilised by a Support Vector Machine to establish an accurate categorisation border. The model was assessed using a publicly available ultrasonography kidney dataset obtained from Kaggle. The proposed system attained an accuracy of 97.6%, precision of 96.9%, recall of 98.2%, F1-score of 97.5%, and AUC-ROC of 98.7%, outperforming baseline models like VGG16, InceptionV3, and standalone ResNet. This design enhances diagnostic reliability and reduces false positives and negatives, offering a clinically feasible and scalable method for kidney stone identification in medical imaging.

title: Intent-Driven Multi-Agent Coordination for Edge Computing under Resource and Communication Constraints
authors: Shilpi Mittal, Kavita Gupta
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Keeping edge nodes coordinated efficiently with limited communication and computation resources is becoming increasingly challenging as edge computing becomes central to time-critical and distributed systems. Communication overhead and vulnerability to single points of failure make traditional centralized coordination or raw data exchange mechanisms unsuitable. In order to overcome these challenges, we propose a new multi-agent coordination architecture that is driven by intention, where edge agents share high-level goals instead of low-level data and autonomously negotiate resource allocation based on local state and task priorities. The proposed system reduces communication overhead and enhances resilience by allowing agents to communicate high-level intents over raw data. Node availability, bandwidth fluctuations, and energy limitations are taken into account by our approach's dynamic adaptation. Simulations show that traditional coordination mechanisms are not as effective in improving task completion rate, communication efficiency, and system robustness.

title: A Novel Modified Interleaved High-Gain DC–DC Converter Using Asymmetrical Voltage Multiplier and Coupled Inductor With Leakage Energy Recycling
authors: Sk Joni, Avik Bhattacharya, Sharmili Das
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: A high-step-up DC–DC converter architecture featuring coupled inductors is presented in this study to achieve improved efficiency and voltage gain, a clamping circuit, and an asymmetric voltage multiplier structure. This configuration, termed the modified interleaved asymmetrical voltage multiplier cell (MIAVMC)-based converter, enhances voltage gain while maintaining efficiency. The architecture lessens the strain on switches and diodes while achieving good voltage gain across all duty ranges. Interleaving ensures balanced current sharing and low input ripple, enhancing system stability, while the clamping circuit suppresses voltage spikes and protects semiconductor devices. The proposed structure avoids extreme duty cycles and excessive component stress, unlike conventional converters. Diode reverse-recovery and voltage stress are significantly mitigated, and switching losses are reduced as switches and diodes achieve soft switching conditions. In addition, the passive lossless clamp recycles leakage inductance energy and suppresses overshoot, further improving efficiency. PLECS/MATLAB simulations validate the design, showing that a 30 V input can be boosted to 400 V at 400 W, confirming its suitability for renewable energy and other high-voltage applications.

title: Machine Learning–Driven Assistive Technologies for Enhancing Academic Assessment of Students with Learning Disabilities in Kerala’s Secondary Schools
authors: Bibi C B, V. Mrunalini
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: However, persistent issues of validity, fairness, and accessibility plague summative and formative assessments for students with Specific Learning Disabilities (SLD) in India, thereby affecting equitable participation and the accurate assessment of learning outcomes. The goal of this research work is to investigate whether wearable assistive technology (AT) based on machine learning (ML) can enhance the content validity, fairness, usability, and overall effectiveness of assessments in a manner that does not compromise academic standards. A multi-study, mixed-methods research was carried out in government and aided secondary schools of Kerala: Study 1, Baseline Audit and Evidence of Validity for Current Assessment Practices. Experiment 1 piloted an ML-AT toolkit that included speech-to-text conversion, adaptive timing, optical character recognition combined with text simplification, webcam-based eye-tracking features, and real-time analytics in a quasi-experiment. User experiences were investigated through qualitative interviews with students, teachers, and parents in Study 3, and the cost-effectiveness and implementation feasibility were evaluated in Study 4. The follow-up outcome measures were the same as in Study II, except for the item analyses and usability measures (SUS/UEQ) and academic growth. The expected results are that ML-AT will enhance measurement precision, minimize accommodation-related bias, and improve subgroup parity on fairness metrics. Teachers should report a reduction in workload to teach students, and that the instruction has become more concentrated. The findings suggest that ML-AT holds promise for disrupting educational assessment to increase accessibility and psychometric rigor, with caveats that governance processes and teacher capacity development are incorporated into implementation plans.

title: Empirical Analysis of AI Models in STEM Education Using Machine Learning Techniques and Survey Insights
authors: Aditya Pai H, Ganesh Pathak, Manoj Diwakar, Chandrappa S, Sharon Christa, Amol Dande
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The research paper mentions the mixed-method used in the empirical evaluation of the AI tools, such as an AI-enabled tutor approach, the Platform used for adaptive learning, Virtual Labs, and an NLP assessment system. All these tools are integrated in the STEM classrooms. The blend of this study was seen during the survey and during Machine learning classification. Using 500 responses, we analyzed the data using three models, namely, Decision Tree, Logistic Regression and Random Forest. Out of the three, Random Forest achieved, highest accuracy of 89.2%, where it identified the feedback quality, relevance of the content and learner satisfaction. To achieve higher accuracy, a hybrid stack approach with a better accuracy percentage of 91.3%. In the paper, a structured framework is proposed which shows how AI-integrated classrooms emphasize in appropriate tool selection at the given time, and also explains ethical integration.

title: Semantic-Aware Helpdesk Resolution Using Federated Transformers and Real-Time Vector Matching and LLaMA
authors: Varsha Dange, Harshada Sawai, Manoj Mule, Ajinkya Matre, Sanika Shedbale, Piyush Pise, Kamal Joshi
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: This research introduces an advanced intelligent IT support chatbot framework that revolutionizes traditional helpdesk operations through the integration of cutting-edge natural language processing technologies and scalable vector search methodologies. The system addresses critical challenges in enterprise IT support environments, including prolonged response times, inconsistent solution quality, and escalating operational expenditures associated with manual ticket resolution processes. The proposed architecture employs a fine-tuned Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers (BERT) model for sophisticated multi-intent classification and semantic embedding generation of user queries and historical ticket databases. A high-performance Pinecone vector database facilitates rapid similarity-based retrieval through optimized cosine distance calculations, enabling contextually relevant historical issue identification within milliseconds. The system incorporates a domain-specialized Large Language Model for Advanced Reasoning (LLaMA) implementation that generates human-aligned, contextually appropriate responses using retrieval-augmented generation techniques. Key innovations include a privacy-preserving federated learning architecture enabling collaborative model enhancement across organizational boundaries without compromising sensitive enterprise data, integrated JIRA workflow automation for seamless ticket lifecycle management, and an intelligent uncertainty estimation mechanism providing reliable fallback protocols through human-in-the-loop interventions. Comprehensive evaluation demonstrates exceptional performance metrics: 93.2% intent classification accuracy, 180-millisecond average response latency, and 88.6% user satisfaction rates while automating 50% of routine queries with enterprise-grade security standards, representing a significant advancement in intelligent service desk automation technologies for large-scale organizational deployments.

title: Cognitive and Personality Traits in Identifying Learner Profiles for Programming Readiness and Adaptive Instruction
authors: Srinivasa Rao Manchem, Sachikanta Dash, Madina Jayanthi Rao
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The increased need to know computational reasoning and programming skills also emphasizes the need to learn more about cognitive and personality characteristics that determine the performance of learners. The research involved a total of more than 1,000 students of computer programming by means of a combination of a psychometric test, such as Multiple Intelligences, Benziger Thinking Styles, Kolb Learning Styles, Big Five Personality Traits, and Metacognition. In order to combine these constructs, two composite indices were made as measures of the learner capacity- Broad Learning Ability and Programming Readiness. The mean differences differing clustering algorithm was used to classify students into four different groups namely: excellent, good, average, and poor. The findings found out that 34.7% of the learners were classified in the excellent category, 18.7% in the good category, 38.9% in the average category and 22.2% in the poor category. Such profiles provide useful information on diversity of learners and would be a promising step towards adaptive teaching and individualized learning in programming education. The reliability of the classification was supported by the validation of clusters based on the Fowlkes-Mallows index. The framework helps in advancing the area of educational data mining by linking psychometrics with learning analytics. It offers a methodical way of identifying needy learners and also in the process, offers a way to guide more productive and personalized pedagogy to make the program ready and improve the overall academic outcome.

title: Multimodal Deep Learning Framework for Early Stress and Mental Health Detection in Students Using Facial, Speech, and Behavioral Data
authors: Dharani R, Danesh K
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Student mental health issues such as stress, anxiety, and emotional fatigue have become critical concerns in the education sector, particularly after the rapid transition to digital learning environments. Early detection plays a vital role in preventing long-term psychological impacts and improving student well-being. This paper proposes a multimodal deep learning framework that integrates facial emotion analysis, speech-based stress assessment, and behavioural activity features to predict early stress conditions in students. The system uses a hybrid architecture combining Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) for facial image processing, Bidirectional LSTM models for speech signal analysis, and a DNN classifier for behavioural features such as sleep duration, academic workload, and activity levels. A simulated dataset of 1,500 multimodal student samples was used to evaluate the model. Experimental results show that the proposed multimodal fusion model achieves 93.4% accuracy, outperforming unimodal approaches by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 2} - \text{1 8 \%}$</tex>. The findings highlight the effectiveness of combining visual, audio, and behavioral cues for early stress detection and provide a practical solution that can be integrated into educational monitoring systems, mobile health apps, and university counselling platforms.

title: CNN-Based Protection Scheme for Fault Detection and Classification in Low-Voltage PV based DC Microgrids
authors: Rama Prasanna Dalai, Sarat Chandra Swain, Ranjeeta Patel
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: This paper proposes a convolutional neural network (CNN)-based protection framework for fault detection and classification in low-voltage photovoltaic (PV) integrated DC microgrids. In the proposed approach, fault current signals are preprocessed using the Short-Time Fourier Transform (STFT) to obtain time-frequency spectrograms, which are then used as input features for CNN training. The CNN automatically learns distinctive fault characteristics and accurately classifies various fault types such as pole-to-pole, pole-to-ground, and arc faults. Additionally, an Improved Self-Adaptive Solid-State Circuit Breaker (I-SSCB) is incorporated to effectively limit fault current magnitudes and enhance system reliability. Simulation results obtained in the MATLAB/Simulink environment demonstrate that the proposed protection scheme achieves high classification accuracy and rapid response, thereby improving the operational resilience of low-voltage DC microgrids.

title: Design and Implementation of PLC Based Voltage Regulation in DC Generator
authors: Mandakinee Bandyopadhyay, Subrata Chattopadhyay
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: A DC generator’s output voltage control may experience low frequency oscillations with offset or high frequency oscillations without offset, and as the load increases, the voltage output will decrease. These errors can be eliminated using a PID controller. This paper describes the output load characteristics of a DC generator with and without a PLC-based PID controller, as well as the design of the voltage control system for a DC generator.

title: An Efficient and Scalable Hardware Architecture for Number Theoretic Transform on FPGA with Design Automation
authors: Yilan Zhu, Geng Yang, Xingyu Tian, Dilshan Kumarathunga, Liang Kong, Xianglong Deng, Shengyu Fan, Guang Fan, Guiming Shi, Lei Chen, Bo Zhang, Yisong Chang, Shoumeng Yan, Zhenman Fang, Mingzhe Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) has become a promising approach to protecting data privacy in emerging application scenarios. Unfortunately, FHE suffers from significant processing speed degradation compared to plaintext computation, with one of the primary bottlenecks being the time-consuming Number Theoretic Transform (NTT). Therefore, accelerating NTT to accommodate various FHE parameters is crucial to advancing FHE towards practical use. With highly reconfigurable and performant logical fabrics, Field Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) have exhibited great potential in NTT acceleration. By decomposing large-point NTT with strong data dependency into independent and simple small-point NTTs, the emerging Ten-step NTT (TNTT) algorithms intuitively enable higher parallelism and thereby have the potential to explore better performance compared to traditional algorithms. However, our quantitative analysis reveals that TNTT exhibits significant performance degradation as parallelism increases due to additional varying-size transpositions and Hadamard products. This paper proposes AutoNest, an efficient and scalable hardware architecture, along with an accelerator auto-generation framework for TNTT. The proposed hardware architecture maximizes performance by 1) adopting a 2D block decomposition dataflow to address critical path delays in transpose logic, thereby improving clock frequency. 2) integrating algorithm-level costfree twiddle factor fusion to reduce the number of modular multiplications in Hadamard products, thereby allowing higher parallelism on chip. Moreover, we also deliver an accelerator generation framework conducting automated design space exploration to elaborate a performant TNTT architecture under the target FPGAs' resource budget for user-defined FHE parameters. Experimental results on the AMD-Xilinx U280 FPGA demonstrate that NTT accelerators generated by AutoNest achieve an average speedup of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.31 \times$</tex> compared to prior designs.

title: Data Fusion of Ble and 77 GHz Radar for Forklift-Worker Collision Avoidance
authors: Marek Neruda, Liam Friel, Theo Mandelaire, Lukas Vojtech, Jakub Unucka
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: Forklift-worker collisions often result in serious injuries. Although researchers have addressed this issue, a fully satisfactory solution remains elusive. This paper presents an analysis of technologies suitable for preventing forklift-human collisions. The analysis considers factors such as accuracy, range, computational cost, localization, and identification capabilities. Based on these criteria, a data fusion approach combining Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) and 77 GHz radar technologies is investigated. This combination enhances identification using BLE technology and radar detection accuracy. By developing a data fusion application, we demonstrate that the proposed approach is an effective solution for mitigating forklift-worker collisions.

title: Techno-Economic Assessment and Optimisation of Green Hydrogen and Green Ammonia in the Australian National Energy Market
authors: Hassan Khalili, Alireza Salmachi, Virginie Masson, Derek Abbott
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: To harness the increasing volume of curtailed variable renewable energy (VRE) and advance Australia's green energy export potential, this study presents a techno-economic assessment and optimisation of industrial-scale green hydrogen and ammonia production within the National Electricity Market (NEM). In this study, a novel PLEXOS-based modelling framework is developed, integrating and combining both electricity and universal classes to reflect real-world project configurations under Australia's Safeguard Mechanism (SGM) policy. The model co-optimises renewable generation, hydrogen infrastructure, and ammonia synthesis over a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex>-year horizon, capturing market dynamics, emissions profiles, and economic trade-offs-while highlighting ammonia's role as a high-density hydrogen carrier and export-ready commodity. Results show that green hydrogen can achieve a competitive levelised cost, driven by declining renewable electricity prices, falling electrolyser costs-particularly due to recent large-scale investment from China-and the growing economic value of emissions reduction. As a benchmark, blue hydrogen and ammonia, derived from natural gas with carbon capture and storage (CCS) are analysed as well.

title: Electrification Pathways for Indian Road Transport:Trends, Emissions and Technological Challenges
authors: V Mohammed Shijas, Panithan Chakkuchan, Santanu Kumar Dash, Kamon Thinnsurat, Mohd Khair Hassan, Saichol Chudjuarjeen, Suratsavadee Korkua
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: India’s urbanization has seen an increase in car ownership and subsequently, emissions, despite policies like BS-VI. This research analyzes the patterns of internal combustion engine (ICE) car growth, emissions patterns, and electric vehicle (EV) growth through the example of Bengaluru. The comparison of ICEVs versus EVs will take into account pollution, cost, and infrastructure. Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) analysis shows that EVs become more cost effective for certain users. Government funding of FAME-II and state regulations initiated the uptake of EVs. Challenges remain with charging infrastructure, battery management, and capacity of the grid. This report demonstrates various methods to promote targeted EV uptake and stresses the significance of aligning efforts in technologies, policies, and infrastructure in command to follow sustainable urban transportation.

title: A Hybrid Identity Authentication System Using Deep Learning for Biometric and Facial Recognition
authors: Ratnesh Kumar Choudhary, Prasanna Lohe, Om Shrikhande, Ishita Nimje, Devashish Pillay, Vaishnavi Puri, Uday Gawande
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid identity authentication system by integrating facial recognition and fingerprint verification. DeepFace with ResNet50 and MobileFaceNet are used in the facial recognition component for deep feature extraction, while fingerprint verification uses HOG with Cosine Similarity. This system achieves an accuracy of 99.99%, along with precision, recall, and F1-scores, making it better than the unimodal methods. The primary contribution is the multimodal fusion augmented by integrated AES-256 encryption and SHA-256 hashing or blockchain logging, all rendering the system resistant to spoofing and tampering. The applications range from banking, healthcare, e-governance, to digital identity management.

title: Recommendation System-Based E-Commerce Website Using Machine Learning Algorithms
authors: Bharat Data, Tushar, Srijan Bhardwaj, Vaibhav Agarwal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: E-commerce offers convenience to consumers by transforming the business landscape using recommendation systems driven by Machine Learning (ML) algorithms. It enhances user experience and boosts sales by understanding consumer behavior. In addition, ML improves various aspects of e-commerce operations, like fraud detection, inventory management, and supply chain optimization. ML-driven inventory systems forecast demand accurately, reduce stock-outs, and minimize excess inventory, leading to cost savings and operational efficiency. By leveraging ML across e-commerce functions, businesses can adapt quickly by mitigating risks and seizing opportunities in the ever-evolving digital marketplace. This paper proposes the implementation of ML techniques to enhance the competitiveness of e-commerce websites. The primary focus is on leveraging techniques such as recommendation systems, predictive analysis, and customer churn management to provide personalized recommendations and forecast product revenue, consequently boosting the profitability of e-commerce platforms. Integrating these ML techniques will offer a strategic advantage by tailoring the user experience, optimizing revenue generation, and fostering customer retention.

title: AIoT for Sentiment-Driven Automation: Leveraging RNNs for Intelligent IoT Device Control
authors: Uzzal Mondal
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) has revolutionized communication. However, there are challenges in human intentions to control smart devices through IoT, such as controlling the fan by human feeling, which is very significant in the modern era. This study connects AI with IoT and is called AIoT for smart communication by approaching sentiment analysis techniques for decision-making adaptability without the help of a Large Language Model (LLM). In this study, we used Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) for model building and the MQTT protocol for communication in IoT. AIoT operates in three phases: converting human voice to text, analyzing human emotions, and applying it to IoT devices. This proposed model achieved 99.31% accuracy based on the basic contribution data for fan control, according to human perception. We applied this technique to the automatic control of the fan speed. We applied this technique to the automatic control of fan speed, which defined a significant path for future research.

title: WiSol-Charge: A Smart Solar-Powered Wireless Vehicle Charging Station with Automated Control and IoT Readiness
authors: Jayanthi M, Kirthiga J S, Varshav, Likitha R
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: There is Growing Demand for a Convenient, Safe and Eco-friendly Charging System of Electric Vehicles. World is Moving Towards Green Economy using Electric Vehicles. The vast majority of wireless charging models' sustainability is primarily limited mainly by the excessive dependence of these models on the grid power. This work presents a WiSol-charge, a small solar-powered wireless charging station that is created for educational and small electric vehicle applications to fill that major gap and overcome related problems. The system mentioned here is controlled using Arduino-based control unit and consists of photovoltaic panel, lithium-ion storage and inductive power transfer configuration. A <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$16 \times 2$</tex> LCD is provided to display the actual condition time of the system. The charging coil will be automatically turned on by the infrared sensor as it senses the arrival of a vehicle. Because of natural sunlight utilization in this prototype, safe battery regulation and steady solar generation are ensured. Moreover, effective wireless power transfer over short distances has been achieved. The results show that this system can function off the grid and is a very easy and hygienic alternative to a plug-in charger. This study demonstrates the practical mix of renewable energy and wireless charging. Furthermore, it highlights potential areas for development, which include MPPT, IOT monitoring, and high power designs.

title: Smart IoT Emergency Response System with Real-Time Health Monitoring
authors: Naolin Gregory Vaz, Hosmani Bhagyashree Narayan, Bhavani, Tejaswini C, Akshatha G Dhongadi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: This paper presents a Smart IoT Emergency Response System designed to enhance personal safety and provide real-time health monitoring during critical situations. The system employs a NodeMCU microcontroller integrated with a pulse sensor, temperature sensor, and GPS module to continuously monitor vital parameters and determine location. Unlike GSM-based solutions, it leverages Wi-Fi communication and the Twilio cloud platform to transmit emergency alerts via SMS, enabling faster and more reliable messaging. A pushbutton acts as a manual emergency trigger, while a lightweight cybersecurity mechanism monitors abnormal activation patterns to prevent misuse. The proposed system demonstrates a cost-effective, scalable, and intelligent approach to emergency handling—especially valuable in healthcare, elderly care, and personal safety domains.

authors: Bhavya, Mohd. Rehan Ghazi, Ninad Gangodkar, Kshitij Joshi, Amritanshu Shekhar, Akhand Pratap Singh
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)

title: A Novel Recurrent Equilibrium Network Based Control Method for Vanadium Redox Flow Battery in DC Microgrids
authors: Chengyan Zheng, Wendong Feng, Yulin Liu, Joshua Watts, Herbert Iu, Tyrone Fernando, Xinan Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel machine learningbased control approach for vanadium redox flow batteries (VRBs) in DC microgrids, which is designed to achieve superior steadystate and dynamic performance while ensuring the closed-loop stability. To alleviate the heavy computational cost of both offline neural network (NN) training and online control implementation, the method utilizes the direct parameterization technique and the recurrent equilibrium network (REN). This approach significantly improves dynamic voltage regulation of the VRB at low computational complexity. The closed-loop stability of the VRB system is thoroughly proven. Besides, validation using hardware-in-the-loop experiments demonstrates the improved performance of the proposed method relative to other control techniques.

title: Enhancing Early Diagnosis of Parkinson’s Disease Using a Deep Learning Framework Based on Bidirectional LSTM Neural Architecture
authors: Ranu Pandey, Priyanka Bande, Aakash Sonkar, Devbrat Sahu, Gousia Hazra Anjum Khan, Rovin Tiwari
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Parkinson's Disease (PD) is a neurodegenerative disorder that is characterized by progressive manifestations where its manifestation during the early stages is not diagnosed, yet there are non-obvious and overlapping symptoms. Early diagnosis is very important in order to delay the progression of the disease as well as enhancing good life. In this paper, the author uses a Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (BiLSTM) neural network to predict the onset of Parkinson Disease by applying voice-based biomedical characteristics and identifies patterns and features. BiLSTM has been constructed so that it can help to successfully represent both temporal trends and bi-directional relations in the sequence of voice data which have been traditionally under-represented by the regressions and other traditional machine learning models. Based on a publicly used Parkinson dataset, The proposed model scored an impressive accuracy, precision, recall, and AUC rating and compared favorably to traditional classifiers including Support Vector Machines and Random Forests. These findings indicate that BiLSTM can be a valid and accurate tool that works non-invasionally and is precise in predicting early-stage Parkinson disease by means of vocal biomarkers.

title: A Comparative Study of Ensemble Models for Smart Grid Security Under Class Imbalance Condition
authors: Ghalia Nassreddine, Obada Al-Khatib, Mohamad Nassereddine, Ali Rammal, Haile Rajamani
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The smart grid is a crucial component of smart cities and contemporary electricity distribution systems. However, various forms of cyberattack can compromise the stability of the network. This study employs machine learning models to develop advanced intrusion detection systems for smart grid environments to identify such attacks. The primary objective is to improve classification performance in the context of class imbalance by employing an advanced ensemble learning approach alongside a hybrid over-sampling and undersampling technique. This research examines and compares three prominent ensemble methods: stacking, boosting, and bagging. A hybrid resampling method that combines SMOTE and Tomek is used to address the drawback of the imbalanced class. The paper examines the impact of employing embedded feature selection methods on improving detection accuracy. The application of the proposed approach on the Morris dataset shows that ensemble models are effective in detecting intrusions. Furthermore, the stacking model surpassed other ensemble models, with an accuracy of 98.79%. The findings further suggest that the integrated feature selection strategy can improve the efficacy of e nsemble l earning models. The research proposes a robust and effective categorization framework tailored for practical smart grid applications.

title: Site-Specific Load Modeling for Public Car Park EV Charging Infrastructure
authors: Sidharth, Muhammad Khalid, Georgios Konstantinou
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) have experienced accelerated adoption globally in recent years, and although Australia is lagging behind much of the world, sales have been doubling each year. This rapid growth is driving a corresponding expansion of EV charging infrastructure. High-power EV charging facilities, especially in concentrated settings such as airports, universities, hospitals, and shopping centres, introduce significant and unpredictable loads on the power distribution grid. If not properly managed, these fast-varying charging demands can lead to local network stress, voltage fluctuations, and capacity issues. This paper aims to develop a flexible and data-driven load modelling framework for EV charging demand in large public facilities. The modelling approach combines real-world usage data with stochastic simulation techniques to capture the variability in EV arrival times, battery states, charging behaviours, and infrastructure constraints. The comprehensive methodology for EV load forecasting in high-density urban settings can be updated with newer data and assumptions in order to support a reliable, efficient, and sustainable transition to EVs and electric mobility.
