title: Vision Transformer-Based Framework for Enhanced Lung Cancer Detection in CT Images
authors: Sharon Jessika, Ramya Panneerselvam, Tamilselvi M, Gunasundari C, Natarajan B, Saranya S
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Globally, lung cancer accounts for one of the highest numbers of cancer deaths and this can be attributed to the difficulty in early diagnosis. Therefore, this research work aims to develop and test more effective early-stage detection methods for lung cancer through the evaluation of a Vision Transformer (ViT) model that is responsible for classifying CT scan images of lung tissues. The dataset used contains four primary categories namely; ‘adenocarcinoma’, ‘large cell carcinoma’, ‘normal’, and ‘squamous cell carcinoma’. In order to increase the strength of the model, other methods of data augmentation were also used which included random flipping, rotation, zooming in, contrast change, and brightness change. The architecture of the ViT model consists of a pre-trained backbone, after which there are layers for flattening, batch norm, dense layers with GELU activations and ending with a softmax for multiclass outputs classification. A model of this kind was trained and validated on a widely used dataset resulting in a validation accuracy of 97.72% and a validation loss of 0.7091. Results such as these indicate the promising capability of ViT models in classifying the different types of lung cancer seen in CT images, enabling efficient detection of such cancers at earlier stages. This research will further increase the body of knowledge on the use of deep learning in medical imaging, which presents great potential for enhancing the diagnosis of lung cancers.

title: Intelligent Fault Detection, Classification, and Localization Using Machine Learning Algorithms for Self-Healing in Smart Grid Protection
authors: N Praneeth, P. Swati Patro, Stp Srinivas, Ojashwini Dubey, Tiasha Roy, Palak Dwivedi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Modern electric power systems are evolving to-ward intelligent, self-adaptive networks that require sophisticated monitoring and control capabilities to process real-time data and make autonomous decisions for maintaining grid stability. This paper presents a comprehensive investigation of machine learning (ML)-based parametric monitoring techniques for fault detection, classification, and localization in smart grids. Three distinct ML approaches, gated recurrent unit (GRU), informer transformer, and t-distributed stochastic neighbor embedding (t-SNE) are systematically compared using the IEEE 9-bus system as the experimental framework. The test system is designed and modeled in DIgSILENT PowerFactory for electromagnetic transient simulation, generating comprehensive datasets from three-phase voltage and current measurements under normal and various fault conditions. A robust training dataset of 288,180 samples encompasses multiple short-circuit scenarios with different fault types and transmission line locations to ensure model generalizability. The proposed ML-based monitoring framework demonstrates enhanced sensitivity, selectivity, and response speed, supporting smart grid resilience and self-healing protection systems for reliable power network operation.

title: Clustering Analysis of Financial Literacy Among Students
authors: Sukma Harining Cakraningrat, Benny, Mahaning Indrawaty Wijaya
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Financial literacy is a critical skill that young adults need to operate in the world of more complex and digitally mediated financial situations. This paper uses unsupervised machine learning to classify financial literacy profiles of 401 undergraduate students using the OECD/INFE framework, consisting of financial knowledge, attitudes, behavior, digital literacy, and risky financial practices. K-Means clustering was carried out and assessed through Elbow Method, Silhouette Score and Davies-Bouldin Index after z-score standardization to guarantee compatibility with Euclidean distance. The k = 2 solution generated the best validation values, with the creation of two distinct student type: a High-Literacy Low-Risk group, with high financial aptitude and defensive utilization of online financial products, and a Low-Literacy High-Risk group with weaker literacy and a higher score on risky behavior (use of online loans and paylater services). The results have demonstrated that financial literacy among students is heterogeneous and strongly related to risk-taking behavior, which calls the need to implement specific financial education and behavioral reinforcement strategies.

title: Intent Detection in Search Queries with SEBERT Embeddings
authors: Swathi J B, J Umamageswaran, Divyashree S, Prabu M
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: In this paper, I will introduce a method of intent detection in search queries with the help of SBERT and cosine similarity… Search engines are important in a digital world today. Users desire prompt, precise and pertinent responses. The problem with traditional software is that it may not comprehend semantic meaning due to the use of the traditional systems that relied on a keyword system. The contemporary approaches to NLP demonstrate the potential to fill this gap with intent detection. Although this has been improved, the existing systems are still lacking a solution to the is- sues of short, vague queries, field-specific variations, and high computational cost of deep learning models. Bag-of-words and Standard TF-IDF approaches lack semantic relationships. We propose an SBERT-based intent detection algorithm, which utilizes even simple BERT to conduct large-scale similarity searches, which are not efficient. This starts with the preprocessing stage that involves tokenization, deletion of stop words and lemmatization. We then encode the queries, which are then dense vectors obtained through a siamese network of SBERT. The first step would be to enter the query in the SBERT embedding generator. Step 2 will be to store embeddings in fixed-length vector space. In Step 3, intent classification: to apply a supervised classifier such as Logistic Regression, SVM, or shallow neural networks to the embeddings or (b) apply unsupervised grouping algorithms such as K-Means or Agglomerative to form automatic groups of similar intent. Step 4 aims at calculating the semantic similarity between queries, and the cosine similarity is used to expand the intent categorization. Lastly,Step 5 assesses the results in reference to ground-truth labels in accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score.Our method is much better than baselines. TF-IDF+SVM has an approximate 78 percent accuracy on benchmark datasets like ATIS and SNIPS. The accuracy of vanilla BERT fine-tuning is 86 and our SBERT-based system has an accuracy of 92 to 94 and F1-score is as high as 20. Moreover, SBERT removes the time of pair-wise comparison by almost 60 percent, and it is very scalable to execute queries in real time. Overall, contrary to current models, the SBERT framework is more accurate and efficient in intent detection in search engines and con- versational AI systems, and is a practical and scalable solution.

title: Expert-Agnostic AI for Intelligent Tutoring Systems: Leveraging Self-Supervised Knowledge Mining
authors: S.V.N. Sreenivasu, Tippanaboina Ramesh, Shaik Mohammad Fayaz, Kinnera Yarra Jakraiah, Dharmapuri Siri, Sesha Bhargavi Velagaleti
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Expert-tailored annotations and domain-specific rules are usually unavoidable in traditional Intelligent Tutoring Systems (ITS), restricting scalability and flexibility. This paper presents a new expert-agnostic approach to intelligent tutoring based on self-supervised learning to promote more personalized education with-out depending on domain experts. We develop and evaluate multiple deep learning models—GRU, BiLSTM, LSTM, CNN, Transformer, MLP, and hybrid Embedded GRU-CNN—trained on student interaction datasets using automatic representation learning techniques. Our approach leverages the sequential nature of learning behaviours and embeds contextu-alized features to identify optimal learning interventions. Among the tested architectures, Embedded GRU-CNN and BiLSTM, CNN models demonstrated superior accuracy (up to 99%) in predicting learner needs and engagement levels. The findings demonstrate substantial student modelling performance improvement without hand-crafted labels, affirming the promise of self-supervised methods in ITS. The work opens to scalable, domain-agnostic intelligent tutoring systems that can adapt and provide feedback in real time, a step toward democratizing AI-facilitated education for multiple types of learners.

title: Enhancing Order Fulfillment Efficiency Using Particle Swarm Optimization
authors: Abhishek Nair, Priyanshi Furiya, Ami Munshi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: This paper presents a Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) approach to optimize order fulfillment in the dairy supply chain. The goal is to minimize costs by efficiently allocating products from multiple warehouses to stores, considering unit price, transportation penalties, and product shelf-life. The PSO method effectively identifies near-optimal allocation strategies, prioritizing lower-priced and fresher products while minimizing waste from expiring stock. Experimental results demonstrate strong performance, with the model achieving high Freshness Indicator Scores (FIS 100%) and Customer Experience Scores (CES 88.94%), alongside efficient inventory management (RMI 43.77%) and reduced expiration losses (LES 81.69%). This study confirms PSO as a viable solution for complex supply chain optimization in perishable goods.

title: A Secure Aadhaar-Integrated Approach to Digital Health Record Management
authors: S Neeraj, Anupam Bhatia
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: It is possible that implementing Blockchain technology into the field of healthcare will change the way know-how is safeguarded, accessed and managed in terms of patient records. This paper proposes a new way to safely store and manipulate electronic health records (EHRs) using blockchain with Aadhaar identification, an Indian biometric identity system. The proposed system will ensure the integrity of data, data confidentiality, and interoperability and prevent unlawful access and alteration. The resolution will use a decentralized ledger that will remove sources of single points of weakness, reduce the threat of data compromise and enable a convenient access to patient records irrespective of the provider used. Aadhaar authentication secures an established connection between patients and medical data, which assures that only the privileged users can have access to vital health data. Smart contracts make the access control process even more automated, allowing permission-based exchange of records between, and across, healthcare providers, insurance firms and regulatory bodies. This paper also discusses barriers such as scalability and ability to meet data security requirements, the potential risks associated with Aadhaar-based health data. The proposed architecture favors greater transparency, the reduction of fraud, and increased control of the patients over their health information through the combination of Blockchain and verification via the Aadhaar scheme.

title: LMI-Based Delay Margin Design in Deregulated Hybrid Power Systems with Energy Storage Integration
authors: Gargi Roy, Soumen Biswas, Anagha Bhattacharya, Deboduti Upadhaya, Susanta Dutta
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Integrating renewable energy sources, especially solar and wind, into modern power networks has drawn a lot of attention in recent decades as a practical means to guarantee sustainability and dependable electricity production.However, system stability and synchronization are significantly challenged by communication delays and the inherent nonlinear behaviors of thermal units, including governor dead band (GDB), boiler dynamics (BD), and generation rate constraints (GRC).This study utilizes a Linear Matrix Inequality (LMI) approach to ascertain the Margin of Allowable Delay Bound (MADB) that guarantees delay-dependent stability of the Automatic Generation Control (AGC) system.The study models a two-area hybrid power system comprising thermal, solar, and wind generation, integrated with energy storage units such as an Aqua-Electrolyzer (AE), Fuel Cell (FC), and Ultra-Capacitor (UC), operating in a deregulated environment.A Proportional-Integral (P-I) controller is subsequently designed to manage its operation.A Chaotic Atomic Search Optimization (CASO) algorithm is used to optimize the controller gains.Simulation results demonstrate greater dynamic performance and increased tolerance to communication delays, confirming the efficacy of the suggested approach.The proposed methodology provides valuable insights for selecting P-I gains based on permissible delays, ensuring robust frequency regulation in thermal-solar-wind hybrid systems. Index Terms-Chaotic atomic search algorithm (CASO),Communication delay, Load Frequency Control, MADB (Margin of allowable delay),P-I controller.

title: Optimized Random Forest Classifier Using Particle Swarm Optimization for Accurate Fault Detection and Classification in Transmission Systems
authors: Sagarika Mohanty, Ranjan Kumar Mallick, Pravati Nayak, Sairam Mishra, Ajay Kumar Swain
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Reliable fault detection and classification in electrical transmission systems is essential for maintaining system stability, safety, and performance. This paper presents an intelligent fault diagnosis framework employing a Random Forest (RF) classifier optimized using Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) for the classification of faults in high-voltage transmission lines. The proposed model is designed to classify a total of 12 classes, comprising 11 distinct fault types and one healthy (no-fault) condition. The Random Forest algorithm, known for its robustness and ensemble learning capabilities, is enhanced by optimizing its most critical hyper parameter, the number of decision trees through the PSO met heuristic. PSO efficiently searches the solution space to determine the optimal tree count that maximizes classification accuracy. The system is trained and evaluated using a comprehensive dataset generated through simulation studies under varying fault conditions, fault resistances, inception angles, and system configurations. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the PSO-optimized RF model, achieving an outstanding classification accuracy of 99.6914%, confirming its ability to accurately distinguish between complex fault scenarios.

title: Controlled Islanding for Mitigating Cascading Failures in Power Systems
authors: Mudasser Hassan, Michael Negnevitsky, Evan Franklin, Waqas Hassan, Pooyan Alinaghi Hossienabadi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Controlled islanding (CI) is a crucial strategy to prevent cascading failures in power systems. In emergency conditions, operators often lack sufficient time to fully restore system stability, with immediate load shedding commonly employed as a last resort to avert system collapse. CI offers a more effective alternative by minimizing the required load shedding under stressed network conditions. The challenges of determining when to initiate CI and how to stabilize the resulting islands require systematic methodologies to support operators with automated solutions. This paper proposes an expert system-based CI framework that performs steady-state analysis to eliminate critical transmission line overloads and voltage violations during emergencies. The method evaluates corrective actions for maintaining system interconnection and, when necessary, partitions the network into stable islands. For each island, corrective actions are automatically identified to ensure operational feasibility while maximizing load supply. The approach integrates Newton-Raphson load flow analysis with a hierarchical graph spectral clustering (HGSC)-based partitioning method, and a brute-force search algorithm validates the optimality of HGSC results. Simulation studies on a modified IEEE 14-bus system show that the proposed framework under stressed conditions, reduce total load shedding by 23.8% (from 31.5 MW to 24 MW) through controlled islanding, compared with continuing to operate without islanding. These results demonstrate that the framework enhances service continuity, mitigates cascading failure risks, and provides a practical decision-support tool for real-time power system emergency management.

title: A PSO-Optimized CNN-BiLSTM Model for Enhanced Gesture Classification from Multichannel Surface EMG Signals
authors: Ajit Rathor, Gautam Shah, Abhinav Sharma, Abhinav Sharma, Medhavi Vishnoi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Wearable sensors like EMG, FMG, IMU, and MMG provide the muscle activity information which has various applications in robotics, human machine interface, gaming, virtual reality and sign language recognition. Hand movement detection is made easier by DL algorithms; however, they are computationally demanding and need huge training datasets. This study proposes a CNN-BiLSTM model to classify predefined movements into distinct categories with enhanced accuracy. The model integrates customized CNN-BiLSTM model for extracting the time and frequency features from 21 hand movements and the extracted features are integrated and employed to optimize the convolution layer filters and learning rate with the Particle Swarm Optimization Algorithm (PSOA). The optimized values attain impressive metrics such as accuracy of 97.32%, precision of 97.29%, F1-score of 97.26%, recall of 97.30%, MCC of 0.9715, FPR of 0.13 %, FNR of 2.7%, and specificity of 99.87%. The proposed CNN-BiLSTM PSO optimized DL model is trained on a publicly available dataset of hand movements based on Indian population using surface electromyography signal outperforms other models in both accuracy and complexity.

title: Analyzing Smart Cities: Cybersecurity Architecture, Threats, Mitigation, Challenges, and Open Problems
authors: Md Ismail Hossain, Ragib Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: Smart cities provide better services, a healthier urban life, and exciting new ideas with connected technology, data analysis, and infrastructure linking. However, this digital transformation introduces significant cybersecurity challenges threatening public safety, privacy, and critical services. In this paper, we offer a detailed analysis of the cybersecurity challenges smart cities encounter. We introduce a multi-layered cybersecurity framework designed specifically for urban settings, examine real world cyber threats, including IoT exploitation and supply chain attacks, and describe technical strategies for mitigation, such as network microsegmentation, blockchain-based integrity checks, and intrusion detection systems. We also emphasize existing surveys alongside the unique contributions of this study. Similarly, we highlight some significant research challenges that need to be addressed, like developing adaptable security frameworks and ensuring privacy in data analytics. These issues are crucial for building safe and resilient smart cities to ensure public safety. Our findings will help policymakers, technologists, and researchers create secure urban environments.

title: Predictive Analytics for Youth Mental Health Leveraging ML to Identify Early Signs of Depression and Sleep Disorder
authors: Ranjit Kaur, Punam Rattan, Raminder Pal Singh, Madhav Dua, Mokesh Nagpal
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Recent Advances in Computing and Systems (REACS)
abstract: Mental strength enables persons to maintain healthy emotional processes which support total well-being. Mental health condition greatly influences both problem resolution during stressful moments and social relationships and produces decisive outcomes for decision making. The first mental health condition which occurs most often is depression since it generates persistent feelings of sadness and emotional withdrawal symptoms. Depression left untreated results in individuals facing major daily routine interruptions together with bad academic performance along with social segregation and drug addiction and self-harming tendencies. Detecting depression symptoms at an early stage has become essential to prevent prolonged complications because researchers developed an effective depression detection system using machine learning (ML) methods. The predictive analytics system relies on Decision Trees, Random Forest, and Logistic Regression models to analyze behavioral and physiological data therefore discovering patterns for depression diagnosis. Random Forest analyzes multiple trees to produce superior predictive mental health risk variable outcomes while Decision Trees uses such variables to classify patients. The probability assessment made by Logistic Regression depends on psychological data and lifestyle information. Evidence-based sentence generation assessment tools extract mental status assessment data from electronic health records using patient information obtained from social platforms and wearable devices.

title: Coordinated Control of Grid-Forming and Grid-Following Inverters During Parallel Operation
authors: Akramul Haque, Danny Soetanto, Kashem Muttaqi
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper investigates load-sharing dynamics between grid-following inverters (GFLIs) and grid-forming inverters (GFMIs) operating in parallel. A 37-bus system, modelled in Power World Simulator and PSCAD, was used to analyze system responses to sudden load changes under both gridtied and island modes. The performance of GFLIs was evaluated under fixed power control and droop control schemes. Results demonstrate that replacing fixed power-controlled GFLIs with droop-controlled configurations significantly reduces frequency deviations, thereby enhancing system stability. Specifically, during the island operation, the system frequency reduced from 51.1 to 50.25 Hz for incorporating droop control-based GFLI. In case of a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 M W}$</tex> load step in island mode, frequency reached 51.7 Hz, which reduced to 50.5 Hz for grid supporting GFLI. These improvements highlight the importance of equipping GFLIs with droop functionality when operating alongside GFMIs and facilitate a smoother transition to renewable-dominated power systems while mitigating risks associated with inverter-based resource (IBR) integration.

title: Switching-Cell Buck-Boost DC-AC Inverter with Shared Ground
authors: Ying Wen Chen, Hafiz Furqan Ahmed, Mohsin Jamil
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Renewable energy generation systems are inherently affected by fluctuating environmental conditions, resulting in wide variations in output voltage that challenge conventional power-conversion methods. This paper presents a novel single-stage, non-isolated inverter architecture that combines a switching-cell buck-boost topology with a commonground design. The proposed configuration enables seamless stepup/down voltage regulation and DC-AC inversion within a single power stage, eliminating shoot-through risks without requiring dead-time PWM. The switching cell structure allows for the independent control of each leg, simplifying modulation and improving the dynamic response. Meanwhile, the common-ground connection between the photovoltaic source and grid neutral clamps the high-frequency common-mode voltage, effectively mitigating leakage currents in transformerless systems. To verify its performance, detailed simulations and hardware experiments were conducted.

title: Explainable Deep Learning Approach for Early Detection of Colorectal Cancer via Multiclass Histopathology Classification
authors: Animesh Dey, Naim - Ul Hossain Anik, Naznin Sultana, MD Nahid Hasan, Tahmid Kabir Shrabon, MD Kaikobad
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Colorectal cancer (CRC) is a leading cause of cancer-related mortality worldwide, primarily due to delayed diagnosis. Early detection significantly improves prognosis, yet conventional histopathological examination is labor-intensive, subjective, and lacks interpretability when paired with standard deep learning methods. In this study, we propose an interpretable deep learning approach to classify colorectal histopathology images into clinically meaningful categories: Normal, Precancerous, and Cancerous. We utilized a modified version of the publicly available NCT-CRC-HE-100K dataset, restructured to emphasize early-stage identification. The proposed architecture integrates a ResNet-50 backbone enhanced with Convolutional Block Attention Modules (CBAM), effectively guiding the model's attention towards diagnostically relevant tissue features. To further increase clinical trust, we incorporate Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) for visual explanation of model decisions. Evaluation on the independent CRC-VAL-HE-7K dataset demonstrated an overall accuracy of 94%, with macro-average precision, recall, and F1-score values reaching 95%. Notably, the model achieved exceptional recall rates for Normal (100%) and Precancerous (96%) classes, highlighting its effectiveness in early cancer detection. These results substantiate our method’s potential as a robust and explainable tool, facilitating timely and accurate CRC diagnosis and enhancing clinical decision-making processes.

title: Grey Wolf Optimization based MPPT of PV System Powered Water Pumping System Driven by SRM under Partial Shading Conditions
authors: Cholleti Harish, P. Loganathan, G Sudha, K. Trinadh Babu, Sumit Pundir, S. Kanmani Jebaseeli, Krishna Chaithanya Janapati, Ajay Sudhir Bale Senior, Kandi Bhanu Prakash
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: The WPS plays a crucial role in various daily human activities, including agriculture, drinking water supply, and industrial applications. In particular, the agricultural sector is heavily dependent on WPS, as it serves as the main source of irrigation for crops. To reduce the strain on the electrical grid and ensure a consistent power supply for WPS in rural areas, a standalone PV powered WPS installed locally offers a practical solution. An electrical motor is essential for operating the pump, which draws water from underground sources to irrigate fields. Among the different types of motors available, SRMs have attracted considerable attention from researchers for their potential use in WPS due to their many benefits. This paper examines an 8/6 pole SRM designed to deliver the maximum torque required to lift water from significant depths. However, incorporating batteries into WPS systems can result in higher costs and increased maintenance, which may be a barrier for farmers. Therefore, this study emphasizes a PVpowered SRM based WPS that functions without batteries, utilizing a speed sensorless DTC in conjunction with a SMC. The GWO based P&O algorithm is employed and integrated with a dc-link voltage controller to optimize power extraction from the PV system even operating efficiently during PSCs by managing the proposed converter. As a result, an additional DC-DC converter for MPPT is rendered unnecessary. Detailed results from HIL testing are presented to validate the proposed system’s performance under both transient and steady-state conditions.

publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)

title: Image-Based Detection of Herpes Zoster Using Deep Learning
authors: Murali M, Jeba Emilyn, Gowri Shankari K, Bhavithra R S, Prabakaran N, Harini S
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Herpes Zoster, or shingles, is an infection caused by reactivation of the Varicella-Zoster Virus. Early detection is vital to avoid this infection. This project helps us understand how one can use deep learning for automatic Herpes Zoster diagnosis from medical images. Lightweight convolutional neural network models like MobileNetV2 and VGG16 (optional) were used in combination with basic image pre-processing. For comparison, traditional classifiers like Support Vector Machine (SVM) and K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) were also applied. The proposed system has high accuracy with small data. To provide results in a simpler form, Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) was used to create heatmap visualizations showing the affected area and ensuring the models focus on relevant parts. Results show that even using simple models and small datasets, we can identify early-stage HZ accurately, which can be a cost-effective tool to aid healthcare professionals.

title: Paddy Disease Recognition Using Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: N Siva RamaKrishna, T. Anitha, N. Charan, P. Vijay Kumar, I V S L. Haritha, M. Shanthi, S. Varaprasad
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Rice is one of the critical staple foods for the world, supporting food security within countries and many economies on a national level. However, its productivity is adversely affected by leaf-borne pathogens, including bacterial blight, brown spot, and leaf smut, and can reduce yield by 5 10 percent. To address these losses, this research proposed an automated system to identify and classify three widespread rice-leaf diseases from images, using convolutional neural network (CNN)-based transfer-learning. The study employs a hybrid strategy based on some CNN architectures and pre-trained CNNs like ResNet50, InceptionV3, Xception, and EfficientNet. First, experimental attempts with the tailor-made CNN models from the beginning did not produce good results as the dataset size was small. Subsequent to this implementations of transfer learning techniques with source datasets such as ImageNet. Of the models tried, the Xception architecture worked the best with the highest training accuracy of 99.7This research briefly addresses the most urgent work of early disease detection in rice farming that would enable farmers to acquire a low-price, fast, and precise diagnostic tool. The created system will assisting in timely intervention for disease management thereby assisting in reducing crop loss and enhancing the quality of the paddy produce. Since to dead training another problem is overfitting. While the results are promising, more data sets will provide larger gains. Finally, the performance of deep learning algorithms of greater datasets will yield improved results.

title: MobileNet Architecture for Deep Learning-Based Automated Detection and Classification of Medullary Sponge Kidney in Computed Tomography Imaging
authors: Ragini Y P, Hayel Khafajeh, T. Vijetha, Akila Venkatraman, J Thirumalai, V. Abarna
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The paper introduces a deep learning framework that identifies and classifies Medullary Sponge Kidney (MSK) automatically in computer tomography (CT) imagery with the MobileNet framework. The curated dataset was used to train the proposed model and test it on, using data augmentation and transfer learning to improve generalization. Confusion matrix, ROC curve, and classification statistics showed an ideal classification, with 100 % accuracy, precision, recall, and F1-score. ROC curve area under the curve (AUC) was 1.0, which established the discriminative power of the model. The training and validation accuracy curves showed quick convergence with very little loss, which means there was excellent learning without overfitting. These outcomes show that MobileNet can be a computationally effective yet very precise MSK detection method in CT scanning, which can be used in faster, automatic clinical decision-making. Such a method has the potential to be implemented in radiology workflows to enhance the effectiveness of diagnosis and help early identify disorders of the kidneys.

title: Cognitive IoT Networks with Bio-Inspired Embedded AI for Predictive Maintenance and Sustainable Energy Optimization
authors: Harivallabi S, Sathyanarayanan S, Praveen M
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Increasing system complexity poses significant difficulties for green energy management and predictive maintenance, primarily due to limited adaptability and centralization of AI systems. Such difficulties are eliminated in this work by synthesizing Cognitive IoT (CIoT) and bio-inspired embedded Artificial Intelligence, as a consequence of which a self-adaptive and energy-efficient industrial ecosystem is being designed. The proposed industrial ecosystem utilizes swarm intelligence, neural adaptation, and self-organizing feedback loops for decentralized decisions and real-time fault identification. Embedded AI modules embedded at IoT edge nodes learn automatic behavior during operation, predict likely faults, and optimize scheduling for repairs. Moreover, a hybrid framework implementing evolutionary optimization and deep neuro-symbolic learning maintains dynamic resource allocation and energy-aware computation. The novelty is dependent upon bio-inspired cognitive architecture, and self-evolving and environmentally adaptable operation is achieved by means of multi-agent coordination and hybrid learning paradigms. The end objective is improved reliability of a system, decreased downtime, and ecological sustainability for next-generation industrial networks.

title: A Comprehensive Study of Lithium-Ion Battery Modeling, Health Estimation Technique
authors: Kalpana Meena, Nishant Kumar
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: As a critical technology in the transition to greener energy systems, lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) have found applications in electric vehicles (EVs), grid-scale energy storage, and portable devices. They are the ideal option for modern energy storage because of their high energy density, efficiency, and scalability; yet, problems including limited lifespan, safety risks, and performance degradation still pose significant barriers to wider adoption. To address these issues, exact health assessment methods including State of Charge (SOC), State of Health (SOH), State of Energy (SOE), and Remaining Useful Life (RUL) as well as accurate modeling of electrochemical, thermal, and aging behaviors are required. Additionally, robust lifecycle management strategies are required. This paper offers a complete evaluation of battery modeling techniques, including hybrid data-driven methodologies, electrochemical, equivalent circuit, and thermal approaches. The application of state estimation methods in real-time battery management systems is also investigated. Furthermore, analyzed are lifecycle optimization strategies such as temperature regulation, charging control, predictive diagnostics, and the expanding application of digital twin technologies. By integrating existing research findings, industry practices, and future possibilities, the study identifies knowledge gaps and offers a path for the development of intelligent, scalable, and sustainable battery management solutions for next-generation energy systems.

title: Advancing Dermatological AI: A Unified Deep Learning Pipeline for Skin Lesion Analysis
authors: A S Manjunath, Shashidhar R, Shashank M P, Mayura Tapkire, Roopa M, Kruthika S
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Skin cancer is one of the most common types of cancer worldwide, and early and accurate diagnosis is required to avert the advancement of malignant cases. The current dermatology diagnosis system depends on expert human evaluation of dermoscopic images, which produces limited accessibility and inconsistent results. This study developed a comprehensive deep learning framework that combines lesion segmentation with multiple dataset classification, severity assessment, and explainable AI to improve diagnostic performance and clinical confidence. The system uses Attention U-Net for lesion segmentation, which produces a Dice coefficient of 0.9661 and IoU score of 0.9361 on the ISIC 2018 dataset. The classification module used EfficientNet models trained on the ISIC 2018 (seven classes) and PAD-UFES-20 (six classes) datasets and produced 87.68% and 73.33% accuracy, respectively. The severity prediction module, which uses clinical metadata attributes, provides a reliable assessment of lesion urgency. The heatmaps generated by the Grad-CAM visualization demonstrate decision-critical regions in an interpretable manner. The integrated system surpasses the performance of single components, while segmentation-assisted classification provides better results than raw image analysis. AI capabilities are matched to clinical demands through this unified approach.

title: Enhancing Public Safety in Smart Cities Through LLM-Based Misinformation Detection
authors: Saeed H. Al-Shamrani, Ragib Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: The concept of smart cities holds great potential for enhancing urban management. Using modern information and communication technologies (ICTs), smart cities can efficiently improve the quality of life of their inhabitants in multiple domains, including public safety. While smart cities are constantly employing technologies such as Internet of Things (IoT) sensors and cameras to make informed decisions, the lack of infrastructure and resources has led them to resort to data crowdsourcing. This increasing dependence on a wide range of data sources also creates a wide range of issues. One particular area of concern is the difficulty of ensuring the trustworthiness of every piece of information. Data in smart cities can be susceptible to a variety of issues that can compromise the trustworthiness of data; hence, decisions can be flawed, which can indeed affect public safety. While information obtained from ICTs can be guarded using technical solutions, information provided by citizens can contain misinformation. Furthermore, most public safety platforms depend on users' reports through designated apps or social media, which allows for the possibility of misinformation, hence compromising the trustworthiness of data. In this paper, we propose a fine-tuned Large Language Model (LLM) using misinformation datasets to detect misinformation in texts within the context of public safety. We achieve an accuracy of 98% in detecting misinformation. We also highlight the open issues in this area and briefly discuss future directions.

title: Metaweave: Reconfigurable Metasurface Framework for Real-Time Beam Steering in mmWave Wireless Systems
authors: R Saravanakumar
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) communication demands agile beam control to sustain high data rates and link reliability in dense and mobile environments. Real-time beam steering is essential to concentrate energy toward intended users while maintaining low latency and modulation fidelity. Current approaches such as passive reflectarrays, varactor-tuned surfaces, and RF-microelectromechanical systems (RF-MEMS) trade off reconfiguration speed, efficiency, or implementation complexity. This work introduces MetaWeave, a reconfigurable metasurface framework that employs positive-intrinsic-negative (PIN) diode based 2-bit phase control and a Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) driven low-latency controller to produce compact phase masks for real-time adaptation. Evaluation was performed on a 28 GHz testbed with 1 GHz bandwidth over a 3 m link using line-of-sight, non-line-of-sight, scanned-beam, and pedestrian mobility scenarios. Experimental outcomes show an SNR improvement up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 8. 8 ~ d B}$</tex> (pedestrian) and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{+ 8. 2 ~ d B}$</tex> (line-of-sight), throughput rising from 3.2 Gbps to 6.4 Gbps in the best case, sub-millisecond switching near 0.9 ms, error vector magnitude (EVM) reduction from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 7. 9 \%}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7. 4 \%}$</tex>, and a peak antenna gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 5. 0 ~ d B i}$</tex> with 71% aperture efficiency at a total power near 2.0 W. The results indicate that discrete phase quantization combined with lowlatency control can deliver practical, energy aware beam steering for next generation mmWave wireless links.

title: Modeling Hydrogen Electrolysers for Power System Applications
authors: Kalim Ullah, Evan Franklin, Michael Negnevitsky, Waqas Hassan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Hydrogen electrolysers are currently being deployed in power systems to meet an emerging demand for green hydrogen. On the other hand, electrolysers can be operated as flexible loads to support the further integration of renewable generation, by providing rapid and controlled response to match the fast ramping of intermittent renewable energy sources, to mitigate variation in system load or to respond to unplanned loss of generation or load. Fast-responding electrolysers can rapidly increase or decrease their power consumption following a contingency that causes a supply-demand imbalance, thereby helping restore system frequency. However, understanding the capabilities of electrolysers in providing this type of dynamic power system support requires the development of accurate and reliable electrolyser models. One of the most effective approaches is to develop an electrical equivalent circuit, with equivalent circuit parameters being determined either from first-principle electrochemistry or via experimental analysis. In this paper, we investigate various methods for experimental determination of the proton exchange membrane (PEM) electrolyser equivalent circuit parameters. We identify electrochemical impedance spectroscopy as the most suitable method for accurate extraction of parameters, both for individual electrolyser cells or for small or large electrolyser stacks. Additionally, we present a comprehensive transfer function for the electrolyser, providing valuable insights into its dynamic behaviour and potential for providing rapid power system support. Based on a combination of published data for individual electrolyser cells and publicly available information on large-scale electrolyser stacks, we develop an equivalent circuit model for a large-scale commercially available PEM electrolyser system, which can subsequently be used for dynamic power system integration studies. Simulation case-studies yield steady-state performance consistent with the nameplate ratings of a commercially operating system, while demonstrating its transient response characteristics.

title: On Integrating an IPMSM with Well's Turbine of an OWC Based Wave Energy Plant
authors: S. Muthukumar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: In the OWC (Oscillating Water Column) based near-shore wave energy plants, among various self-rectifying air-turbines, Well's turbine has established itself over the past four decades. Its easily maintainable simple structure often overcomes the main bottle-neck associated with its ‘stall’ behavior. Its flow co-efficient range of operation avoiding such stalling behavior, has gained attention leading to improvement by changing the blade tip-clearance and by addition of end-rings. On the electrical side, PMSG based power excavation is preferred in comparison with previously used DFIG based schemes. Inertia of the turbine-electrical machine cascade is also a critical parameter in allowing quick acceleration/ deceleration of the shaft, thereby controlling the flow-efficient and hence aiding in avoidance of the stalling behavior. This paper proposes integrating IPMSM with Well's turbine, by inserting the permanent magnets into the turbine blades and building the stator winding within the outer frame, This leads to an IPMSM with aspect ratio different from normal design. Integrating the two components of the cascade, leads to reduction in the overall inertia. Using Simcenter MagNet software, FEM based analysis is carried out on the IPMSM designed with constant chord type Well's turbine. With six number of blades used as 6-poles of the spoke type IPMSM, the terminal voltage obtained, when the turbine is run at constant speed, are presented, considering three cases of stator windings, i) 36 slot full-pitched single layer winding, ii) 45 slot double layered fractional slot short-pitched winding, and iii) 30 slots, 5-phase tooth type winding. THD analysis is carried out and the results are compared.

title: Efficient and Robust Phishing URL Detection based on Lexical Feature Extraction and Ensemble Learning
authors: Astha Shrivastava, Rohit Agrawal, Ranjeet Kumar Singh, Aditya Shastri
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: 抓取摘要失败: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='ieeexplore.ieee.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /document/11411181/ (Caused by ProtocolError('Connection aborted.', ConnectionResetError(104, 'Connection reset by peer')))

title: Streamlined on-Chip Temporal Prefetching
authors: Quang Duong, Calvin Lin
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: In this paper, we present the Streamline temporal prefetcher, which introduces a stream-based metadata representation that produces three significant benefits over Triangel, the previous state-of-the-art in temporal prefetching. First, it removes redundancy present in Triangel's metadata. Second, it prioritizes the storage of those metadata entries that have higher prefetch utility. Third, it eliminates the need for the untenable LLC traffic that is induced when Triangel dynamically adjusts the size of its metadata store. The end result is that for memory-intensive SPEC 2006, SPEC 2017, and GAP benchmarks, Streamline outperforms Triangel by 6.7 percentage points on an 8-core system with a stride prefetcher. This performance benefit comes from Streamline's improved storage efficiency: It holds 33% more correlations than Triangel, which translates to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 5}$</tex> percentage points better prefetch coverage. Significantly, Streamline matches the performance of Triangel even when Triangel is given twice the metadata storage.

title: UniFHE: Faster Accelerator for FHE with Diverse Algebraic Structure and Balanced Memory System
authors: Qingyun Niu, Lutan Zhao, Ming Cai, Kai Li, Dan Meng, Rui Hou
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) enables computations on encrypted data. Existing FHE schemes are primarily categorized into RLWE-based word-wise schemes and LWEbased bit-wise schemes. Efficient combination of different FHE schemes adapted to real-world applications has emerged as a research focus. This paper proposes UniFHE, the first FHE accelerator that supports diverse algebraic structures using general arithmetic units to achieve higher performance. UniFHE is compatible with both RLWE-based and LWE-based FHE schemes without modifications to their original algorithmic designs. To support both finite ring and complex field operations, UniFHE introduces a general arithmetic unit and further constructs core computation structures. To balance on-chip memory demands across different schemes, UniFHE adopts a multi-pipeline architecture for LWE-based schemes. The core functional units for RLWE-based schemes are spliced based on the LWE-based pipelines. Furthermore, an on-chip plaintext encoding mechanism significantly reduces off-chip memory bandwidth demands. Experimental results show that, beyond superior area and energy efficiency, UniFHE delivers up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$13.6 \times$</tex> higher performance compared to scheme-specific accelerator combinations. Moreover, in hybrid schemes, UniFHE achieves a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.2 \times$</tex> speedup over the state-of-the-art unified FHE accelerator Trinity.

title: An Explainable Multi-Model Framework for Detecting Self-Stimulatory Behaviors in Real-World Autism Videos
authors: Balla Kumar Basavaraju, T. Senthil Kumar, Kuladeep Reddy Chappidi, Veluri Pavan Vignesh, Abhiram Bikkina, Palanisamy T
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Identification of Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) Autism Spectrum Disorder Early intervention for Autism Spectrum Disorders (ASD) needs early diagnosis; the problem is, though, that the process of early diagnosis remains challenging and is based on subjective judgment. Autistic Stereotypies consist of actions such as hand flapping and spinning. These actions are typical of yet not extensively researched as markers of ASD. These actions occur in daily situations and can be recorded via a smartphone. This manuscript presents an automatic, objective, and interpretation-ready approach for the detection and classification of such patterns in unrestrained videos. By resorting to the only publicly available benchmark dedicated to full-body repetitive patterns in unconstrained environments—the Self-Stimulatory Behaviour Dataset (SSBD)—we assess the performance of three different techniques: the Extended Bag-of-Visual-Words method employing Space-Time Interest Points, the hybrid CNN-LSTM network architecture, and the fine-tuned Inflated 3D Convolutional Network (I3D). The proposed models perform greatly on the benchmarked task and deliver superior results to all former baselines. In addition to that, SHAP and LIME methods of Explainable AI assist in giving spatiotemporal explanations specific to behaviors. The proposed system offers a non-invasive scalable solution to preliminary screening of ASD on a smartphone

title: AI-Driven Waste Segregation and IoT-Enabled Monitoring: A Smart Dustbin for Urban Waste Management
authors: Roopesh Ramesh, Nelamangala Nagaraju Shwetha, Mayur Rajendra Badiger, Shreya Santhosh, Tejasvi Bellubbi, Aishwarya Bagi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: This paper proposes an intelligent AIoT-enabled waste management system that autonomously classifies and segregates waste at the point of disposal while simultaneously monitoring real-time bin fill levels. The system combines edge-deployable Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), capacitive moisture sensing, and ultrasonic distance measurement within a modular embedded framework comprising Raspberry Pi and ESP32 microcontrollers. Waste classification is achieved through a lightweight CNN model optimized for resource-constrained environments, enabling near real-time inference with sub-second latency. The classified waste is physically sorted into biodegradable, recyclable, and non-recyclable compartments using a servo-actuated mechanism, thereby minimizing human intervention. IoT connectivity facilitates continuous bin status updates to a cloud-based dashboard (ThingSpeak), with automated alerts upon threshold breaches or system anomalies. Experimental validation demonstrates a classification accuracy exceeding 90%, a sorting success rate of 96%, and fill-level sensing precision within ±2 cm. This scalable, low-cost solution holds significant promise for deployment in smart cities, public infrastructure, and urban sustainability frameworks.

title: Enhancing Malware Detection with Malware-BERT: A Hybrid Approach Using Multi-Head Attention
authors: Santosh Rani, Princy Tyagi, Diksha Saini, Shivani, Swati Rawat, Neetu Tyagi
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The growing complexity of malware poses an intensifying risk to cybersecurity, especially with strategies formulated to circumvent conventional detection mechanisms. Signature-based and heuristic-based methodologies frequently prove inadequate in detecting innovative or evasive malware, hence forcing the advancement of more sophisticated detection techniques. This study presents Malware-BERT, a model explicitly engineered to identify evasive malware concealed within PDF documents. The newly created dataset, Evasive-PDFMal2022, comprises 5,557 malicious and 4,468 benign samples, and the model utilizes BERT embeddings together with multi-head attention methods to improve detection efficacy. The research illustrates the efficacy of Malware-BERT, attaining an accuracy of 96.3% with the RoBERTa model. These findings underscore the efficacy of hybrid BERT-based designs in enhancing cybersecurity measures against sophisticated PDF-based malware.

title: A Novel Improved Adaptive Network-Fuzzy Inference System Approach for Effective Battery Charging Control
authors: S Resmara, Ch Hussaian Basha, S Venkateswarlu Reddy, Fini Fathima
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: Within the new paradigm of power management in EVs, there is a trend to integrate EVs into microgrid systems, and many of them are not at high voltage. These voltage drops are, as a rule, initiated by differences in energy yield through renewable energy resources such as wind. Regulation of charging/discharging of EV batteries- stabilizing across the smart grid is a mammoth task. In the study, an Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) shall be implemented to control the operation of the battery to maximize the usage of the electric vehicles and extend the battery lifetime. The augmented 0-constraint algorithm will be used in order to find efficient solutions along the Pareto-optimal frontier, which would assure optimal voltage regulation between the smart grid and EVs by providing optimal solutions. The given strategy has been applied to microgrid systems to determine the efficiency of EVs and how they can be used in various cases.

title: Efficient Power Curve Modelling and Generation Forecasting for Wind Power Plant
authors: Lokesh Vadla, Ramesh Kumar Ravi, Vineet Kumar, Bhumireddy Poojitha Reddy, Thupakula Pradeep, Paineni Reddy Prasad, Manoj Kumar Debnath
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: This study presents an analysis of wind speed and power generation data from the Anantapur Wind Farm, a 226.8 MW onshore wind power project located in Andhra Pradesh, India. Several forecasting methods have been developed for wind power prediction and power curve modelling. A logistic function model is employed for power forecasting, while the theoretical wind power equation is used for power curve design. Metaheuristic optimization techniques such as Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO), Genetic Algorithm (GA), Harris Hawks Optimization (HHO), and Hippopotamus Optimization (HO) are applied for parameter estimation of the logistic S-function model and power curve. The performance of these approaches is evaluated in terms of the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) between predicted and actual power. The results highlight the superiority of the proposed HHO method, which provides more accurate predictions in both cases.

title: Predictive scaling using reinforcement learning
authors: Rajnish Kumar Chaturvedi, Swastik Srivastava, Nikhil Mangla, Akshay Dheeraj
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The escalating complexity of cloud resource management demands solutions beyond traditional reactive scaling. This paper introduces the Self-Evolving Predictive Engine (SEPE), a novel architecture for autonomous cluster scaling in cloud environments. SEPE leverages a multi-stage approach: initially, a predictive baseline is established by training a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model on historical workload data, with the model stored in DynamoDB. Subsequently, a process running on EC2 instances continuously monitors cluster performance and adjusts scaling based on real-time demands, accommodating dynamic workloads. Post-scaling, CloudFormation is utilized to assess resource utilization (CPU, RAM, etc.), providing critical design considerations related to usage limitations. Finally, a Reinforcement Learning (RL) component continuously optimizes decision-making based on these parameters, ensuring iterative performance improvement. This self-evolving system moves beyond traditional reactive scaling, like Kubernetes’ HPA, by predicting workload surges and analyzing capacity based on anticipated or event-driven demands. Empirical results demonstrate that SEPE achieves a 40 percent reduction in resource inefficiency and a 30 percent reduction in scaling time compared to standard reactively scaled systems. The SEPE framework promises to fundamentally reshape cloud infrastructure management, delivering enhanced performance, efficiency, and scalability for diverse cloud-based operational environments.

title: Attention-Driven Hybrid U-Net Framework for Explainable Retinal Disease Diagnosis Using OCT Images
authors: N. Yuvarani, V. Sumalatha
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The prevention of vision loss requires the diagnosis of retinal disease with the help of Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT). This paper presents an Attention-Based Hybrid U-Net that can be used to analyze retinal diseases accurately and in ways that can be explained. The model combines U-Net based segmentation with an attention mechanism to boost the extraction of subtle pathological information. Thereafter, a CNN classifier recognizes four retinal types Choroidal Neovascularization (CNV), Diabetic Macular Edema (DME), DRUSEN, and Normal based on the features of the attention-enhanced segmentation. The proposed system was trained on 84,495 OCT images with preprocessing and augmentation to achieve a 97.8 % accuracy, which was better than the baseline deep learning models. Findings show that attention addition will enhance the feature localization and classification consistency, and this provides a reliable instrument to assist ophthalmologists in early diagnosing.

title: A Novel Machine Learning Framework for Diabetes Prediction Using Ensemble Techniques
authors: Savir Ali, Mohammad Ubaidullah Bokhari, Savita Gautam, Shahnwaz Afzal, Syed Kavish Ali
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Diabetes mellitus which is more often referred to as just diabetes is a disorder of metabolism defined by consistently high blood glucose levels. If it is feasible to make accurate early predictions, the severity of diabetes, as well as the chance of having the condition might be considerably decreased. There are promising opportunities for the early identification of diabetes thanks to the latest developments in ensemble machine learning algorithms, which are renowned for being quicker and less expensive than traditional methods. This paper presents an ensemble method of detecting diabetes. The data preprocessing stage encompasses several essential tasks, such as detecting outliers, normalizing and picking the most pertinent features. The framework integrates multiple machine learning techniques and augments their prediction efficacy through the utilization of ensemble approaches. The performance of these techniques is evaluated using various metrics to determine the accuracy of their predictive outcomes. This research employs the Pima Indian Diabetes (PIDD) dataset for experimental analysis. This dataset is generally used to predict the onset of diabetic symptoms of especially people belonging to India. Our findings indicate that the SVM + DT ensemble model achieved the highest accuracy at 98.48%, followed by the KNN + DT model with 96.74%, the RF + LR + DT model with 97.42%, and the DT + LR model with an accuracy of 95.56%. Among all the individual machine learning models and ensemble techniques evaluated, the SVM + DT ensemble model demonstrated the highest accuracy, making it the most effective approach to accurately predict diabetes.

title: HHO-Optimized Dual-Band Patch Antenna for 5G Wireless Systems
authors: S. Janarthanan, M. Anto Bennet, B. Hemalatha, M. Subalatha, S. Varalakshmi, N. Nisha Rosebel
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Sub-6 GHz <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{5 G}$</tex> wireless systems require antennas that are compact, economical, and efficient, while remaining easy to integrate into user equipment and small-cell platforms. Dualband microstrip patch radiators are well suited for this role, yet their design must balance footprint, impedance bandwidth, and radiation characteristics, particularly when realized on lossy substrates such as FR-4. Many existing designs rely on manual parameter sweeps or conventional metaheuristic algorithms, which often converge slowly, favour larger electrical dimensions, and provide only moderate bandwidth, especially when dualband performance is considered. In addition, several reported optimization studies concentrate on a single operating band or do not explicitly improve both impedance matching and gain at the two resonant frequencies. This work introduces an Improved Harris Hawks Optimization (HHO) scheme for the synthesis of a dual-band microstrip patch antenna designed to operate at 3.50 GHz and 4.90 GHz on a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$30 \times 30 ~\text{mm}^{2}$</tex> FR-4 substrate. The optimizer adjusts six key geometrical variables associated with the patch, slots, and parasitic stubs using a dual-frequency fitness function that minimizes the combined reflection coefficient at the two target bands. A large set of candidate geometries is evaluated through full-wave electromagnetic simulations across the sub- 6 GHz region, which effectively generates a design dataset capturing the nonlinear relationship between structural parameters and antenna responses. This dataset serves to benchmark the Improved HHO against recent nature-inspired optimizers, including SMA, AO, MPA, and GBO, under identical stopping conditions. The optimized antenna achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$S_{11}$</tex> levels below <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-24 \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex>, with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-10 \mathbf{~ d B}$</tex> bandwidths of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 2. 0 \%}$</tex> and 7.3%, realized gains of 5.12 dBi and 6.05 dBi, and radiation efficiencies above 83 % in both bands. These results indicate that the Improved HHO framework yields a compact, high-performance dual-band antenna suitable for 5 G applications and can be adapted to the design of more complex multi-band or array configurations.

title: Message from General Chair
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Welcome to the 32nd IEEE International Symposium on High-Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA 2026). HPCA has long been a premier forum for the computer architecture community to share new ideas and rigorous results, and to connect across academia and industry. Many of us have grown with HPCA—through the papers we read, the collaborations we form, and the students and colleagues we mentor along the way. It is a true honor for me to serve as the General Chair of HPCA 2026.

title: A Novel Fault Monitoring Method for Vertical Roller Mill Bearings Integrating Vibration Transmission Path Analysis and Knowledge Graph
authors: Junnan Guo, Zhiqiang Lu, Yuning Sun, Jiarui Zhang, Lei Mao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: As the critical load bearing component within vertical roller mills (VRMs), the grinding roller (GR) bearing system operates under extreme conditions, including low speeds, heavy loads, intense impacts, and high dust contamination. These conditions make it highly susceptible to failure, highlighting the importance of reliable real-time fault monitoring of rolling bearings for safe and stable VRM operation. However, the harsh environment and complex mechanical structure lead to attenuation and aliasing of the fault signals of GR bearings, resulting in extremely low signal-to-noise ratios (SNR) that pose a fundamental challenge to accurate measurement and monitoring. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel fault monitoring method that integrates vibration transmission path analysis with a knowledge graph (VTP-KG) for GR bearings. The proposed approach begins by quantifying the energy contribution of vibration transmission paths using power flow theory. Subsequently, a knowledge graph (KG) for GR fault monitoring is established by integrating multi-source information. Finally, a triple matching method is designed to accurately identify both the location and type of faults under multi-fault coupling scenarios. Compared to existing methods, the proposed VTP-KG approach achieves superior monitoring performance, including high accuracy and low false alarm rates, and demonstrates strong robustness against data imbalance.

title: SDR-Based Bidirectional Precise Wireless Channel Sounding for Physical Layer Key Generation
authors: Ji Gu, Liang Yin, Xin Zhang, Hongwen Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Wireless channel state information has been widely utilized in physical layer key generation (PLKG) as shared random sources. The temporal randomness and reciprocity of the channel parameters are utilized, posing a requirement for high-precision time-division duplexing (TDD) channel measurement. However, existing real channel measurement-based PLKG studies suffer from significant channel estimation errors, which degrade the measured channel reciprocity and increase the key disagreement rate. Furthermore, PLKG in static environments has aroused extensive attention. In static environments, the channel temporal variation is very weak, and is generally considered insufficient for reliable key generation. To address these challenges, in this paper, a high-precision bidirectional wireless channel fluctuation measuring platform was designed and implemented based on generic software-defined radio (SDR) architectures. The hardware impairments of multiple SDR platforms with different architectures were systematically analyzed. With such hardware impairments, traditional channel estimation methods, such as least squares (LS) and minimum mean square error (MMSE), were found suboptimal for measurement of the received signal strength indication (RSSI), and a tailored TDD RSSI measurement algorithm was proposed for generic SDR devices. With the proposed algorithm, TDD measurement with 200∼500μs period was achieved with root mean square (RMS) RSSI measurement error of less than 0:002dB on all tested devices, significantly outperforming the state-of-the-art result in the same field. The wireless channel fluctuations in indoor office static environments were measured using the proposed system, and results have shown that RSSI fluctuations smaller than 0:5dB can be detected with high reciprocity between opposite directions, which provides fine-grained RSSI data for PLKG. Through PLKG experiment based on the measurement data, a high KGR of 80bit/s can be achieved with an acceptable KDR of 0:05∼0:2, highlighting the critical role of precise and fine-grained RSSI measurements in enabling efficient PLKG.

title: Balance Field Electromagnetic Pipeline Crack Detection Method with Multiple Correlation Demodulation
authors: Wenxue Zheng, Jiayin Li, Zhenrong Pan, Yajing Li, Bin Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: Balanced field electromagnetic technique (BFET) is an effective method for in-line internal inspection of pipeline cracks. To address the issue of tilted lift-off noise from sensors during the inspection process, this paper proposes a multiple correlation demodulation method that uses frequency-correlated processed detection signals for phase correlation calculations. In this paper, the crack detection principle of balanced electromagnetic technology is studied. The electromagnetic propagation characteristics of the crack detection signal and tilt lift-off noise are analysed. The reasons for the difference between the two waveforms and the signal characteristics are clarified. A multi-correlation demodulation model is established. Simulation and experimental results show that the phase peak of tilted lift-off noise remains constant. When the correlation coefficient is 0.33, multiple correlation demodulation can effectively remove tilted lift-off noise while preserving the amplitude and phase information of the crack detection signal. The phase peak of the crack detection signal after multiple correlation demodulation increases approximately linearly with increasing crack depth and also increases linearly with crack angle. After multiple correlation demodulation, the peak of the induced voltage increases linearly with increasing crack depth, but first decreases and then increases with increasing crack angle. This characteristic provides a foundation for further quantification of crack parameters and serves as a reference for noise elimination and signal identification in electromagnetic non-destructive testing of pipeline cracks.

title: GSwap: Realistic Head Swapping With Dynamic Neural Gaussian Field
authors: Jingtao Zhou, Xuan Gao, Dongyu Liu, Junhui Hou, Yudong Guo, Juyong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
abstract: We present GSwap, a novel consistent and realistic video head-swapping system empowered by dynamic neural Gaussian portrait priors, which significantly advances the state of the art in face and head replacement. Unlike previous methods that rely primarily on 2D generative models or 3D Morphable Face Models (3DMM), our approach overcomes their inherent limitations, including poor 3D consistency, unnatural facial expressions, and restricted synthesis quality. Moreover, existing techniques struggle with full head-swapping tasks due to insufficient holistic head modeling and ineffective background blending, often resulting in visible artifacts and misalignments. To address these challenges, GSwap introduces an intrinsic 3D Gaussian feature field embedded within a full-body SMPL-X surface, effectively elevating 2D portrait videos into a dynamic neural Gaussian field. This innovation ensures high-fidelity, 3D-consistent portrait rendering while preserving natural head-torso relationships and seamless motion dynamics. To facilitate training, we adapt a pretrained 2D portrait generative model to the source head domain using only a few reference images, enabling efficient domain adaptation. Furthermore, we propose a neural re-rendering strategy that harmoniously integrates the synthesized foreground with the original background, eliminating blending artifacts and enhancing realism. Extensive experiments demonstrate that GSwap surpasses existing methods in multiple aspects, including visual quality, temporal coherence, identity preservation, and 3D consistency.
