title: Iterative Learning Model Predictive Control of Underwater Flexible Structure With Harmonic Actuation by Macrofiber Composites
authors: Zekai Wang, Junqiang Lou, Jiale Shen, Jiaxu Shen, Tehuan Chen, Yuguo Cui, Chao Xu, Yanding Wei
abstract: The precise trajectory tracking of underwater flexible structures driven by smart materials is essential for bionic underwater propulsion systems, thus attracting increasing attention. In this work, we introduce a compound controller that combines an iterative learning model predictive control (ILMPC) with a dynamic hysteresis compensator. This approach realizes effective trajectory tracking of an underwater flexible structure through harmonic actuation of macrofiber composites (MFC) across a relatively broad frequency range. We present a dynamic hysteresis model that includes a state space model and a modified Prandtl–Ishlinskii (MPI) model. Experimental results indicate that the proposed hysteresis model effectively captures the underwater dynamic hysteresis nonlinearity of the MFC-actuated structure. Then, we develop the ILMPC method, which integrates model predictive control and iterative learning to reject real-time hydrodynamic disturbances and model uncertainties. To generate effective input trajectories, we design a generalized objective function that allows for independent adjustment of the weighting factors for manipulated variables to time indexes and interactive predictive horizons. We also discuss the convergence and robustness of the ILMPC. Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed compound controller over a broad frequency range. These findings are meaningful for MFC-actuated flexible structures in applications of autonomous underwater vehicles, robotic fish, and other applications.

title: A Joint Pilot-Data Sparse Vector Transmission Framework for Short-Packet URLLC
authors: Xuewan Zhang, Keqi Wang
abstract: Sparse vector transmission (SVT) has garnered attention for short-packet communications due to its simple implementation and reliable transmission. However, its reliability in mobile scenarios remains an open challenge. To address this issue, a joint pilot-data SVT scheme tailored to the SVT block coding structure is proposed, in which both pilots and data are mapped into a unified sparse vector, and then forms a transmitted vector over an orthogonal frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM) symbol through random spreading. At the receiver, a joint iterative channel estimation and data detection algorithm is designed to progressively refine performance by selecting highly reliable decoded data as pseudo-pilots. Simulations indicate that the proposed SVT scheme outperforms both conventional separated pilot-data mapping and power-allocation-based superposition in block error rate (BLER) performance. Impressively, its first-iteration performance is already close to the converged performance of the power-allocation-based superposition scheme.

title: Multiagent Multitarget Search with Multi-Head Attention
authors: Huiqin Pei, Zilong Luo
abstract: In swarm drone applications, the problem of multitarget self-organizing search in unknown environments has demonstrated immense potential. In this problem, agents need to collaboratively search for multiple dynamic targets while only observing their immediate surroundings. However, the computational complexity and uncertainty in traditional multiagent search processes have been critical factors limiting their performance. Therefore, this article focuses on the multitarget search problem. First, a multiagent search environment including agents, targets, and obstacles is constructed. Subsequently, this article proposes a multiagent multitarget search method (MASOS) in complex obstacle environments. This method integrates the Actor–Critic reinforcement learning algorithm and multi-head attention for UAV swarm collaborative control, enabling agents to focus more on critical information such as other agents, targets, and obstacles during observation. It aims to enhance search, collaboration, and obstacle avoidance capabilities in multitarget self-organizing search tasks. Experimental results show that the MASOS method outperforms other commonly used multiagent reinforcement learning algorithms in terms of coordination strategies. In large-scale self-organizing search tasks, the capture success rate of the MASOS method is nearly 100%. Finally, By designing multitask scenario experiments, conducting comparative analyses combining multilayer perceptron and multi-head attention mechanism, and performing ablation studies on key modules, the effectiveness and interpretability of the MASOS method are verified from two aspects: Performance and decision-making logic.

title: FLET: Game-Theoretic Free-Riding Mitigation via Test Tasks in Federated Learning_supp1-3668944.pdf
authors: zhou Su
abstract: Federated learning (FL) is a widely studied framework for privacy-preserving collaborative training among multiple clients. However, real-world deployments reveal a persistent challenge: free-riders, i.e., participants who benefit from the system without contributing meaningful updates. If not properly addressed, free-riders can discourage honest contributors and ultimately impair both the fairness and efficiency of FL ecosystems. Existing defenses mainly rely on post-hoc per-client, per-round evaluation, leading to limited deterrence and high resource overhead, particularly in large-scale deployments. To tackle these problems, we propose FLET, a novel federated learning framework with test tasks. FLET introduces dedicated test tasks into the training process, blending them with real FL tasks while concealing their types from participants. These test tasks, generated from the reference datasets, serve as decoys that enable accurate detection of free-riding behavior. To enforce accountability, an economic penalty mechanism is employed, achieving both proactive (ex-ante) deterrence and reactive (expost) detection.We analyze the interactions between the server and participants through a free-riding suppression game model with asymmetric information (i.e., task type), and develop a strategic information disclosure scheme (i.e., revealing task requirements) to mislead attackers and proactively shape participant behavior. We characterize both pure-strategy and mixed-strategy perfect Bayesian Nash equilibria, and propose a lightweight strateg-ymaking algorithm that guides players toward equilibrium strategies under different conditions with modest overhead. Extensive experiments validate that FLET effectively suppresses free-riding and enhances the utility of both the server and participants. Our findings provide insights for designing cost-effective free-riding defenses in practical FL.

title: A Dual-Cycle Assimilation Approach for Rapid, High-Resolution Fusion of Model-Simulated and Satellite-Observed Land Surface Temperatures
authors: Zehua Huo, Xupeng Sun, Liang Li, Min He, Haiyi Yang, Jun Qin
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing

title: Improving Atomic Layer Deposition Process of Silicon Oxide (SiO₂) with single and double pulse processes
authors: Long Lei, Pauline Dill, Erik Schumann
publish: IEEE Transactions on Components, Packaging and Manufacturing Technology
abstract: Due to hardly known film properties, precursor savings and hardware modifications of the atomic layer deposition (ALD) equipment, the bis(diethylamino)silane (SiBDEA)/ozone-oxygen (O₃/O₂) silicon oxide (SiO₂) process requires re-optimization. Using design of experiments (DoE), the old double pulse (ODP) process was transformed into a new single pulse (NSP) process for SiO₂ deposition. This NSP process achieves film properties comparable to the ODP process, while reducing precursor consumption by 50%. However, unlike literature reports [1], no ALD window was observed across the temperature range of 150 °C–300 °C. The coverage ability on high aspect ratio (HAR) structures (1:32) was also demonstrated. It was determined that at least 50 cycles are necessary for effective protection using the NSP process, which corresponds to a thickness of approximately 4.5 nm. Keeping the precursor saving from NSP process, while enhancing film properties, we investigate another new double pulse (NDP) process. The measurements indicate that the NDP process improves film properties like homogeneity and optical performance.

title: A Novel Coupling Suppression Method Based on D-CM Path Synergy for Dual-Band Shared-Aperture Base Station Antenna Design
authors: Yue-Hui Gao, Xue-Song Yang, Wen-Hui Shi, Bing-Zhong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A novel coupling suppression method, namely Differential-Common Mode Path Synergy (D-CMPS) technique, is proposed for shared-aperture dual-band base station antennas. The D-CMPS method employs two key strategies: Elimination of Reverse-Flow Current Radiation and Mitigation of In-Phase Current Radiation. The strategies guide high-band (HB) induced currents into precisely engineered differential mode (DM) and common mode (CM) paths that are deliberately integrated within the low-band (LB) antenna structure. This approach provides an innovative perspective for effectively mitigating cross-band coupling. Specifically, the HB-induced currents generated by illumination from the HB antenna are strategically redirected and confined within the DM and CM paths. Through the synergistic interaction of the DM and CM pathways, coupling from the LB antenna onto the HB antenna is significantly reduced, ensuring that the HB antenna’s radiation patterns remain undistorted. Meanwhile, the additional DM and CM paths have negligible impact on the LB antenna’s inherent performance. To experimentally validate this concept, a shared-aperture dual-band antenna array incorporating the Differential-Common Mode Path (D-CMP) structure was designed, fabricated, and measured. The results demonstrate that, compared to existing the coupling-suppression techniques, D-CMPS approach not only achieves robust suppression of cross-band coupling but also streamlines the design and fabrication process, thereby reducing manufacturing costs.

title: Conformal Metasurface Radomes for Beamshaping
authors: Christopher J. M. Barker, Ashwin K. Iyer
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper introduces a design method for creating conformal single-layer passive metasurfaces (MTSs) that tailor the radiation pattern of an enclosed antenna array by lining the inside wall of an arbitrarily shaped radome structure. An example transformation is performed where the radiation pattern of a dipole array, operating in the Canadian 5G New Radio (NR) n78 band (3.3 GHz - 3.8 GHz) with a broadside beam with 108° half-power beam width (HPBW) in the azimuth, is transformed into a 40° HPBW pointed 60° off broadside through the insertion of the proposed MTS. Simulation results for the MTS radome nearly achieve the desired radiation pattern criteria at the design frequency of 3.55 GHz. The addition of the MTS decreases the system efficiency from 88.9% down to 81.9%, while the change in pattern resulted in an increase in the peak realized gain by 2.6 dB at the design frequency of 3.55 GHz. Simulation results are confirmed with an experiment. The MTS radome exhibits a 10 dB feed bandwidth of 9.75% and a 3 dB gain bandwidth of 13.9% in simulation. Measurement results demonstrated better feed and gain bandwidth, exceeding the 14% bandwidth covered by the n78 band.

title: Benefits of a ComSoc Membership
authors: N/A

title: Learning-based Channel Estimation and Beamforming Framework for Battery-Free Backscatter Communications
authors: Jaemin Shin, Yusung Kim
publish: IEEE Transactions on Green Communications and Networking
abstract: Channel estimation in backscatter communication (BackCom) systems remains a significant challenge due to the battery-free device nature, which often fail to respond to traditional pilot signals. While prior works have predominantly relied on simulation-based evaluations with ideal reception assumptions, such methods fall short in practical scenarios where pilot reachability and BackCom devices’ response cannot be guaranteed. In this work, we present the fully implemented and evaluated channel estimation framework for battery-free BackCom systems using a real-world testbed. We introduce Directional Random Phase Perturbation, a novel pilot beamforming technique that combines directional scanning with randomized phase perturbation to induce backscatter responses even in environments where pilot signal reachability is uncertain. We further propose a neural channel estimation model based on self-attention. The model is carefully designed to integrate both pilot signal features and Least Square-based (LS) initial estimates as inputs and to explicitly decompose the channel into transmit and receive components for improved estimation accuracy. In addition, we introduce task-specific data augmentation methods, including random phase shifting and antenna pseudo-flipping, to enhance the model under limited training data. Evaluated on the real testbed, our framework improves beamforming gain by up to 73.8% and achieves 28.8% higher data transmission success rates compared to conventional methods.

title: Guaranteed Global Optimal Path Planning Through Convex Segmentation and Homotopy guidance_supp2-3669766.mp4
authors: Xiangrong Xu
abstract: Trajectory planning and optimization in three dimensional space remains fundamental challenges in high dimensional motion planning. This paper presents ConvexSegTopoChainOpt (Csto), a trajectory optimization framework that integrates convex decomposition and topological guidance. The proposed method partitions obstacle space into a sequence of connected, collision-free convex polyhedral via geometric decomposition. Homotopy vectors are introduced to enforce topological consistency, ensuring that the trajectory traverses a continuous chain of convex regions. A global convex optimization problem is formulated using an auxiliary variable relaxation strategy, enabling efficient computation of the optimal trajectory. Csto has been implemented and validated on the Kinova Jaco2 robotic platform, demonstrating its effectiveness in physically realistic experimental settings.

title: An invariance principle-based concentration result for large-scale stochastic pairwise interaction network systems
authors: Giacomo Como, Fabio Fagnani, Sandro Zampieri
abstract: We study stochastic pairwise interaction network systems whereby a finite population of agents, identified with the nodes of a (directed) graph, update their states in response to both individual mutations and pairwise interactions with their neighbors. The considered class of systems includes the main epidemic models —such as the SIS, SIR, and SIRS models—, certain social dynamics models —such as the voter and antivoter models—, as well as evolutionary dynamics on graphs. Since these stochastic systems fall into the class of finite-state Markov chains, they always admit stationary distributions. We analyze the asymptotic behavior of the stationary distributions of stochastic pairwise interaction network systems in the limit as the population size grows large, while the interaction network maintains certain mixing properties. Our approach relies on the use of Lyapunov-type functions to obtain concentration results on these stationary distributions. Notably, our results are not limited to fully mixed population models, as they do apply to a much broader spectrum of interaction network structures, including, e.g., Erd¨ os-R´ enyi random graphs.

title: Predicting Distribution Transformer Vulnerability to Non-Compliance: An Interpretable XGBoost Framework for Power Quality Management with Real-World Metering Data
authors: Abhijit Palakurthy, Rishi Malyala, Jayashri Ravishankar
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper introduces a machine learning framework for proactively predicting distribution transformer vulnerability to power quality non-compliance (voltage and THD excursions) using real-world Australian smart meter data of over 1000 transformers. The challenges of unstructured NEM12 data, severe class imbalance, and generalizability are addressed. The methodology involves meticulous data processing, comprehensive temporal feature engineering, and a robust GroupKFold timeseries split to prevent data leakage. An XGBoost model, optimized via RandomizedSearchCV, strategically handles class imbalance. Results demonstrate strong discriminatory ability with an ROC AUC of 0.79 on unseen data, effectively ranking vulnerable transformers. Feature importance highlights THD and voltage fluctuations as key predictors, validating generalizability by learning physical patterns, not asset identities. The framework provides insights into precision-recall trade-offs in rare event prediction, crucial for operational deployment. This offers actionable insights for proactive grid management, enhancing power quality reliability and network resilience.

title: Automated Code Review and Quality Assurance System Using Static Analysis and Complexity Evaluation in Flask-Based Web Application
authors: Javvaji Venkatarao, Muthyala Harshitha, Javvaji Manvitha, Khushbu Kumari, Gooty Deekshitha
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Development projects often face difficulties due to inconsistent code quality, which leads to increased technical debt and ineffective manual reviews. These issues make software difficult to maintain and reduce its reliability. This paper presents the Automated Code Review and Quality Assurance System, which examines coding style and performs static analysis, linting, and complexity evaluation. Flask is used as the framework to develop the web application. Developers can submit their source code and receive immediate feedback on potential issues. Radon is used to evaluate cyclomatic complexity, while Pylint performs style and error checking for Python files. The system is modular and can be extended to support other programming languages. For instance, tools such as Checkstyle or PMD can be used for Java. The application also provides linting warnings and categorizes functions based on their complexity. This step assists developers in identifying areas of the codebase that require modification. The design principles of the proposed real-time system emphasize modularity, scalability, and improved effectiveness compared to manual code review. The aim of this paper is to enhance software maintainability and reduce bugs by automating code quality checks and providing useful feedback. The roadmap for future enhancements includes support for multiple programming languages, integration with CI/CD pipelines, and the development of a visual dashboard with code fitness statistics. Experimental evaluation indicates that the proposed system provides faster and more consistent code quality assessment than traditional manual review approaches.

title: SSBleed: Non-Speculative Side-Channel Attacks via Speculative Store Bypass on Armv9 CPUs
authors: Chang Liu, Hongpei Zheng, Xin Zhang, Dapeng Ju, Dongsheng Wang, Yinqian Zhang, Trevor E. Carlson
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Modern CPUs employ Speculative Store Bypass (SSB) to reduce load latency and improve performance. In response to transient attacks such as Spectre, CPU vendors have also introduced mitigations to prevent incorrect speculation from leaking data. In this work, we show that the SSB on Armv9 CPUs introduces a previously unexplored form of non-speculative data leakage. Specifically, we find that the SSB on Armv9 performance cores is governed by an undocumented predictor. Through reverse engineering, we uncover the design of this predictor and show that it lacks isolation across security domains. Furthermore, existing mitigations such as SSBS are insufficient to prevent leaks. Based on this, we present SSBleed, the first non-speculative side-channel attack via SSB on Armv9 CPUs. We validate the practicality of SSBleed through five case studies, including crossprocess RSA signature and key generation attacks on the latest version of MbedTLS and WolfSSL, interrupt detection, and improved data transmission in two transient attacks. Finally, we propose a flush-based mitigation through a kernel patch, which incurs an average performance overhead of 0.46 %.

title: DP-HLS: A High-Level Synthesis Framework for Accelerating Dynamic Programming Algorithms in Bioinformatics
authors: Anshu Gupta, Yingqi Cao, Jason Liang, Yatish Turakhia
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Dynamic programming (DP) is a widely used algorithmic paradigm, particularly in bioinformatics, finding applications in a wide spectrum of tasks, including read assembly, homology search, gene annotation, basecalling, and phylogenetic inference. Due to its computationally intensive nature, many ASIC- and FPGA-based accelerators have been proposed in recent years to accelerate specific tasks. However, DP algorithms in bioinformatics can vary considerably, and most existing solutions are customized for a single application, representing just one design point within the broader DP space. These implementations typically rely on low-level hardware description languages (HDLs), often requiring months of manual implementation effort. This paper introduces DP-HLS, a novel framework based on High-Level Synthesis (HLS) that simplifies and accelerates the development of a vast set of bioinformatically relevant 2-D DP algorithms in hardware. DP-HLS achieves this by introducing a new abstraction layer that decouples the front-end specification from predefined HLS-based back-end optimizations, enabling users to efficiently develop new 2-D DP kernels in C++ and deploy them on FPGAs without needing any expertise in hardware design or HLS. In our experience, DP-HLS significantly reduced the development time of new kernels (months to days) and produced designs with comparable resource utilization to open-source hand-coded HDL-based implementations and performance within <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7. 7 - 1 6. 8 \%}$</tex> margin. DP-HLS is compatible with AWS<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">®</sup> EC2 F1 FPGA instances. To showcase its versatility, we implemented 15 diverse 2-D DP kernels using the DP-HLS framework, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.38-41 \times$</tex> improved cost-efficiency over state-of-the-art GPU and CPU baselines and providing the first open-source FPGA implementation for several of them. The DP-HLS codebase is available freely under the MIT license at https://github.com/TurakhiaLab/DP-HLS.

title: GustavSNN: Unleashing the Power of Gustavson's Algorithm on SNN Acceleration with Column-Parallel Tick-Batch Dataflow
authors: Sangwoo Hwang, Donghun Lee, Jahyun Koo, Jaeha Kung
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Spiking neural networks (SNNs) require sequential computation over long timesteps, introducing substantial memory and energy overheads due to frequent updates of neuron membrane potentials. Previous SNN accelerators address this by employing tick-batch techniques, which process all timesteps within a layer before moving on to the next. However, existing approaches rely on neuron-centric scheduling, limiting their ability to exploit temporal sparsity. In this work, we propose a novel scheduling approach along with its hardware architecture for Gustavson product (GP)-based SNN acceleration. We introduce a column-parallel tick-batch (CPTB) dataflow that partitions the spike matrix into multiple submatrices and processes each submatrix of a timestep in parallel while maintaining tickbatch semantics. To support this, we present the first GP-based SNN accelerator, named GustavSNN, which avoids accessing the global membrane potential memory by updating neuron states directly in local registers. In addition, we propose a non-zero row vector (NRV) spike format that enables fine-grained skipping of inactive spike rows. As a result, our proposed architecture achieves up to 11.8× higher energy efficiency (GOPS/W) than naïve GP-based accelerator and 1.43× higher energy efficiency compared to state-of-the-art SNN accelerators.

title: AccelFlow: Orchestrating an On-Package Ensemble of Fine-Grained Accelerators for Microservices
authors: Jovan Stojkovic, Abraham Farrell, Zhangxiaowen Gong, Christopher J. Hughes, Josep Torrellas
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Microservices suffer from the execution of auxiliary operations known as datacenter tax, such as RPC and TCP processing, and data (de)serialization, (de)encryption, and (de)compression. To minimize this tax, multiple hardware accelerators have been proposed. However, it is unclear how these accelerators should be orchestrated. Past work has focused only on orchestrating accelerators in coarse-grained environments with monolithic applications. In this paper, we characterize the needs of orchestrating an ensemble of on-package accelerators in microservice environments. We observe that orchestration frameworks need to be highly dynamic and nimble. The basic operations to be accelerated are fine grained, potentially taking only tens of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mu \mathrm{s}$</tex>. Moreover, the sequence of accelerators to use is often affected by “branch conditions” whose real-time resolution determines the set of subsequent accelerators needed. To address these challenges, we present AccelFlow, the first orchestration framework for onpackage accelerators of microservices. In AccelFlow, CPU cores build software structures called Traces that contain sequences of accelerators to call. A core enqueues a trace in an accelerator in user mode and, from then on, the accelerators in the trace execute in sequence without CPU involvement. A trace can include branch conditions whose outcomes determine the trace control flow. Compared to state-of-the-art accelerator orchestrators, AccelFlow on average reduces P99 tail latency by 70 %, reduces average latency by 38 %, and increases throughput by 120 %.

title: Photovoltaic Based Standalone EV Charging System
authors: Hiren Jariwala, Nilesh Shah
publish: 2025 International Conference on Power Electronics and Energy (ICPEE)
abstract: As electric vehicles become more prevalent, the importance of developing efficient and sustainable charging mechanisms continues to increase. This paper presents a standalone photovoltaic (PV)-based EV battery charging system integrated with battery energy storage system (BESS) to ensure a continuous and stable power supply. In the proposed configuration, the PV system operates as the primary energy source, while surplus energy generated during peak solar irradiance is stored in the BESS for later use. During periods of low or no solar availability, such as adverse weather conditions or nighttime, the stored energy supports uninterrupted EV charging. A control strategy is implemented to regulate the charging and discharging processes. The proposed system not only enhances energy utilization efficiency but also reduces dependency on the conventional power grid, thereby contributing to the development of an environmentally friendly EV charging infrastructure. MATLAB/Simulink is employed for modeling and analysis of the standalone PV-powered EV charging system. The performance and effectiveness of the system under varying environmental conditions are evaluated through simulations.

title: Design of a Probabilistic Link Adaptation Model for Fifth Generation Millimeter-Wave Networks Using Adaptive Modulation and Coding and Channel Prediction to Minimize HARQ Retransmissions
authors: Prabhu R, Rangayya
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The advancement of Fifth Generation millimeter-wave networks necessitates a reliable link adaptation model that is suitable to maintain optimal data rates despite fluctuating channel conditions. The conventional link adaptation model relies on average Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio measurements that suffer from delayed and inaccurate modulation as well as coding selection of unpredictable channel variations, user mobility, and feedback latency. These models reject the probabilistic nature of fading and interference, resulting in suboptimal throughput, high packet error rates, and unstable performance in dynamic propagation environments. These limitations are addressed by the proposed Probabilistic Link Adaptation with Channel Prediction model by merging channel forecasting, reliability-aware throughput estimation, and adaptive recursive learning. It employs a probabilistic autoregressive channel model that predicts future channel coefficients, captures the mean and uncertainty of time-varying links. Further, the Gaussian-based Signal-to-Interference-plus-Noise Ratio distribution estimates packet error probability for each Modulation and Coding Scheme. The reliability-constrained optimization rule applies to attain an optimal Modulation and Coding Scheme that improves throughput. The experimental validation is undertaken by various measures and attains a higher prediction accuracy of 97.4 % and throughput of 99.8 Mbps, respectively.

title: Optimization of Energy Storage Systems Congestion Management Using Multi Agent Reinforcement Learning
authors: Hojun Lee, JiWoong Choi, Don Hur, Seung Pil Moon, Minhan Yoon
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel method for optimizing the scheduling of Energy Storage Systems (ESSs) in power systems with high penetration of renewable energy and significant transmission congestion. The method employs Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) to train ESS agents that can cooperate or compete to minimize total system operating cost while avoiding transmission congestion under both <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}-0$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{N}-1$</tex> conditions. The ESS agents are modeled as lithium-ion batteries, with actions constrained by transmission thermal margins derived from Power Transfer Distribution Factors (PTDFs) and Line Outage Distribution Factors (LODFs). The proposed method is applied to the IEEE 30-bus test system with two ESS units and one photovoltaic (PV) generator. Simulation results demonstrate that MARL-based ESS scheduling achieves more economical and secure system operation compared to the baseline case without ESSs.

title: GenPairX: A Hardware-Algorithm Co-Designed Accelerator for Paired-End Read Mapping
authors: Julien Eudine, Chu Li, Zhuo Cheng, Renzo Andri, Can Firtina, Mohammad Sadrosadati, Nika Mansouri Ghiasi, Konstantina Koliogeorgi, Anirban Nag, Arash Tavakkol, Haiyu Mao, Onur Mutlu, Shai Bergman, Ji Zhang
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: Genome sequencing has become a central focus in computational biology due to its critical role in applications such as personalized medicine, disease outbreak tracking, and evolutionary research. A genome study typically begins with sequencing, which produces millions to billions of short DNA fragments known as reads. Extracting meaningful biological insights from these reads requires a computationally intensive step called read mapping, where each read is aligned to a reference genome. Read mapping for short reads comes in two forms: single-end and paired-end, with the latter being more prevalent due to its higher accuracy and support for advanced analysis. Read mapping remains a major performance bottleneck in genome analysis as a result of the extensive use of computationally intensive dynamic programming. Prior efforts have attempted to mitigate this cost by employing filters to identify and potentially discard computationally expensive matches and leveraging hardware accelerators to speed up the computations. While partially effective, these approaches have limitations. In particular, existing filters are often ineffective for paired-end reads, as they evaluate each read independently and exhibit relatively low filtering ratios. In this work, we propose GenPairX, a hardware-algorithm codesigned accelerator that efficiently minimizes the computational load of paired-end read mapping while enhancing the throughput of memory-intensive operations. GenPairX introduces: (1) a novel filtering algorithm that jointly considers both reads in a pair to improve filtering effectiveness, and a lightweight alignment algorithm to replace most of the computationally expensive dynamic programming operations, and (2) two specialized hardware mechanisms to support the proposed algorithms. The proposed hardware addresses the high memory bandwidth demands of the read filtering process via orchestration of memory accesses over high-bandwidth memory channels, and accelerates the alignment of candidate reads via simple vectorized logical XOR operators. Our evaluations show that GenPairX delivers substantial performance improvements over state-of-the-art solutions, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1575 \times$</tex> and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.43 \times$</tex> higher throughput per watt compared to leading CPU-based and accelerator-based read mappers, respectively, all without compromising accuracy.

title: AI in Public Procurement: Case Study of tender Evaluation AI-assisted tender evaluation framework
authors: Ravi Roshan, Mehebub Alam
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: This paper presents an AI-assisted tender evaluation framework integrated with ChatGPT API, which is a large language model, to streamline the bid/tender evaluation workflow. The proposed system automates key tasks involved in a public procurement bid evaluation system such as reading of bidder’s documents using Optical Character Recognition (OCR), extraction of data related to qualification criteria from bidders document, calculation of work experience, extraction and analysis of financial data from bid documents and word to word clause-wise compliance. The framework was tested on some archived tenders issued by a central public utility, with eligibility criteria including experience in similar assignments, financial capability thresholds, and statutory compliance declarations.Through a developed application all extracted data were sent to AI language model with related prompts to interpret scanned bid submissions and produce structured evaluation sheets aligned with qualification requirements. The AI-assisted system achieved over 90% accuracy data extract and clause matching compared to manual review. It also reduced the average bid evaluation time by more than 60%. The output included references of evaluation to enable source traceability, enhance audit-readiness and develop confidence in reviewer. The framework follows human-in-the-loop approach. It ensures all AI-generated outputs undergo validation. The system is modular based which include support for regional languages, domain-specific fine-tuning, and blockchain integration for audit trails. This study has a practical application of generative AI in public procurement and offers measurable improvements in terms of transparency, efficiency, and evaluation process. The findings indicate that with appropriate safeguards and regulatory alignment, AI can play a transformative role in modernizing public procurement workflows.

title: Predicting COVID-19 Cases with Dimensionality Reduction and Synthetic Data Generation: A TVAE and Logistic Regression Approach
authors: B Sarada, Saikonda Vaishnavi
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Respiratory illnesses such as COVID-19 and Pneumonia have a highly significant impact on global health. Timely and accurate classification of chest X-ray images can aid in early diagnosis of such diseases, which is effective in controlling widespread global pandemics. This research paper proposes a deep learning-assisted pipeline where grayscale X-ray images from diseases such as COVID-19, Pneumonia, along with Normal patients are resized, flattened, and combined to form a tabular dataset. Further on, due to the high dimensionality of image data, Principal Component Analysis (PCA) has been applied in order to reduce complexity. The reduced features are up sampled using TVAE(Tabular Variational Autoencoder) and then classified using logistic regression, are used to predict disease categories with an accuracy of 85%. Accuracy, Precision, Recall and the F1 score performance matrix have been measured to evaluate the proposed model's performance. SHAP a explainable ai has been used to find out how many components are contributing for the prediction of each label. This pipeline aims to offer an interpretable and efficient solution for early diagnoses in healthcare systems.

title: Multi-Temporal Remote Sensing Analysis for Deforestation Detection Using a Siamese CNN-Transformer Hybrid Network
authors: Selvin Retna Raj Thavasimuthu, M. Arul Prasanna, Ramya S, S. Balamurugan, J. Relin Francis Raj, M. Suganthi
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: Deforestation detection remains a critical engineering challenge due to inconsistent temporal observations, frequent cloud interference, and natural vegetation variability within satellite imagery. Current monitoring systems often rely on limited temporal snapshots, restricting the ability to recognize subtle canopy changes and fragmented forest disturbances. These constraints reduce reliability, hinder operational readiness, and delay actionable environmental decision-making. To address these limitations, a hybrid deep-learning framework is developed using a Siamese-based spatial encoder combined with a temporal attention mechanism capable of interpreting extended multi-temporal sequences. This integrated design strengthens spatial-temporal feature representation, enabling clear differentiation between seasonal fluctuations and true forest degradation. Multi-scale decoding enhances structural boundary recovery, facilitating accurate detection of abrupt clearing as well as small-area degradation. The proposed system produces consistent outputs across diverse ecological conditions, increases robustness against cloud-related data gaps, and improves interpretability through attention-based feature highlighting. Engineering relevance is emphasized through automated processing pipelines, scalable computation, and efficient sequence modeling suitable for large-area remote-sensing applications. The framework supports reliable forest disturbance mapping required for environmental assessment, conservation planning, and long-term land-use monitoring. The resulting model contributes to advanced sensing solutions aimed at improving ecological data quality, enhancing remote-sensing analytics, and supporting sustainable resource governance across broad geographic regions.

title: A Precise Commutation Firing Time Adjustment Method in HVDC Considering Wideband Dynamic Distortions
authors: Hongjian Sun, Zongshuai Jin, Yi Jiang, Caihai Zou, Xinhao Wang, Jiacheng Ruan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Continuous commutation failure poses a serious threat to the safe and stable operation of conventional HVDC systems. The integration of a large number of power electronic devices has exposed the receiving-end grid to severe challenges such as reduced system inertia and wideband distortions. Postdisturbance commutation voltage exhibits dynamic characteristics, further exacerbating the commutation process and risking continuous commutation failures. Traditional commutation failure mitigation methods, which rely on advanced firing, exhibit significant errors in scenarios with wideband dynamic distortions of commutation voltage and fail to meet the requirements for preventing continuous commutation failures. To address this issue, this paper proposes a firing time control algorithm that adapts to the amplitude-frequency dynamic distortions of commutation voltage after large disturbances. Firstly, a non-parametric model of commutation voltage under amplitude-frequency dynamic scenarios is established. Then, based on the time-area theory of commutation voltage, an analytical expression for commutation area that accounts for amplitude-frequency dynamics is presented. Subsequently, a firing time adjustment method is proposed to meet the limit of available turn-off time of the converter valve, thereby avoiding continuous commutation failures. Finally, numerical simulations demonstrate that the proposed method can effectively prevent continuous commutation failures after large disturbances under various wideband dynamic distortion scenarios in the power grid, proving the accuracy of the method and the effectiveness of the prevention measures.

title: A Blockchain–IoT–Edge Framework for Real-Time Patient Data Privacy and Management
authors: Kimmi Kumari, M Mrunalini
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: The rapid growth of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in healthcare presents significant issues related to real-time data privacy, latency, and safe data management. Current centralised solutions face challenges related to bandwidth limitations latency in response times, and accessibility to security breaches. This research presents a Blockchain–IoT–Edge integrated system aimed at facilitating secure, real-time processing and management of patient data. The solution utilises IoT sensors for ongoing data acquisition, edge nodes for localised analysis, and blockchain smart contracts for safe and transparent data management. The suggested method utilises AES encryption, role-based access control, and edge-based anomaly detection to reduce central dependencies and improve trust. Experimental results indicate that the system attains 96% accuracy, 95% precision, 97% recall, and an F1-score of 96%, substantially improving baseline models by more than 10% in all metrics. This framework offers a scalable, regulation-compliant architecture suitable for contemporary healthcare settings that need both efficiency and privacy. The findings validate the practical feasibility of decentralised health data systems in improving clinical decision-making and security.

title: Selective Auto-Reclosing for Three Terminal Mixed Transmission Lines Based on Line Differential Measurements
authors: Neethu George, O.D. Naidu, Ashok Kumar Pradhan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy has led to a rise in three terminal mixed line configurations combining overhead lines and underground cables. Reliable selective auto-reclosing on these lines is essential for system reliability and operational continuity, while minimizing unnecessary breaker operations and equipment stress. This paper proposes a selective auto-reclosing scheme for three terminal single circuit and double circuit mixed lines using local voltage and current measurements, along with remote current measurements. Local voltage is accessible in today's digital substations while current measurements from all terminals are already being communicated as part of line differential protection. Unlike existing approaches requiring voltage and current data from all terminals, the proposed scheme enables seamless retrofit into existing line differential IEDs without the need for additional communication cards for remote voltage acquisition. For double circuit configuration, the scheme does not need any measurements from the healthy parallel circuit. Instead, healthy parallel circuit currents are estimated using the limited measurements from the faulted circuit and used for accurate mutual coupling compensation. This offers significant advantages over existing methods that require full measurements from the healthy parallel circuit too, often practically unavailable especially when it is open and grounded. Verification through simulation tests, comparison with existing method and validation using field event demonstrate reliability and practical applicability of the proposed scheme.

title: Adaptive Propagation Mechanism in Deep Graph Neural Networks for Intelligent Retrieval in E-commerce and News Web Platforms
authors: Ajay Kumar Miryala, Vemula Anvesh, M Narayanan
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The growth of online platforms has led to the growth of unstructured information, which demands the application of smart systems to identify the contents effectively and efficiently. The absence of the contextuality and flexibility in the conventional search engines stimulates the development of smarter solutions. This study breaks these limitations by proposing the AP-DGNN-HSO model, which should enhance the ability of intelligent search in news and e-commerce websites. It aims at processing complex (textual and behavioral) data using an integrated pipeline. The initial data sources are the RetailRocket (e-commerce) data and the Gossipcop (news) data. They were scaled by Min-Max normalization to make the datasets similar, feature extraction was conducted with the help of the Discrete Orthogonal S-Transform (DOST) in order to grasp significant time and semantic content of the content. These aspects were fed into the Showcase pipeline by the Adaptive Propagation Deep Graph Neural Network (AP-DGNN) that transferred the corresponding data between graphs that are correctly structured. The Hyperbolic Sine Optimizer (HSO) fine-tuned the weights of the model for faster convergence. Results showed good performance able to achieve 99.4% accuracy on RetailRocket, and 99.3% accuracy on Gossipcop, with very low error rates of 0.6% and 0.7% respectively. This systematic procedure represents the mission of demonstrated robust, scalable smart search effectiveness across multiple platforms.

title: Exploration of Grid Integrated PV Inverter Using Ant Colony Optimization
authors: V. Narasimhulu, Shaik Ayesha Anjum
publish: 2026 7th International Conference on Mobile Computing and Sustainable Informatics (ICMCSI)
abstract: The increasing penetration of photovoltaic (PV) systems into power grids has intensified the need for efficient, reliable, and intelligent control strategies for grid-integrated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{P V}$</tex> inverters. Grid-connected PV inverters play a critical role in ensuring maximum power extraction, power quality improvement, voltage regulation, and stable interaction with the utility grid under varying environmental and load conditions. This paper presents an exploration of a grid-integrated PV inverter optimized using Ant Colony Optimization (ACO), a nature-inspired metaheuristic algorithm known for its robustness in solving complex, nonlinear optimization problems. The proposed approach applies ACO to optimize key inverter control parameters, enabling enhanced maximum power point tracking (MPPT), reduced harmonic distortion, improved dynamic response, and better grid synchronization. The study models a grid-connected PV system comprising a PV array, DC-DC converter, inverter, and grid interface, where ACO is employed to fine-tune control variables such as switching angles, modulation indices, and controller gains. Simulation-based analysis is conducted under diverse operating conditions, including irradiance fluctuations, temperature variations, and grid disturbances, to evaluate system performance. The results demonstrate that the ACO-optimized inverter achieves faster convergence to optimal operating points, improved power conversion efficiency, and lower total harmonic distortion compared to conventional control and optimization techniques. Additionally, the proposed method enhances system stability and ensures compliance with grid codes related to power quality and reactive power support.

title: Channel Estimation in Cross-Frame OTFS System With Ultra-High Mobility
authors: Zhenyu Zhang, Qianli Wang, Gang Liu, Pingzhi Fan, FeiFei Gao
abstract: In the upcoming next-generation wireless communication systems, high-mobility support serves as an essential part. OTFS (Orthogonal Time Frequency Space) modulation is regarded as a candidate waveform for 6G systems for its robustness in high mobility scenarios. However, its performance degrades when Doppler spreads beyond the subcarrier spacing with ultra-high speed, a condition referred to as the out-of-range Doppler (OD) channel. We analyze the input-output relationship under OD channels and reveal that the Delay-Doppler (DD) domain received signal is a superposition of the transmitted signal with an additional phase term. Based on this insight, we design a cross-frame OTFS framework for channel estimation, utilizing coprime subcarrier configurations to observe varying responses across subframes, and the out-of-range parameters are resolved using the Chinese Remainder Theorem (CRT). Furthermore, an effective channel estimation method is developed to handle multipath scenarios by decomposing subframes and matching response taps using fractional and amplitude information, which transforms the OD channel estimation problem into multiple single-domain problems by processing DD domain signals in layers. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in estimating OD channels, outperforming existing approaches that fail under such conditions.

title: Report on the Second Workshop on Simulations for Information Access (Sim4IA 2025) at SIGIR 2025
authors: Philipp Schaer, Christin Katharina Kreutz, Krisztian Balog, Timo Breuer, Andreas Kruff, Mohammad Aliannejadi, Christine Bauer, Nolwenn Bernard, Nicola Ferro, Marcel Gohsen, Nurul Lubis, Saber Zerhoudi

title: Enhancing Consumer Experience and Purchase Decisions through AI-Based Chatbot Systems in eCommerce
authors: Monika Jangra, Gaurav Jangra, Kamal, Rishabh Attri, Naresh Anguralia
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Over the last few years, the growth of the eCommerce platform has brought with it a growing need to introduce the concept of AI-based chatbot systems as one of the most critical tools of supporting and facilitating consumer interaction. In the current research, the aim is to examine the role of AI-based chatbot systems on the experiences and buying behaviors of consumers in Amazon and Flipkart. The data was obtained using a structured questionnaire that contained questions to be asked to 400 respondents and resulted to selected only 320 observations after removing outliers. To measure perceptions, 5-point Likert scale was used, and correlates were measured with the help of regression analysis. Findings indicate that AI chatbots systems have an influence of significant magnitude on the customer shopping behavior and buying decisions. The results help eCommerce sites and advertisers on the implementation of AI to motivate better customer interaction and conversion.

title: PIMphony: Overcoming Bandwidth and Capacity Inefficiency in PIM-Based Long-Context LLM Inference System
authors: Hyucksung Kwon, Kyungmo Koo, Janghyeon Kim, Woongkyu Lee, Minjae Lee, Gyeonggeun Jung, Hyungdeok Lee, Yousub Jung, Jaehan Park, Yosub Song, Byeongsu Yang, Haerang Choi, Guhyun Kim, Jongsoon Won, Woojae Shin, Changhyun Kim, Gyeongcheol Shin, Yongkee Kwon, Ilkon Kim, Euicheol Lim, John Kim, Jungwook Choi
publish: 2026 IEEE International Symposium on High Performance Computer Architecture (HPCA)
abstract: The expansion of long-context Large Language Models (LLMs) creates significant memory system challenges. While Processing-in-Memory (PIM) is a promising accelerator, we identify that it suffers from critical inefficiencies when scaled to long contexts: severe channel underutilization, performancelimiting I/O bottlenecks, and massive memory waste from static KV cache management. In this work, we propose PIMphony, a PIM orchestrator that systematically resolves these issues with three co-designed techniques. First, Token-Centric PIM Partitioning (TCP) ensures high channel utilization regardless of batch size. Second, Dynamic PIM Command Scheduling (DCS) mitigates the I/O bottleneck by overlapping data movement and computation. Finally, a Dynamic PIM Access (DPA) controller enables dynamic memory management to eliminate static memory waste. Implemented via an MLIR-based compiler and evaluated on a cycle-accurate simulator, PIMphony significantly improves throughput for long-context LLM inference (up to 72B parameters and 1M context length). Our evaluations show performance boosts of up to 11.3× on PIM-only systems and 8.4× on xPU+PIM systems, enabling more efficient deployment of LLMs in real-world long-context applications.

title: Optimal Charging Control for Electric Vehicle Fleet Incorporating Energy Storage System Using Major-Minor Mean Field Game Theory
authors: Jehad Hedel, Ram K. Saket, Nga Nguyen
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Refining charging mechanisms for a fleet of electric vehicles (EVs) while considering the role of energy storage systems in optimizing energy distribution is a complex task. Therefore, this paper develops an innovative approach that integrates machine learning with major-minor mean field game theory to address this concern. Major-minor mean field game theory facilitates the decision-making process for a large number of EVs by adopting their charging strategies based on the aggregate probability distribution and the influence of major players in the system, such as batteries in energy storage systems. Since the key to finding the optimal charging protocols for EV fleet is to maximize efficiency and cost savings, it requires solving nonlinear partial differential equations (PDEs) such as Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman (HJB) and Fokker-Planck-Kolmogorov (FPK). However, solving these equations analytically is very complicated. Consequently, numerical methods should be used to find the approximate solution. The Reinforcement learning actor-critic approach is used to coordinate the interactions between the EV fleet, energy storage systems, and the grid. Heterogeneity is considered through the major players, who are evaluated based on different charging efficiencies, power ratings, and energy capacities. In contrast, EVs are assumed to be homogeneous. The proposed approach aims to capture the influence of energy storage systems on electricity prices and the charging behaviors of EVs, and further evaluates the resulting optimal control rates. Additionally, Bayesian hyperparameter optimization is employed to enhance model performance during the training phase. A numerical simulation will be implemented to verify the proposed approach.

title: Multi-Scale Temporal Fusion with Cyclic-Aware Transformer for Electricity Load Forecasting
authors: Lu Zhou, Yu Liu, Ziyu Qu, Yuchun Gong, Chaochuang Wang, Tianshun Lu
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: Accurate load forecasting across heterogeneous temporal scales is fundamental for secure and efficient grid operations. Due to the distinct characteristics of forecasting tasks at varying time scales, existing research has predominantly focused on single-scale models, limiting the exploration of practical, unified multi-scale forecasting frameworks and underutilizing cross-scale dependencies. To address these gaps, this study proposes a unified multi-scale framework for one-step forecasting across multiple resolutions. A scale-aware shared Transformer encoder with learnable scale embeddings is introduced, offering a unified parameterization that facilitates effective cross-scale information exchange. Resolution-specific branches are employed to align model capacity with the statistical properties of each scale. Additionally, a resolution-adaptive training objective is formulated, utilizing scale-specific loss functions with tunable weights to balance robustness, convergence, and inter-task optimization. Evaluations on real-world load data across three temporal resolutions demonstrate the framework's capability to deliver comparatively accurate forecasts across scales.

title: SlideBERT-DTI: An improved framework using Deep learning-based approach for Drug-Target interaction prediction
authors: Ashima Mittal, Poonam Rani, Ankush Jain
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: The discovery of novel Drug-Target Interactions (DTIs) is essential for accelerating drug discovery and repositioning, particularly as resistance to existing antibiotics grows. Traditional in vitro and in vivo screening methods, though reliable, are resource-intensive and slow. We propose framework named "SlideBERT-DTI" that fuses transformer-based embeddings with convolutional neural networks to predict DTIs directly from drugs sequence and protein sequence. First, SMILES strings and amino-acid sequences are embedded via ChemBERTa and ProtBERT, respectively. To overcome the 512-token limit of BERT models on long protein sequences, we apply a sliding-window segmentation with overlap, ensuring full coverage and contextual feature extraction. Each modality’s embeddings are passed through a three-layer 1D CNN (with integrated attention and dropout) to capture local patterns and reduce dimensionality. Following feature fusion, a fully connected network produces binary interaction predictions. We address label imbalance in the Davis dataset via SMOTE oversampling and fine-tune hyper-parameters. This work demonstrates that combining advanced transformer embeddings, CNN feature extractors, and robust data-handling techniques can markedly improve the model’s performance in DTI prediction.

title: Enhanced Energy Management Scheme for Secure, Emission-Conscious, and Optimized Operation of Networked Microgrids
authors: Ahmed N. Sheta, S.M. Suhail Hussain
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: The growing imperative to address environmental concerns and reduce dependence on fossil fuels is accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy systems. In this context, microgrids (MGs), which integrate diverse distributed energy resources (DERs) such as solar photovoltaics and wind turbines, are emerging as a robust and eco-friendly energy solution. Despite their advantages, the inherent intermittency of renewable sources and the dynamic nature of grid conditions introduce complexities in maintaining stable supply-demand balance, reducing emissions, and ensuring cost-efficient MG operation. To this end, this paper proposes an intelligent energy management system (EMS) scheme integrated with machine learning (ML) to enhance forecasting accuracy, optimize energy scheduling, and support reliable and low-emission operation of networked MGs (NMGs). The proposed system uses ML models trained on historical generation profiles, weather patterns, and grid parameters to predict renewables generation. These forecasts are integrated into an optimization model, solved using the Hippopotamus Optimizer (HO), to obtain optimal DER dispatch and energy transactions among NMGs. Detailed case studies highlight the effectiveness of the proposed scheme in maintaining energy balance, reducing emissions, and improving system reliability, highlighting its potential to support the deployment of sustainable and resilient networks.

title: Energy Efficient Cluster-Based Protocol with Primary and Secondary Cluster Head Selection for Wireless Sensor Networks
authors: Amit, Geeta Hanji
publish: 2025 2nd Asia Pacific Conference on Innovation in Technology (APCIT)
abstract: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) are utilized in several applications that require considerable reliability and fault tolerance properties. However, the operation of a WSN may not only rely on the coverage area or network density, but the components of each node must also continuously operate without hardware failures, and the power must last through energy-efficient deployment schemes. Any break could terminate all operations of the networking application, which certainly would be unacceptable in mission-critical applications outside of research applications. There are time delays for user-generated data to travel back through a WSN to a host platform; therefore, when you have long-term application failures, you can experience several outcomes (loss of data, operational confusion, etc.). We propose a Energy energy-efficient cluster-based Protocol with Primary and Secondary Cluster Head Selection for Wireless Sensor Networks to maximize WSN’s energy efficiency and reliability.In the proposed work, we leverage a dynamic clustering mechanism whereby each cluster has a designated SCH that will immediately take over the primary Cluster Head (CH) responsibilities upon complete failure of the CH. Our proposed architecture allows uninterrupted functionality to allow the proposed WSN to maximize its energy efficiency and reliability. Simulation results over multiple rounds demonstrate that the proposed Energy Efficient Cluster-Based Protocol (EECBP) retains all 50 nodes active and conserves more energy, maintaining a consistent 0.02J average advantage by Round 50. Starting with 0.499J in Round 1, EECBP concludes with 0.486J, showing only a 2.6% energy depletion. Between Rounds 20–35, EECBP exhibited minimal energy expiry of just 0.005 J, further highlighting its efficiency. The protocol’s use of energy-to-distance-aware cluster head selection, periodic re-clustering, and SCH contributes to stable energy distribution and network longevity. These results validate EECBP’s reliability for real-world WSN applications.

title: AQGSM: Efficient Multimodal AVQA via Ensemble Transformer Optimization
authors: Samyuktha Jagannath, G. Anitha
publish: 2025 2nd Global AI Summit - International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Emerging Technology (AI Summit)
abstract: Audio-Visual Question Answering (AVQA) requires reasoning across the interdependencies of the audio and visual modalities, therefore, making the task more challenging than the traditional Visual Question Answering (VQA). Current architectures such as the Collective QuestionGuided Network (CQGN) can perform well on benchmark tasks such as MUSICAVQA; however, these systems are hindered by redundancy in question generation and large computation costs. In response to the short-comings, we propose an augmented CQGN architecture with an Adaptive Question Generation and Selection Module (AQGSM). It is a module that applies an ensemble of transformer encoders, namely, DeBERTa-v3-base, RoBERTa-base, ELECTRA-base, and BERT-base, with the weight of 0.28, 0.25, 0.24, and 0.23 respectively, to make use of context-sensitive question generation and selection.Image representations are acquired through the CLIP ViT-B/32 encoder, whereas audio representations are acquired through Wav2Vec 2.0, and the two modalities are then combined in a multimodal integration component.With a systematic hyperparameter search on a 45,000 instance AVQA corpus, the weighted ensemble accuracy is 93.24 percentage on the validation split, and the expected performance can be improved to 94.7 percent with additional optimization. Additionally, the architecture has lowered inference time to 0.65s/batch.These findings imply an improved efficiency, scalability, and usability over baseline systems like CQGN (77 per cent accuracy) and the MAV-LLM/LAVA pipelines.

title: FIF-Drone: A Forensic Investigation Framework for Drones
authors: N. M. Istiak Chowdhury, Ragib Hasan
publish: 2025 IEEE World Forum on Public Safety Technology (WF-PST)
abstract: The increasing use of drones in public safety, agriculture, and surveillance presents new challenges in security, accountability, and safety. Although drones operate autonomously or semi-autonomously, ensuring the integrity of their operational data and enabling forensic investigation in the event of an incident is critical. In this paper, we present FIF-Drone, a forensic investigation framework that collects, stores, and verifies logs of drone operations to ensure public safety. The framework integrates secure log, integrity verification, and proof generation to facilitate post-incident investigations. FIF-Drone prevents tampering with critical operational logs and ensures transparency during safety and security investigations, making it a valuable tool for incident resolution, insurance claims, and compliance with aviation regulations. We perform security analysis and experimental evaluation to demonstrate the feasibility of FIFDrone in different adversarial scenarios.

title: Securing UAV Communication Systems: Architectures, Attacks and Solutions
authors: Mouad Moustahcine, Tomader Mazri
publish: 2025 8th International Conference on Networking, Intelligent Systems &amp; Security (NISS)
abstract: Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) or drones are irreplaceable in various industries, including surveillance, agriculture, and logistics, due to their multi-functionality and capability to reach inaccessible zones. Such systems, nevertheless, are prone to cyber-attacks such as GPS spoofing, RF jamming and Denial of Service (DoS) attacks by virtue of utilizing wireless communication. This study provides various communication architectures of UAVs through the comparison of centralized and decentralized systems. Furthermore, it compares well-known wireless technologies such as Wi-Fi, LTE, 5G, and satellite communication. Significant security vulnerabilities which are applicable to UAVs, GCS, and communication channels are also highlighted in this paper with a special focus on potential threats such as eavesdropping of information, unauthorized access, and signal jamming. In order to yield a solution for these threats, this paper provides a collaborative security solution that employs blockchain for tamper-evident and secure information sharing and AI for adaptive responses and real-time detection of threats. With the help of cryptographic algorithms, authentication protocols, and dependable communication protocols, this paper focuses on effective security to offer confidentiality, integrity, and availability of UAV operations. As daily UAV uses grow, it becomes increasingly important to reinforce their cybersecurity posture to ensure operational reliability amidst sophisticated and ever-growing hostile operating conditions.

title: Assessing Grid Stability Under Direct Wind Power Charging of Electric Vehicles
authors: Xiangyang Wang, Xue Jun Li
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Energy Technologies for Future Grids (ETFG)
abstract: With the growing integration of renewable energy and electric vehicles (EVs), power systems are facing increasing challenges in maintaining voltage stability, frequency regulation, and power balance. The intermittent nature of wind power, coupled with the impulsive demand for clustered EV charging, can significantly disrupt grid operation. This paper proposes a wind-priority charging strategy in which wind power is directly used to charge EVs when available, with the grid providing supplementary power during shortages, and excess wind energy fed back to the grid. A comprehensive simulation model is developed in MATLAB/Simulink to evaluate and compare three operating scenarios—grid-only operation, grid-supplied EV charging, and wind-priority EV charging with grid support. Key stability indicators, including frequency response, voltage deviation, and active/reactive power fluctuations, are analyzed. Simulation results indicate that the proposed strategy effectively mitigates grid disturbances, reduces power fluctuation impacts, and improves system dynamic performance, offering a practical approach for the coordinated operation of wind power and EV loads.
